# **What is Blendata Enterprise?**

        The all-in-one platform seamlessly manages big data processing and streamlines data management tasks with a user-friendly, no-code web application. Users can effortlessly navigate and manipulate their big data for an end-to-end process, ensuring a hassle-free experience. Enabling user to explore and discover the full potential of their big data resources. Through a set of four key features, it transforms raw data into a powerful asset for comprehensive data management and analytics. Users can leverage these features to extract valuable insights, make data-driven decisions, and optimize their operations.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384544/Blendata-Enterprise-1.png?api=v2)

# **Data Ingestion**

        Connect and collect the data from the various sources and difference data formats into the Blendata platform for effective data processing and further analysis. The platform offers three distinct data importing options.

### [**Import Data**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data)

There are three main types of data importing as following:

**1. Internal table:** To replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data preparation. With this internal type able to control the processing performance without any unexpected circumstances.

**2. External table:** Virtual table that the users prefer to retain their data on their own server while allowing the platform to establish a direct connection with the customer's data table. In this setup, we keep only the data structure or schema, ensuring data privacy and security.

3.**Chang Data Capture(CDC)**: Another type of internal table for the RDBMS source that has operational change such as insert, update, or delete in the source data will be captured and apply these changes in the internal table on our platform.

**Supported Source Data**

- **Flat Files**: Plain text, Shape, CSV, JSON, Excel, XML, ORC, and Parquet.

- **Database**: Oracle, MS SQL, MySQL, Cassandra, Mongo DB, and other RDBMS through JDBC/ODBC connector.

- **Others:** Cloud Storage, Kafka Stream, REST API, Google Sheet, File Path, and Search Engine.

### **Import Options**

**1. Streaming:** Near real-time events/messages by using Kafka service. The user able to set the interval for data streaming.(minimum 60 seconds).

**2.** **Schedule:** Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

**3. One Time:** a one time import the data into the platform.

---

# **Data Management**

        The user able to mange the data asset file through this Blendata Enterprise platform including preview your data asset, access management, data security, and data service management.

### **Data Catalog**

This section specifies the data location within your Schema for importing into the platform. Once imported, this data is referred to as a 'Data Asset.' You can view comprehensive details for each Data Asset, including source file type, field names, data previews, shared users, and the data journey. Additionally, users can manage access authorizations and collaboration settings on this page.

**Schemas** are set up by the Super Administrator. Users can view specific sets of data assets based on their schema permissions.

### **Data Access Management**

Grant access to data assets at the role or user level within the Data Catalog page. The owner able to authorize the team member with the access for each data asset with four operational permissions such as **View, Edit Role, Delete,** and **Manage Role.**

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |

                     Slide to see the screenshots.

### **Security (Internal Importing Data)**

**Data Encryption:** Implemented as a robust security measure to deter unauthorized access and data theft attempts. In the event of any unconventional access methods, the data will only be visible in its encrypted form and safeguarding sensitive information.

**Row and column level security:** This security approach offers two distinct protections.

     For column level security, the platform provides the user-interface to setup the security. Firstly, users are restricted from viewing specific columns within the data asset called as **"Secret level"**. Secondly, "**Confidential level"** which the field value are masked to protect sensitive information but these masked column values remain workable for data processing.

     With the advance mode for row and column level security, using the SQL function in Data Exploration to restrict the visibility of specific rows or columns by specifying roles or users within the SQL commands.

### **Management Service (Internal Importing Data)**

To create the automate the service job. In case the user don't set the management service. The platform will use the default setting for initial setup.

     - **Data Compaction:** To reduce the space of storage by dividing the data into difference stage including Hot, Warm, and Cold.

     - **Data Refresh:** Refresh the data in the data asset.

     - **Data Recovery:** Refresh the data in the partition.

     - **Data Retention:** Remove the data when it reach the specific time.

     - **Delta Optimize:** To improve the performance by merging the storage data file.

---

# **Data Processing**

        The various provided operational functions to extract the data with NO-CODE or advanced mode with SQL command. Moreover, the user is able to perform the data transformation combining the data set together, cleaning the data, or even creating the advance analytics for the meaningful insights.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |

Slide to see the screenshots.

### **Data Extract Features**

**Simple Query:** The way of querying data without coding, the user can select the data asset(table) and add the conditions to get the number of the matching record with the percentage of the result. Also able to download the data into your laptop with CVS and PDF file.

**Data Preparation:** To prepare the data for ready to use for visualization or new data asset creation. The user can combine more than two data assets via join function and specific columns in the new data asset.

**Data Exploration and Search mode:** With the advanced query mode, the user is able to use SQL command to explore the data (result limitation is 3,000 records). For search mode, you can input the keyword then, the system will display the matching value from the data table.

**SQL Editor**: For advanced users, who are familiar with the SQL command. Provide the data asset information and enable copy data table name or field name and replace the query in the workspace.

**Notebook:** Creating advanced analytics solutions menu. This handles creating analytic pipelines with PySpark and Scala, in which it leverages the benefit of spark computational resource management.

### **Data Extract Tables**

**Aggregate and View table:** Once you export the data into the table, there are two types including Aggregate and View table. Aggregate table will create a new data asset; on the other hand, the View table saves only the SQL command and automatically updates after opening this View table.

     The platform also provides the Data Processing Service menu which help scheduling and updating the aggregate table and set the alert notification (API).

---

# **Data Utilization**

The Workflow Management feature introduces powerful capabilities for optimizing processes through precise task sequencing, Success and Fail condition control, and streamlined chained job execution. Users gain greater flexibility and efficiency in organizing complex workflows, arranging tasks with precision, and ensuring automated execution proceeds uninterrupted until successful completion or specified fail conditions are met.

![WF with color condition.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384544/WF%20with%20color%20condition.png?api=v2)

        Turn your data asset into visualization file with serval chart options, export your data to do further step, or publish your data asset to the third party via API.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384544/Demo.png?api=v2)

**Visualization:** To turn the data into various types of visualization including standard charts (bar, horizontal, pie, bubble, line, and etc.) and advance charts (Word Could, Topology, Area, Sankey, Leaflet map, or Heat map). Moreover, the user can create the dashboard with the charts and able to generate the URL for the dashboard.

**Export Data:**Export the data into flat file type, file path, and database with schema.

**API:** Our platform offers authorized users the capability to securely share data assets with external applications. You can generate data asset links for API integration. Once data assets and visualizations are published, they can be exported to third-party applications via URL links without the need for additional permissions

# Quick Start Guide

To learn about getting started with the Blendata Enterprise platform via a quick tour video to utilize your big data from raw data and turn data into meaningful insights.

<https://youtu.be/ApJiSHlCGUw?feature=shared>

In this guide, using the CSV file as example for data importing and we will go through all the main features of our platform. To show how our platform can help you with the Big data solution and get your insights. You can download the example files below and follow the guide instruction to get your dashboard.

[sale\_all\_country.csv](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120384653/Quick+Start?preview=%2F2120384653%2F2120384688%2Fsale_all_country.csv)[sale\_all\_country2.csv](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120384653/Quick+Start?preview=%2F2120384653%2F2120384679%2Fsale_all_country2.csv)

## **Step 1**: Import and Transform Data

To import the CSV files into the Blendata platform, the user need to identify the Delimiter, Quote, and EscapeTo integrate the data into the Blendata Enterprise platform. We support a variety of data sources. For other source types, see more [Import data](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data).

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Dataset Internal Table**“ menu to import the data into the platform.
3. Select “**Upload File**“ option.
4. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next: .
5. Select the File type as “**CSV**“ and identify the pattern of data such as delimiter, quote, or escape as per your data format. For more on CSV file components, see more the [CSV file](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2230648866/CSV) page.

   ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384653/auto%20discovery.png?api=v2)
6. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
7. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
8. Preview your data table, fill in the table information such as Table name, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), and [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning). Then, save this new data asset.
9. Click “**Data Catalog”** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

---

## **Step 2**: Manage and Protection Data

Once your new data asset is ready. You are able to manage the data access right by role or user level by setting the operation permission for this data asset in Data Catalog page. Then your members can access your data asset and work with this data.

1. Click your data asset. Your data asset information will pop up at the below the screen.
2. Click “**Shared**“ tab :share: to manage the authorization
3. Set up the access for role level

   1. Click ”**Manage Roles**” button :role: and select a role to set the operation permission.

      ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384653/roles%20desc.png?api=v2)
      1. **View Role**: Can see the information and preview of the data asset only.
      2. **Edit Role:** Can see the information, preview, edit the description, upload a new table, and edit tag of the data asset but this role is unable to delete the data
      3. **Delete Role**: Can access the data table and delete the data but cannot edit the data asset.
      4. **Manage Role**: Can access, edit, and delete the data asset. Moreover the user is able to see the security option to manage the user and role of the member.
   2. Click “**Save**“ :save: to update the permission.
4. Set up the access by each user level

   1. Click ”**Manage Users**” button :users: and select your member user to set the operation permission, the permission definition is similar to role level.
   2. Click “**Save**“ to update the permission.

In this platform, the suitable role is already set for each person based on the responsibility since the project setup and each role has different action. In each data asset, the owner able to grant the access to some roles or some person and add more permissions for each role and the user as well.

For Administrator role, please see more information about role management via User management menu, see the [**user management**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201420007/User+Management) page.

---

## **Step 3**: Processing Data

In case you want to create the new data asset(new data table) by combining two data assets and organize the fields, the platform provides the workspace with the Data Preparation feature that allow the user to do a data transformation in the platform.

1. Go to “**Data Preparation**“ menu :prep:.
2. Click “**Create Workspace**“ button :n\_prep:.
3. Search and select the available datasets, able to select multiple data assets. Then click “**Add Selected**“ button :add\_selected:.
4. Drag and drop your selected datasets into the workspace. Dataset on the workspace is called **Data Node**.
5. Click one dataset and click “**Action**“ button :action: and select the “**Add Connect**“ :connect: to create the connection node in the workspace.
6. Set the link between dataset and the connection node. Then choose the join types(Inner join, Full join, Left join, or Right join) and condition by selecting the same field value.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384653/connect%20node.png?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384653/join.png?api=v2)

7. Click connection node. Go to “**Action**“ button and click “**Add Refine Dataset**“ :refine:.
8. Select the field name into **Select \*** part and Click “**Preview Result**“ button :preview: to preview your new data.
9. Click ”**Save As**” button :save\_as: to save the updated data preparing workspace. The user can add more data assets or change the join condition in this data workspace page.
10. Click the refine node. Go to “**Action**“ and “**Save as Table**“ :save\_table:. Then click “**Save as aggregate table**“ option:agg\_table: to create new data asset.
11. Select the Save Mode as **Save as new table**.

There are two options for data saving, which are Save to an existing table and Save as new table.

1. **Save to an existing table**: Save this data into the existing table, and you need to configure the save mode in the Aggregate Settings, choosing between the append or overwrite actions.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Append**: add new records at the end of the existing data.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Overwrite**: replace the entire data records with the new records.

2. **Save as new table**: Create the new data asset.

7. Fill in the data asset information and click “**Create**“ button :create:.
8. The new data asset will show on the **Data Catalog** page :catalog: .

---

## **Step 4**: Data Utilization (File and Visualization Dashboard)

Once the data transforming process is done, the user can export the data to excel file or create the dashboard with bar chart or visualization files.

#### (info) **Export the data to the excel file.**

1. Go to Data Preparation menu :prep:. Open your workspace and click the refine node.
2. Click “**Preview Result**“ button :preview: and click “**Action**“ button :action:.
3. Click “**Export Result**“ :export\_result: and click “**Export File**“ :export\_file:.
4. Fill in the information of this new file and select the File type as **Excel**.

   1. Several file types for data exporting such as Excel, CSV, JSON, Plain text, and Parquet.
   2. Able to limit the record number or export all records.
5. Then click “**Export**“ button :export: .
6. Click ”**Check Exported File**” :check\_export: to see the export process status on the first page in **Recent Export Data** part.
7. If the status is **SUCCESS** :success:, you can download the file by clicking the download icon:download:.

#### (info) **Create the dashboard.**

1. To create a new dashboard, Go to Project Management menu :proj: . There are the three components under Project management as following:

   1. **Project**: The top level of project management that help to organize the application.
   2. **Application**: The subset of project, which the project owner can manage the user access, who can access the application.
   3. **Dashboard**: The location of the visualizations that the user can add into the dashboard for data analysis.
2. Click “**Create New**” button :create\_new: and “**Create Project**“ option :project: . Then put the project name, description, and project image.
3. Create a new Application by clicking “**Create New**“ button :create\_new: and “**Create Application**“ option :app\_create: . Put the application name, description, and application image.
4. Create a new Dashboard by clicking “**Create New**“ button :create\_new: and “**Create Dashboard**“ option :dashboard: . Put the Dashboard name, description, and dashboard image.
5. Enable “**Edit mode**” :edit\_mode: and click “**Add Viz**“ button :add\_viz: to create the visualization file.
6. Click “**Create New**“ button :create\_new\_viz: and click “**Add Dataset**“ button :dataset:.
7. Select “**Simple Mode**“ and search your dataset name “sale\_all\_country”. Then click “**Add**“ button :add\_bt:.
8. Select the chart type as **bar chart** and identify field for the X and Y axis. To set charts style or decorate your chart, you can click at “**Option**“ tab:options:.

   1. **Y axis of bar chart** is numeric that can apply the operator functions such as sum, count, average, min, max, or specific your own function.
   2. **X axis of bar chart** is the type or display data.
   3. **Split by**: Select the filed name and the bar will appear in the chart.
   4. **Sorting**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order.
   5. **Custom Sorting**: Sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
   6. **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
   7. **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.
9. Click “**Preview Result**“ button :preview: to see the chart.

   1. To show all the data in the chart tick “**All time select**” box :all\_time:.
10. Click “**Save As**“ button :save\_as:to save the new visualization and put the visualization name or description.
11. Back to Project Management menu :proj: and go to your Dashboard. Then enable “**Edit mode**” to add the visualization file.
12. Click “**Add Viz**“ button :add\_viz: and select your visualization to add into your dashboard.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384653/bar%20chart.png?api=v2)

13. You can organize your dashboard with the the chart files. The user able to rearrange, resize the chart, add more visualizations, or add text.

    ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384653/Demo.png?api=v2)

All in all, once you complete these entire steps, you are able to utilize your raw data and produce the insight visualization with the key features in our Blendata Enterprise.

---

This is landing page, containing main features information, your recent activity in Blendata and a list of recently exported data.

## Recent Update

All your updated dashboard list is accessible, allowing you to click directly on a dashboard for editing or reviewing your visualization files. Additionally, your favorite dashboards will also appear on this page.

![landing page 2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384977/landing%20page%202.png?api=v2)

## Recent Export Data

All your exported file data is available for download, and you can save these files in file formats such as excel or CSV file.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120384977/my%20profiles2.png?api=v2)

- **Status**: The status of the export process.
- **File Name**: The file name.
- **Query**: The SQL command of your data.
- **Timerange**: The time range of your data.
- **File Format**: The output file format of your data.
- **Create Date**: The create date of this exported data file.
- **Action**: To download the exported file, which can be zip or file types.

# Explore and Process Data Features

## Simple Query (GUI Filter)

The way of querying data without coding, the user can select the data asset(table) and add the conditions to get the number of the matching record with the percentage of the result. Also able to download the data into your laptop with CVS and PDF file. For more information about how to create a simple query, see more [Simple Query](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385048/Simple+Query) page.

## Data Exploration (SQL)

**Data Exploration and Search mode:** With the advanced query mode, the user is able to use SQL command to explore the data (result limitation is 3,000 records). For search mode, you can input the keyword then, the system will display the records with the matching value from the data table. See more details, go to [Data Exploration](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385111/Data+Exploration) page.

The supported SQL syntax document is available on the Blendata website. For more information, go to the [website](https://blendata.com/blendata-enterprise-data-type-and-sql-syntax-guide/).

## Data Preparation

**Data Preparation** helps the user to prepare the data to use for visualization or the creation of new data assets. Users can merge multiple data assets using the join function and specify columns in the resulting data asset. There are 3 components including Search, Create Workspace, and Workspace. See more details, go to [Data Preparation](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385206/Data+Preparation) page.

## SQL Editor

Improve the Data Exploration menu, advanced users, who are familiar with the SQL command. Provide the data asset information and enable copy data table name or field name and replace the query in the workspace. See more details, go to [SQL Editor](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385279/SQL+Editor) page.

The supported SQL syntax document is available on the Blendata website. For more information, go to the [website](https://blendata.com/blendata-enterprise-data-type-and-sql-syntax-guide/).

## Notebook

**Notebook** in Blendata is a tool for creating advanced analytics solutions. It handles creating analytic pipelines with PySpark and Scala, in which it leverages the benefit of spark computational resource management.

Use cases that may require using Blendata Notebook include but are not limited to building supervised or unsupervised model, advanced data modeling, and handling stream of data. For more information about notebook, see more [Notebook](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385372/Notebook) page.

This notebook features can be found in Blendata version 4.0 and above.

---

# Action

## Create Aggregate Table

An aggregate table preserves the data result from the SQL query and stores it in our platform's storage as an Internal table. See more how to create an aggregate table.

## Create View Table

A view table stores only the SQL query used to create this table from the selected data asset table extraction.

## Export File

Within Blendata Enterprise, the majority of functions support exporting data to various file formats. We provide instructions on exporting your data to CSV, JSON, Plain Text, Parquet, and Excel. Once exported, the data asset file can be accessed from your My Profile page. See the instruction how to export the file, go to [Export File](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385706/Export+File) page.

## Export to External RDBMS

Our platform also provides the export option to user that the user can export the data to an external database including RDBMS and NoSQL. See the instruction how to export the file, go to [Export to External Database](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385760/Export+to+External+Database) page.

---

## Simple Query

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
