# How to Create a New Simple Query

1. Click “**Create New**“ button and fill in the Query details.

![image-20251026-092824.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385048/image-20251026-092824.png?api=v2)

- **Query Name**: Simple Query Asset name.
- **Description**: The description of simple query asset.
- **Dataset**: Select the dataset for query.
- **Conditions**: Set the conditions by filtering the field name.
- **Estimate**: Button to process the query and show the result at query result.
- **Estimate Result**: Percentage value of number of the record from the query process.

  - **Preview Query Result**: show the result as a table style.

    - **Refresh Result**: To update the latest result.
    - **Download Result**: To download the data into your device with the record limitation as per setup in the interface and able to select the file types as CSV and PDF.
    - **Export All Result**: To export the result data, follow the three methods below.

      - **Export File:** Export the Data Asset as File type. The exported data asset file will appear in the [My Profile](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2198863965/My+Profile) page. For more information about export file, see [Export File](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385706/Export+File) page.
      - **Export to External storage**: Export the data to external storage by filling in the URL such as Amazon S3 or Azure blob Storage.
      - **Export to Database**: Export the data to database by putting the URL of the Database such as My SQL or No SQL. See the instruction how to export to database, go to [Export to External Database](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385760/Export+to+External+Database) page.
  - **Export**: This section applies the same three export methods as described above in “**Export All Result**”.

During the “**Estimate Result**” process, you will see the percentage of data being downloaded. If any problem occurs, you can perform the “**Kill this query job**” action.

2. Click “**Save Query**“ to save the new query and your new query will show on the interface.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385048/sq3.png?api=v2)

Moreover, the user able to select other actions for the query as per below.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385048/sq1.png?api=v2)

- **Copy Query**: Copy the selected query and duplicate as a new query.
- **Delete Query**: Delete the selected simple query asset.
- **Save as Table**

  - **Save as view table**: Save the SQL command and create as a new table by using this query condition.
  - **Save as aggregate table**: Create a new [aggregate table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385677/Aggregate+Table) with the result from query. (The data will be stored in our storage and can see this table on the Data Catalog page.)
  - **Create aggregate service**: Create a new aggregate table with the result from query and able to setup the service to update the aggregate table automatically.

**Note**: When overwrite mode is selected for aggregate table, data processing cannot be performed during the overwrite operation. The time required to overwrite data into the aggregate table will depend on your resources.

![save as table.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385048/save%20as%20table.png?api=v2)

- **Export Result**:

  - **Export File:** Export the data as a file you can see your export file on the [My Profile](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2198863965/My+Profile) page. For more information about export file, see more [Export File](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385706/Export+File) page.
  - **Export to External storage**: Export the data to external storage by filling in the URL such as Amazon S3 or Azure blob Storage.
  - **Export to Database**: Export the data to database by putting the URL of the Database such as My SQL or No SQL. See the instruction how to export to database, go to [Export to External Database](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385760/Export+to+External+Database) page.

![export result.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385048/export%20result.png?api=v2)

---

## Data Exploration

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385111/Data+Exploration |
| **Last modified** | 2026-04-01T08:52:56.728Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data |

There are two modes for Data Exploration: Query mode and Search mode. In Query mode, users have access to a query command workspace to write SQL queries and view the resulting data. The other mode allows users to search for records using keywords from the data table.

# Data Exploration Query Mode

![Data explo-20251026-081847.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/Data%20explo-20251026-081847.png?api=v2)

1. Select the data asset table from the dropdown list.
2. Set the number of records in the Limit field (default: 3000) to limit the records displayed in the query result.
3. Select the desired time range period (default: Last 7 Days).

![image-20251010-051227.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/image-20251010-051227.png?api=v2)

3. Write the SQL command in the query panel, which supports some SQL commands based on Spark version 3.5.3. For more details, please refer to this [website](https://blendata.com/blendata-enterprise-data-type-and-sql-syntax-guide/).

   - For data assets under the default schema, users can use the table name as usual. However, for data assets in other schemas, users must specify them as “**schema\_name.table\_name**”.
   - The user can restore the previous query or old queries from the **Query History** button. See more details, Go to [Query History](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2120385111/Data+Exploration#Query-History) part.
   - You can also format the query from the ![image-20251026-091509.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/image-20251026-091509.png?api=v2) button by selecting “**Format Query”** or following keyboard shortcuts in the query panel:

| Action | For Windows | For Mac |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Comment and Uncomment content | `Ctrl + - + /` | `Command + - + /` |
| Format query (auto adjust line breaks and indentation) | `Ctrl + Shift + F` | - `⌘ + Shift + F` |

4. Click “**Run Query**“ and the result will display on Query Result.

The platform can execute one query at a time. If there are many queries on the query panel, the user able to highlight one query and run the query.

5. Save the query for future used by click “**Save Query**“ button and put the query name and description.
6. Download or Export the query result.

![de8.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/de8.png?api=v2)

## Data Exploration Search Mode

![Data explo search-20251026-083250.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/Data%20explo%20search-20251026-083250.png?api=v2)

1. Select the data asset table from the dropdown list.
2. Set the number of records in the Limit field (default: 3000) to limit the records displayed in the query result.
3. Select the desired time range period (default: Last 7 Days).

![image-20251010-051722.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/image-20251010-051722.png?api=v2)

- Query should not exceed 200MB: Records maximum is 3000 (Show the record as per SQL Query) 200MB is data response from query extraction from Zeus, which can be edit on Hera config/application.properties.

```
#maximum response length in bytes
query.response.limit.maxLength.enable=true
query.response.maxLength.bytes=209715200
query.response.limit=2000
```

3. Put the keyword on the search panel by following the **Text Search Instruction**.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/de6.png?api=v2)

4. Click “**Run Query**“ to show the search result.
5. Download or Export the query result.

When users use **Search** **Mode** and switch to **Query Mode**, the platform will convert the wording search to SQL command in query panel.

---

## Advanced Options

To select a Zeus and Pools for data processing, users can choose a Zeus or use the default Zeus provided by the platform.

1. Click :se\_setting: button then select “**Compute Options**” to open the “**Advanced Options**” dialog, where you can select the Zeus and Pools.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/se4.png?api=v2)

2. Click **Submit**.

---

# Query Management

## Saved Query

When the user click “**Saved Query**“ button ☰ and click three dots for query actions such as Edit, Share, and Delete.

![de4.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/de4.png?api=v2)

- **Edit:** Edit the query information such as query name and description.
- **Share:** Share the saved query to other collaboration by choosing roles or users and also can put the notification massage to your team.

When the you shares the query to others, the receiver has to click “**Add to Save Query List**“ on the notification box.

- **Delete:** Delete the saved query.

## Query History

The platform will store the historical query and user able to restore the historical query via this menu.

1. Click “**Query History**“ button.

![de5.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/de5.png?api=v2)

2. Select your recent query and click “**Select**“ button.
3. Your selected query is displayed on the query panel.

---

# Result Management

## Add Function

The user able to hide or mask the value of field in the query result, which is sensitive or important information such as Telephone number or bank account number. Click **setting icon** on the field and click “**Function**“

![de mask.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/de%20mask.png?api=v2)

- **Alias Name**: The new filed name after apply the function.
- **Function 1**: The function options for user to select.

  - **Change Format:** To rearrange the data to be more easier for reading like revise the data format of telephone number.
  - **Masking Value:** Hide the sensitive or important information.

![de mask1.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385111/de%20mask1.png?api=v2)

The supported data type for each function.

| **Type of data** | **Change Format** | **Masking Value** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| General Text data | (error) | (tick) |
| Email data | (error) | (tick) |
| Timestamp data | (tick) | (tick) |
| Telephone Number data | (tick) | (tick) |
| Credit Card data | (tick) | (tick) |

Alias name value must be A-Z, a-z, 0-9, or \_.

---

# Export the Data Result

## Download Result

Download the data into your device/laptop with the maximum record limit setting from Query page. The user need to fill in the details as per below:

- **Filename:** Name of the output file
- **File Type:** Type of the output file (CSV or PDF).

## Save as Table

- **Save as view table:** Save the SQL command in term of View Table to use the same SQL query for the result.
- **Save as aggregate table:** Use the result data from the SQL query to create the new table and keep this data in our storage via Internal table.

**Note**: When overwrite mode is selected for aggregate table, data processing cannot be performed during the overwrite operation. The time required to overwrite data into the aggregate table will depend on your resources.

- **Create aggregate service:** Use the result data from the SQL query to create the new table and keep this data in our storage via Internal table. Then, create the service to update the data of this table with the schedule setting.

## Export Result

Export the result data without any record limitation.

If the data has time series and the user select the time range for export data, the result file will show as per time range setting only.

- **Export File:** Export the Data Asset as File type. The exported data asset file will appear in the [My Profile](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2198863965/My+Profile) page. For more information about Export File, see [Export File](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385706/Export+File) page.
- **Export to External storage**: Export the data to external storage by filling in the URL such as Amazon S3 or Azure blob Storage.
- **Export to Database**: Export the data to database by putting the URL of the Database such as My SQL or No SQL. See the instruction how to export to database, go to [Export to External Database](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385760/Export+to+External+Database) page.

---

## Data Preparation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385206/Data+Preparation |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:36:55.475Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data |

# Prepare your New Data Asset

You can follow the instruction below to prepare your new data asset.

1. Click “**Create Workspace**” :n\_prep: to create the new workspace.
2. Click “**Add Dataset**“ :add\_dataset: to search the available dataset.

3. Select the available datasets, able to select multiple data assets. Then click “**Add Selected**“ button :add\_selected:.
4. Drag and drop your selected datasets into the workspace. Dataset on the workspace is called **Data Node**.

![dp2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385206/dp2.png?api=v2)

5. Create the connection node to all selected data assets.

   1. Drag and drop

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) Drag the first data asset into the workspace.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) Drag the second data asset and drop over the first data asset. Then, the connection node will create automatically.

b. Using **Add Connect** option :connect:.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) Drag all selected data assets into the workspace.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) Click one dataset and click “**Action**“ button :action: and select the “**Add Connect**“ to create the connection node in the workspace.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) Set the link between dataset and the connection node.

![connect node.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385206/connect%20node.png?api=v2)

6. Setup a connection node by setting condition type and create the refine node.

   1. **Join**: Indicates the condition to join both datasets such as join types(Inner join, Full join, Left join, or Right join) and condition by selecting the same field value.

![join.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385206/join.png?api=v2)

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Join Dataset**: Display the two joined datasets name.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Join Type**:

- **Inner Join**: Join two datasets and display only the records that have matching values in both datasets.

- **Full Join**: Join two datasets and display all records including matching values and unmatched values. When no matching rows exist for a row in the left dataset, the columns of the right dataset will have NULLs for those records. Similar to right dataset, if there is no matching rows exist for a row in the right dataset, the columns of the left dataset will have NULLs for those records.

- **Left Join**: Join two datasets and display all matching records likes Inner Join but the platform will show the result from left dataset (first dataset) and the right dataset (second dataset) will display as null. The data from right dataset (second dataset) will show only the matching records with the left dataset.

- **Right Join**: Join two datasets and display all matching records likes Inner Join but the platform will show the result from right dataset (second dataset) and the left dataset (first dataset) will display as null. The data from left dataset (first dataset) will show only the matching records with the right dataset.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Join Condition**: Select the field from both dataset for a condition of two joined datasets.

b. **Union:** Combined both datasets or join and display a duplicated line as one record.

c. **Union all:** Combined both datasets or join the dataset as a one dataset by extracting all data records without checking the duplicated lines.

d. **Actions:** When the user click “**Connection node**”, the platform will show action button on right side.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Add Connection:** To create a new connection node and link with the selected connection node.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Add Refine Dataset:** To create a new Refine node and link with the selected connection node.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Rename:** Rename the selected connection node.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Delete Node:** Delete the selected connection node.

e. **Refine Dataset:**

![dp4.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385206/dp4.png?api=v2)

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Dataset Field List:** The field list of the two joined datasets.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Conditions:** Set the condition for the new data asset.

- Select: Select the field of Dataset to represent in the new data asset.

- Sort: Sort the record data.

**Default Sorting:** Indicate the sorting of the records in the preview data by field name in a Custom Sorting box and follow the default Sorting setting.

**ASC:** sort the data by ascending order.

**DESC:** sort the data by descending order.

**Custom Sorting:** Indicate the record order in the preview data by the fields name in the Custom Sorting box.

**None:** Sort the record by default sorting option.

**ASC:** sort the data by ascending order.

**DESC:** sort the data by descending order.

- Filters: To filter the records in the preview data by setting the filed condition.

f. **Preview**

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Preview Result:** Click to update the records that apply the conditions from Conditions Panel.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Limit:** The maximum number of records to show on the preview result.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Time range:** Select the time range to display the data.

- All Time Select: Select all time range.

- Date-time Select: Specific the time range.

g. **Actions:** When the user click “**Refine Dataset**”, the platform will show the action menu as per below.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Add Connection:** To create a new Connection node and link with selected refine node.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Add Refine Dataset:** To create a new Refine node and link with selected refine node.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Rename:** Rename the selected refine Dataset.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Save as Table**

![save as table.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385206/save%20as%20table.png?api=v2)

- **Save as view table**: Save the SQL command in term of View Table, after the user previews the refine dataset result.

- **Save as aggregate table**: Use the result data from the SQL query, after the user previews the refine dataset result. Then, create the new table and keep this data in our storage via Internal table.

**Note**: When overwrite mode is selected for aggregate table, data processing cannot be performed during the overwrite operation. The time required to overwrite data into the aggregate table will depend on your resources.

- **Create aggregate service**: Use the result data from the SQL query, after the user previews the refine dataset result. Then, create the new table and keep this data in our storage via Internal table. Also, create the service to update the data of this table with the schedule setting.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Export Result:** Export the result data without any record limitation.

If the data has time series and the user select the time range for export data, the result file will show as per time range setting only.

![export result.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385206/export%20result.png?api=v2)

- **Export File:** Export the Data Asset as File type. The exported data asset file will appear in the [My Profile](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2198863965/My+Profile) page. For more information about export file, see [Export File](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385706/Export+File) page.

- **Export to External storage**: Export the data to external storage by filling in the URL such as Amazon S3 or Azure blob Storage.

- **Export to Database**: Export the data to database by putting the URL of the Database such as My SQL or No SQL. See the instruction how to export to database, go to [Export to External Database](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385760/Export+to+External+Database) page.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Delete Node:** Delete the selected Refine Dataset.

7. When the new dataset is ready, the user can save this workspace by clicking “**Save**“ in the current workspace or click “**Save As**“ to save as a new workspace.

---

# Workspace Management

## Open data preparation

To open and use the other workspaces, which you are the owner.

1. Click “**Create Workspace**” :n\_prep: to create the new workspace.
2. Select your workspace.
3. Click “**Open**“.

## Edit Workspace

Revise the Workspace name or the Workspace details.

1. Click action button and click “**Edit**“ button.
2. Revise the name and description of your workspace.

## Delete Workspace

Delete the selected Workspace.

1. Click action button of your selected workspace and click “**Delete**“ button.
2. Revise the name and description of your workspace.

---

## SQL Editor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385279/SQL+Editor |
| **Last modified** | 2026-04-01T08:53:17.552Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data |

# SQL Editor Components

![image-20250828-034746.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/image-20250828-034746.png?api=v2)

- **Data Asset**: The list of all data asset, which displays the data asset information such as schema, data asset name, data asset type, field name and field type of each data asset, and copy action. (can copy the data asset name and field name)

- **My Queries**: Your saved queries.
- **Query History**: Your historical queries linked with the Data Exploration page.
- **TabNew**: A tab to work with SQL query including workspace, action, and the query result.

  - **SQL query**: A workspace to create the SQL query.
  - **Record Limit**: To limit the number of display records. (The default number is 2000 records)
  - **Time Range**: To specific the time range of this SQL command. (The default is last 7 days)
  - :se\_save: **action**: To save this SQL query.
  - :se\_setting: **action**: Select the Zeus to process the SQL query.
  - **Run Query**: Click to execute the query in the SQL query workspace.
  - **Query Result**: Results from the SQL query.
  - **Filter Data**: To filter the record using keyword.
- :plus: **action**: Create a new SQL query tab.
- You can also use the following keyboard shortcuts in the query panel:

| Action | For Windows | For Mac |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Comment and Uncomment content | `Ctrl + - + /` | `Command + - + /` |
| Format query (auto adjust line breaks and indentation) | `Ctrl + Shift + F` | - `⌘ + Shift + F` |

Users can also format the query from the ![image-20251026-091509.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/image-20251026-091509.png?api=v2) button by selecting “**Format Query”**.

---

# SQL Editor Features

## Multi-Tab Workspace

Users can navigate between different open tabs within the workspace. Furthermore, the system retains the current workspace, allowing users to easily pick up where they left off.

1. Click :plus: icon to add a new tab.
2. A new tab and workspace appear on the page.
3. Create your own SQL query in the workspace.

## Simplify the data processing with data structure details

This feature provides data structure details on the left side. Users can view all schemas, data asset names and their corresponding fields. Additionally, users have the capability to copy both data asset and field names directly from the Data Asset tab and use these names in SQL query workspace.

![image-20250926-151827.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/image-20250926-151827.png?api=v2)

## Support SQL Syntax

This menu supports some SQL commands, which are based on Spark version 3.5.3. For more details, please refer to this [website](https://blendata.com/blendata-enterprise-data-type-and-sql-syntax-guide/).

## Store your SQL in My Queries

Save frequently used SQL queries in the **My Queries** menu, allowing users to edit, share, and delete these queries as needed. Additionally, users can utilize the saved queries in the current tab or create a new tab.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/se10.png?api=v2)

- **Edit**: Edit Query Information such as query name and description.
- **Share**: Share the selected query to other roles or users.

1. Select “**Share**” option and the popup will appear.

2. Manage the operational permission for each roles or by users.

3. Click “**Share**“.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/se11.png?api=v2)

- **Delete**: To delete the selected query.
- **Replace Query**: Use the saved query in the current tab or create a new tab.

1. Select the saved query.

2. Replace this query in the current tab or a new tab.

![se16.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/se16.png?api=v2)

## Query History

This section contains historical queries previously executed by users. These queries are associated with the Data Exploration page. Running a query will update the latest historical queries displayed in both the SQL Editor and Data Exploration.

1. Click **History Queries** tab to see all historical query.
2. Select the historical query and users can choose to replace this query in the current tab or a new tab.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/se9.png?api=v2)

---

# SQL Editor Management

## Create New SQL Query

To generate a new SQL query in the workspace, users can write a SQL command in the SQL query workspace.

1. Write the SQL command in the SQL Query workspace. Users can write many queries in the workspace.

For data assets under the default schema, users can use the table name as usual. However, for data assets in other schemas, users must specify them as **schema.data\_asset\_name**.

2. Click the **Run Query** button :runq: to execute your query. If there are multiple queries, users should highlight the desired query and click **Run Query** for data processing.
3. Save this SQL query workspace clicking **Save** button :se\_save: and put your query information likes name and description. Then, your saved query will be saved in the **My Queries** tab.

## Advanced Options

To select a Zeus and Pools for data processing, users can choose a Zeus or use the default Zeus provided by the platform.

1. Click :se\_setting: button then select “**Compute Options**” to open the “**Advanced Options**” dialog, where you can select the Zeus and Pools. (Specified pool option is available for Zeus stand-alone only.)

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/se4.png?api=v2)

2. Click **Submit**.

## Query Result

After running the query, the result will be displayed below the SQL query in a table format. Users can setup a field on the result, download this result, save it as a new table, or export the result in various file types, databases, or external storage. Additionally, users can filter records using keywords and apply functions to each column.

### Field Setting

Select specific fields to display in the result data for filtering; however, when users download the result, all fields will be included.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/se14.png?api=v2)

1. Click:se:button to see the field of result data.
2. Tick or untick the field name.

### Filter Data

To quick search some keyword in the query result.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/se13.png?api=v2)

1. Fill in a keyword in the filter data box.
2. The result will display only records containing this keyword.

### Add Function

The user able to hide or mask the value of field, which is sensitive or important information such as Telephone number or bank account number. Click **setting icon** on the field and click “**Function**“

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/de%20mask.png?api=v2)

- **Alias Name**: The new filed name after apply the function.
- **Function 1**: The function options for user to select.

  - **Change Format:** To rearrange the data to be more easier for reading like revise the data format of telephone number.
  - **Masking Value:** Hide the sensitive or important information.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/de%20mask1.png?api=v2)

The supported data type for each function.

| **Type of data** | **Change Format** | **Masking Value** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| General Text data | (error) | (tick) |
| Email data | (error) | (tick) |
| Timestamp data | (tick) | (tick) |
| Telephone Number data | (tick) | (tick) |
| Credit Card data | (tick) | (tick) |

Alias name value must be A-Z, a-z, 0-9, or \_.

### Result Action

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/se8.png?api=v2)

- **Download Result**: Download the result data file directly in CSV, PDF, or JSON file format.
- **Save as Table**

  - **Save as view table**: Save the SQL command in term of View Table, after the user previews the refine dataset result.
  - **Save as aggregate table**: Use the result data from the SQL query, after the user previews the refine dataset result. Then, create the new table and keep this data in our storage via Internal table.
  - **Create aggregate service**: Use the result data from the SQL query. Then, create the new table and keep this data in our storage via Internal table. Also, create the service to update the data of this table with the schedule setting.

**Note**: When overwrite mode is selected for aggregate table, data processing cannot be performed during the overwrite operation. The time required to overwrite data into the aggregate table will depend on your resources.

![save as table.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/save%20as%20table.png?api=v2)

- **Export Result:** Export the result data without any record limitation.

  If the data has time series and the user select the time range for export data, the result file will show as per time range setting only.

  - **Export File:** Export the Data Asset as File type. The exported data asset file will appear in the [My Profile](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2198863965/My+Profile) page. For more information about export file, see [Export File](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385706/Export+File) page.
  - **Export to External storage**: Export the data to external storage by filling in the URL such as Amazon S3 or Azure blob Storage.
  - **Export to Database**: Export the data to database by putting the URL of the Database such as My SQL or No SQL. See the instruction how to export to database, go to [Export to External Database](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385760/Export+to+External+Database) page.

![export result.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385279/export%20result.png?api=v2)

---

## Notebook

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385372/Notebook |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T11:32:54.451Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data |

# **Notebook Interface Introduction**

From the menu on the left of Blendata, select the menu with Blendata Notebook icon :nb:. This will bring you to the main Notebook page, which display all workspaces as shown

![image-20250828-144820.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-144820.png?api=v2)

The latest “**Selected Zeppelin Environment**” is now remembered even after refreshing the page or switching menus.

The main menu bar are on the top of the page. The main function are shown below.

![image-20250828-145031.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-145031.png?api=v2)

1. ![image-20250801-145918.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250801-145918.png?api=v2) **ZEPPELIN**: select environments.
2. ![image-20250801-095113.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250801-095113.png?api=v2) **Data Assets**: Users can view the list of data assets added to environments.

if this button does not appear, it means there is no need to add any data assets because Zeus and Zeppelin share the same metastore.

In Blendata v4.6.12+, access to the “**Default\_schema**” is restricted for general users.  
Only “**Super Admin**” users can access it.  
This restriction can be disabled if required. <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AgADqw>

![image-20250801-104113.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250801-104113.png?api=v2)

3. **Changing page view**:view additional workspaces.
4. ![image-20250828-145237.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-145237.png?api=v2) **+ Workspace**: create a new workspace.
5. ![image-20250828-145305.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-145305.png?api=v2) **Upload note**: Upload your Notebook file (currently supported formats are .zpln, .ipynb, and zip file for multiple Notebooks), please click to see more details [Upload Note](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2471329794/Workspace+Management#Upload-Note).
6. **Actions** : provide actions to support both Git Workspace and Local Workspace.

   - **Manage Permission**: to assign workspace permission to other users. see more details [Manage Permission](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AYCykw).
   - please click to see another actions for more details [Workspace Actions](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2471329794/Workspace+Management#Workspace-Actions).
7. **Search**: find a workspace.
8. **My Workspaces**: click to show only my workspace.
9. **Sort by**: sort workspaces by Title or Creation Date.

Before version 4.6.9, the Workspace feature was not available in the Notebook, and users could only view individual notes directly. After upgrading to version 4.6.9, all existing notes can be migrated to the “**Shared Workspace**” by [initializing it via the HERA API](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2486501381/Product+Migration+Guide+For+Notebook+workspace+From+Version+4.6.8+to+4.6.9#Step-5%3A-Shared-Workspace-Creation). (This is required not only for upgrades but also for new installations of version 4.6.9.)

---

# **Workspace Creation**

There are two types of workspace creation you can create as below:

1. **Git Workspace**: is connected to a Git repository and provides an area for storing notebooks under a Zeppelin environment. Our platform offers a user-friendly interface to support the connection and synchronization process. See more details in [Sync Notebook Profile between Zeppelin and Git](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/DwCeiQ).

![image-20250829-032437.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250829-032437.png?api=v2)

2. **Local Workspace**: is not connected to Git and provides an area for storing notebooks under a Zeppelin environment.

![image-20250801-155314.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250801-155314.png?api=v2)

See more details about workspace setting, go to [Workspace Management](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2471329794/Workspace+Management) page.

---

# **Actions Details Within Workspace**

Notebook menu provide two types of workspaces for storing notes: Local Workspace and Git Workspace.

![image-20250828-152705.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-152705.png?api=v2)

## Local Workspace

1. **Navigation path**: shows your current location.
2. ![image-20250828-152902.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-152902.png?api=v2) **Kebab menu**: action for workspace.

   1. **Edit Workspace Details**: to edit description or tag for workspace.
   2. **Set as Default**: to set the currently open workspace as default workspace.
   3. **Manage Permission**: to assign workspace permission to other users.
   4. **Delete**: remove workspace.
3. **Sync:** all new notes from the local notebook environment or Git (if available) will be automatically created here, with the sync executor set as the owner.
4. **Changing page view**:view additional notebooks.
5. **+ Notebook**: you can perform two actions including create a new notebook and upload notebook.
6. **Actions**: allow users to perform additional actions like open a notebook, export a notebook, and delete a notebook.
7. **Search**: find a notes.
8. **Only My Assets**: click to show only my notes.
9. **Sort by**: sort notebooks by Title, Creation Date or Last access.

## Git Workspace

![image-20250828-154022.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-154022.png?api=v2)

1. **Navigation path**: shows your current location.
2. ![image-20250828-152902.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-152902.png?api=v2) **Actions menu**: action for workspace.

   1. **Edit Workspace Details**: to edit description or tag for workspace.
   2. **Refresh Git Connection:** Refresh the connection status with the Git repository.
   3. **Git Settings**: Update Git credentials (username and token) when expired or changed.
   4. **Set as Default**: to set the currently open workspace as default workspace.
   5. **Manage Permission**: to assign workspace permission to other users.
   6. **Delete**: remove workspace.

See more details about git workspace actions, go to [Workspace Actions](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2471329794/Workspace+Management#Workspace-Actions).

3. **Sync:** all new notes from the local notebook environment or Git (if available) will be automatically created here, with the sync executor set as the owner.
4. **Branch**: This is the branch location your current Git server.

   1. **Checkout**: Switch the notebook environment by changing the Git branch. This will overwrite all notebooks in the current local environment with those from the selected branch.
   2. **Create New Branch**: To create a new branch in your environment, but the new branch will duplicated from current branch.
   3. **Manage Git Branch**: to manage the Git branch and local branch by deleting the branch in this option
5. **Repo:** show Repository name in Git
6. **Changing page view**:view additional notebooks.
7. **+ Notebook**: you can perform two actions including create a new notebook and upload notebook.
8. **Actions**: allow users to perform additional actions like open a notebook, edit note details, push to git, export a notebook, and delete a notebook.
9. **Search**: find a notes.
10. **Only My Assets**: click to show only my notes.
11. **Sort by**: sort notebooks by Title, Creation Date or Last access.

---

# **Notebook Management**

In Blendata Notebook, the environment is managed via ‘Zeppelin’. The Zeppelin configures the environment on the backend, which some of them include:

- Python Environment
- Data Assets for Notebook
- Spark configurations

This section walk you through the steps of configuring basic components for Blendata Notebook before start using them.

## **Adding Data Asset**

The management of data assets for Zeppelin in Blendata Notebook is managed differently from the Data Catalog function. This is to prevent unwanted action to data asset for advanced transformation or manipulation through scripting within the notebook. To add the data asset, follow the instruction below:

1. Click the “**Add Data Assets**” menu.
2. A pop-up window will appear as shown in the following figure.

The details are as followed:

1. **Search box**: Use this box to search for assets’ name.
2. **Table(s) list**: Current table(s) that the user has permission to and is matching the search conditions.
3. **Add to**: Zeppelin environment to be used.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb4.png?api=v2)

After adding the data asset(s) to Zeppelin environment, user(s) can access those data assets within the Blendata Notebook.

Please note that in some cases, there may be multiple Zeppelin environments due to the system architecture design.

If Zeus and Zeppelin share the same metastore, this action will be disabled, as there is no need to add data assets in that case.

## **Creating Notebook**

Select your workspace and create a new Blendata Notebook, go to “**Create New**” menu and select “**Create New Note**”, as shown in the figure below.

![image-20250828-042018.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-042018.png?api=v2)

The pop-up will appear for filling out the information. The necessary information are as follows:

1. **Note Name**: Fill in your Notebook name.
2. **Note Description**: Fill in the Notebook description.
3. **Tags (Optional)** – This option is useful for organizing notebooks for different purposes.

![image-20250828-042215.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-042215.png?api=v2)

## **Importing Notebook**

To import a new Blendata Notebook or multiple Notebooks, go to “**Create New**” menu and select “**Upload Note**”, as shown in the figure below.

![image-20250926-152122.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250926-152122.png?api=v2)

The pop-up will appear for filling out the information. The necessary information are as follows:

1. **Upload File**: Upload your Notebook file (currently supported formats are .zpln, .ipynb, and zip file for multiple Notebooks)
2. **Tags (Optional)** – This option is useful for organizing notebooks for different purposes.
3. Enabled an option to prevent upload if the Note ID already exists in the current environment.

![image-20250828-042313.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-042313.png?api=v2)

After importing the notebook, the notebook will appear on the main page. Re-check your data asset dependencies before executing the code in the notebook.

## **Exporting Notebook**

Every Blendata Notebook can be exported to either .zpln or .ipynb format. To export the notebook, click:3dots: option on the notebook file, and choose ‘Export’, then select the file format of your choice.

You can select a single Notebook or multiple Notebooks for exporting. If you export multiple Notebooks, the output will be Zip file.

![image-20250828-042706.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-042706.png?api=v2)

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250530-080031.png?api=v2)

## **Notebook List Sorting**

Users can sort the Notebook list via **Sort by** options including Last access (newest first or oldest first), Title (A–Z or Z–A), and Created Date (Newest or Oldest).

![image-20250828-043736.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250828-043736.png?api=v2)![sorting 4.6.6.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/sorting%204.6.6.png?api=v2)

## **Sync Notebook Profile between Zeppelin and Git**

Users are able to reuse code paragraphs across multiple Notebooks with sync the Notebook profile between Zeppelin and Git in both directions. If a new Notebook profile is added to the Git location, it will be updated in Zeppelin to ensure the latest version is available.

User accounts must be set up the **Manage** in the User Management section to manage the Notebook environment. See more details about [how to enable plugin Github storage](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2309783569/Enable+Github+Storage+Plugin+in+Notebook).

![image-20250829-045106.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20250829-045106.png?api=v2)

See more details go to [Sync Notebook Profile between Zeppelin and Git](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=2308833295&draftShareId=6aca2f5e-0e39-4f00-a184-a9c71f0e77d9&atlOrigin=eyJpIjoiN2FjMmUxODUwMWM0NGIwZDg2YzRmMzZkOTZiZjY0OWIiLCJwIjoiYyJ9) page.

---

# **Notebook Features**

This section will walk you through the main functions and scripting features within the Blendata Notebook, which covers basic usage and utilization for standard analytic pipeline.

## **Menu bar**

Within the Blendata Notebook page, the menus on the top are as follows:

![nb9 (1).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb9%20(1).png?api=v2)

1. Run all paragraphs.
2. Show/hide code in all blocks.
3. Show/hide output in all blocks.
4. Clear all output.
5. Version control features (see next subsection).
6. Find and replace.
7. Notebook scheduling.

## **Version Control**

In version control within Blendata Notebook, you can commit your code along with the commit message by clicking on the icon as show in the figure below.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb10.png?api=v2)

After you have committed the code, you can view and select the version using the dropdown menu [1] as shown below. Then clicking on the [2] button will allow you to set the selected version as the current version.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb11.png?api=v2)

Finally, the compare version menu allows you to compare differences between each version you have committed to Blendata Notebook.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb12.png?api=v2)

## **Find and Replace**

The find and replace function in Blendata Notebook allows you to find certain keywords in all code block and replace them with your desire word or phrase. The figure below shows an example of the Find and Replace function UI.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb13.png?api=v2)

## **Scheduler**

The Notebook scheduler within Blendata Notebook creates schedule job for the notebook. This will execute all code block withing the Blendata Notebook at the specified cron expression. The cron expression is a 7-field format cron expression.

Please note that by scheduling jobs, Spark on Blendata Enterprise requires separate resource pool for scheduled Notebook job. If you are uncertain about the resource availability, please contact support for further information.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb14.png?api=v2)

## **List of shortcuts**

![image-20260223-110446.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20260223-110446.png?api=v2) Click to show the list of Notebook keyboard shortcuts available for use.

## Settings (Interpreter binding)

![image-20260223-110628.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20260223-110628.png?api=v2) To code in Notebook is to leverage the benefit of flexibility in using cross-language programming. Blendata Notebook supports 3 main interpreters with multiple syntaxes. This allows you to mainly codes in the following languages:

- Python (via PySpark interpreter)
- PySpark
- Scala
- SQL
- Shell Command
- Markdown language

The figure below shows the interpreter available on the system. You can specify the interpreter of choice using ‘%\_\_interpreter\_name\_\_’ at the top of each code block.

The circular icon in front of each interpreter allows user to easily restart the interpreter if needed. Please note that you will lost all your variable values stored in the memory.

![image-20260223-110953.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20260223-110953.png?api=v2)

## Paragraph Display Mode

![image-20260223-111149.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/image-20260223-111149.png?api=v2) Select the display format for all paragraph outputs.

- **Default** - Displays the paragraph exactly as originally shown.
- **Simple** - Displays the paragraph and execution result in a simplified view.
- **Report** - Displays only the title and result, suitable for presentation.

## **Code Block**

For code block in Blendata Notebook, you can start coding after specifying the interpreter on the top of the code block. The menu are show in the figure below. To run the code block, you can use the run button (play icon) or short key ‘Shift+Enter’. The other buttons are to show/hide code, show/hide output, and settings, respectively.

The markdown code block with ‘%md’ will display the markdown results after execution. While the code block with other interpreters (%pyspark, %sql), will display the code and the results. The example are show in the following figures.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb16.png?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb17.png?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb18.png?api=v2)

---

# **Scripting**

Scripting within Blendata Notebook is as simple as it gets. Here are some examples with different languages.

## **SQL**

In SQL, using ‘%sql’ on the top of the code block, you can access the data assets that have been added to the Zeppelin environment with Spark SQL commands. The results are shown through Zeppelin visualization. The figure shows the example of running SQL commands in the notebook.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb19.png?api=v2)

## **Shell Command**

In shell command, using ‘%sh’ on the top of the code block, you can access the shell command of the machine hosting Zeppelin service. This allows you to manage the data sources or the data file within the environment. Moreover, this also allows you to utilize PIP for Python package installation. The figure shows example of running ‘pip freeze’ commands, which show the installed packages of the current Python on this Zeppelin environment.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb20.png?api=v2)

To install any python package, make sure that your Zeppelin environment has access to the Internet. Then, run ‘pip install \_\_package\_name\_\_’ from ‘%sh’ code block.

## **PySpark and SCALA**

### **Basic Usage**

PySpark and Scala can be accessed to either ‘%pyspark’ or ‘%scala’ within the code block. Both languages leverage the benefit of Spark in handling data, the differences are just syntax.

The figure below shows an example of running code with PySpark.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb21.png?api=v2)

### **Zeppelin Visualization**

Zeppelin environment comes with a very useful and powerful visualization tool. For any Spark dataframe variables, instead of running action commands like df.show(), you can call z.show(\_\_df\_name\_\_) to visualize the dataframe in a much cleaner way. This also allows you to do simple exploratory data analysis on the dataframe. The figure shows an example of plotting the data frame as a bar chart from Zeppelin visualization tool.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb22.png?api=v2)

### **Writing Data to Blendata**

To create a data pipeline that reads, process, and write the data back to Blendata Enterprise platform, you would need to prepare a code block that write the data back to the asset. This mainly can be done in two ways as follows:

1. **Write to CSV file.**

Writing a dataframe to CSV create a CSV file at the specified path on Zeppelin hosted machine. This is useful when:

- Writing the dataframe as a one-time processing task
- Writing the dataframe to a file for initializing data asset on Blendata Enterprise

For the latter option, make sure that Blendata Enterprise has access to the path specified, so that you can import the data as an ‘internal file path’.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb23.png?api=v2)

2. **Write to Parquet file.**

Writing to Parquet file allows you to write back to the data asset. Hence, inserting or overwriting new data into the existing table. The code below does the following:

- Write the dataframe to the actual data asset path.
- If you data is partitioned, specify the partition column using partitionBy(‘column\_name’). If your data is not partitioned, omit this part of the code.
- The following code does not use encryption key in writing the parquet file, if your data asset requires encryption, please contact support for further information.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385372/nb24.png?api=v2)

After completing the process, you would need to do the following:

- If your data asset is not partitioned, go to data catalogue, locate your data asset, and select ‘refresh table’
- If your data is partitioned, got to data catalogue, locate your data asset, and select ‘recovery table’

For automating both of this tasks, you can create service and schedule the service job specifically to your task.

---

## SDK JAVA/Scala and Python to Import the Data Asset

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385514/SDK+JAVA+Scala+and+Python+to+Import+the+Data+Asset |
| **Last modified** | 2025-05-27T03:55:42.598Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data > Notebook |

We have enhanced a library to import the data asset including internal data and Delta table into the Data Catalog via Notebook. This feature supports only Spark(Scala) and Python language.

1. Go to Notebook.
2. Import the related libraries. (files are attached at the bottom of the page)
3. Define your table schema: Fill in your field name, type, and null setting.
4. Prepare your data into this table.
5. Create the data frame such as your table name, description, storage name, time series, partition, and etc.
6. Review your data asset in your Data Catalog menu.

**The parameters definition**

- **tableName**: The table name.
- **description**: Description of table.
- **sink**: Sink of table.
- **sinkOptions**: The Sink options.
- **sc**: The Spark context.
- **storageName**: The storage name of Blendata Enterprise. You can check the storage name in the Storage Management menu. (Default value is 'Default zeus path')
- **timeField**: The time field, which uses to create partitions.
- **partitions**: The fields list, which uses to create partitions.
- **isEncryptParquet**: Setup the table to be encrypt by setting the true or false. (no need for Delta table)
- **enableService**: Setup the services in the Blendata Enterprise. If 'true' means refresh and recovery job services will create automatically.

For the Delta table asset, there is one more parameter, which is **deltaKeys**. This parameter is the Delta keys list that uses for merging in aggregate.

For PySpark, sinkOptions parameter has to use the HashMap from Java, so user need to set sinkOptions = spark.\_jvm.java.util.HashMap()

**The Spark example**:

![notebook SDK.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385514/notebook%20SDK.png?api=v2)

**The Python example**:

![SDK python.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385514/SDK%20python.png?api=v2)

**The Spark with Delta table example:**

![notbook delta SDK.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385514/notbook%20delta%20SDK.png?api=v2)

**You can find the SDK template in below:**

[blendata\_util\_pyspark\_ex…](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385514/SDK+JAVA+Scala+and+Python+to+Import+the+Data+Asset?preview=%2F2120385514%2F2120385526%2Fblendata_util_pyspark_example.zpln)

## SDK Run Parallel Log

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385571/SDK+Run+Parallel+Log |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-26T10:49:29.073Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data > Notebook |

This Software Development Kit(SDK) library is designed to run multiple workflows as parallel and store the result history as a publish Notebook URL, which helps convert old message results into a table format. Users can view these results within 30 days.

1. Import blendata\_util
2. Fill all **Parameters**.

- **concurrentParallel:** The number of parallel Notebook to run in the same time.(can be set to be less than the number of total parallel notebook.)
- **language:** The code language that user can choose only “python” or “scala”.
- **timeSleep:** The delay time that is setup to start this Notebook Run parallel service. The best practice should be 10 second. (Unit is second.)
- **timeout:** Fill in the specific time for timeout. (Unit is second.)
- **notebookAddParameterRequest:** To setup the parameter under the parallel Notebook.

  - **notebookId:** Fill in the notebook ID.
  - **notebookName:** Fill in either only the notebook name or the full path in the format `"workspace_name/notebook_name"`.
  - **addParameterMapFromTitle**

    - **titleName:** Fill in a title name of the selected Notebook. If the user needs to replace the parameter of all titles under this Notebook, users can use “all” instead of titleName parameter.
    - **parameter:** Name of parameter.
    - **value:** Value of parameter.
  - **sparkConf** **(Optional):** You can configure Spark by adding the parameters as shown below.

    - **spark.driver.memory**: Fill in a number of driver memory.
    - **spark.executor.memory** : Fill in a number of executor memory.
    - **spark.cores.max** : Fill in a maximum number of CPU that executor can use.
- **description** For enable function to return all result of all paragraph
- **runningId** unique id from this run/default is null
- **workspaceName:** The workspace where the target notebook resides.

  - If you **provide a value**, the function will look for the notebook in the specified workspace.
  - If you do **not provide a value**, the function defaults to using the workspace where the current notebook is located.

3. Call function **blendata\_util.hera\_api\_runParallelLog(…)**.

This run Parallel log is available for Python and PySpark only.

**For example**:

```
# pyspark
import blendata_util

concurrentParallel = 1
language = "python"
timeSleep = 10
timeout = 3000
workspaceName = "wf_path_local"

notebookAddParameterRequest = [
    {
        "notebookId": "ZM8Z5XXXX",  # has an ID
        "addParameterMapFromTitle": {}
    },
    {
        "notebookName": "wf_path_local/note_path_lc",  # only path/name
        "addParameterMapFromTitle": {}
    },
    {
        "notebookName": "note_path_df",  # only name
        "addParameterMapFromTitle": {}
    }
]

description = False
```

You can find the SDK template in below:

[blendata\_util\_pyspark\_ex…](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385571/SDK+Run+Parallel+Log?preview=%2F2120385571%2F2120385583%2Fblendata_util_pyspark_example.zpln)

blendata\_utill without workspaceName and Conf parameters.

[blendata\_util\_pyspark\_ex…](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385571/SDK+Run+Parallel+Log?preview=%2F2120385571%2F2476474375%2Fblendata_util_pyspark_example_4_6_10.zpln)

blendata\_utill with workspaceName and Conf parameters.

Because some parameters use workspaceName and config Conf, this function is available for Blendata version 4.6.9 and above.

## SDK Encrypt User

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385613/SDK+Encrypt+User |
| **Last modified** | 2025-05-27T03:56:18.494Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data > Notebook |

In the past, the SDK could not be run in the Workflow Management menu or Run Scheduler for Notebooks. With this limitation, we have created an Encrypted User configuration to support Notebook SDK running using the Workflow Management menu and Run Scheduler option. Users can configure this in Zeppelin or Blendata Management. If the configuration is run as normal via the Notebook run option, it will use the details of the currently logged-in user; otherwise, it will use the details of the encrypted user.

# Zeppelin

1. Import blendata\_util
2. Fill the username, which uses for Blendata Enterprise login.
3. Call function **blendata\_util.encryptUser(…)** based on your interpreter.
4. Get encrypt text from result and replace into value of **hera.user** in zeppelin-site.xml.
5. Restart Zeppelin.

**For example**: Pyspark

```
%pyspark
import blendata_util

username = "your_username"
password = "your_password"

blendata_util.encryptUser("your_username", "your_password")
```

- **username:** the username of Blendata Enterprise user.
- **password:** the password of Blendata Enterprise user.

# Blendata Management

1. Open the Blendata Management
2. Go to your project.
3. Select Zeppelin module and click “**Configuration**” tab.
4. Click “**Config**“ of zeppelin site and add Hera value configuration.
5. Click action:3dots:button and select restart option.

**Limitations for Workflow Management and Run Scheduler**:

1. For the Run Parallel Log SDK, if an encrypted user cannot access the Notebook, an error will be displayed.
2. When importing a Data Asset via the Notebook SDK, the result will be displayed in the encrypted user's Data Catalog page.

**You can find the SDK template in below:**

[blendata\_util\_pyspark\_ex…](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385613/SDK+Encrypt+User?preview=%2F2120385613%2F2120385625%2Fblendata_util_pyspark_example.zpln)

## View Table

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385655/View+Table |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:45:31.569Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data |

The table stores the SQL command and uses it to create a new table asset with the SQL command from the table. When the user previews this view table, it executes the SQL query to retrieve the latest data from the table.

![view table.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385655/view%20table.png?api=v2)

## How to save the result as View table

1. Click:3dots:and select “**Save as table**“.
2. Click “**Save as view table**”.
3. Edit your SQL query, select an accessible of new view table.
4. Fill in the table name or setup an advanced options.
5. click “**Save**“.
6. Your view dataset is created on Data Catalog page.

## Aggregate Table

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385677/Aggregate+Table |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T13:57:14.166Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data |

To create a new data asset with aggregate table, which uses the result data from the SQL query to create the new table and keep this data in our storage via Internal table. Then, create the service to update the data of this table with the schedule setting.

# ​Create Aggregate Table

1. Click:3dots:and select “**Save as table**“.
2. Click “**Save as aggregate table**” and fill in a basic information of your new aggregate table.

![save as agggregate table.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385677/save%20as%20agggregate%20table.png?api=v2)

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage for internal and Delta table only.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

- **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)

3. Setup the aggregate setting.

![image-20260223-135513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385677/image-20260223-135513.png?api=v2)

![image-20260223-135627.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385677/image-20260223-135627.png?api=v2)

- **Time Range**: Select your data period or choose all time range.
- **Number of File Output**

  - **Enter Number:** Put the number of file.
- **Save Mode**: Two options including Append and Overwrite.

  - **Append**: add new records at the end of the existing data.
  - **Overwrite**: replace the entire data records with the new records.

When overwrite mode is selected, data processing cannot be performed during the overwrite operation. The time required to overwrite data into the aggregate table will depend on your resources.

- **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Advanced Option**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

4. Set the time domain. In case there is no date/time field in your data asset, can skip this setup.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

5. Set the partition of this new data asset. See more details of partition, go to [Partitioning](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709664/Import+Data#Partitioning)

page.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

6. Setup your related source jobs based on your table source type.

![service setting - state.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385677/service%20setting%20-%20state.png?api=v2)

![service setting - delta.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385677/service%20setting%20-%20delta.png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables. See more state details, go to [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.
- **Service Enable**: The related service jobs that come with the source type and Time Domain settings. Users can change the job service settings to either use the system default.

7. Your aggregate data asset will display on Data Catalog page.

## Export File

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385706/Export+File |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:48:21.176Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data |

# How to Export the file.

1. Click :3dots:icon and click ”**Export Result**”.
2. Select “**Export File**“ option.
3. Fill in the information for the output file as per below.

## Basic Information

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385706/export%20data.png?api=v2)

- **Export SQL Query**: SQL command to export the data.
- **Exported File Name**: The name of the exported file.
- **Exported type**: The type of exported file such as CSV, JSON, Plain text, Parquet, and Excel.
- **Export Record Limit**: Limit the number of record.

  - **All Result**: Export all records.
  - **Enter Limit**: Indicate the number of the record.
- **Number of File Output**

  - **Use System Default**: Use the default number of file output option from the platform.
  - **Enter Number:** Put the number of file.
- **Zip File Output**

  - **Zip folder only**: Compress only the folder of exported file.
  - **Every Export File**: Compress all exported files.

## **Extract to Folder**

Extract the data to folder based on a selected field.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385706/sq5.png?api=v2)

- **No Extracted Folder**: No extracted folder.
- **Create Extracted Folder**: Select the field for the extracted folder creation.

## **Service Setting**

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385706/sq6.png?api=v2)

**Job-Triggering**: Service Job options

- **One time**: Export this data only one time.

  - **Service Name**: The name of Job Service.
  - **Data Time Range**: Time range setting for the exported file.
  - **Query data with all-time range**: Export the data with all time range.
  - **Operating On**: Select [Zeus](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869512741/Zeus+Configuration) for the service operation.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385706/ef%20-%20job.png?api=v2)

- **Job Scheduler**: setup the job scheduler as per user’s request.

  - **Service Name**: The name of Job Service.
  - **Data Time Range**: Specify your data time range to export the data as interval.

    ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385706/ep%20-%20time%20range.png?api=v2)
  - **Create File Interval**: To setup a time for a job processing with 7 options including Minutes, Hourly, Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Yearly, and Cron Expression.

    ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385706/ef%20-%20job2.png?api=v2)
  - **Operating On**: Select [Zeus](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869512741/Zeus+Configuration) for the service operation.

## Export to External Database

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385760/Export+to+External+Database |

# **How to Export to External Database**

1. Click “**Action**“ button or:3dots:icon and click ”**Export Result**”.
2. Select “**Export to Database**“ option.
3. Select a database type.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385760/ep%20-%20db.png?api=v2)

4. Fill in the database information for the output file likes URL, username, and password as per below and click “**Connect to JDBC**“ button. Then, click “**Next**“ to proceed the next step.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385760/ep%20-db2.png?api=v2)

5. Mapping the the field between a destination table and a source table and put the other details.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385760/ep%20-%20db3.png?api=v2)

- **Table to Map**: Select the destination table from the external database.
- **Write Mode**: Select the write options, which are Append and Overwrite.

  - **Append**: add new records at the end of the existing data.
  - **Overwrite**: replace the entire data records with the new records.
- **Service Setting**

  - **Job Triggering**: Select only one time or need to set the schedule for this job.

1. **One time**: Export this data only one time.

- **Service Name**: The name of Job Service.
- **Query data with all-time range**: Export the data with all time range.
- **Operating On**: Select [Zeus](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869512741) for the service operation.

2. **Job Scheduler**: setup the job scheduler as per user’s request.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385760/ep%20-%20db4.png?api=v2)

- **Service Name**: The name of Job Service.
- **Data Time Range**: Specify your data time range to export the data as interval.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385760/ep-db5.png?api=v2)

- **Export JDBC Table Interval**: To setup a time for a job processing with 7 options including Minutes, Hourly, Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Yearly, and Cron Expression.

  ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2120385760/ef%20-%20job2.png?api=v2)
- **Operating On**: Select [Zeus](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869512741) for the service operation.
