# What is Blendata Enterprise?

**Blendata Enterprise is a streamline big data management with all functions under one platform.**  
It is a platform that acts as a medium between data and users to streamline complex data management through a convenient all-in-one system covering data ingestion, processing, analysis, and usage readily available for all data missions of the data-driven generation. This version 4.6.0 document is applicable to **Blendata Enterprise - Log Management**, which is focus on log management feature.

**Blendata Enterprise - Log Management** enhanced the log management feature to minimize the data retention period to 90 days. This ensures that old records in the data asset are retained for 90 days or longer before the system automatically removes them.

# Key Benefits

- **Less effort, Reduce time**: It streamlines data management, reduces installation time, and allows you to manage all processes under one platform, from data integration to utilization. It is both engineering and end-user low-code or no-code solutions that do not require any coding knowledge.
- **Cutting edge big data architecture**: Say goodbye to big data single-server expansions. The platform works on a new-gen decoupling compute & storage architecture powered by Blendata’s proprietary Distribute In-Memory Twin Processors. It developed from Apache Spark with a processing speed of 1.5 times higher.
- **Connect everything, Say goodbye to silos**: Multi-interface connections whether structured, semi-structured, and unstructured (text-based), or from cloud storage sites such as Amazon S3, Azure Blob, or on-premise environments such as flat files (CSV, JSON, text), logs, Kafka, HDFS, or enterprise databases (Oracle, MS SQL Server, MongoDB, etc.) through dynamic data ingestion.
- **Open, yet secured platform**: Multi-interface connections whether structured, semi-structured, and unstructured (text-based), or from cloud storage sites such as Amazon S3, Azure Blob, or on-premise environments such as flat files (CSV, JSON, text), logs, Kafka, HDFS, or enterprise databases (Oracle, MS SQL Server, MongoDB, etc.) through dynamic data ingestion.

# **What Blendata Enterprise Do?**

**Blendata Enterprise**: Comprehensive big data management under one platform

## Data Ingestion

- **Multiple connectivity**:Connect and collect data from all sources including flat files (CSV, JSON, Plain text, Parquet, etc.), databases (Oracle, MS SQL, MySQL, etc.), Cloud and Stream through built-in connectors without coding.
- **Dynamic data import**:Import data from flat files, log databases, or cloud storage automatically through built-in connectors in the following schemes.

  - Scheduled replication (Batch/Stream)
  - Data virtualization
  - Changed data capture
- **Extract, transform, and load upon import (ETL)**:Transforms data upon importing with Spark SQL comes with various data transform functions to support big data.
- **Automatic schema creation**: The engine of Blendata automatically searches the attributes and creates the schema for all import tables including files, databases, or streaming through Kafka is modifiable by the user through GUI.

## Data Management

- **Customizable user access**: Customizable access rights in clear detail covering data access, visualization, dashboard, and all functions on the platform.
- **Global standard data security**: Row-level security, table or columnar encryptions, and integration with Key Management System (KMS) externally to enhance the encryptions process (additional option).
- **Lifecycle Management**: Customizable data tiering level whether Hot/Warm/Cold to determine the data compression and data retention category.
- **Multi-location storage**:Support multi-storage location interlinking and multiple-device data tiering.
- **Advanced data management**: Support advanced data management such as data compaction, data compression, data lifecycle management (hot-warm-cold), data skipping, predicated push-down, dynamic partition pruning, and physical file partitioning.

## Data Processing

- **SQL Analytics**: Cover data preparations, transformations, correlations, aggregations, and data exploration UI that enables analyzers or engineers to query with familiar grammar, together with ANSI SQL + Spark SQL functions, and allows analyzers or engineers to survey, manage, or schedule processing job for their own data.
- **Data Preparation Workspace**: Offer the same data preparation engine and functions as SQL analysis (data exploration UI) but with a more user-friendly approach to support low-code/no-code data preparations for non-technical users within a matter of seconds.
- **AI/ML with Notebook Interface**: Offer a familiar experience to data engineers and ML developers with a notebook interface that supports all well-known programming languages, such as Python, R, Scala, and SQL.
- **Job scheduler and chain job setting**: Set tasks to run on a schedule with the built-in Job Scheduler function and link all tasks together for complex jobs from the beginning to the end.

## Data Utilization

- **Visualization & Dashboard**: Transform search and analysis results into user-friendly visualization that can be dragged and dropped directly on the interface with many presentation charts available which can be categorized on a dashboard right away. It comes with data filtering and drill-down functions that can be created as a big data application ready to be shared among co-workers.
- **Notification**: SQL-based notifications are to be made via the web console, email, script, or API (additional option).
- **API**: Prepare REST API with the OAuth2 authentication system to fetch data by using standard HTTP queries or linking with Spark ODBC/Hive2server JDBC to integrate data at a low level.
- **Export**: Support exports of processed data in many formats and downstream media such as files, databases, or cloud storage.

**“Bring out the competitiveness of your business, overcome all limitations in big data management.”**

## Data Catalog

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105409/Data+Catalog |
| **Last modified** | 2026-06-09T10:25:00.446Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 |

Data Catalogs page is a location of your data assets. The users can view both their own assets and shared assets. Each box represents a Data Asset, allowing you to edit asset information and manage access permissions.

# Create Table and Import Data

To create a new asset schema or import a data into the existing table. See more details, go to [Create Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468362/Create+Table) and [Import Data](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data) page.

---

# Search your Data Asset

## Schema

The representation of the databases is used to manage the permission of a set of databases.

- **Default**: The default schema of system and published data asset. (Accessible only by **Super Admin** users in Blendata v4.6.12+).
- **Global**: The external source type and published data asset.
- **Connection Profile**: The location of Kafka profile.

In Blendata v4.6.12+, access to the “**Default\_schema**” is restricted for general users.  
Only “**Super Admin**” users can access it.  
This restriction can be disabled if required. <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AgADqw>

![external schema.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/external%20schema.png?api=v2)

![connection profile schema.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/connection%20profile%20schema.png?api=v2)

The latest “Selected Schema” is now remembered even after refreshing the page or switching menus.

## Search Bar

Users can use the search bar to search your own asset or shared asset with the keywords.

1. Put the keywords into the search box.
2. Click “**Only my assets**” to display only your own asset.

**Matching result**: Show the number of workspace that matching with your keyword in the search bar.

## Search via Filters

This search provides the filter options to search your asset. There are three types to search your data assets.

1. **Search the asset via Tag**: (Typing data assets tag) Fill in the tag keyword in the field box.
2. **Search the asset via Source Type**: Search your asset with Source Types as per below.

   - **Delta**: A table asset that supports CRUD operations for data processing.
   - **Kafka Profile:** An asset is imported via the Kafka service, which can be used to import a data asset with spark-table-internal type.
   - **Spark-table-external:** External data, not stored in Blendata storage. The platform connects with the external data source, and the data table updates immediately when the source is updated.
   - **Spark-table-internal:** Internal data is stored in our Blendata storage. If there is an update in the source data and the new data is not imported into the platform, the internal data will not be updated to reflect the changes in the source data.
   - **View-table:** A view table stores only the SQL query used to create this table from the selected data asset table extraction.
3. **Search the asset via Data Type**: Search your asset with Data Types.

   - Non-time series
   - Time series

---

# View the Data Asset information

The data asset page displays all your data asset or the shared data asset from others, which you can access. Also show the data asset information such as name, description, stream service, and latest modified date&time.

When users click on a data asset, the information, preview, and shared tabs display relevant data asset details.

![update_datacat.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/update_datacat.png?api=v2)

## **Information**

**Basic Information**

- **Name**: An asset name.
- **Source**: A source of data asset.
- **Data Type**: A data type of asset.(time domain)
- **Created by**: The owner of asset.
- **Created Date**: Date of asset creation.
- **Modified by**: A username, who modifies the latest asset related to the data profile. if any **information** within the Data Catalog is modified, this field and the field will be updated automatically.
- **Modified Date**: A latest modified date of an asset related to the data profile. if any **information** within the Data Catalog is modified, this field and the field will be updated automatically.
- **Latest imported**: The number of latest records.
- **Latest data update**: The latest update date of this data asset.

Modified By and Modified Date do not update from CRUD operations on Delta tables.

**Description:** The description of data asset.

**Tags:** A tag of data asset. The user can add or delete the tags for your asset. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20260223-054646.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20260223-054646.png?api=v2)

**Column-level Security**: Set confidentiality levels and define the expected roles that can access each column.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/data%20cat4.png?api=v2)

- **Confidentiality Level**

  - **Confidential**: The field value are masked to protect sensitive information but these masked column values remain workable for data processing.
  - **Secret**: Restricted from viewing specific columns within the data asset.

**Data Source Option:** Display data asset information in JSON format, presenting details such as source, field mapping, and destination.

**Size Statistic**: The table size information in the catalog page that uses to see the current total size of data and the statistic number of table size in specific time range.

**Relate Service:** The related services of this asset. The user able to check related services via Service Management page, but the user needs to have a permission of the services.

**Column Customization**: Select columns to display as default columns, when preview table in Simple Query.

**Data Dictionary:** Display a schema and each field definition of data asset.

- **Data name**: The field name of data asset.
- **Datatype**: The data type of field likes string, integer, double, and etc..
- **Description (Editable)**: The field definition is editable by users with the edit permissions. (maximum 255 characters)

## **Preview**

To display the sample of the selected data asset with the latest 100 records.

![dln2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/dln2.png?api=v2)

For View tables, the default data preview is set to 24 hours, and a time range filter is available to view data within a specific time range, enhancing the preview performance of the View table.

## **Shared**

The tab used for managing access or permissions for roles and users.

- **Role**: Manage access for user groups, referred to as roles.
- **User**: Manage access for individual users.

![dln3.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/dln3.png?api=v2)

1. Click your data asset. Your data asset information will pop up at the below the screen.
2. Click “**Shared**“ tab :share: to manage the authorization
3. Set up the access for role level

   1. Click ”**Manage Roles**” button :role: and select a role to set the operation permission.

      ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/roles%20desc.png?api=v2)
      1. **View Role**: Can see the information and preview of the data asset only.
      2. **Edit Role:** Can see the information, preview, edit the description, upload a new table, and edit tag of the data asset but this role is unable to delete the data
      3. **Delete Role**: Can access the data table and delete the data but cannot edit the data asset.
      4. **Manage Role**: Can access, edit, and delete the data asset. Moreover the user is able to see the security option to manage the user and role of the member.
   2. Click “**Save**“ :save: to update the permission.
4. Set up the access by each user level

   1. Click ”**Manage Users**” button :users: and select your member user to set the operation permission, the permission definition is similar to role level.
   2. Click “**Save**“ to update the permission.

In this platform, the suitable role is already set for each person based on the responsibility since the project setup and each role has different action. In each data asset, the owner able to grant the access to some roles or some person and add more permissions for each role and the user as well.

For Administrator role, please see more information about role management via User management menu, see the [**user management**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201420007/User+Management) page.

## **Data Lineage**

Data Lineage involves tracking and visually representing the flow of data from its source to downstream usage. It facilitates understanding of data movement through system processes and transformations. This includes details about data origin, transformations, and final destinations. The visual representation helps in understanding data journeys, ensuring transparency and traceability in complex data workflows. See more details, go to [Data Lineage](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1917976580/Data+Lineage) page.

![dl4.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/dl4.png?api=v2)

---

# Manage your Data Asset

The operation action options, which the users use to manage the data asset such as permission access, export, and delete a data asset.

![image-20250901-031731.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20250901-031731.png?api=v2)

The additional actions of the selected data asset, which is :3dots: icon.

**Export File:** To export the data asset as a file type and users can download the file from [My Profile](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1907130447/My+Profile) page. For more information about how to export, See more [Export File](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869676752/Export+File) page.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20210820-021035.png?api=v2)

**Recreate Table**: To registers the table into the Metastore based on the datasource profile to enabling assets [imported from JSON files](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2229207161/Import+Asset+JSON+file+and+Internal+Spark) to be queryable.(for Internal table and Delta table)

**Delete Asset:** To delete the selected asset, but the user can backup raw data into a backup folder(Backup location is setup during zeus installation.) by selecting Backup raw data option.

After an asset is deleted, it is moved to the **Trash Path** as `.zip` file. If **"Backup Data"** is selected during deletion, it is moved to the **Backup Path** as `.parquet` file. Both files are retained for 90 days before permanent deletion.

The default retention period is 90 days, but it can be configured by Super\_Admin.

**Table Actions:** Table actions differ depending on the type of your “**SourceType table**” and **DataType**.

spark-table-internal with non-time series

![image-20251020-071450.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20251020-071450.png?api=v2)

spark-table-internal with time series and partitions

![image-20251020-071609.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20251020-071609.png?api=v2)

delta-table

![image-20251020-072242.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20251020-072242.png?api=v2)

- **Update Table**: Update the table using the job service, and this action will be executed based on the following conditions:

  - The user is either the owner of the data asset or has the management permissions for the data asset.
  - A data asset is an internal table that imports data via file path, or RDBMS using scheduled method options.
  - A data asset is not a stream/state table that stores data in each state including hot, warm, cold.
  - A data asset is not an aggregate table.
- **Refresh Table**: Reload the table to retrieve the most recent records.
- **Recovery Table**: To refresh the data table and create new partitions when new data has been added.
- **Delta Compaction Table**: Used to compact the data files(parquet file) to reduce the number of files. For more about how to do the Delta Compaction table. See more [Delta Compaction Job](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1975353356/Delta+Compaction+Job) page.
- **Vacuum Table**: Used to delete the old data files(parquet file) based on the specified period setting. The default is 7 days. For more about how to do the Vacuum table. See more [Vacuum Job](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1975091237/Vacuum+Job) page.
- **Upload New Data**: To Upload new records by choosing either Append or Overwrite mode.
- **Refresh Table Connection**: To refresh the temp table for the new records. (for spark-table-external)

![image-20251020-075203.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20251020-075203.png?api=v2)

**Public Actions:** The platform enables users to share their data assets with third parties or individuals who don't have a Blendata user account. This includes sharing record, field, and schema information, which is made accessible to the Development service.

Sharing data assets is only supported in the “**Default**” and “**Global**” schemas. Users must have management permissions to perform this action. Once shared, other Blendata users can view the data asset.

![image-20250901-031917.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20250901-031917.png?api=v2)

- **Get Link**: Get a public link of data asset and the user is able to copy this link and share to others.
- **Unpublish**: To cancel the published data asset.

**Truncate Table:** This function allows users to delete all records in a data table at once. The action is now easier and more efficient to perform through the user interface.

![image-20250521-145939.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20250521-145939.png?api=v2)

**Important**: Users must have delete permission to use this feature.

# Additional Management Actions

When the users select the data asset by tick on the box and click “**Action**” button :action: or click at selected data asset and click “**Action**” button.

![delta vacuum.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/delta%20vacuum.png?api=v2)

- **Add Asset Permission**: To add the permission of the selected asset.
- **Export Data Profile**: To export a selected asset data profile as a Json File.
- **Vacuum Table**: To delete the old data files(parquet file) based on the specified period setting.

This option will displays for Delta data asset only.

- **Delete Selected**: To delete the asset. (Can delete multiple assets at the same time).

  ![image-20250801-083812.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/image-20250801-083812.png?api=v2)
- **Copy JSON Schema:** The user can copy a schema of selected data asset in JSON format.
- **Copy Column Names:** The user can copy the name column of an asset.
- **Sync Data Dict:** Reset the data dictionary to its original version starting from the import data step.
- **Download as CSV**: The user can download this data asset as CSV file.
- **Import Data Dict**: The user can import record from external as CSV file.

---

# Compute Task Monitoring

This feature displays your compute task queue and progress status, which is the Zeus(Spark jobs) via the Blendata user interface. User can kill your own task jobs such as export or data query task and can see the jobs under Zeus that you have an access permission.

![Compute Task Monitoring.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/Compute%20Task%20Monitoring.png?api=v2)

If you click the “**See all compute task monitoring**”, it will show a popup of service tasks, as per the below screenshot. You can see the running job of each Zeus and can click the kill icon to stop this running job.

![compute monitor details.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/compute%20monitor%20details.png?api=v2)

**Note**:

- The compute task monitoring list will refresh the data every 5 seconds.
- This feature does not support Zeppelin jobs.

---

# Data Asset User Management (Admin Users)

The Admin users are able to manage the access or operation permissions for roles and users.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105409/roles%20desc.png?api=v2)

1. **View**: Can see the information and preview of the data asset only.
2. **Edit:** Can see the information, preview, edit the description, upload a new table, and edit tag of the data asset but this role is unable to delete the data
3. **Delete**: Can access the data table and delete the data but cannot edit the data asset.
4. **Manage**: Can access, edit, and delete the data asset. Moreover the user is able to see the security option to manage the user and role of the member.

For the information about how to mange the roles or user, see more [Manage and Protection Data](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120384653/Quick+Start#Step-2%3A-Manage-and-Protection-Data).

The admin need to set the view permission to the user, before the user access to a data asset.

## Data Lineage

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105612/Data+Lineage |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-07T09:49:17.119Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Catalog |

# Understanding Data Journey

With this feature, users can see the data movement which represents the relationship from data origin to downstream. Each asset, users are able to see the data asset details of the upstream, downstream, or unlinked asset and can directly access to related assets through this menu.

The Data Lineage tab shows the assets details as following.

- **Upstream**: The data origin.
- **Downstream**:The final destination data.
- **Unlinked Asset**: The unused source that has severed any connection with the selected asset.

## **Data Asset**

There are active tables including view table, aggregate table, or streaming.

![image-20240115-045858.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105612/image-20240115-045858.png?api=v2)

- **Asset Name**: The asset name.
- **Description**: The description of data asset.
- **Dependency Date**: The initiation date of the relationship with the selected data asset.
- **Tag**: The data asset tag.
- **Data Asset details**: The data asset information such as asset publish, source type, data type, and encrypted details.
- **Created by**: The owner of the data asset.

## **Notebook**

The asset is created when the user add asset in the Notebook page.

![image-20240118-085246.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105612/image-20240118-085246.png?api=v2)

- **Environment Name**: The environment name.
- **Description**: The description of environment.
- **Dependency Date**: The initiation date of the relationship with the selected data asset.
- **Added by**: The person who add the data asset in the Notebook.

## **Service**

Service that relates with the table asset.

![image-20240118-085545.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105612/image-20240118-085545.png?api=v2)

- **Service Name**: The service name.
- **Description**: The description of service.
- **Dependency Date**: The initiation date of the relationship with the selected data asset.
- **Tag**: The data asset tag.
- **Service Type:** The service type, which is related to table data. See more service information, [Service](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321510/Services) page.
- **Status**: The latest status of the service.
- **Created by**: The owner of the service.

## **Service Chains**

Service Chains that relates with the table asset.

![image-20240118-090439.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105612/image-20240118-090439.png?api=v2)

- **Chain Name**: The service chain name.
- **Description**: The description of service chain.
- **Dependency Date**: The initiation date of the relationship with the selected data asset.
- **Status**: The latest status of the service chain.
- **Created by**: The owner of the service chain.

## **Visualization**

The visualization file is created from the source data. There is only Downstream and Unlinked Asset options.

![image-20240118-090609.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105612/image-20240118-090609.png?api=v2)

- **Visualization Name**: The visualization name.
- **Description**: The description of visualization.
- **Dependency Date**: The initiation date of the relationship with the selected data asset.
- **Tag**: The visualization tag.
- **Related dashboard**: The related dashboard, which consists of this visualization.
- **Created by**: The owner of the visualization.

**Note**: In the event that a user lacks access to the asset, the asset name cannot be clicked. If the asset is removed from the enterprise, the asset information disappears.

![dl5.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105612/dl5.png?api=v2)

## **Workflow**

User can see the streamline of Workflow and can access to your workflow from this menu. The Workflow upstream will display when users already used the SQL service.

![workflow lineage.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105612/workflow%20lineage.png?api=v2)

- **Workflow Name**: The workflow name.
- **Description**: The description of workflow.
- **Dependency Date**: The initiation date of the relationship with the selected data asset.
- **Tag**: The workflow tag.
- **Related Version**: The deployed version.
- **Linked by**: The workflow owner name.

---

# Access to related assets

Users can directly click on a related asset name to view the associated assets via this feature, provided they have the necessary authorization.

![dl2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105612/dl2.png?api=v2)

---

# Limitation

As of now this feature does not support Kafka profile, System Service, Customize Service & Logstash service.

## Import Data

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data |
| **Last modified** | 2026-03-05T02:27:33.488Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 |

![image-20251105-025300.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105672/image-20251105-025300.png?api=v2)

In version 4.6.12, general users cannot access the “**default\_schema**.”  
If a general user has no access to any schema, all options under the import menu will be disabled.  
To enable importing or creating internal tables, general users must be granted access to at least one schema first.  
You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

# (blue star) Import Dataset (Internal Table)

Our platform allow the user to import the data file as per below support source details and the data will be replicated into our Blendata storage with Parquet or Delta table based on your data processing.

![image-20251120-020537.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105672/image-20251120-020537.png?api=v2)

## Support Source

- [**Flat Files**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2229076007/Upload+File): Plain text, Shape, CSV, JSON, Excel, XML, ORC, and Parquet.
- [**Database**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2229207125/RDBMS): Oracle, MS SQL, MySQL, Cassandra, Mongo DB, and other RDBMS through JDBC/ODBC connector.
- [**Others**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2227863572/Import+Dataset): Cloud Storage, Kafka Stream, REST API, Google Sheet, File Path, and Search Engine.

## Table Data Type

A table consists of the date or time value for instance, order data including date and time or member data with Log-in time field is classified as “**Time Series**“. On the other hand, **Non-Time Series** does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.

## Partitioning

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value. To helps the efficiency of data searching, processing and also reduces the data extraction time.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105672/partition.png?api=v2)

**Note**: **A time series data**

The platform will automatically create a 'partition\_date' based on the selected field name, and the user can also set up a partition level with the following options.

- [Generate] Year (yyyy)
- [Generate] Month (yyyyMM)
- [Generate] Hour (yyyyMMddHH)
- [Generate] Minute (yyyyMMddHHmm)

## **Importing Action:**

Internal table will be stored the data in term of Parquet file format by default, which able to do two actions when import the new dataset including Append and Overwrite action.

- **Append action**: add the new record/dataset at the end of the last record.

- **Overwrite action**: replace the entire existing record with the new data.

Another one is Data Lake called “**Delta table**”, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size. See more how to setup your data asset as Delta table, go to <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106476> page.

---

# (blue star) Creating Dataset Connection (External Table)

Keep only the configuration and the data structure/schema on the platform likes virtual table. The platform will establish a direct connection with the customer's data table. Users cannot modify fields or set up outputs such as services, partitions, or time series. The import process is similar to Import Dataset (Internal Table) but there is no need to upload the files or create the Kafka profile.

## Support Source

![image-20251120-020713.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121105672/image-20251120-020713.png?api=v2)

---

# (blue star) Add Kafka Profile

To create a Kafka profile, select a Kafka topic from the Kafka server and manage the Kafka server in Kafka Management via the backend. Users can then import Kafka data through the Import Data menu by selecting the newly created Kafka profile.

### **Kafka Management** (Kafka server) for Administrator role\*

To manage the streaming data importing by using Kafka server, data transformation, and manage the authorization for the Kafka importing data.

---

# (blue star) Change Data Capture (Beta)

Be able to monitor or capture all changes including insert, update, or delete a record from the data source in real time via log, which Kafka subscribes those logs and keep those logs in the Kafka topic (log of events). Then deliver those changes into a downstream process or system for consistency between source and destination data. For more information about how to import this CDC, go to [CDC](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106445/Change+Data+Capture) page.

### <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105972> (Structured Database)

- MySQL
- MS SQL
- Oracle
- PostgreSQL

We have implemented Debezium 2.7, which supports various database versions through its connectors:

- **MySQL**: Database versions 8.0.x and 8.2 are supported, with Driver 8.3.0.
- **MS SQL**: Database versions 2017, 2019, and 2022 are supported, with Driver 12.4.2.jre8
- **Oracle Database EE**: Versions 19, 21, and 23 are supported, requiring the LogMiner adapter and Oracle JDBC driver ojdbc8 21.6.0.0.
- **PostgreSQL**: Database versions 12, 13, 14, 15, 16 (Plug-ins: decoderbufs, pgoutput), with Driver 42.6.1.

Reference: <https://debezium.io/releases/2.7/>

---

# (blue star) Import Assets (JSON file and Internal Spark)

This option is used when there is migration updated or need to integrate the data from your collaboration’s device to your device, which support the JSON file that export from the data catalog via Blendata Enterprise. The JSON file contains of data structure/schema only. Furthermore, for advanced analytics where data is stored in the Spark Meta-store, our platform is able to import those Spark Meta-store files into the Data Catalog.

Users cannot select some Spark Meta-store files. The platform will import all Spark Meta-store files into Data Catalog page.

---

## MySQL

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106032/MySQL |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T06:03:37.893Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > RDBMS |

**MySQL** is an open-source relational database management system (RDBMS). This stores the data in the table style including row and column. Also support the SQL to do the data processing. The advantage of this system are multi-user and multi-threaded.

1. Connect to MySQL server by filling all server information as per below:

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/ms4.png?api=v2)

- **URL:** the database server URL with this pattern *“**jdbc:mysql://<hostname>:<port>/<databaseName>**”*
- **Username**: authorized username of this database server.
- **Password**: password of this database server.

2. Click “**Connect Database**“ button to connect with the database server.
3. Select a table Method and click “**Next**“.

   - **Select Database Table**: Select your data tables. The user able to select more than one data tables with Multi-select option. This method cannot import the data into the existing table. If users need to import the data into existing table, please select “**Input Query Command (SQL)**” method go to **Select Database Table** topic.
   - **Input Query Command (SQL)**: Search a data table by using the SQL commands for a single data asset. Go to **Input Query Command (SQL)** topic.

## **Select Database Table Method**

1. Select the data table and click setting icon :setting: to setup your table information.

You can select multiple tables for the data importing.

![mysql setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/mysql%20setting.png?api=v2)

2. Select an upload method, Zeus path(Storage Name), and a data schema.

a. Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

**Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Time setting**: Can select by minutes, hourly, Daily or using cron expression.

**Execute immediately**: Can select to execute this time setting immediately or as per setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/rdbms%20-%20batch.png?api=v2)

b. Select the **Zeus path**(Storage Name), which is a storage path. (The platform set default Zeus as an initial setup)

c. Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

When you select a Schema for importing a data asset, it will automatically be saved to the storage configured for that schema. However, users can still manually change the storage location during the import process.   
[See more details on how to configure storage associated with a schema.](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management)

3. Fill in the database information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), and [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning).

- **Import Data default information field**: To add import data default field (gtp\_timestamp) into the data asset.
- **Time Domain** : Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

  - **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
  - **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

- **Partitions**:With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

  - **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
  - **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.
- **Delta table** : To create the Delta table asset.

  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
  - **Service Enabled**: To enable Delta services including Delta Compaction Job and Vacuum Job.
- **Encrypt Data** : To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
- **Other configurations**

  - Spark Source Configuration
  - Advanced Sink Configuration

4. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.

![image-20251009-084735.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/image-20251009-084735.png?api=v2)

5. Click “**Next**“ button :next: and go back to the Data Catalog page :catalog:.

---

## **Input Query Command (SQL) Method**

1. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

![MySQL (SQL command).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/MySQL%20(SQL%20command).png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.
- **Schema**: To select a schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![RDBMD upload method.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/RDBMD%20upload%20method.png?api=v2)

- **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
- **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

  - **Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type and Upload Method.

Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type.

![upload file custom service setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106032/upload%20file%20custom%20service%20setting.png?api=v2)

2. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

SQL server is developed by Microsoft known as Microsoft SQL server. SQL server is widely used for relational database management system (RDBMS) and supports the SQL language.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106079/ss1.png?api=v2)

- **Username**: authorized username of this database server.
- **Password**: password of this database server.

2. Click “**Connect Database**“ button to connect with the database server.
3. Select a table Method and click “**Next**“.

   - **Select Database Table**: Select your data tables. The user able to select more than one data tables with Multi-select option. This method cannot import the data into the existing table. If users need to import the data into existing table, please select “**Input Query Command (SQL)**” method go to **Select Database Table** topic.
   - **Input Query Command (SQL)**: Search a data table by using the SQL commands for a single data asset. Go to **Input Query Command (SQL)** topic.

  - **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
  - **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

## **Input Query Command (SQL) Method**

1. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![RDBMD upload method.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106079/RDBMD%20upload%20method.png?api=v2)

## Oracle

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106136/Oracle |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T06:01:15.654Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > RDBMS |

**Oracle** is a powerful relational database management system (RDBMS) developed by Oracle Corporation. The following step shows you how to import the Oracle database or database service into the Blendata Enterprise.

1. Connect to Oracle by filling all information as per below:

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/oracle.png?api=v2)

- **Import the Oracle database name**

  - **URL:** the database server URL with this pattern *“**jdbc:oracle:thin:@<hostname>:<port>:<databaseName>**”*
  - **Username**: authorized username of this database server.
  - **Password**: password of this database server.
- **Import the Oracle database service name**

  - **URL:** the service server URL with this pattern *“**jdbc:oracle:thin:@//<hostname>:<port>/<databaseServiceName>**”*
  - **Username**: authorized username of this database server.
  - **Password**: password of this database server.

2. Click “**Connect Database**“ button to connect with the database server.
3. Select a table Method and click “**Next**“.

   - **Select Database Table**: Select your data tables. The user able to select more than one data tables with Multi-select option. This method cannot import the data into the existing table. If users need to import the data into existing table, please select “**Input Query Command (SQL)**” method go to **Select Database Table** topic.
   - **Input Query Command (SQL)**: Search a data table by using the SQL commands for a single data asset. Go to **Input Query Command (SQL)** topic.

## **Select Database Table Method**

1. Select the data table and click setting icon :setting: to setup your table information.

You can select multiple tables for the data importing.

2. Select an upload method, Zeus path(Storage Name), and a data schema.

a. Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

**Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Time setting**: Can select by minutes, hourly, Daily or using cron expression.

**Execute immediately**: Can select to execute this time setting immediately or as per setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/rdbms%20-%20batch.png?api=v2)

b. Select the **Zeus path**(Storage Name), which is a storage path. (The platform set default Zeus as an initial setup)

c. Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

When you select a Schema for importing a data asset, it will automatically be saved to the storage configured for that schema. However, users can still manually change the storage location during the import process.   
[See more details on how to configure storage associated with a schema.](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management)

3. Fill in the database information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), and [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning).

- **Import Data default information field**: To add import data default field (gtp\_timestamp) into the data asset.
- **Time Domain** : Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

  - **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
  - **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

- **Partitions**:With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

  - **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
  - **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.
- **Delta table** : To create the Delta table asset.

  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
  - **Service Enabled**: To enable Delta services including Delta Compaction Job and Vacuum Job.
- **Encrypt Data** : To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
- **Other configurations**

  - Spark Source Configuration
  - Advanced Sink Configuration

4. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.

![image-20251009-084735.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/image-20251009-084735.png?api=v2)

5. Click “**Next**“ button :next: and go back to the Data Catalog page :catalog:.

---

## **Input Query Command (SQL) Method**

1. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

![output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/output%20setting.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.
- **Schema**: To select a schema for your data asset.

In v4.6.12, general users can’t access or create datasets in the “**default\_schema**.”   
Only the **Super\_Admin** can view and operate within it.

- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage path**: Select the Zeus path for each state (Internal state table)

When you select a Schema for importing a data asset, it will automatically be saved to the storage configured for that schema. However, users can still manually change the storage location during the import process.   
[See more details on how to configure storage associated with a schema.](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management)

- Other options depend on table source type selection

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![RDBMD upload method.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/RDBMD%20upload%20method.png?api=v2)

- **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
- **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

  - **Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type and Upload Method.

Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type.

![upload file custom service setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106136/upload%20file%20custom%20service%20setting.png?api=v2)

2. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

---

## PostgreSQL

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106168/PostgreSQL |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T06:01:32.497Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > RDBMS |

**PostgreSQL** is open-source relational database management system (RDBMS). It is commonly used for handling large and complex datasets and supporting a wide range of applications.

1. Connect to PostgreSQL by filling all server information as per below:

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106168/pro.png?api=v2)

- **URL:** the database server URL with this pattern *“**jdbc:postgresql://<hostname>:<port>/<databaseName>**”*
- **Username**: authorized username of this database server.
- **Password**: password of this database server.

2. Click “**Connect Database**“ button to connect with the database server.
3. Select a table Method and click “**Next**“.

   - **Select Database Table**: Select your data tables. The user able to select more than one data tables with Multi-select option. This method cannot import the data into the existing table. If users need to import the data into existing table, please select “**Input Query Command (SQL)**” method go to **Select Database Table** topic.
   - **Input Query Command (SQL)**: Search a data table by using the SQL commands for a single data asset. Go to **Input Query Command (SQL)** topic.

## **Select Database Table Method**

1. Select the data table and click setting icon :setting: to setup your table information.

You can select multiple tables for the data importing.

2. Select an upload method, Zeus path(Storage Name), and a data schema.

   1. Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

      - **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
      - **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

**Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Time setting**: Can select by minutes, hourly, Daily or using cron expression.

**Execute immediately**: Can select to execute this time setting immediately or as per setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106168/rdbms%20-%20batch.png?api=v2)

b. Select the **Zeus path**(Storage Name), which is a storage path. (The platform set default Zeus as an initial setup)

c. Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

When you select a Schema for importing a data asset, it will automatically be saved to the storage configured for that schema. However, users can still manually change the storage location during the import process.   
[See more details on how to configure storage associated with a schema.](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management)

3. Fill in the database information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), and [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning).

- **Import Data default information field**: To add import data default field (gtp\_timestamp) into the data asset.
- **Time Domain** : Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

  - **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
  - **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

- **Partitions**:With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

  - **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
  - **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.
- **Delta table** : To create the Delta table asset.

  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
  - **Service Enabled**: To enable Delta services including Delta Compaction Job and Vacuum Job.
- **Encrypt Data** : To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
- **Other configurations**

  - Spark Source Configuration
  - Advanced Sink Configuration

4. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.

![image-20251009-084735.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106168/image-20251009-084735.png?api=v2)

5. Click “**Next**“ button :next: and go back to the Data Catalog page :catalog:.

## Other RDBMS

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106200/Other+RDBMS |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T06:01:49.961Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > RDBMS |

Blendata Enterprise also supports other RDBMS using JDBC, Hive, or Cloudera Impala. The user can follow the instruction below to import the data into the platform.

1. Install the Driver.

   - Go to **zeus/extra-libs** location.
   - Put the **Java Driver library** in this location.
2. Fill in the JDBC Driver with Driver Class name.
3. Connect to RDBMS by filling all information as per below:

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106200/jdbc%20drier.png?api=v2)

- **URL:** the JDBC server URL

  - **Hive:** jdbc:hive2://<host>:<port>/<databaseName>
  - **Cloudera**: jdbc:impala://<host>:<port>/<databaseName>
- **Username**: authorized username of this database server.
- **Password**: password of this database server.

4. Click “**Connect Database**“ button to connect with the database server.
5. Select a table Method and click “**Next**“.

   - **Select Database Table**: Select your data tables. The user able to select more than one data tables with Multi-select option. This method cannot import the data into the existing table. If users need to import the data into existing table, please select “**Input Query Command (SQL)**” method go to **Select Database Table** topic.
   - **Input Query Command (SQL)**: Search a data table by using the SQL commands for a single data asset. Go to **Input Query Command (SQL)** topic.

1. Select the data table and click setting icon :setting: to setup your table information.

You can select multiple tables for the data importing.

2. Select an upload method, Zeus path(Storage Name), and a data schema.

   1. Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

      - **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
      - **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

**Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Time setting**: Can select by minutes, hourly, Daily or using cron expression.

**Execute immediately**: Can select to execute this time setting immediately or as per setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106200/rdbms%20-%20batch.png?api=v2)

b. Select the **Zeus path**(Storage Name), which is a storage path. (The platform set default Zeus as an initial setup)

c. Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

When you select a Schema for importing a data asset, it will automatically be saved to the storage configured for that schema. However, users can still manually change the storage location during the import process.   
[See more details on how to configure storage associated with a schema.](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management)

3. Fill in the database information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), and [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning).

  - **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
  - **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

- **Partitions**:With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

  - **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
  - **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.
- **Delta table** : To create the Delta table asset.

  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
  - **Service Enabled**: To enable Delta services including Delta Compaction Job and Vacuum Job.
