Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106200/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106200/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106200/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106200/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106200/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106200/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type.

![upload file custom service setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106200/upload%20file%20custom%20service%20setting.png?api=v2)

2. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

---

## Change Data Capture

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106445/Change+Data+Capture |
| **Last modified** | 2026-06-24T09:51:27.650Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data |

1. Connect with your Structured Database by filling in the detail below.

![cdc2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106445/cdc2.png?api=v2)

- **URL:** the database server URL with each RDBMS patterns. (You can see the pattern of each database, see more [RDBMS](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2229207125/RDBMS) page.)
- **Username**: authorized username of this database server.
- **Password**: password of this database server.

2. Click “**Connect Database**“ and click “**Next**“ for next setting.
3. Select table to import and click :setting: icon to see the data fields of selected table.

![image-20250828-053215.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106445/image-20250828-053215.png?api=v2)

4. Set each state path or use the default path.
5. Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

When you select a Schema for importing a data asset, it will automatically be saved to the storage configured for that schema. However, users can still manually change the storage location during the import process.   
[See more details on how to configure storage associated with a schema.](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management)

6. Set the Database Timezone.
7. Set the Stream Settings by specific the days for streaming and each states.

   - **Steaming Setting**

     - **Interval (sec):** To specific the data stream interval using second value.
     - **Number of files output:** To indicate the number of the files output.
     - **Max offsets per trigger**: To fill in a maximum number of Kafka offsets (data records) during each streaming session.
     - **Continue without data loss prevention:** Enable the platform to continue even if there is data loss during the data streaming.
   - **Change to state:** Manage the storage of data to improve the efficiency of other dependent jobs. (only for Time-Series asset and create partition file using timestamp)

     - **Use System Default:** Use the system default setting.
     - **Customize Refresh Job:** Customize the job by users. See more details about State table, go to [State Tables](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table#Data-Compaction) page.
   - **Refresh Job:** To setup an automatically update a new data in your data asset.

     - **Use System Default**: Use the system default job setting.
     - **Customize Refresh Job**: Customize the job by users.
   - **Recovery Job:** To setup an automatically update a new partition in your data asset. (Note: If users import the data using Internal Table type and setup a partition, the platform will auto create a Recovery Job.)

     - **Use System Default**: Use the system default job setting.
     - **Customize Refresh Job**: Customize the job by users.
8. Setup the **Advanced options** for Zeus to run.
9. Setup the **CDC Advanced options**.
10. Click “**Preview Table**“ button :preview: to preview your data and click “Next“ button :next: .
11. Fill in the database information such as Table name, Time Domain, Partition, and Data Encry. Then click “**Next**“ button :next:. Your data will appear in the Data Catalog page.

---

## Import the Data into Existing table

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:37:17.100Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data |

This feature allows users to import the data into the existing table, which is not related to import services(Table update, Stream, and Aggregate), service chain, and Workflow service. There are 3 upload methods for import with one time including One Time (will have another option for Overwrite Source), Schedule, and Stream.

Upload to Existing Table option is only available to those with **'Edit' or higher permissions** on the data asset.

# How to Import into Existing table

1. Click “**Create New**“ and select “**Import Data**” option.
2. Select your source type, which is capable for single table asset only.

For RDBMS and NoSQL, which has multiple tables, needs to use Input Query Command(SQL) mode to select only one table for data importing only.

3. Select your file or set the file connection. Then, setup the input option and customize fields.
4. Setup the output option using **Upload to Existing Table**.
5. Select your existing table name. You cannot revise the table setting such as Encrypt Data, Time Domain, and Partition.

![upload to existing table.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106502/upload%20to%20existing%20table.png?api=v2)

6. Select your upload method.

   - **One-Time Upload**: To upload the data into the existing table one time.

     - **Overwrite source option**: Select this option to modify the source path of existing table.
   - **Scheduled Upload**: Select overwrite or append action for save mode.

     - **Move to Trash after finishing import data**: Enable the platform to move the file to trash.
     - **Backup data if process failed**: Tick this option to backup the data for overwrite save mode.
   - **Stream Upload**: To do the stream upload. (Unavailable for Delta type)

![upload method.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106502/upload%20method.png?api=v2)

**Note**:

- In case you import the data using **Upload File** option, the Upload Method menu won’t display.
- If the existing table has an inactive service, users can select **One-time upload** option only.

7. Map the data schema between the existing table and the source table.

![schema mapping.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106502/schema%20mapping.png?api=v2)

8. Enable your related services, which depends on the data asset type and upload method.

![image-20240905-112710.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106502/image-20240905-112710.png?api=v2)

- **Internal Table**

  - **Scheduled Upload**: Able to specific a time for an import service via **Change Customization** option.
  - **Stream Upload**: Able to set up the Streaming via **Change Customization** option.
- **Delta Table**

  - **One-time Upload**: Enable Delta services via **Change Customization**.
  - **Scheduled Upload**: Able to specific a time for time update job and enable Delta services via **Change Customization**.

If the existing table has Delta services enabled, they cannot be disabled in this service setting.

- **Internal State Table**

  - **Scheduled Upload**: Able to specific a time for an import service via **Change Customization** option.
  - **Stream Upload**: Able to set up the Streaming via **Change Customization** option.

---

## Visualization & Dashboard

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106579/Visualization+Dashboard |
| **Last modified** | 2025-08-13T10:16:56.586Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 |

# Visualization

To transform raw data into a visual representation that is more readable and easily understandable, the platform offers a variety of chart types for users to explore. For more details about visualization, see more [Visualization](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106605/Visualization) page.

# Dashboard

To consolidate multiple visualization files into a single location for comprehensive data analysis and insights from different perspectives. For more details about Dashboard, see more [Dashboard](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121107006/Dashboard) page.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106579/Demo.png?api=v2)

---

# Permission Metrics

## Project/Application/Dashboard

The Application and Dashboard permissions will be inherited from the project management settings, but users can adjust the permission levels later.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Project, Application, Dashboard Management** | **Guest** | **Edit** | **Manage** | **Owner** |
| Create new Project | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Create new Application, or Dashboard | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Edit Project, Application, or Dashboard | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Export Project, Application, or Dashboard | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Delete Project, Application, or Dashboard | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Manage Permission | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Import Visualization into Dashboard | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

Users must have the data asset permission at least **View** level to see the visualization data and export the visualization from dashboard.

## Visualization

![vz2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106579/05b9ca42-9951-42ad-ae3e-ee24517058a7%23media-blob-url=true&id=49f67cfb-fd75-4e74-85fd-9124c0d1b15b&contextId=2121106941&collection=contentId-2121106941?api=v2)

The visualization permission levels are global permissions, consisting of three layers: Shared, Owner, and Creator.

- **Shared**: The visualization is shared with all users under the same tenant. Others can view, edit, and also add this visualization to their dashboards.
- **Owner**: The visualization is shared with you and assigned to you as the owner.

  - **Owner** (orange color): This visualization was created by someone else and shared with you. You are assigned as the owner by the original creator.
  - **Owner** (blue color): This is a visualization you created yourself, but ownership has been assigned to you via a role.
- **Creator**: This is a visualization you created entirely by yourself.

---

## Visualization

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106605/Visualization |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-26T11:01:19.372Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard |

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106605/viz.jpeg?api=v2)

# Visualization Components

## 1. Search

The search bar to search your visualization files or shared visualization files.

- **Only my Viz**: Search only your own visualization files.
- **Share with me**: Show the Visualization files that others shared with you.
- **Matching result**: The matching search result.

## 2. View

To display the visualization file as a list style or table style. :view:

## 3. Create Viz

This button is used to create a new visualization file. When the user clicks “+ **Create Viz**”, the platform will display an '**Add dataset**' popup to select the dataset for visualization creation. The visualization workspace will then appear as shown below. For additional menu details, refer to the information below:

In v4.6.12, general users can’t access or retrieve datasets from the “**default\_schema.**”  
Only the “**Super\_Admin**” can view and operate within it.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106605/vz4.png?api=v2)

- Main actions

  - **Save As:** To save a current visualization as a new file and will display on Visualization Catalog page.
  - **+ Create New:** To create the new visualization.
  - **Open Viz:** Open your own visualization file or shared visualization files.
  - **Settings**

    - **Advanced Options:** Able to select the Zeus to do visualization processing and able to save the selected Zeus for data query.
- **Dataset:** Show the fields list from the selected data asset. There are two actions that users can manage the dataset as below:

  - **Change datasets**: Change the datasets.
  - **Delete**: Delete the current dataset and ask to select the new dataset.

The platform tracks all configurations when there is a dataset change or SQL change.

- **Design:** The Visualization design that the user need to fill in and setup on this panel.

  - **Default Trace**: A trace dropdown list for initial trace.
  - **Trace**: the trace box for setting and design the data in this trace.
  - **Add Trace**: Add a new trace with the same dataset by clicking “**+**” on the right top of design panel. The user can select only one trace for a Default Trace.
  - **Trace Actions**: Expand the Trace box :resize: or organize the Trace :move: .
  - **More Actions** (:3dots:) : Consists of a trace rename and delete trace options.
  - **Viz Type**: Chart or graph types.
- **Preview:** The preview of visualization panel after create a visualization or revise the setting. To see the visualization result, the user need to click “**Preview result**“.

  - **Time range:** Select the time range to display the data

    - **All Time Select**: Select all the periods.
    - **Date-time Select**: Specify the period to display in the visualization.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106605/image-20210301-155825.png?api=v2)

During the visualization processing, the user is ables to kill the query job to stop the task.

## 4. Action

- **Import Viz:** To import the visualization with Json file type by clicking “**Open the File Browser**” or Drag & Drop file into the box > click “**Import**”.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106605/vz5.png?api=v2)

- **Export Viz:** To export the selected visualization and download the visualization file as a Json file type.

---

# Visualization Creation

Our platform offers a variety of chart types, including:

1. [Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106707/Table) 9. [Horizontal bar](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106883/Horizontal+Bar)
2. [Bar Chart](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106729/Bar+Chart) 10. [Gauge Chart](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106905/Gauge+Chart)
3. [Scatter Chart](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106751/Scatter+Chart) 11. [Single Value](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2618982441/Single+Value)
4. [Bubble Chart](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106773/Bubble+Chart) 12. [Wordcloud](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2624421890/Wordcloud)
5. [Line Chart](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106795/Line+Chart) 13. [Topology](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2625339416/Topology)
6. [Area Chart](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/gYVtfg)  14. [Sankey](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2628878342/Sankey)
7. [Pie Chart](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106839/Pie+Chart) 15. [Leaflet map](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2627436557/Leaflet+Map)
8. [Doughnut Chart](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106861/Doughnut+Chart) 16. [Heatmap](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2628288522/Heatmap)

---

# Manage & Sharing

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106605/vz.png?api=v2)

Once your visualization file is complete, you are able to manage this visualization file with edit, manage the permissions, and also delete the visualization files. See more details, go to [Manage & Sharing](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106941/Visualization+Management).

---

# Customize Visualization

For advanced users, our platform enables customization of visualization charts by configuring parameters and settings, facilitating the creation of new chart types. For more information about how to create a customize visualization, see more [Customize Visualization](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323788/Advanced+user+Custom+visualization+API+reference) page.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106605/image-20210729-051336.png?api=v2)

---

## Visualization Creation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106676/Visualization+Creation |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-26T11:01:35.291Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization |

There are two modes for the visualization creation including Simple Mode and SQL Mode.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106676/v4.PNG?api=v2)

In v4.6.12, general users can’t access or retrieve datasets from the “**default\_schema.**”  
Only the “**Super\_Admin**” can view and operate within it.

# Simple Mode

The user ables to select your own asset or shared asset to you only.

1. Enable “**Edit mode**” :edit\_mode: and click “**Add Viz**“ button :add\_viz: to create the visualization file.
2. Click “**Create New**“ button :create\_new\_viz: and click “**Add Dataset**“ button :dataset:.
3. Select “**Simple Mode**“ and search your dataset name. Then, click “**Add**“ button :add\_bt:.
4. The platform will show a new visualization workspace as per below screenshot. There are the options showed on this page. For more information about visualization workspace, see more [Visualization](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106605/Visualization#%3Ainfo%3A-Visualization-Components).

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106676/v1.PNG?api=v2)

5. Select the chart type and identify a field for the X, Y axis, an other setting as per your selected chart.

**Chart types**

- Table
- Bar Chart
- Scatter Chart
- Bubble Chart
- Line Chart
- Area Chart
- Pie Chart
- Doughnut Chart

- Horizontal bar
- Gauge Chart
- Single Value
- Word Could
- Topology
- Sankey
- Leaflet map
- Heatmap

5. Set charts style or decorate your chart, you can click at “**Option**“ tab:options:.
6. Click “**Preview Result**“ button :preview: to see the chart.

   1. To display all data for a time-series dataset in the chart, tick the “**All time select**” box :all\_time: or select a specific time range.
7. Click “**Save As**“ button :save\_as:to save the new visualization and put the visualization name or description.

---

# **SQL Mode**

The platform allows users to utilize SQL queries to extract data and convert it into visualizations.

1. Enable “**Edit mode**” :edit\_mode: and click “**Add Viz**“ button :add\_viz: to create the visualization file.
2. Click “**Create New**“ button :create\_new\_viz: and click “**Add Dataset**“ button :dataset:.
3. Select “**SQL Mode**“ and write the SQL command. Then, click “**Add**“ button :add\_bt:.
4. Select the [chart type](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106605/Visualization#Creation) and identify a field for the X, Y axis, an other setting as per your selected chart.
5. Set charts style or decorate your chart, you can click at “**Option**“ tab:options:.
6. Click “**Preview Result**“ button :preview: to see the chart.

   1. To display all data for a time-series dataset in the chart, tick the “**All time select**” box :all\_time: or select a specific time range.
7. Click “**Save As**“ button :save\_as:to save the new visualization and put the visualization name or description.

---

## Table

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106707/Table |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:53:15.337Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

The common chart style that represents data in a table format. This type is suitable for detailed data and can be used to view summary numbers from operations such as Sum, Count, and Average.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106707/vz3.png?api=v2)

- **Select**: A box that enables users to drag and drop fields from a dataset on the left-hand side and display them in the visualization.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

---

## Bar Chart

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106729/Bar+Chart |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-17T09:26:32.576Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

The bar chart consists of the X and Y axes, represented as a vertical bar chart. This chart type visualizes data heights, providing an advantage for numerical comparisons.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106729/bar.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Y Axis / X Axis** : Select the field of dataset and represent as X axis and Y axis.
- **Spilt by**: To spilt the data on Y axis.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Option

- **Chart Option**

  - **Main Aspect Ratio**: To set a chart as main aspect ratio.
- **Y Axes Option**

  - **Axes Y Position**: Location of Y axis (left or right).
  - **Begin Zero**: Option to begin the data from zero.
  - **Enable Axis Y Label**: To display the the Y axis label on the chart.
  - **Axis Y Label**: To set the Y axis name.
  - **Axis Y Max**: To maximize the vertical axis number.
  - **Axis Y Min:** To minimize the vertical axis number.
- **X Axes Option**

  - **Enable Axis X Label**: To display the the X axis label on the chart.
  - **Axis X Label**: To set the X axis name.
- **X Axes Time Option**

  - **Time Min Unit**: To select the time unit of x axis data.
  - **Display Formats Millisecond**: To set a millisecond format.
  - **Display Formats Second**: To set a second format.
  - **Display Formats Minute**: To set a minute format.
  - **Display Formats Hour**: To set a hour format.
  - **Display Formats Day**: To set a day format.
  - **Display Formats Week**: To set a week format.
  - **Display Formats Month**: To set a month format.
  - **Display Formats Quarter**: To set a quarter format.
  - **Display Formats Year**: To set a year format.
- **Bar Option**

  - **Fill Color Set**: To select the chart or graph color by filling in the color code or color name for instance (Pink, Red, Black) for 3 colors.
  - **Border Color**: To set the border color of a chart or graph.
  - **Border Width**: To set the border width of a chart or graph.
  - **Border Skipped**: To hide the border of a chart or graph one side at a time.
  - **Hover Fill Color**: To highlight the color of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
  - **Hover Border Color**: To highlight the border color of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
  - **Hover Border Width**: To expand a border width of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
- **Zoom Option**

  - **Pan**: Enable to move the chart or graph on the visualization panel.
  - **Zoomable**: To expand and shrink a chart or graph.
- **Layout**

  - **Padding Left**: To set a margin from a left border.
  - **Padding Right**: To set a margin from a right border.
  - **Padding Top**: To set a margin from a top border.
  - **Padding Bottom**: To set a margin from a bottom border.
- **Legend**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a legend option.
  - **Position**: To set the location of a legend (top, bottom, left, or right).
  - **Align**: To align the legend location as a center, left, or right.
- **Title**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a title option.
  - **Title Text**: To set the title name.
- **Tooltip**

  - **Enable Tooltip**: To enable the legend on a chart, when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
- **Tread Line**

  - **Enable Tread Line**: เปิดใช้งาน Tread Line
  - **Line Style**: To choose the line styles, which are linear, exponential, and polynomial.
  - **Line Color**: To set a line color.
  - **Line Width**: To set the line width.
- **Annotation**

  - **Add annotation here**: To add a strip line for data indication.
- **Label Config**

  - **Label Value Type**: Show the label on a graph by setting type as per below.

    - **None**: Not show the value.
    - **Label**: Show a trace name on a graph.
    - **Value**: Show the real value on a graph.
    - **Percentage**: Show the value as a percentage on a graph.
  - **Rotation**: rotate a label.
  - **Position**: To change the label position
  - **Font Size**: To specify a size of font.
  - **Font Color**: To set a font color.
- **Theme**

  - **Theme Color**: The theme color list to apply in a chart.
  - **Fixed Color List**: To specify the color of a chart or graph as per field value setting.

---

## Scatter Chart

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106751/Scatter+Chart |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-17T09:26:45.052Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

Scatter chart, also known as a scatter plot or scattergram, displays individual data points on a two-dimensional plane, with each point representing the values of two variables, usually plotted on the X and Y axes. It is a useful tool for visualizing the relationship between two continuous variables and identifying patterns or trends in the data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106751/scatter.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Y Axis / X Axis**: Select the field of dataset and represent as X axis and Y axis.
- **Spilt by**: To spilt the data on a chart.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Chart Option**

  - **Main Aspect Ratio**: To set a chart as main aspect ratio.
  - **Responsive**:
  - **Stack Bar**: Combine all data into one bar chart.
- **XY Axes Option**

  - **Axis X Begin Zero**: Option to begin the data from zero.
  - **Enable Axis X Label**: To display the the X axis label on the chart.
  - **Axis X Max**: To maximize the horizontal axis number.
  - **Axis X Min:** To minimize the horizontal axis number.
  - **Axis Y Begin Zero**: Option to begin the data from zero.
  - **Enable Axis Y Label**: To display the the Y axis label on the chart.
  - **Axis Y Max**: To maximize the vertical axis number.
  - **Axis Y Min:** To minimize the vertical axis number.

- **Scatter Options**

  - **Transparency**: To set the transparency level of the chart. (*Example*: 0-1 likes 0, 0.5, or 1.)
  - **Fill Color Set:** To select the chart or graph color by filling in the color code or color name for instance (Pink, Red, Black) for 3 colors.
  - **Legend Color Set**: To set a legend color.
  - **Legend Border Color**: To set a legend border color.
  - **Legend Border Width**: To set a legend border width.
  - **Border Color**: To set the border color of a chart or graph.
  - **Border Width**: To set the border width of a chart or graph.
  - **Border Skipped**: To hide the border of a chart or graph one side at a time.
  - **Hover Fill Color**: To highlight the color of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
  - **Hover Border Color**: To highlight the border color of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
  - **Hover Border Width**: To expand a border width of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
  - **Point Hit Radius**: To set the width of point hit radius.
  - **Point Style**: To choose the plot styles such as circle, star, or etc.
  - **Point Radius**: To specify the size of a point radius.
  - **Point Rotation**: To set the point rotation by using degree number.
- **Zoom Option**

  - **Pan**: Enable to move the chart or graph on the visualization panel.
  - **Zoomable**: To expand and shrink a chart or graph.
- **Layout**

  - **Padding Left**: To set a margin from a left border.
  - **Padding Right**: To set a margin from a right border.
  - **Padding Top**: To set a margin from a top border.
  - **Padding Bottom**: To set a margin from a bottom border.
- **Legend**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a legend option.
  - **Position**: To set the location of a legend (top, bottom, left, or right).
  - **Align**: To align the legend location as a center, left, or right.
- **Title**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a title option.
  - **Title Text**: To set the title name.
- **Label Config**

  - **Label Value Type**: Show the label on a graph by setting type as per below.

    - **None**: Not show the value.
    - **Value**: Show the real value on a graph.
  - **Rotation**: rotate a label.
  - **Position**: To change the label position
  - **Font Size**: To specify a size of font.
  - **Font Color**: To set a font color.
- **Tooltip**

  - **Enable Tooltip**: To enable the legend on a chart, when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
- **Annotation**

  - **Add annotation here**: To add a strip line for data indication.
- **Tread Line**

  - **Enable Tread Line**: To activate a tread line option.
  - **Line Style**: To choose the line styles, which are linear, exponential, and polynomial.
  - **Line Color**: To set a line color.
  - **Line Width**: To set the line width.
- **Theme**

  - **Theme Color**: The theme color list to apply in a chart.
  - **Fixed Color List**: To specify the color of a chart or graph as per field value setting.

---

## Bubble Chart

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106773/Bubble+Chart |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-17T09:27:04.319Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

This represents data with varying point sizes in the chart, known as bubble charts, with two axes. Similar to a scatter chart, the bubble chart displays values in three dimensions, including the X-axis, Y-axis, and the size of the bubble.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106773/bubble.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Y Axis / X Axis** : Select the field of dataset and represent as X axis and Y axis.
- **Spilt by**: To spilt the data on Y axis.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Chart Option**

  - **Main Aspect Ratio:** To set a chart as main aspect ratio.
- **X Axes Option**

  - **Axis X Begin Zero**: Option to begin the data from zero.
  - **Enable Axis X Label**: To display the the X axis label on the chart.
  - **Axis X Max**: To maximize the horizontal axis number.
  - **Enable Axis X Ticks**: To display the value of X axis field on the chart.
  - **Show Axis X Grid Line**: To show X axis grid line.
- **Y Axes Option**

  - **Axis Y Begin Zero**: Option to begin the data from zero.
  - **Enable Axis Y Label**: To display the the Y axis label on the chart.
  - **Axis Y Max**: To maximize the vertical axis number.
  - **Enable Axis Y Ticks**: To display the value of Y axis field on the chart.
  - **Show Axis Y Grid Line**: To show Y axis grid line.
- **Bubble Options**

  - **Fill Color Set:** To select the chart or graph color by filling in the color code or color name for instance (Pink, Red, Black) for 3 colors.
  - **Border Color**: To set the border color of a chart or graph.
  - **Border Width**: To set the border width of a chart or graph.
  - **Hover Fill Color**: To highlight the color of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
  - **Hover Border Color**: To highlight the border color of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
  - **Hover Border Width**: To expand a border width of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
  - **Hover Radius**: To expand a point size of a chart or graph as per setting ,when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
  - **Point Style**: To choose the plot styles such as circle, star, or etc.
  - **Rotation**: To set the point rotation by using degree number.
- **Zoom Option**

  - **Pan**: Enable to move the chart or graph on the visualization panel.
  - **Zoomable**: To expand and shrink a chart or graph.
- **Layout**

  - **Padding Left**: To set a margin from a left border.
  - **Padding Right**: To set a margin from a right border.
  - **Padding Top**: To set a margin from a top border.
  - **Padding Bottom**: To set a margin from a bottom border.
- **Legend**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a legend option.
  - **Position**: To set the location of a legend (top, bottom, left, or right).
  - **Align**: To align the legend location as a center, left, or right.
- **Title**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a title option.
  - **Title Text**: To set the title name.
- **Annotation**

  - **Add annotation here**: To add a strip line for data indication.
- **Label Config**: To display a value on the graph as per label value type setting.

  - **Label Value Type**: Show the label on a graph by setting type as per below.

    - **None**: Not show the value.
    - **Value**: Show the real value on a graph.
  - **Font Color**: To set a font color.
  - **Font Size**: To specify a size of font.
  - **Label Pattern**: To specify the label pattern of x, y, and z.
- **Theme**

  - **Theme Color**: The theme color list to apply in a chart.
  - **Fixed Color List**: To specify the color of a chart or graph as per field value setting.

---

## Line Chart

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106795/Line+Chart |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-17T09:27:20.225Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

A line chart is a visual representation that displays data points on a two-dimensional plane, connecting them with straight lines. This chart type is particularly useful for showing trends or patterns over a continuous interval or time series.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106795/line.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Y Axis / X Axis**: Select the field of dataset and represent as X axis and Y axis.
- **Spilt by**: To spilt the data on a chart.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Chart Option**

  - **Main Aspect Ratio**: To set a chart as main aspect ratio.
- **Y Axes Option**

  - Axis Y Position:
  - **Axis X Begin Zero**: Option to begin the data from zero.
  - **Enable Axis X Label**: To display the the X axis label on the chart.
  - **Axis X Max**: To maximize the horizontal axis number.
  - **Axis X Min:** To minimize the horizontal axis number.
- **X Axes Option**

  - **Enable Axis X Label**: To display the the Y axis label on the chart.
- **X Axes Time Option**

  - **Time Min Unit**: To select the time unit of x axis data.
  - **Display Formats Millisecond**: To set a millisecond format.
  - **Display Formats Second**: To set a second format.
  - **Display Formats Minute**: To set a minute format.
  - **Display Formats Hour**: To set a hour format.
  - **Display Formats Day**: To set a day format.
  - **Display Formats Week**: To set a week format.
  - **Display Formats Month**: To set a month format.
  - **Display Formats Quarter**: To set a quarter format.
  - **Display Formats Year**: To set a year format.
- **Line Option**

  - **Connect Null Data**: To link a null value with other values that are greater than one.
  - **Stepped Line**: To connect between points likes a stepped line.
  - **Transparency**: To set the transparency level of the chart. (*Example*: 0-1 likes 0, 0.5, or 1.)
  - **Line Tension**: To set a line tension.
  - **Line Color Set**: To set a color of the line.
  - **Border Width**: To set a border width of the line.
  - **Point Background Color**: To set a background color of the point.
  - **Point Border Color**: To set a border color of the point.
  - **Point Border Width**: To set a border width of the point.
  - **Point Hover Background Color**: To highlight the background color of a point based on settings when the cursor moves over a point on the line.
  - **Point Hover Border Color**: To highlight the border color of a point based on settings when the cursor moves over a point on the line.
  - **Point Hover Border Width**: To expand/ the border width of a point based on settings when the cursor moves over a point on the line.
  - **Point Hover Radius**:
  - **Point Hit Radius**: To set the width of point hit radius.
  - **Point Style**: To choose the plot styles such as circle, star, or etc.
  - **Point Radius**: To specify the size of a point radius.
  - **Point Rotation**: To set the point rotation by using degree number.
- **Zoom Option**

  - **Pan**: Enable to move the chart or graph on the visualization panel.
  - **Zoomable**: To expand and shrink a chart or graph.
- **Layout**

  - **Padding Left**: To set a margin from a left border.
  - **Padding Right**: To set a margin from a right border.
  - **Padding Top**: To set a margin from a top border.
  - **Padding Bottom**: To set a margin from a bottom border.
- **Legend**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a legend option.
  - **Position**: To set the location of a legend (top, bottom, left, or right).
  - **Align**: To align the legend location as a center, left, or right.
- **Title**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a title option.

    - **Title Text**: To set the title name.
- **Label Config**

  - **Label Value Type**: Show the label on a graph by setting type as per below.

    - **None**: Not show the value.
    - **Label**: Show a trace name on a graph.
    - **Value**: Show the real value on a graph.
    - **Percentage**: Show the value as a percentage on a graph.
  - **Font Size**: To specify a size of font.
  - **Font Color**: To set a font color.
  - **Precision**: Put a value from 0-5.
- **Tooltip**

  - **Enable Tooltip**: To enable the legend on a chart, when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
- **Annotation**

  - **Add annotation here**: To add a strip line for data indication.
- **Tread Line**

  - **Enable Tread Line**: To enable a tread Line.
  - **Line Style**: To choose the line styles, which are linear, exponential, and polynomial.
  - **Line Color**: To set a line color.
  - **Line Width**: To set the line width.
- **Theme**

  - **Theme Color**: The theme color list to apply in a chart.
  - **Fixed Color List**: To specify the color of a chart or graph as per field value setting.

## Area Chart

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106817/Area+Chart |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-17T09:27:38.208Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

The area chart presents data as a line trend filled with color below the line. This chart type is used to visualize trends and the cumulative total data at each point.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106817/area.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Y Axis / X Axis**: Select the field of dataset and represent as X axis and Y axis.
- **Spilt by**: To spilt the data on a chart.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Chart Option**

  - **Main Aspect Ratio**: To set a chart as main aspect ratio.
- **Y Axes Option**

  - **Axis Y Position**: To set a position of Y axis. (left or right)
  - **Axis X Begin Zero**: Option to begin the data from zero.
  - **Enable Axis X Label**: To display the the X axis label on the chart.
  - **Axis X Max**: To maximize the horizontal axis number.
  - **Axis X Min:** To minimize the horizontal axis number.
- **X Axes Option**

  - **Enable Axis X Label**: To display the the Y axis label on the chart.
- **X Axes Time Option**

  - **Time Min Unit**: To select the time unit of x axis data.
  - **Display Formats Millisecond**: To set a millisecond format.
  - **Display Formats Second**: To set a second format.
  - **Display Formats Minute**: To set a minute format.
  - **Display Formats Hour**: To set a hour format.
  - **Display Formats Day**: To set a day format.
  - **Display Formats Week**: To set a week format.
  - **Display Formats Month**: To set a month format.
  - **Display Formats Quarter**: To set a quarter format.
  - **Display Formats Year**: To set a year format.
- **Area Option**

  - **Connect Null Data**: To link a null value with other values that are greater than one.
  - **Stepped Line**: To connect between points likes a stepped line.
  - **Transparency**: To set the transparency level of the chart. (*Example*: 0-1 likes 0, 0.5, or 1.)
  - **Line Tension**: To set a line tension.
  - **Fill Color Set**: To set a color of the line.
  - **Border Color**: To set a border color.
  - **Border Width**: To set a border width of the line.
  - **Point Background Color**: To set a background color of the point.
  - **Point Border Color**: To set a border color of the point.
  - **Point Border Width**: To set a border width of the point.
  - **Point Hover Background Color**: To highlight the background color of a point based on settings when the cursor moves over a point on the line.
  - **Point Hover Border Color**: To highlight the border color of a point based on settings when the cursor moves over a point on the line.
  - **Point Hover Border Width**: To expand/ the border width of a point based on settings when the cursor moves over a point on the line.
  - **Point Hover Radius**: To set the width of point hover radius.
  - **Point Hit Radius**: To set the width of point hit radius.
  - **Point Style**: To choose the plot styles such as circle, star, or etc.
  - **Point Radius**: To specify the size of a point radius.
  - **Point Rotation**: To set the point rotation by using degree number.
- **Zoom Option**

  - **Pan**: Enable to move the chart or graph on the visualization panel.
  - **Zoomable**: To expand and shrink a chart or graph.
- **Layout**

  - **Padding Left**: To set a margin from a left border.
  - **Padding Right**: To set a margin from a right border.
  - **Padding Top**: To set a margin from a top border.
  - **Padding Bottom**: To set a margin from a bottom border.
- **Legend**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a legend option.
  - **Position**: To set the location of a legend (top, bottom, left, or right).
  - **Align**: To align the legend location as a center, left, or right.
- **Title**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a title option.

    - **Title Text**: To set the title name.
- **Label Config**

  - **Label Value Type**: Show the label on a graph by setting type as per below.

    - **None**: Not show the value.
    - **Label**: Show a trace name on a graph.
    - **Value**: Show the real value on a graph.
    - **Percentage**: Show the value as a percentage on a graph.
  - **Font Size**: To specify a size of font.
  - **Font Color**: To set a font color.
  - **Precision**: Put a value from 0-5.
- **Tooltip**

  - **Enable Tooltip**: To enable the legend on a chart, when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
- **Annotation**

  - **Add annotation here**: To add a strip line for data indication.
- **Trend Line**

  - **Enable Tread Line**: To enable a tread Line.
  - **Line Style**: To choose the line styles, which are linear, exponential, and polynomial.
  - **Line Color**: To set a line color.

## Pie Chart

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106839/Pie+Chart |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:53:19.718Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

This chart displays the data as a slice in pie chart that used to compare the proportionate part of the whole, and the size of each slice is proportional to the quantity it represents. The entire circle represents the total data set or 100%.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106839/pie.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Value(numeric)/Label**: Select the field of dataset and represent in a pie chart.
- **Spilt by**: To spilt the data on Y axis.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Chart Option**

  - **Main Aspect Ratio**: To set a chart as main aspect ratio.
- **Pie Options**

  - **Fill Color Set**: To select the chart or graph color by filling in the color code or color name for instance (Pink, Red, Black) for 3 colors.
  - **Border Color**: To set a border color of a graph.
  - **Border Width**: To set a border width of a graph.
  - **Border Align**: To set a align style for a graph.
  - **Hover Fill Color**: To highlight the color of a slice based on settings when the cursor moves over the slice in the pie chart.
  - **Hover Border Color**: To highlight the border color of a slice based on settings when the cursor moves over the slice in the pie chart.
  - **Hover Border Width**: To expand the border width of a slice based on settings when the cursor moves over the slice in the pie chart.
- **Layout**

  - **Padding Left**: To set a margin from a left border.
  - **Padding Right**: To set a margin from a right border.
  - **Padding Top**: To set a margin from a top border.
  - **Padding Bottom**: To set a margin from a bottom border.
- **Legend**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a legend option.
  - **Position**: To set the location of a legend (top, bottom, left, or right).
  - **Align**: To align the legend location as a center, left, or right.
- **Title**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a title option.

    - **Title Text**: To set the title name.
- **Tooltip**

  - **Enable Tooltip**: To enable the legend on a chart, when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
- **Label Config**

  - **Label Value Type**: Show the label on a graph by setting type as per below.

    - **Label**: Show the label data on a graph.
    - **Value**: Show the real value on a graph.
    - **Percentage**: Show the value as a percentage.
  - **Position:** To change the label position

    - **Border**: Display the label on chart border.
    - **Default**: Display the label by default value.
    - **Outside**: Display the label outside the graph.
  - **Precision**: To show the precision for percentage value. (Put a value from 0-5)
  - **Font Color**: To set a font color.
  - **Font Size**: To specify a size of font.

  **Theme**

  - **Theme Color**: The theme color list to apply in a chart.
  - **Fixed Color List**: To specify the color of a chart or graph as per field value setting.

---

## Doughnut Chart

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106861/Doughnut+Chart |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:53:20.461Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

A doughnut chart is similar to Pie chart but there is a hole in the center likes a ring. This chart visually represents the proportion of each category or segment in relation to the whole dataset.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106861/doughnut.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Value(numeric)/Label**: Select the field of dataset and represent in a pie chart.
- **Label**: Select the field as a label name.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Chart Option**

  - **Main Aspect Ratio**: To set a chart as main aspect ratio.
- **Doughnut Options**

  - **Fill Color Set**: To select the chart or graph color by filling in the color code or color name for instance (Pink, Red, Black) for 3 colors.
  - **Border Color**: To set a border color of a graph.
  - **Border Width**: To set a border width of a graph.
  - **Border Align**: To set a align style for a graph.

    - **Center**: To set a border alignment as center.
    - **Inner**: To set a border alignment as inner.
  - **Hover Fill Color**: To highlight the color of a slice based on settings when the cursor moves over the slice in the pie chart.
  - **Hover Border Color**: To highlight the border color of a slice based on settings when the cursor moves over the slice in the pie chart.
  - **Hover Border Width**: To expand the border width of a slice based on settings when the cursor moves over the slice in the pie chart.
- **Layout**

  - **Padding Left**: To set a margin from a left border.
  - **Padding Right**: To set a margin from a right border.
  - **Padding Top**: To set a margin from a top border.
  - **Padding Bottom**: To set a margin from a bottom border.
- **Legend**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a legend option.
  - **Position**: To set the location of a legend (top, bottom, left, or right).
  - **Align**: To align the legend location as a center, left, or right.
- **Title**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a title option.

    - **Title Text**: To set the title name.
- **Tooltip**

  - **Enable Tooltip**: To enable the legend on a chart, when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
- **Label Config**

  - **Label Value Type**: Show the label on a graph by setting type as per below.

    - **Label**: Show the label data on a graph.
    - **Value**: Show the real value on a graph.
    - **Percentage**: Show the value as a percentage.
  - **Position:** To change the label position

    - **Border**: Display the label on chart border.
    - **Default**: Display the label by default value.
    - **Outside**: Display the label outside the graph.
  - **Precision**: To show the precision for percentage value. (Put a value from 0-5)
  - **Font Color**: To set a font color.
  - **Font Size**: To specify a size of font.

  **Theme**

  - **Theme Color**: The theme color list to apply in a chart.
  - **Fixed Color List**: To specify the color of a chart or graph as per field value setting.

## Horizontal Bar

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106883/Horizontal+Bar |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:53:21.256Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

The bar chart consists of the X and Y axes, represented as a horizontal bar chart. This chart type visualizes data heights, providing an advantage for numerical comparisons.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106883/horizontal.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Y Axis / X Axis** : Select the field of dataset and represent as X axis and Y axis.
- **Spilt by**: To spilt the data on Y axis.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Chart Option**

  - **Main Aspect Ratio**: To set a chart as main aspect ratio.
- **XY Axes Option**

  - **Begin Zero**: Option to begin the data from zero.
  - **Stack Bar**: To stack the bars into a combined style.
  - **Enable Axis X Label**: To display the the Y axis label on the chart.
  - **Axis X Max**: To maximize the vertical axis number.
  - **Axis X Min:** To minimize the vertical axis number.
  - **Enable Axis Y Label**: To display the the Y axis label on the chart.

    - **Axis Y Label**: To set the Y axis name.
  - **Show Axis X Grid Line**: To display the X axis grid line.
  - **Show Axis Y Grid Line**: To display the Y axis grid line.
- **Y Axes Time Option**

  - **Time Min Unit**: To select the time unit of x axis data.
  - **Display Formats Millisecond**: To set a millisecond format.
  - **Display Formats Second**: To set a second format.
  - **Display Formats Minute**: To set a minute format.
  - **Display Formats Hour**: To set a hour format.
  - **Display Formats Day**: To set a day format.
  - **Display Formats Week**: To set a week format.
  - **Display Formats Month**: To set a month format.
  - **Display Formats Quarter**: To set a quarter format.
  - **Display Formats Year**: To set a year format.
- **Horizontal Bar Option**

  - **Fill Color Set**: To select the chart or graph color by filling in the color code or color name for instance (Pink, Red, Black) for 3 colors.
  - **Border Color**: To set a border color.
  - **Border Width**: To set a border width of the line.
  - **Border Skipped**: To hide a border of a horizontal bar only one side including bottom, left, top, or right.
  - **Hover Fill Color**: To highlight the color of a horizontal bar based on settings when the cursor moves over the horizontal bar in the chart.
  - **Hover Border Color**: To highlight the border color of a horizontal bar based on settings when the cursor moves over the horizontal bar in the chart.
  - **Hover Border Width**: To expand the border width of a horizontal bar based on settings when the cursor moves over the horizontal bar in the chart.
- **Zoom Option**

  - **Pan**: Enable to move the chart or graph on the visualization panel.
  - **Zoomable**: To expand and shrink a chart or graph.
- **Layout**

  - **Padding Left**: To set a margin from a left border.
  - **Padding Right**: To set a margin from a right border.
  - **Padding Top**: To set a margin from a top border.
  - **Padding Bottom**: To set a margin from a bottom border.
- **Legend**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a legend option.
  - **Position**: To set the location of a legend (top, bottom, left, or right).
  - **Align**: To align the legend location as a center, left, or right.
- **Title**

  - **Enable Legend**: To enable a title option.

    - **Title Text**: To set the title name.
- **Tooltip**

  - **Enable Tooltip**: To enable the legend on a chart, when a cursor move to the chart or graph.
- **Annotation**

  - **Add annotation here**: To add a strip line for data indication.
- **Label Config**

  - **Label Value Type**: Show the label on a graph by setting type as per below.

    - **None**: Not show the value.
    - **Label**: Show a trace name on a graph.
    - **Value**: Show the real value on a graph.
    - **Percentage**: Show the value as a percentage on a graph.
  - **Position**: To change the label position
  - **Font Size**: To specify a size of font.
  - **Font Color**: To set a font color.
- **Theme**

  - **Theme Color**: The theme color list to apply in a chart.
  - **Fixed Color List**: To specify the color of a chart or graph as per field value setting.

---

## Gauge Chart

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106905/Gauge+Chart |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-17T10:27:35.190Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

To represent the single value or compare the related data with the color range visualization.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106905/gauge.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Value(numeric)\***: Select the numeric field of dataset and represent in a Gauge chart.
- **Mode**: Choose the Gauge chart present likes Solid or Limit.
- **Percentage**: Show the value as percentage.
- **Range**: Set the percentage value range such as 0,35,75, and 100.
- **Min Value**: Specific the minimum value of this chart.
- **Max Value**: Specific the maximum value of this chart.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Gauge Option**

  - **Fill Color Set**: Setup the chart color or graph using color code or color name such as

    #ffffff, Pink, Red, or Black.
  - **Value Color**: Setup the text value color.
  - **Value Position**: Setup the value position like center or bottom.
  - **Min Max Front Color**: Setup the front color both the max and min text value.
- **Title**

  - **Title Text**: Fill in the title text of the chart.
  - **Title Text Color**: Setup the title text color.
- **Icon Option**

  - **Icon**: Fill in the icon name, which you can use from this [website](https://fontawesome.com/v4.7.0/icons/). (Using the name like “fa fa-male”)
  - **Icon Color**: Setup the icon color.

## Visualization Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106941/Visualization+Management |

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106941/vz.png?api=v2)

- **Edit Viz Detail**: Edit the visualization name and description.
- **Manage Permissions**: To manage permissions for the selected visualization file, the owner can assign access rights by entering a username in 'Users Assigned Owner' or specifying a role in 'Roles Assigned Owner.' Furthermore, you can make this visualization accessible to all users.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121106941/vz2.png?api=v2)

- **Delete Viz**: Delete the selected visualization file.

## Dashboard

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121107006/Dashboard |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:53:24.654Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard |

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107006/pj%20mng.png?api=v2)

# Project

This function is used to organize and manage the project in our Blendata Enterprise, which including a group of application, where each application consists of dashboards. Users can manage access permissions for the project, application, and dashboards.

# Application

The Application is a subset of a project, displaying all accessible a group of dashboards. Users can manage access permissions for application and dashboards.

# Dashboard

The location of the visualization files. We allow the user to add serval visualization files into the dashboard to get the data insight. Users can manage access of dashboard permissions.

---

## Project Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121107028/Project+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:53:25.404Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Dashboard |

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107028/p1.jpg?api=v2)

# **1. Search**

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107028/1.PNG?api=v2)

A search bar to search a project that the user has an project access.

- **Only my projects:** Search only my Project.
- **Matching result:** The search result number.

# **2. Action (Project’s Action)**

- **Add to favorite:** To set a Project as a favorite. There is a star symbol at the end of the project name and the favorite projects will display on a project homepage.
- **Export Project:** Choose a Project, Application, or Dashboard, and select the “Export” option. Subsequently, the platform, will download the file as a Json file.
- **Edit Project:** To edit the project name, description, and project picture of your own file or shared project file.
- **Delete Project:** To delete the selected Project.

# **3. Create Project**

**3.1 Create a project:** To create the project, where gather Applications.

- **Project name:** The name of a project.
- **Project Description:** The description of a project.
- **Project Image**

  - **Upload Image:** The cover picture of a project.

**3.2 Import Project:** The user can import a project with Json file. With these steps: click “**Create New**” > select “**Import Project**” > choose the file by clicking “**Open File Browser**” or drag & drop a file > click “**Import**”.

# **4. Project Page**

On a project page, users can view all accessible application files. The project owner has the ability to configure access and grant permissions to other users and roles.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107028/p2.jpg?api=v2)

**4.1 Project Name:** The name of a project.

**4.2 Search:** A search bar to search an accessible application.

- **Only my Application:** Search only you own application.
- **Matching result:** The search result number.

**4.3 Application :** If users can access to an application under the project, the user can see all applications on this page.

- **Action (Application’s Action)**

  - **Add to favorite:** To set a Application as a favorite. There is a star symbol at the end of the application name and the favorite application will display on a homepage.
  - **Export Project:** Choose an Application or Dashboard, and select the “Export” option. Subsequently, the platform, will download the file as a Json file.
  - **Edit Project:** To edit the application name, description, and application picture of your own file or a shared application from the project owner.
  - **Delete Project:** To delete the selected Application.

**4.4 Member :** To set a permission for a user and a role.

The platform will show a popup the set the access for roles and users.

- **Manage Role:** To set a permission for roles.
- **Add User:** To set a permission for users.

The permission level consists of below:

- **Guest:** Guest permission can view an application or dashboard under the application.
- **Editor:** Editor permission can edit the project name or an application name and create a dashboard.
- **Manager:** Manager permission can edit and change a project name or an application name. Furthermore, it enables setting permissions for members in both the Application and Dashboard.
- **Owner:** Owner can edit, create, or delete an Application or a Dashboard.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107028/p2.PNG?api=v2)

**4.5 Create new**

- **Create Application**

  - **Application Name:** The name of an application.
  - **Application Description:** The description of an application.
  - **Application Image:** The cover picture of an application.
- **Import Project:** The user can import an application with Json file. With these steps: click “**Create New**” > select “**Import Project**” > choose the file by clicking “**Open File Browser**” or drag & drop a file > click “**Import**”.

---

## Application Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121107065/Application+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:53:27.746Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Dashboard |

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107065/p3.jpg?api=v2)

**5.1 Application Name:** The name of an application.

**5.2 Search:** The search bar to search an accessible dashboard.

- **Only my Application:** Search only you own dashboard.
- **Matching result:** The search result number.

**5.3 Dashboard:** If users can access to an application under the project, the user can see all dashboard on this page.

- **Action (Dashboard’s Action)**

  - **Add to favorite:** To set a dashboard as a favorite. There is a star symbol at the end of the dashboard name and the favorite dashboard will display on a homepage.
  - **Export Dashboard:** Choose a dashboard, and select the “Export” option. Subsequently, the platform, will download the file as a Json file.
  - **Edit Dashboard:** To edit the dashboard name, description, and dashboard picture of your own file or a shared dashboard from the project owner.
  - **Delete Dashboard:** To delete the selected dashboard.

**5.4 Member**: To set a permission for a user and a role.

The platform will show a popup the set the access for roles and users.

- **Manage Role**: To set a permission for roles.
- **Add User**: To set a permission for users.

The permission level consists of below:

- **Guest:** Guest permission can view an dashboard under the application.
- **Editor:** Editor permission can edit the application name or an dashboard name and create a dashboard.
- **Manager:** Manager permission can edit and change an application name or a dashboard name. Furthermore, it enables setting permissions for members in dashboard.
- **Owner:** Owner can edit, create, or delete an dashboard or a application.

**5.5 Create New**

- **Create Dashboard**

  - **Dashboard Name:** The name of a dashboard
  - **Dashboard Description**: The description of a dashboard.
  - **Dashboard Image:** The cover picture of a dashboard.
- **Import Project:** The user can import a dashboard with Json file. With these steps: click “**Create New**” > select “**Import Project**” > choose the file by clicking “**Open File Browser**” or drag & drop a file > click “**Import**”.
