## Dashboard Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121107087/Dashboard+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-22T13:33:12.279Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Dashboard |

# Dashboard Components

The Dashboard page, the user able to present the visualization file, edit, or compare the visualization files on this page.

The user can resize the panel on the dashboard page to see all results with 12 columns.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107087/image-20221123-072158.png?api=v2)

# **6.1 Dashboard Name**

The name of dashboard.

# **6.2 Dashboard Content**

To display the visualization files or text of this dashboard.

Need to enable “**Edit Mode**” option to see the dashboard content.

**6.2.1 Save Change:** To save the change on the dashboard page.

**6.2.2 Add Viz:** Users can import an existing visualization file or create a new one. There are some menus popup when you click “**Add Viz“** as per below details.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107087/v1.png?api=v2)

- **Search:** A search bar to search a accessible visualization.

  - **Only my Viz:** Search only you own visualization file.
  - **Share with me:** The visualization file are shared from other members.
  - **View**: To change the visualization list on the page.
- **Create new:** To create a new visualization file.
- **Multi-Select:** Users are able to select multiple visualization files at the same time.

**Note:** When a user adds a visualization to a dashboard, the platform will send a notification reminding the user to save the changes in the dashboard.

**6.2.3 Add Text:** The platform will display a text panel for setting up text style and typing a message for a dashboard page.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107087/vz12.png?api=v2)

**6.2.4 Setting:** Configure a time for automatic dashboard refresh to reflect newly imported data.

- **Dashboard Refresh Time Interval:** Set a time by minutes to refresh a dashboard page.
- **Autoload visualize data:** Tick this option to automatically update the data on the dashboard.
- **Selected Zeus:** To choose a Zeus for data processing in the visualization and save this setting. If users do not specify a Zeus, the platform will select the default Zeus for it.

**6.2.5 Presentation**

- **Print:** To save a dashboard page into PDF file.
- **Exit Presentation:** To exit from this presentation page.

**6.2.6 Manage Global Filters**

filter data in visualization files, allowing users to manage all visualizations on a dashboard, whether they are your file or shared files. See more details, go to [Global Filter Management](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121107244/Global+Filter+Management) page.

**6.2.7 Publish Dashboard:** Used to publish this dashboard to others, who don’t have an authorization of Blendata. The others will see all visualization file on the dashboard.

The owner only can publish a dashboard.

- **Get Link**: The window to show URL and the user can copy the URL and paste to your browser. Also, the user can choose Get Embed Code option for your work.
- **Unpublish**: If the users need to set to stop sharing this dashboard, the user can click “**Unpublish**“.

The data assets need to set as a publish asset so the dashboard can be publish.

**6.2.8 Manage Dynamic Viz**

To select value on a filter to refresh the visualization data as per filter condition.

- **Condition Name:** Set a name of condition.
- **Condition List:** Set a condition for visualization by click “**Global filter**“ and setup the filter conditions (able to select many filter conditions from the existing filter).
- **Apply Panel:** Select the visualization file that need to apply the filter. The visualization need to have matching field of dataset with the global filter condition.

The Manage Dynamic Viz box will appear after the filer creation is done.

**6.2.9 Visualize Panel**

- **Visualize name:** The visualization name (on the left of the window).
- **View:** Change a view from visualization to table format.
- **Refresh:** Click this to update, when there is a change on the source visualization.

During the data processing, the users are able to click “**Stop loading**” to stop processing.

- **Setting**

  - **Edit Viz**

    - **Edit Style:** The plat form will show popup to edit the selected visualization style.
    - **Edit in Workspace:** Edit a visualization on the workspace.
    - **View Viz details:** The details of visualization.
- **Exports File**

  - **Export File:** Export the Data Asset as File type. The exported data asset file will appear in the [My Profile](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2198863965/My+Profile) page. For more information about export file, see [Export File](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385706/Export+File) page.
  - **Download CSV:** Download this visualization to CSV file.
- **Manage Filter:** To manage the filter for this visualization by creating a new filter as known as a local filter(Can follow the instruction of Global Filter 6.2.6)
- **Drilldown Options:** To drill down into the selected data and can link with other related dashboards that are setup a condition as per below.

  - **Drill-down Option Name:** To set the name of drill-down
  - **Event Action:** Select the action for drill-down.

    - **On Click:** Set on click action to call this event.
    - **On Double Click:** Set double click action to call this event.
  - **Event Position:** Select the location of drill-down.
  - **Event Condition:** Create condition for drill-down.

    - **Field:** Select a field for drill-down.
    - **Operation:** Set a condition for drill-down.
    - **Value:** Set a value for drill-down.
  - **Apply with Application:** Select an application for drill-down.
  - **Apply with Dashboard:** Select a dashboard for drill-down.
  - **Apply with Global Filter:** Select a global filter of a dashboard for drill-down.
  - **Apply with Local Filter:** Select a local filter of a dashboard for drill-down.
- **Delete Viz:** Remove a visualization from a dashboard.

# **6.3 Member**

To set a permission for a user and a role. The platform will show a popup the set the access for roles and users.

- **Manage Role**: To set a permission for roles.
- **Add User**: To set a permission for users.

The permission level consists of below:

- **Guest:** Guest permission can view an dashboard under the application.
- **Editor:** Editor permission can edit the application name or an dashboard name and create a dashboard.
- **Manager:** Manager permission can edit and change an application name or a dashboard name. Furthermore, it enables setting permissions for members in dashboard.
- **Owner:** Owner can edit, create, or delete an dashboard or a application.

---

## Dashboard Creation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121107165/Dashboard+Creation |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:53:34.450Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Dashboard > Dashboard Management |

For the initial setup, users need to create a project and application to organize their dashboards.

# Create a Project and Application

1. To create a new dashboard, Go to Project Management menu :proj: . There are the three components under Project management as following:

   1. **Project**: The top level of project management that help to organize the application.
   2. **Application**: The subset of project, which the project owner can manage the user access, who can access the application.
   3. **Dashboard**: The location of the visualizations that the user can add into the dashboard for data analysis.
2. Click “**Create New**” button :create\_new: and “**Create Project**“ option :project: . Then put the project name, description, and project image.
3. Create a new Application by clicking “**Create New**“ button :create\_new: and “**Create Application**“ option :app\_create: . Put the application name, description, and application image.

# Create a Dashboard

1. Create a new Dashboard by clicking “**Create New**“ button :create\_new: and “**Create Dashboard**“ option :dashboard: . Put the Dashboard name, description, and dashboard image.
2. Enable “**Edit mode**” :edit\_mode: and click “**Add Viz**“ button :add\_viz: to create the visualization file or select an existing visualization.

**Note:** For more details about how to create a new visualization, go to [Visualization Creation](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106676/Visualization+Creation) page. Once the user finish creating a new visualization file, the user has to go to Project Management menu :proj: and go to your Dashboard.

3. Click “**Add Viz**“ button :add\_viz: and select your visualization to add into your dashboard.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107165/bar%20chart.png?api=v2)

4. You can organize your dashboard with the the chart files. The user able to add more charts, rearrange, resize the chart, add more visualizations, or add text.

---

## Global Filter Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121107244/Global+Filter+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2024-10-16T10:53:41.131Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Dashboard > Dashboard Management |

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107244/data%20cat9.png?api=v2)

# Manage Global Filters

To filter data in visualization files, allowing users to manage all visualizations on a dashboard, whether they are your file or shared files.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107244/vz6.png?api=v2)

## **Add new Filter**

A button to create a new filter.

## **Filter Conditions**

Establish a filter condition to display specific results on the dashboard. There are filter condition filter as per below:

1. **Input**: Users are required to input values for filtering.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107244/vz6.png?api=v2)

- **Filter Customization**

  - **Value Type:** The value types of filter such as Text and Number.
  - **Default Value:** Set a default value for filtering.
  - **Hidden:** An option to hide a input box but the filter still process.

2. **Combo box**: A filter with value list.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107244/vz7.png?api=v2)

- **Filter Customization**

  - **Create Value List Method**

    - **Create own value list:** To create your own filter using your own value list. The users need to set a new name on the value list box.

      - **Enter value in field:** Put a value from a field.
      - **Enter value in label:** Put a new value names.
    - **List from select dataset:** To create the filter using a dataset and field. (your own file or shared data).

      - **Dataset used to filter:** Select a dataset that you can access or your own file.
      - **Field set as value:** Select the field from the dataset.
      - **Field display as label:** Set a label for a field to display on the filter.

        ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107244/image-20210817-082623.png?api=v2)
    - **Query with SQL:** To create a filter using SQL query.
  - **Multi-Option select:** Enable to select multi-values for filtering.

**3 Range:** A number filter, the user need to put the number range. Only apply with a number data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107244/vz8.png?api=v2)

- **Filter Customization**

  - **Start of range:** The start range number.
  - **End of range:** The end of range number.

**4 Datetime:** A date and time filter, the user need to put the date and time range.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107244/vz9.png?api=v2)

- **Filter Customization**

  - **Start of range:** The date and time for starting.
  - **End of range:** The date and time for ending.

## **Apply with**

To setup a dataset and filed for a filter, set up a visualization to apply with a filter.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107244/vz10.png?api=v2)

- **Apply all time series tables:** If the user select the filter condition as a Date time, the platform provides “**Apply all time series tables**” option. To apply this filter for all time series tables.
- **Dataset/Parameter list**: Click **+ Add new value** to add a dataset and field for this filter.
- **Applied Visualization List:** Show the visualization list from this dashboard.
- **Apply with Filter**: Filer list of this dashboard.

## **Basic Information**

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2121107244/vz11.png?api=v2)

- **Label:** To set a filter name.
- **Width**: To set the width of a filter box.
- **Require field:** If the user tick this box, the platform requires to fill in the filter condition. The platform will send a notification and the result won’t show, if there is no filter condition is applied.
- **Read only:** Set a input filter to be read only and cannot edit.

---

## Workflow Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2122809345/Workflow+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:53:32.987Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 |

The Workflow Management feature introduces powerful capabilities for optimizing processes through precise task sequencing, Success and Fail condition control, and streamlined chained job execution. Users gain greater flexibility and efficiency in organizing complex workflows, arranging tasks with precision, and ensuring automated execution proceeds uninterrupted until successful completion or specified fail conditions are met. This feature empowers users to enhance operational efficiency and customize process arrangement, making it a valuable tool for optimizing workflows.

# Workflow Components

![image-20251029-092642.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251029-092642.png?api=v2)

- **Search bar**: A search bar to search an existing workflow.
- **Latest Result**: A latest workflow result dropdown list to filter the workflow.
- **Status**: A workflow status dropdown list to filter the workflow.
- **Reset**: To reset the filter.

The “**Search & Filter**” section is now remembered even after refreshing the page or switching menus.

- **Matching Results**: The search result number.
- **Create Workflow**

  - **Create Workflow**: To create a new workflow.
  - **Import Workflow**: To import the workflow into the Blendata Enterprise. (JSON file)
- **Action**: To do an action for selected workflow including:

  - **Activate Workflow**: Enable the selected workflow to start running.
  - **Deactivate Workflow**: Disable the selected workflow to stop it from running.
  - **Deploy Selected**: Execute deployment of the selected workflow.
  - **Add Asset Permission**: Add permission to access multiple assets, and not changing other permissions assets setting.
  - **Export Selected**: Export the selected workflow as JSON file.

    - **Workflow Suffix Name (Optional)**: Enter a suffix name (up to 19 characters). It will be added after the workflow name.
  - **Delete Selected**: To delete the selected workflow.
- **Workflow Panel**: Show the workflow list and the workflow details.

  - **Workflow Name**: A workflow name.
  - **Workflow ID**: The workflow ID number.
  - **Tag**: A tag of a workflow. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)
  - **Owner**: A workflow owner name.
  - **Latest Result**: A latest result of workflow.
  - **Schedule**: A schedule time for this workflow
  - **Latest Fire Time**: The most recent execution time of the workflow.
  - **Next fire time**: The next scheduled execution time of the workflow.
  - **Status**: The status of a workflow including Active, Inactive, Deploying, and Deploy Failed.
  - **Action**: The action options of a workflow. See more details, go to [Workflow Management Actions](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2122809345/Workflow+Management#Workflow-Management-Actions) topic.

---

# Workflow Features

## Create a new Workflow

1. Click “**Create Workflow**“ and select “**+ Create Workflow**“.
2. Fill in the workflow name and description and tags.
3. Select your workflow and click “**Workflow Configuration**“ tab to set a configuration.
4. Click “**+ Create Task**“ to add new task into your workflow.
5. Fill in the task information as per below details.

   - **Task name**: The task name.
   - **Task description**: The task description.
   - **Service Parameters**: The location of JSON editor that user can setup or create a code for the task.
   - **Retry Policy**: Jobs that fail are retried a number of times based on the following policy. You can specify a maximum number of attempts for a run and a minimal interval between attempts.
   - **Timeout Duration**: Maximum completion time for this task. If the task runs for longer than this threshold we'll stop the task run.
   - **Process**: To set a task to run after this task and status condition(Failed or Success).
6. Click “**Save**“.
7. If you want to create another task, you can repeat the step 3 to 5.
8. Set the second task to proceed, after the previous task is done by setting the **Process** part on step 4. The diagram will display condition colors as per setting. Green means **Success** condition and Red means **Fail** condition.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf14.png?api=v2)![WF with color condition.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/WF%20with%20color%20condition.png?api=v2)

9. Set a schedule for your workflow. (Default is enable schedule.)

   - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule to run as per setting.
   - **One Time**: To run this workflow one time.

![set schedule.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/set%20schedule.png?api=v2)

10. Click “**Deploy Now**“ to deploy your workflow and can setup the workflow to run after deploy steps is successfully.

**Example dialogs**: Deploying a Deactivated workflow (left) vs. an Active workflow (right).

![image-20250929-043521.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250929-043521.png?api=v2)

![image-20250929-043543.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250929-043543.png?api=v2)

11. Click “**Deploy**“ or “**Stop & Deploy**“ and you can check your workflow status on Workflow Management page.

## Create a new Workflow with Advance Mode

For advance users, who have a Python skill and able to create the Python script to manage the workflow. Users can select the Advanced option and upload the Python script instead of creating a new task, as in the previous steps.

1. Click “**Create Workflow**“ and select “**+ Create Workflow**“.
2. Fill in the workflow name and description.
3. Select your workflow and click “**Workflow Configuration**“ tab to set a configuration.
4. Click:3dots:icon and select “**Advanced Mode**“ ![wfa2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wfa2.png?api=v2).
5. Download the default Python script for the code guideline by clicking “**Download Template**“ button:ptt:.
6. Upload the your script clicking “**Upload Config**“. ![wfa5.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wfa5.png?api=v2)
7. Click “**Open File Browser**“ and choose your Python file. After that, click “**Upload**“.

![wfa4.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wfa4.png?api=v2)

8. Your Python script will display on the Advance Config panel.

![wfa8.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wfa8.png?api=v2)

9. Click “**Save**“.
10. Set the second task to proceed, after the previous task is done by setting the **Process** part on step 4. The diagram will display condition colors as per setting. Green means **Success** condition and Red means **Fail** condition.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf14.png?api=v2)![WF with color condition.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/WF%20with%20color%20condition.png?api=v2)

11. Set a schedule for your workflow. (Default is enable schedule.)

    - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule to run as per setting.
    - **One Time**: To run this workflow one time.

![set schedule.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/set%20schedule.png?api=v2)

12. Click “**Deploy Now**“ to deploy your workflow and can setup the workflow to run after deploy steps is successfully.

**Example dialogs**: Deploying a Deactivated workflow (left) vs. an Active workflow (right).

![image-20250929-043521.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250929-043521.png?api=v2)

![image-20250929-043543.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250929-043543.png?api=v2)

13. Click “**Deploy**“ or “**Stop & Deploy**“ and you can check your workflow status on Workflow Management page.

## Import the Workflow

1. Click “**Create Workflow**“ and select “**Import Workflow**“.
2. Click “**Open File Browser**“ or drag and drop the JSON file.
3. Click “**Next**“ for the next step. Then, click “**Done**“ and go back to the Workflow list.

If the workflow name in the imported file is the same as an existing name in Blendata, the platform will consider this workflow as the old one and will use the same workflow.

## Notification setting

There are two ways of notification setting including In-App Setting and Email Setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf16.png?api=v2)

- **In-App Setting**

1. Select your workflow and click “**In-App setting**“
2. Setup a workflow status notifications for In-App roles and users.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf17.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Save**“.

- **E-mail Setting**

1. Select your workflow and click “**E-mail setting**“
2. Fill in the email and select the workflow status for notification. The user can add more than one email for the status notification by clicking “**+ Add**“.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf18.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Save**“.

## Performance overview

Summarize the historical tasks status and the performance in the simple visualization.

![image-20251026-123310.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251026-123310.png?api=v2)

1. Select your workflow and go to **Overview** tab.
2. All performance status will display as a bar chart with the status information.
3. Users can click each of bar chart to see the summary.

See more details, go to [Workflow Detail Page](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2122809345/Workflow+Management#Workflow-Detail-Page) topic.

## Rerun Historical Workflow

This function allows the user to rerun your workflow or a task under your workflow.

1. Select your workflow and go to **Overview** tab.
2. Click the workflow bar or the task box on a chart, after that the **Rerun** button will appear.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf21.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Rerun**“ button and select the rerun options.

   - **Rerun This Round**: Rerun this workflow.
   - **Rerun This Task**: Rerun only a selected task.
   - **Rerun This Task & Down Stream**: Rerun a selected task and the chain task.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf19.png?api=v2)

4. Your workflow or your task will be automatically rerun.

## Scheduling a time for workflow

The platform provides the schedule setting for users to run this workflow as a routine.

1. Select your workflow and go to **Workflow Configuration** tab.
2. Click “**Go to Workflow Editor**“ :wf:.
3. Click schedule box to setup a schedule.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf23.png?api=v2)

4. Select your schedule options.

   - **Cron Schedule**: Set the time using Cron expression.
   - **One time**: Specify a date and time to run your workflow.
5. Click “**Save**“.

---

# Workflow Detail Page

This section provides definitions and usage guidelines for all features and buttons available in the selected workflow page.

![image-20251031-063516.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251031-063516.png?api=v2)

The top of the page displays ![image-20251029-100953.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251029-100953.png?api=v2) Toggle, which is used to activate or deactivate the workflow.  
This section also shows the workflow name, workflow ID, [Actions](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2122809345/Workflow+Management#Workflow-Management-Actions) and includes tabs that provide detailed information about the workflow, see the details of each tab below for more details.

## Information

Shows the workflow’s basic information.

### Basic Information

- **ID**: Show the unique identifier automatically generated for the workflow.
- **Name**: Show the workflow name defined by the user.
- **Description**: Show the explanation or purpose of the workflow.
- **Tag**: Show the labels used to categorize and organize the workflow.
- **Run As**: Show the user account that executed the latest the workflow.
- **Created date**: Show the date and time when the workflow was initially created.
- **Owner**: Show the user who owns the workflow and has permission to manage it.
- **Modified date**: Show the date and time when the workflow was last updated.
- **Modified by**: Show the user who last modified the workflow (shows “–” if never modified).
- **Schedule**: Show the defined schedule for automatic execution (shows “–” if not scheduled).
- **Last fire time**: Show the most recent date and time the workflow was executed.
- **Next fire time**: Show the next scheduled date and time the workflow is set to run.

### Latest activity

- **Run ID**: Show the unique identifier of the workflow run with a timestamp template (e.g., scheduled\_\_2025-10-10T07:08:46+00:00).
- **Status**: Show the current status of the workflow (e.g., Active, Inactive).
- **Latest Result**: Show the final result of the latest run (e.g., Success, Failed).
- **Start time**: Show the date and time when the workflow started running.
- **End time**: Show the date and time when the workflow finished running.
- **Duration**: Show the total time taken for the workflow execution.

This panel displays only the latest activity of this workflow.

### Notification

- **In-App Notification**: Configure in-app alerts for specific roles and users to send notifications when the workflow is Deployed, encounters a Deployed error, or Fails.
- **Email Notification:** Configure email notification and specify which workflow statuses or results will trigger notifications.

## Overview

Shows a summary of the execution results for the workflow.

![image-20251026-123310.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251026-123310.png?api=v2)

- **Version**: Select the workflow deployment version to display results for. (Only the latest 20 versions are available in the dropdown list.)
- **Time range**: Select the period time of workflow.
- **Run Limits**: Select the number of workflow runs for the *Total Runs Displayed* field and to display in the chart.

You can view the run summary, either overall or for each workflow run, by clicking on the chart. It will display the corresponding run details.

![image-20251030-103230.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251030-103230.png?api=v2)

![image-20251030-103257.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251030-103257.png?api=v2)

### Workflow Runs Summary

- **Total Runs Displayed**: Shows the number of workflow runs based on the selected Run Limits field.
- **Total Success**: Shows the total number of runs that completed successfully.
- **First Run Start**: Shows the start time of the earliest run in the displayed summary.
- **Last Run Start**: Shows the start time of the most recent run in the displayed summary.
- **Max Run Duration**: Shows the longest duration among the displayed runs.
- **Mean Run Duration**: Shows the average duration of all displayed runs.
- **Min Run Duration**: Shows the shortest duration among the displayed runs.
- **Total Tasks**: Shows the total number of tasks in this workflow. (See tasks in the Workflow Configuration tab.)

### Each Workflow Runs Details

- **Status:** Shows the execution status of the workflow.
- **Run ID:** Shows the unique identifier of the workflow run with a timestamp template.
- **Run Type:** Shows the trigger type of the workflow run.

  - **Execute**: run immediately.
  - **Execute w/ Config**: run with custom parameters.
  - **Schedule**: run by a predefined schedule.
  - **Rerun**: when clicking rerun, the workflow will run again and show this run type.
- **Run Duration:** Shows the total time taken for the workflow execution.
- **Last Scheduling Decision**: Shows the last time a scheduler attempted to schedule task instances for this workflow run.
- **Queued At**: Shows the date and time when the workflow was queued before execution.
- **Started:** Shows the date and time when the workflow started running.

  **Ended:** Shows the date and time when the workflow finished running.
- **Data Interval Start:** Shows the start time of the data interval used for this run.
- **Data Interval End:** Shows the end time of the data interval used for this run.

If the workflow is scheduled to run only once, the Data Interval Start and Data Interval End will be the same.

- **Run Config:** Shows the parameters used for this run (only for Execute w/ Config type).

**Queued At**, **Data Interval Start**, and **Data Interval End** correspond to the cron schedule configuration.

For example, if the cron is set to run every 10 minutes, **Data Interval Start** will show the start date and time of the first execution, and **Data Interval End** will be **Data Interval Start** + 10 minutes, and the **Queued At** time is calculated based on this schedule.

## Workflow Configuration

Shows task flow and dependency relationships in the workflow and, users can edit workflow configuration on this tab.

### Workflow Editor

![image-20251031-043443.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251031-043443.png?api=v2)

- **Cron Expression**: Set the schedule time for a one-time or cron schedule.
- **Add to Version**: Save current changes as a new workflow version.
- **Version History**: View workflow versions and perform actions such as restoring, duplicating, or editing version information, or revert to a specific version by clicking its name.
- **Restore This Version**: Revert the currently displayed workflow version and configure it.
- **View Deployed Version**: Switch to the deployed version view of the currently displayed workflow.
- **Deploy Now**: Deploy the current workflow configuration immediately.
- **Go to Workflow Editor**: Click to open the restored workflow version for editing.
- **Kebab menu(⋮)**: Show additional workflow actions.

  - **Advanced Mode**: Open the workflow in advanced configuration mode.
  - **Duplicate Workflow**: Create a copy of currently displayed workflow, shown as a new item in the Workflow Management page.
- ![image-20251031-044433.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251031-044433.png?api=v2): Add a new task node to the currently displayed workflow version.

## History

Shows detailed history of all workflow runs.

![image-20251031-051240.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251031-051240.png?api=v2)

- **Version**: Select a specific workflow version or “**All**” to view its run history.
- **Time range**: Select a time period to filter workflow runs within the selected range.
- **Limit (Max: 1000)**: Set the maximum number of records to display in the history table.
- **Refresh Result**: Click to refresh results with the currently selected filters.
- **Matching Results**: Shows the total number of workflow runs that match the selected conditions.
- **Search**: Enter a keyword to find specific workflow.
- **Status**: Select a status to filter workflow runs (e.g., SUCCESS, FAILED, RUNNING, and QUEUED).
- **Actions**: Show additional workflow history actions.

  - **Rerun This Round**: Click to rerun the selected workflow run.
  - **Round History**: Click to pens the “**Round History**” dialog.

Click a Run ID to view the status of each task for each workflow, which opens the “**Round History**” dialog.

![image-20251031-085004.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20251031-085004.png?api=v2)

- **Action in Round History Dialog**: Show additional Round History actions.

  - **Rerun This Round**: Rerun this workflow.
  - **Rerun This Task**: Rerun only a selected task.
  - **Rerun This Task & Down Stream**: Rerun a selected task and the chain task.

---

# Workflow Management Actions

![image-20250929-050557.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250929-050557.png?api=v2)

![image-20250929-044708.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250929-044708.png?api=v2)

![image-20250929-044726.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250929-044726.png?api=v2)

## Activate Workflow

To activate a selected workflow.

1. Select a workflow and click :3dots: icon.
2. Click “**Activate Workflow**” to change the workflow status to Active.

![image-20250929-045457.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250929-045457.png?api=v2)

## Deactivate Workflow

To deactivate a selected workflow.

1. Select a workflow and click :3dots: icon.
2. Select “**Deactivate Workflow**” option and the platform will show popup to reconfirm the deactivate option.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf13.png?api=v2)

3. Tick “**Kill all running task**“ option and click “**Deactivate**“.
4. Then, the workflow status will show “**Inactive**“.

## Execute Immediately

To immediately execute a selected workflow.

1. Select a workflow and click :3dots: icon.
2. Select “**Execute Immediately**” option and click “**Execute**“.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf24.png?api=v2)

3. The platform will execute a latest deployed version of workflow.

## Execute w/Config

To execute a selected workflow with a config.

1. Select a workflow and click :3dots: icon.
2. Select “**Edit Workflow**” option and setup a configuration of the workflow.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf25.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Run with Config**“. Then, the platform will execute the workflow with the latest config setting.

## Deploy Now

To deploy workflows immediately, you can multi-select them through Workflow Management page.

![image-20250929-050727.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250929-050727.png?api=v2)

1. The dialog displays the deployment conditions and the selected workflows. Please review the details before continuing with the deployment.
2. Then, select the workflow options:

   - **Activate after successfully deployed** (optional): The workflow will be active automatically after successfully deployed.
   - **Kill all running task**\*: The system will force kill all running tasks before proceeding.
3. Then, the workflow will be executed and deployed.

## Edit Workflow

To edit the workflow details such as workflow name, description and tag.

1. Select a workflow and click :3dots: icon.
2. Select “**Edit Workflow**” option and the popup will appear.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf12.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Save**“.

## Permission Manage

To manage a permission of the selected workflow.

1. Select a workflow and click :3dots: icon.
2. Select “**Permission Workflow**” option and the popup will appear.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/wf11.png?api=v2)

3. Manage the operational permission for each roles or by users.
4. Click “**Save**“.

## Export Workflow

To export the selected workflow as JSON file into your device.

1. Select a workflow and click:3dots:icon.
2. Select “**Export Workflow**“ option and choose the Workflow version to export (only choose one version)
3. Enter a suffix name (up to 19 characters). It will be added after the workflow name.
4. Click “**Continue**“ button and click “**Export**“ to export the selected workflow version.
5. Your JSON file will be saved into your device.

![image-20250927-013653.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809345/image-20250927-013653.png?api=v2)

## Duplicate Workflow

To duplicate the selected workflow.

1. Select a workflow and click:3dots:icon.
2. Select “**Duplicate Workflow**” option to duplicate the selected workflow and create as a new workflow with the same setting.

## Delete Workflow

To delete the selected workflow.

1. Select a workflow and click :3dots: icon.
2. Select “**Delete Workflow**” option to delete the selected workflow.
