## All Source Type

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2122809504/All+Source+Type |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:47:10.057Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Workflow Management |

# Refresh Table

Set the workflow with Refresh Table service type, which is to update your data table.

```
{
  "tableNames": [
    "table_test_1",
    "table_test_2"
  ],
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **tableNames**: Fill in your data tables name. You can put one table name or a set of tables in this configuration.

---

# Table Update

Set the workflow with Table Update service type, which is to update your data table.

```
{
  "tableNames": [
    "table_test_1",
    "table_test_2"
  ],
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **tableNames**: Fill in your data tables name. You can put one table name or a set of tables in this configuration.

---

# Export

Users can choose the Export service type in the Workflow with many types of output formats that users can configure as per below parameter definitions:

```
{
  "exportType": "filepath",
  "action": "EXPORT",
  "filename": "table_test",
  "sql": "select * from table_test",
  "isConvertSchema": false,
  "sinkMode": "append",
  "sinkFormat": "csv",
  "isInternal": true,
  "sinkOptions": {
    "header": true,
    "delimiter": ",",
    "quote": "\"",
    "escape": "\"",
    "zipFile": true
  },
  "partitions": [],
  "numPartitions": 1,
  "start": "01/01/2020 00:00:00",
  "end": "01/02/2020 23:59:59",
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **exportType**: This is an export type such as file path or JDBC.
- **filename**: Fill in the file name.
- **sql**: The SQL command for this table export.
- **sinkMode**: This is fixed values of export mode, which is append.
- **sinkFormat**: Fill in your export file such as CSV, Excel, JSON, Parquet, and text file.
- **isInternal**: Using Spark to export the output file. (The default is true.)
- **sinkOptions**:

  - CSV format: Fill in the header, delimiter, quote, escape, and zipFile.
  - JSON format: Fill in encoding type and zipFile.
  - Excel format: Fill in the header condition(true or false) and zipFile.
- **partitions**: This part is for the partition setting, users can set the number of file, start date and end date from time range.

---

# Aggregate Table

To set the workflow with the Aggregate table service.

```
{
  "aggregationConfig": {
    "dataType": "non-time series",
    "sinkOption": {
      "mode": "Append",
      "partitions": [],
      "format": "parquet",
      "interval": 0,
      "numPartitions": 1,
      "options": {
        "isEncryptData": "false"
      }
    },
    "tableName": "table_test",
    "sql": "SELECT * FROM table_test where product = 'Product1'",
    "start": "-7d",
    "isRoundTimeStart": false,
    "end": "now",
    "isRoundTimeEnd": false
  },
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **dataType**: Select between non-time series and time series options.
- **sinkOption**: to set about the partition

  - **mode**: There are two mode including overwrite and append options.
  - **format**: Select the format based on the destination data asset, which are parquet or delta.
  - **numPartitions**: Fill in the number of partition file.
  - **isEncryptData**: Set this data to apply the encryption or not.
- **tableName**: Fill in your data tables name.
- **sql**: Fill in your SQL command.
- **start**: Fill in the number of days from the time series that users need to do the extract. (based on the source data asset)
- **isRoundTimeStart**: This option is use to round up the start time to be midnight, if the user choose true value. Otherwise, it will use the actual time.(There are true or false.)
- **end**: Fill in the end period of this data. (based on the source data asset)
- **isRoundTimeEnd**: This option is use to round up the end time to be midnight, if the user choose true value. Otherwise, it will use the actual time.(There are true or false.)

**Append mode** needs to use the same partition with the source data. User can fill in the non-time series partition values with the set values such as ["column\_partition","column\_partition\_2"].

**Overwrite mode**, user can setup the partition as per destination data asset.

---

# Alert Scheduler

There are four types of alert scheduler including email, notification button in the Blendata platform, service log, and script. Users can configure for all types or some of them.

```
{
  "sql": "select * from table",
  "limit": "100",
  "start": "-1d",
  "end": "now",
  "conditionCause": "gt",
  "conditionValue": "1",
  "isRoundTimeStart": "false",
  "isRoundTimeEnd": true,
  "outboundServicesParameters": [
    {
      "publishTypes": "e-mail-alert",
      "publishParams": {
        "subject": "",
        "isInLineTable": false,
        "publishType": "e-mail-alert",
        "to": "",
        "from": "admin@blendata.co",
        "bcc": "",
        "message": "",
        "cc": "",
        "params": [
          {
            "publishType": "e-mail-alert",
            "publishParams": {
              "target": {
                "to": "",
                "cc": "",
                "bcc": "",
                "isAttach": false,
                "isInLineTable": false
              },
              "from": "admin@blendata.co"
            }
          }
        ],
        "isAttach": false,
        "sendTime": "2019-05-11 10:10:00"
      }
    },
    {
      "publishTypes": "web-socket-alert",
      "publishParams": {
        "titleName": "Alert-Web-Socket",
        "message": "123",
        "severity": "low",
        "username": [
          "wisit",
          "super_admin"
        ],
        "role": []
      }
    },
    {
      "publishTypes": "implement-outbound",
      "isSendData": true,
      "publishParams": {
        "params": [
          {
            "publishType": "notisyslog",
            "publishParams": {
              "target": {
                "IP": "172.20.51.227",
                "PORT": "514",
                "HOST": "gt247"
              }
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "publishTypes": "run-script",
      "idRunScript": 2
    }
  ],
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **sql**: Fill in your SQL command that use for condition checking.
- **limit**: Fill in the limit number of result record.
- **start**: Fill in the start period.
- **end**: Fill in the end period of this data.
- **conditionCause**: Fill in the operator likes greater than, less than, or equal to, and etc.
- **conditionValue**: Fill in the number of result. (number of result record)
- **isRoundTimeStart**: This option is use to round up the start time to be midnight, if the user choose true value. Otherwise, it will use the actual time.(There are true or false.)
- **isRoundTimeEnd**: This option is use to round up the end time to be midnight, if the user choose true value. Otherwise, it will use the actual time.
- **outboundServicesParameters**: Select and fill in the parameter values of all alert types.

  - **email**: Fill in the email who is directly receiver or in cc., indicate that there is an attachment or not, and the send time of this email alert.
  - **web-socket-alert**: Fill in the title name, alert message, severity level, username, and role value.
  - **implement-outbound**: Fill in the publish type, host name, and address.
  - **run-script**: Fill in your script ID.

---

# SQL Execution

This service is the SQL execution that you can setup the service to run the SQL command in the Workflow task.

```
{
  "sql": "select * from google_data_story_th_en_01",
  "start": "12/09/2023 00:00:00",
  "end": "12/09/2023 23:59:59",
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **sql**: Fill in your SQL command.
- **start**: Fill in the start date from the time range to scope down the data asset for the data processing.
- **end**: Fill in the end date from the time range to scope down the data asset for the data processing.

---

# Import Table

This service used to create a new data table with schedule into our platform and import the data into an existing table by using Workflow configuration.

```
{
  "datasourceProfile": {
    "name": "test_import",
    "sourceType": "spark-table-internal",
    "dataType": "non-time series",
    "dataDict": null,
    "dataSourceOptions": {
      "time-column": null,
      "partitions": "",
      "sparkOptions": {
        "sourceOptions": {
          "path": "file_path.csv",
          "charset": "UTF-8",
          "delimiter": ",",
          "quote": "\"",
          "escape": "\"",
          "inferSchema": false,
          "header": true,
          "multiLine": false
        },
        "sourceConfig": null,
        "isConvertSchema": true,
        "importType": "filepath",
        "sourceFormat": "csv",
        "sourceDataType": "csv",
        "isInternalProcess": false,
        "isAddDefaultFields": true,
        "schema": [
          {
            "originName": "transaction",
            "originDataType": "string",
            "customName": "transaction",
            "customDataType": "string",
            "dataFormat": null,
            "buddhistEra": false
          },
          {
            "originName": "que",
            "originDataType": "string",
            "customName": "que",
            "customDataType": "string",
            "dataFormat": null,
            "buddhistEra": false
          },
          {
            "originName": "_order",
            "originDataType": "string",
            "customName": "_order",
            "customDataType": "string",
            "dataFormat": null,
            "buddhistEra": false
          },
          {
            "originName": "test",
            "originDataType": "string",
            "customName": "test",
            "customDataType": "string",
            "dataFormat": null,
            "buddhistEra": false
          },
          {
            "originName": "product",
            "originDataType": "string",
            "customName": "product",
            "customDataType": "string",
            "dataFormat": null,
            "buddhistEra": false
          },
          {
            "originName": "payment",
            "originDataType": "string",
            "customName": "payment",
            "customDataType": "string",
            "dataFormat": null,
            "buddhistEra": false
          },
          {
            "originName": "gtp_timestamp",
            "originDataType": "timestamp",
            "customName": "gtp_timestamp",
            "customDataType": "timestamp",
            "dataFormat": null,
            "buddhistEra": false
          },
          {
            "originName": "input_source_name",
            "originDataType": "string",
            "customName": "input_source_name",
            "customDataType": "string",
            "dataFormat": null,
            "buddhistEra": false
          }
        ],
        "dataSourceMode": "internal",
        "uploadFile": true,
        "isStream": false,
        "sinkOptions": {
          "path": "",
          "isEncryptData": false
        },
        "sinkFormat": "parquet",
        "numPartitions": null,
        "partitions": [],
        "keyColumns": [],
        "sinkMode": null
      },
      "isState": false
    },
    "tags": [],
    "description": ""
  },
  "realmId": 2,
  "poolName": null,
  "isUpdateOption": true
}
```

- **name**: Fill in your table name.
- **sourceType**: Fill in the data type such as internal, external, or delta table.
- **dataType**: Choose your time-series type of your table.
- **dataDict**: Fill in your a schema detail and field definition of data asset.
- **dataSourceOptions**:

  - **time-column**: Fill in a field name that used for time-series.
  - **partitions**: This part is for the partition setting, users can set the number of file, start date and end date from time range.
  - **sparkOptions**: (as this example imports csv file, so for other file type may contain other parameters)

    - **path**: Fill in your file path for file path importing.
    - **charset**: Fill in you encoding type.
    - **delimiter**: Identify symbol of the delimiter of your source file.
    - **quote**: To indicate the symbol, which is the starting of the value.
    - **escape**: To indicate the symbol of value field ending location.
    - **inferSchema**: To refer the schema from source table. (If true, you don’t need to fill in schema parameter and put ‘**false**’ for isConvertSchema)
    - **header**: If your file consists of the header row, filling in true.
    - **multiLine**: To separate the values in the same line and create the new line.
  - **sourceConfig**: Fill in other source configurations that is not include in sourceOption such as Cassandra password.
  - **isConvertSchema**: Is it convert the schema of source data or not? If yes, put 'true'.
  - **importType**: Fill in your import type such as upload file.
  - **sourceFormat**: Fill in your data type such as CSV, excel, JSON, and etc.
  - **sourceDataType**: Fill in your data type such as CSV, excel, JSON, and etc.
  - isInternalProcess
  - **isAddDefaultFields**: To indicate that you need to add default field into your data table or not.
  - **schema**: Fill in the field name and field type information.
  - **dataSourceMode**: Fill in the data type such as Spark-table-external, Spark-table-internal, Delta,

    - **uploadFile**: To indiciate that the import method is upload file or not.
    - **isStream**: If it is Stream data type, please fill in ‘**true’**.
    - **sinkOptions**: Relate to export job. (can skip this parameter)

      - **path**: Export path. (can skip this parameter)
      - **isEncryptData**: Set this data to apply the encryption or not.
  - **sinkFormat**: Fill in import file such as parquet.
  - **numPartitions**: Fill in the number of partition file.
  - **partitions**: This part is for the partition setting, users can set the number of file, start date and end date from time range.
  - **keyColumns**: Fill in your key column for Delta asset.
  - **sinkMode**: null (This related to export job.)
- **isState**: True and False that set this table to be state table.
- **tags**: A tag for your table asset.
- **description**: A table description.

---

## Internal Source Type

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2122809519/Internal+Source+Type |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:47:35.452Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Workflow Management |

# Table Retention

This service used to do retention for table. User need to fill in the table name or the file location path. Also fill in the number of days to do this retention action.

```
{
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "nextNumberOfDays": 1,
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the table name.
- **nextNumberOfDays**: Set a number of days to remove the table.

---

# File Retention

This service used to do retention for data file. User need to fill in the table name or the file location path. Also fill in the number of days to do this retention action.

```
{
  "path": "/data/zeus/store/table_test/host=*/dt=*",
  "olderThan": 30,
  "isTrash": true,
  "excludePaths": [
    "/data/zeus/up*/export*"
  ],
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **path**: The file location.
- **olderThan**: Fill in the number of days condition to check the old file for the retention service.
- **excludePaths**: Fill in the excluded path. The files under this location won’t be removed.

---

# Add Partition

To add a new partition service in the Workflow. Users can set the number of days for next new partition.

```
{
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "nextNumberOfDays": 1,
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the table name.
- **nextNumberOfDays**: Set a number of days to create the new partition.

---

# Recovery Partition

To update the Partition data table as per below template.

```
{
  "tableNames": [
    "table_test_1",
    "table_test_2"
  ],
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the table name. Users can update only one partition table or multi-tables.

---

# Compact Data (Hot-Warm)

Compact your data from Hot state to Warm state, or from Warm state to Cold state You can see more details about the state tables, go to [State Tables](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

```
{
  "fromState": "hot",
  "toState": "warm",
  "value": "2",
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "options": {
    "compression": "snappy"
  },
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **fromState**: Fill in the current state.
- **toState**: Fill in the state name that you want to change.
- **value**: The number of days that will keep the data in the first state and will move the data to the second state. (The value unit is number of days.)
- **tableName**: Fill in the table name.
- **options**: Fill in the compression method including gzip or snappy.

---

## Internal and Delta Source Type

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2122809541/Internal+and+Delta+Source+Type |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:47:50.057Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Workflow Management |

# Data Compaction

The Workflow allows users to create a service task related to Delta management like data compaction. User can setup as per below:

```
{
  "deltaCompacts": [
    {
      "tableName": "fruit_test",
      "partitionConditionList": [
        {
          "name": "partitionFieldName",
          "value": "value",
          "comparator": "="
        }
      ],
      "zOrderColumns": [],
      "compressionFileNum": null
    }
  ],
  "poolName": null,
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in your Delta tables name.
- **partitionConditionList**: Setup the condition for a partition.

  - **name**: Fill in the partition field name.
  - **value**: Fill in the partition date name such as 20251104
  - **comparator**: Fill in the relational operators such as equal, grater than, or less than.

If the comparator are the grater than, or less than, the selected field has to be the number type such as integer, floating, double, and etc.

Currently, by default, the file will be compacted on the partition date of the previous day(current date -1). Thus, no need to fill in value in **partitionConditionList** parameter.

- **zOrderColumns**: Fill in the column names (multiple columns supported) to optimize query performance.
- **compressionFileNum**: Fill in the number of compaction files. If you use 'null', it means one file.

---

# Data Vacuum

Users also use the Data Vacuum in the Workflow task to manage your Delta table. User can do data vacuum for one table or multi-table at a time.

```
{
  "vacuumTables": [
    {
      "tableName": "name",
      "retainNum": null,
      "retainUnit": null
    },
    {
      "tableName": "fruit_test2",
      "retainNum": null,
      "retainUnit": null
    }
  ],
  "poolName": null,
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in your Delta tables name.
- **retainNum**: Fill in the number related to retainUnit parameter.
- **retainUnit**: Fill in your unit such as month, day, hour, minute, or second.

In case, you need to do Data Vacuum only one table. You can use below template.

```
{
  "vacuumTable": {
    "tableName": "name",
    "retainNum": null,
    "retainUnit": null
  },
  "poolName": null,
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

---

# Merge Data

This service is use to merge your data table, aiming to reduce the number of files.

```
{
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "realmId": 1,
  "numPartitions": 2,
  "options": {},
  "partitionConditions": [
    {
      "name": "partition_date",
      "value": "20230116",
      "comparator": "eq"
    }
  ]
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the name of the table where the job will write the output.
- **realmId**: Fill in the environment ID where the job will run, for example: `1` for `MASTER_REALM`, `2` for `STAGING_REALM`.
- **numPartitions**: Fill in how many output files the job should generate.

If **numPartitions** is unspecified, the system will automatically determine the optimal number of output files.

- **partitionConditionList**: Setup the condition for a partition.

  - **name**: Fill in the partition field name.
  - **value**: Fill in the partition date name such as 20230116.
  - **comparator**: Fill in the relational operators such as equal, grater than, or less than.

**Comparator:** Users can use either the comparator keyword or its symbol in parameters.

**Supported options include:**

- `lt` = (`<`) less than
- `gt` = (`>`) greater than
- `lte` = (`<=`) less than or equal
- `gte` = (`>=`) greater than or equal
- `eq` = (`=`) equal

---

## Notebook Type

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2122809556/Notebook+Type |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-26T10:52:52.937Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Workflow Management |

# Notebook

This feature helps users run a notebook service in the workflow. Users can set up configurations provided by the platform.

```
{
  "notebookName": "SharedWorkspace/notebook_name",
  "moduleNotebookId": 4,
  "workspaceName": "SharedWorkspace",
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

# The parameter definition

- **workspaceName**: Fill in your workspace name. (“workspaceName”: null will use the default workspace)
- **notebookName**: Fill in either the notebook name or the full path in the format `"workspace_name/notebook_name"`.
- **moduleNotebookId**: The notebook ID.

---

# Notebook Run Parallel

This feature helps users run notebooks in parallel with configurations. Users can set up configurations provided by the platform. Before using this service, the Notebook needs to be set in isolated mode first.

![image-20250828-113440.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809556/image-20250828-113440.png?api=v2)

The parameter in the configuration part supports both Spark and Python. For Spark, users can only use standard data types such as string, integer, float, list, and map, while Python supports all data types, including objects.

# The parameter definition

- **concurrentParallel**: The number of parallel Notebook.
- **language**: The code language that user can choose only Python or Scala.
- **timeSleep**: The delay time that is setup to start this Notebook Run parallel service. (The best practice should be 10 second.)
- **workspaceName**: Fill in your workspace name. (“workspaceName”: null will use the default workspace)
- **timeout**: Fill in the specific time for timeout. (Unit is second.)
- **notebookAddParameterRequest:** To setup the parameter under the parallel Notebook.

  - **notebookId:** Fill in the notebook ID.
  - **notebookName:** Fill in either only the notebook name or the full path in the format `"workspace_name/notebook_name"`.
  - **addParameterMapFromTitle**

    - **titleName:** Fill in a title name of the selected Notebook. If the user needs to replace the parameter of all titles under this Notebook, users can use “all” instead of titleName parameter.
    - **parameter:** Name of parameter.
    - **value:** Value of parameter.
- **(Optional)** You can configure Spark by adding the sparkConf parameters as shown below.

  - **spark.driver.memory**: Fill in a number of driver memory.
  - **spark.executor.memory** : Fill in a number of executor memory.
  - **spark.cores.max** : Fill in a maximum number of CPU that executor can use.

![image-20250829-070728.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809556/image-20250829-070728.png?api=v2)

The example of service parameter:

```
{
  "concurrentParallel": 2,
  "language": "python",
  "timeSleep": 10,
  "moduleNotebookId": 1,
  "workspaceName": "SharedWorkspace",
  "notebookAddParameterRequest": [
    {
      "notebookId": "ZM9CSU9S5",
      "addParameterMapFromTitle": {}
    },
    {
      "notebookName": "SharedWorkspace/notebook_name",
      "addParameterMapFromTitle": {}
    },
    {
      "notebookName": "notebook_name",
      "addParameterMapFromTitle": {}
    }
  ],
  "isAsync": true,
  "zeusId": 3,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

The example of service parameter with sparkConf:

```
{
  "concurrentParallel": 2,
  "language": "python",
  "timeSleep": 10,
  "moduleNotebookId": 1,
  "workspaceName": null,
  "notebookAddParameterRequest": [
    {
      "notebookId": "2AQX36DXF",
      "addParameterMapFromTitle": {
        "titleName": {
          "parameter1": "value1",
          "parameter2": "value2"
        }
      },
      "sparkConf": {
        "spark.driver.memory": "1g",
        "spark.executor.memory": "1g",
        "spark.cores.max": 1
      }
    }
  ],
  "isAsync": true,
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

---

![notebook run para.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809556/notebook%20run%20para.png?api=v2)

There are some notes for this service as below:

- ConcurrentParallel should relate to the number of interpreters on the Zeppelin(The core spec). If the user leaves this parameter blank, this task will run all Notebooks in parallel.
- Language supports only Python(pyspark) and Scala(spark).
- If the user does not fill in a value in Timeout parameter, the task will use the default workflow timeout.
- The Zeppelin has to set as isolated mode before using the Notebook Run Parallel service in the Workflow Management.
- For log fie, there are three status as following:

  - **SUCCESS**: When the notebook finishes, the log will display this status.
  - **TIMEOUT**:If the time runs out and some notebooks have not finished yet, this status will be displayed in the log file.
  - **NOT RUNNIN**: If the timeout occurs but the Notebook hasn't started running, it will display that the notebook is ‘Not Running’.

The example of service parameter:

```
{
  "concurrentParallel": 2,
  "language": "python",
  "timeSleep": 10,
  "notebookAddParameterRequest": [
    {
      "notebookId": "ID1",
      "addParameterMapFromTitle": {
        "titleName": {
          "parameter1": "value1",
          "parameter2": "value2"
        },
        "all" {
          "parameter1": "value1",
          "parameter2": "value2"
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "notebookId": "ID2",
      "addParameterMapFromTitle": {
        "titleName": {
          "parameter1": "value1",
          "parameter2": "value2"
        }
      }
    } 
  ],
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

**Note:**

If the Title and All use the same parameters, the platform will use a parameter value from Title parameter.

---

## Other Services

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2122809583/Other+Services |
| **Last modified** | 2025-06-06T11:10:29.543Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Workflow Management |

# Custom Service

This is the custom service type, which user can setup a configuration to apply with a Zeus or Hera module. The platform provides some service templates to use and adjust the parameter or value. Moreover, users can create your own service parameter in the code panel as well.

![WF custom service.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2122809583/WF%20custom%20service.png?api=v2)

---

# Call API

The Workflow allows user to use API service. Users need to fill in the API information as per below template.

```
{
  "authorizationBody": {},
  "normalBody": {},
  "destinationUrl": "{{url}}/sites/{{site-id}}/connected-applications/",
  "authorizationUrl": "{{url}}/auth/signin",
  "method": "POST",
  "header": {},
  "auth_header": "X-Tableau-Auth",
  "auth_response_attribute": "token",
  "isAuth": true,
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **authorizationBody**: Fill in the username and password information that uses to login and get the credential.
- **normalBody**: Fill in a necessary information that is used for api information.
- **destinationUrl**: The url address of destination api, which is the process api url.
- **authorizationUrl**: Fill in your url of the login api, where stores the credential for login.
- **method**: Set your http method such as GET, POST, PUT, or DELETE.
- **header**: Fill in your http header, which is used to call in the destination url.
- **auth\_header**: Fill in a key of header authorization from authorization url response after finishing authenticating.
- **auth\_response\_attribute**: Fill in the parameter that store the response from authorization url. This value is used in auth header.

In the case of more than one level object, each level will be split by a dot such as credential.token. This case, we will get the value inside attribute of token and token is inside credential object.

- **isAuth**: To indicate that this destination url use authorization or not.

---

# Run Workflow

This service is aim to setup the current workflow to run after other workflows are complete.

```
{
  "workflowId": "",
  "delayTime": null,
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **workflowID**: To indicate the Workflow ID.
- **delayTime**: To set the time that wait an another workflow to finish and then start this workflow. (Time unit is second.)

The Workflow in the configuration needs to be deployed before being used in this service.

---

## My Profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2198863965/My+Profile |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-10T10:43:21.918Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 |

## Export Files

This page is the location of your export file list, which is similar to the landing page. You can see your history export activities, Status export (e.g., Processing (0%), SUCCESS, FAILED) on this page, and you can download these files within 30 days.

![my profile.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2198863965/my%20profile.png?api=v2)

## Published Dashboard

The location that stores your publish dashboard. Users can manage the publish dashboards with an unpublished option and get a link for dashboard sharing.

![publish dashboard.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2198863965/publish%20dashboard.png?api=v2)

## Add Access Token Setting

In this version, we have added a personal token settings page for each user, allowing you to create your own token settings, such as expiration time (lifetime), active or inactive status, multiple personal tokens, and the ability to delete your access token.

![access token.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2198863965/access%20token.png?api=v2)

---

## Service Monitor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2199945282/Service+Monitor |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-27T01:33:54.336Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 |

This new menu is used to monitor all services from Service Management menu in the Blendata platform, a user is able to filter a period with minutes or hours to see the job running overview in the chart and job number and see the service job name that is running currently or in the queue. Moreover, users can see the service lists of each latest result status like Error, Incomplete, and Skipped to see the log or job details.

![service monitor.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2199945282/service%20monitor.png?api=v2)

To describe the details on the Service Monitor page, please refer to the following information:

- **Service Type:** Select the service to monitor.
- **Only my services:** Click to freeze the filter and display only your services, This filter will be applied to the Service Type picklist. if you don’t just, click again to unfreeze and show all services.
- ![image-20251027-010417.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2199945282/image-20251027-010417.png?api=v2) **Refresh button:** Click to refresh and display the most up-to-date information.
- **1D / 7D:** Select the time range (1 day or 7 days) to monitor service activities, All options include data up to the current time.
- ![image-20251027-010731.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2199945282/image-20251027-010731.png?api=v2) **Time Range:** Select the time period to display data.
- 🟨 **Warning (Line)**:Represents unusual job behavior. Click ![image-20251027-011637.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2199945282/image-20251027-011637.png?api=v2) to view jobs with unusual behavior.
- 🟥 **Critical**: Represents Error Job, Incomplete, or Skipped Job. Click ![image-20251027-011540.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2199945282/image-20251027-011540.png?api=v2) to view jobs with unusual behavior.
- 🟩 **Success**: Represents Success Job — all jobs ran and completed successfully.
- **Overview**: Displays the summary of all job execution results within the selected time range.

  - **Total Job Run**: Displays the total number of jobs executed within the selected time range, including all statuses (Success, Error, Incomplete, and Skipped).
  - **Success Job**: Shows the number and percentage of jobs that failed during execution due to system
  - **Error Job**: Shows the number and percentage of jobs that failed during execution due to system.
  - **Incomplete**: Represents jobs that started but did not complete properly, these jobs require investigation to ensure proper completion in the next run.
  - **Skipped Job**: Indicates jobs that were intentionally skipped or not executed due to scheduling rules, dependency conditions.
- **Job Run Time**: Shows job duration statistics (Min, Average, Max) to monitor performance and detect unusual jobs.

  - **Min**: Displays the minimum job execution time recorded within the selected time range.
  - **Average**: Shows the average job execution time of all jobs.
  - **Max**: Displays the maximum job execution time recorded within the selected time range.
  - **Unusual**: Represents the number of jobs with abnormal run times — significantly longer compared to their normal duration.
- **Running Job**: View the jobs that are currently running in real time.
- **Queued Job**: View the jobs that are waiting to run next.

The platform provides categories to view jobs by **Service** and **Service Chain**, allowing users to easily switch between individual service jobs and chained service jobs.

---

To enable this feature, you need to follow the steps below:

1. Go to **Setting** and select **User Management** menu.
2. Select Role or Users, who need to see this menu.

   - Role

     - Click :3dots: icon and select “**Edit Role**” option.
     - Enable “**Service Monitor**“ menu.
     - Save a change.
   - User

     - Click :3dots: icon and select “**Edit User Access Rights**“ option.
     - Enable “**Service Monitor**“ menu.
     - Save a change.
3. Re-login the user account again.

Services exclude Logstash and Stream type.

---

## Transfer Asset

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201255951/Transfer+Asset |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-17T04:36:38.853Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Admin Setting Menus > User Management |

The platform supports transferring asset ownership (datasets, services, service chains, workflows) for all user types, including users created **directly on the platform** and **SSO** or **LDAP**.

1. Go to Setting menu and and select “**User Management**“.
2. Select a user and click :3dots: icon to choose “**Transfer Asset**“ option.

![image-20250116-083050.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201255951/image-20250116-083050.png?api=v2)

3. Choose your transfer options.

- **Absolute Ownership Transfer**: Transfers all asset including data assets, services, service chains, and workflow assets.

  - Review all asset numbers and click next.
- **Partial Asset Transfer**: Transfer some assets from this user. (Assets are listed from all Realms.)

  - Click “**Select Data Assets**” button, select the assets, and confirm you selected assets.

![image-20250116-085526.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201255951/image-20250116-085526.png?api=v2)![transfer partial.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201255951/transfer%20partial.png?api=v2)![confirm partial asset.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201255951/confirm%20partial%20asset.png?api=v2)

4. Review your selected assets and click “**Next“**.
5. Click “**Select New Owner**“ button and choose a new owner from the user list.

![image-20250116-084634.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201255951/image-20250116-084634.png?api=v2)

5. Review the new owner email and confirm the asset transformation.
6. Wait for the platform to transfer your new asset and popup will show as per below.

![image-20250116-085148.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201255951/image-20250116-085148.png?api=v2)

If the assets are stored in another Realm, which a new owner cannot access, the new user has to request Realm access from the super administrator.

Once the asset transfer process is done, the old owner of the assets does not have access to the asset.

---

## Data Policy & Service Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321473/Data+Policy+Service+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-23T13:06:11.308Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 |

# Services

In Blendata, services can be created in two ways:

1. **Creating a service from data assets**  
   You can create a new service by creating a new table, importing a dataset, or creating an aggregate table, the service will depend on the table type, such as delta table, or state table.

![image-20251123-091011.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321473/image-20251123-091011.png?api=v2)

2. **Creating a Service from the Service Management Menu**

The service assists in the automatic management of services within our Blendata Enterprise platform. With this functionality, services can be created if a data asset is owned by the user or if the user has access to the data asset. Users can perform actions such as opening/closing services, editing configurations, or creating new services. See more details, go to [**Service**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321510/Services)page.

![image-20251123-091359.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321473/image-20251123-091359.png?api=v2)

In the Service Management menu, you will find functions for creating services, actions for managing the selected service, and descriptions of these actions as listed below. (See more details on [Services](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321510/Services) page)

- **Services tab**: displays all services in the Blendata.
- **Service Chains tab**: displays chained services that consist of multiple connected services, you can also create a new service chain here. For more details, see [Service Chain Job](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/cII1gw).
- **Only my services**: shows only the services created or owned by the current user.
- **Search**: search for services by name, owner, or other keywords.
- **Create New**: provides two options including **Create Service** and **Import Service.**

The Stream and Data Transformation (Logstash) service cannot use the import service option.

- **Service Management Actions**: provides several operations for managing the selected services, each action is described below.

  - **Start/Stop Scheduled Job**: the service will follow its configured schedule and run automatically based on the defined timing.
  - **Service History**: review the selected service history.
  - **Execute Immediate**: runs the selected service immediately, regardless of its schedule.
  - **Manage Zeus on Selected**: select the Zeus group configuration for the selected services.
  - **Export Selected**: exports the configuration of the selected service(s) into a JSON file.
  - **Delete Selected**: deletes the selected service(s) from the system.
  - **Permission Manage**: allows the service owner to grant user and role permissions for the selected service. for more details, see the [Services Permission](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/HwCfjg) documentation.

If you are not the owner, only “**View Service**” will be available, allowing you to view the service’s parameter details.

---

# State Table

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321473/s3.png?api=v2)

To effectively manage our storage for streaming data or batch-imported data, we organize the data into three state tables using **Data Compaction** and set the time for **Data Retention**. This applies specifically to data with time series characteristics, utilizing the time field from the imported data settings. See more details, go to [**State Table**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322033/State+Table)page.

---

# Schedule Job

The platform enables users to schedule service jobs. When creating a new service, users can set the schedule time under **Job Triggering**. See more details, go to [**Schedule Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322074/Schedule+Job) page.

---

# Chain Job

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321473/s7.png?api=v2)

The Chain Job is used to create a service program that manages services sequentially from the service management. Users need to set the service template or create a new service using SQL queries. See more details, go to [**Chain Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322096/Chain+Job) page.

---

# System Jobs

The System Job is used to monitor job processing, start/stop job operations, and can be configured to manage system jobs for a table asset. See more details, go to [**System Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322153/System+Jobs) page.

---

## Services

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321510/Services |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:51:43.667Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management |

[data-colorid=ubdv6d91qc]{color:#403294} html[data-color-mode=dark] [data-colorid=ubdv6d91qc]{color:#796bcd}[data-colorid=i8hf0oux2y]{color:#403294} html[data-color-mode=dark] [data-colorid=i8hf0oux2y]{color:#796bcd}[data-colorid=i0uqacptpr]{color:#403294} html[data-color-mode=dark] [data-colorid=i0uqacptpr]{color:#796bcd}

# **Data Preparation**

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321510/s1.png?api=v2)

- **Data Transformation (Logstash):** To manage, collect, and transform Logstash data and all events associated with activities, including inputs (importing data), filters (changing or converting raw data to the desired output), and outputs (exporting data into different types).  
  See more detail on [Data Transformation (logstash)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321569/Data+Transformation+Logstash) page.

# **Data Management**

- [**Stream Service**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321601/Stream+Service)**:** Service relates to import the stream data with near-real time into our platform and store in our storage.
- [**Recovery Partition Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321628/Recovery+Partition+Job)**:** The partition update service assists our platform in recognizing newly imported partitions.   
  **Note:** If a user imports data using the Internal Table option and enables the partition option, the platform will automatically create the Recovery Partition Job.
- [**Add Partition Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321650/Add+Partition+Job)**:** To pre-create Metadata and Partition folders, ensuring that when the user imports data into the platform, it is directly stored in the designated folders and ready for use.
- [**Refresh Table Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321672/Refresh+Table+Job)**:** To automatically update new data in the data asset table.
- [**Compact Data (Hot-Warm) Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321694/Compact+Data+Hot-Warm+Job)**:** Service related to efficient data management in our storage using the data compaction for time-series data assets and enabling the partition option with a timestamp. There are three state including Hot, Warm, and Cold.
- [**Table Retention Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321725/Table+Retention+Job)**:** To delete a data record from the table based on the setting period.
- [**File Retention Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321725/Table+Retention+Job)**:** To delete a data file on the server by verifying the file's modified date in a specified path.
- [**Merge Data Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321752/Merge+Data+Job)**:** A service designed to merge table data files in storage, aiming to reduce the number of files, especially in scenarios where the data table contains numerous files, enhancing query processing performance.
- [**Table Update Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321774/Table+Update+Job)**:** This service facilitates the extraction of new data from the source and updates it into the table, exclusively for Internal tables.
- [**Export Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321811/Export+Job)**:** Used to track the data export status**.**
- [**Job Delta Compaction**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321833/Delta+Compaction+Job): Used to compact the data files(parquet file) to reduce the number of files.
- [**Job Vacuum**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321875/Vacuum+Job): Used to delete the old data files(parquet file) based on the specified period setting. The default is 7 days.
- [**System Job**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322153/System+Jobs)**:** To establish the default policy for Data Assets, reducing complexity in user-side service settings and facilitating efficient service management processes.

System Job can create on Import Dataset menu (only File path and Kafka).

# **Data Processing**

- [**Job Aggregate Table**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321922/Aggregate+Table+Job)**:** To create a service for Aggregation Table using Query statement.
- [**Alert Scheduler**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321949/Alert+Scheduler)**:** To setup an service notification.

# **Extension Service**

- [**Custom service**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322006/Custom+Service): Users can customize a service by leveraging existing custom service templates on Hera and Zeus. Additionally, they have the flexibility to modify template parameters and create their own templates on the Custom Service Management page.

In Blendata v4.6.12+, access to the “**Default\_schema**” is restricted for general users.  
Only “**Super Admin**” users can access and use tables in this schema.  
This restriction can be disabled if required. <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AgADqw>

---

# Service Creation

1. **Create Service**: create a new service using a template and the available service configuration options. (Learn more on each service-type page.)
2. **Import Service**: import an existing service configuration file (JSON) to quickly set up a predefined service in another environment or on a new device. (If the service name already exists in the menu, the service won’t be imported.)

   - Click “**Create Service**” and select “**Import Service**” option.
   - Select the service configuration. (JSON file)
   - Click “**Import**” button to import your service.

The Stream and Data Transformation (Logstash) service cannot use the import service option.

![image-20251123-093844.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321510/image-20251123-093844.png?api=v2)

# Service Management

The platform has provided several operations for managing the selected services, each action is described below.

1. **Start Scheduled Job**: the service will follow its configured schedule and run automatically based on the defined timing.
2. **Stop Scheduled Job**: use this when you want to pause the scheduled execution but keep the service configuration unchanged.
3. **Service History**: review the selected service history.
4. **Execute Immediate**: runs the selected service immediately, regardless of its schedule.
5. **Manage Zeus on Selected**: select the Zeus group configuration for the selected services.

![image-20251123-125013.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321510/image-20251123-125013.png?api=v2)

5. **Export Selected**: exports the configuration of the selected service(s) into a JSON file. (If users select multiple services, the JSON file will export all service configurations into one file.)

   - Select one or multiple services.
   - Click “**Actions**” button :action: and choose “**Export Selected**” option.
   - The platform will export the JSON file into your download folder.

![image-20251123-125110.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321510/image-20251123-125110.png?api=v2)

![image-20251123-125120.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321510/image-20251123-125120.png?api=v2)

6. **Delete Selected**: deletes the selected service(s) from the system.
7. **Each service action (⋮)**: provides action options for each service. The available actions may vary depending on whether you are the service owner or a general user.

   - **Permission Manage**: allows the service owner to grant user and role permissions for the selected service. for more details, see the [Services Permission](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/HwCfjg) documentation.

**For service owner**

![image-20251123-095652.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321510/image-20251123-095652.png?api=v2)

As a service owner, you will see more available actions than a general user.

You can access additional options such as role management, user management, and advanced service actions.

**For a general user**

![image-20251123-095844.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321510/image-20251123-095844.png?api=v2)

As a general user, your available actions will be limited.

Some service actions and permission-management functions will not be accessible.

If you are not the owner, only “**View Service**” will be available, allowing you to view the service’s parameter details.

---
