## Stream Service

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321601/Stream+Service |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:52:01.084Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# How to create the Stream Service

Stream Service will be created automatically, when the user imports data with Stream Upload option (as per below screenshot). After import the data is done, the platform will create Stream Service on Service Management page.

1. During table import, if you select Stream Upload in the Upload Method step.

![image-20251022-074340.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321601/image-20251022-074340.png?api=v2)

2. In the Service step, the user can either use the default Stream upload service provided by the system or customize the Stream upload service as needed.

![image-20251022-082714.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321601/image-20251022-082714.png?api=v2)

![image-20251022-082735.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321601/image-20251022-082735.png?api=v2)

3. After creating the service associated with this import table, it will appear on the Stream Service Management page.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321601/show%20stream%20service.PNG?api=v2)

There are some important parameters for your reference as below:

- sourceOptions

  - `failOnDataLoss` : Enable this setting with True and False value to determine if the query should fail when there is a potential data loss.
  - `startingOffsets` : Indicate the offset starts such as earliest or latest.
- `sourceConfig`: A source configuration.
- `isConvertSchema`: Schema conversion is enabled, allowing the system to convert the data format or structure if needed.
- `importType`: The data import type.
- `sourceFormat`: The data source type likes Kafka or file types.
- `sourceDataType`: Specifies the data type of the source such as CSV, JSON, or text. (This parameter is for Streaming)

---

## Recovery Partition Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321628/Recovery+Partition+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:55:00.539Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# How to create Recovery Partition Job

**Step 1**: Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, and put the service tag.

- **File Type Service Name \***: Fill in the name for this service.
- **Job Triggering \***: Define the schedule to automatically run this service.

  - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
  - **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
  - **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.
- **Tags**: Type and press Enter to add a new tag for this service. Tags are useful for searching and organizing your own services. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20251020-100243.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321628/image-20251020-100243.png?api=v2)

**Step 2**:Fill in a config of service on Service Parameters box. The platform already provided the initial template for the user likes tableName and realmId.

```
{
  "tableNames": [
    "table_test_1",
    "table_test_2"
  ],
  "realmId": 1
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the table name in the format `"table_name"` or `"schema_name.table_name"`.  
  This service also supports multiple tables in a single execution.

---

## Add Partition Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321650/Add+Partition+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:55:14.122Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# How to create Add Partition Job

**Step 1**: Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, and put the service tag.

- **File Type Service Name \***: Fill in the name for this service.
- **Job Triggering \***: Define the schedule to automatically run this service.

  - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
  - **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
  - **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.
- **Tags**: Type and press Enter to add a new tag for this service. Tags are useful for searching and organizing your own services. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20251020-102710.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321650/image-20251020-102710.png?api=v2)

**Step 2**: Fill in a config of service on Service Parameters box. The platform already provided the initial template for the user likes tableName, realmId, and nextNumberofDays.

```
{
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "nextNumberOfDays": 1,
  "realmId": 1
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the table name in the format `"table_name"` or `"schema_name.table_name"`.
- **nextNumberOfDays**: Set a number of days to create the new partition.

---

## Refresh Table Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321672/Refresh+Table+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:55:26.398Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# How to create Refresh Table Job?

**Step 1**: Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, and put the service tag.

- **File Type Service Name \***: Fill in the name for this service.
- **ExistingTemplate List**: Select the template type for this service.

  - **List of Tables**: Fill in multiple tables for this service.
  - **Table**: Fill in only a single table for this service.
  - **Tables Pattern**: Fill in a table name pattern (using regex) to match multiple tables automatically.
- **Job Triggering \***: Define the schedule to automatically run this service.

  - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
  - **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
  - **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.
- **Tags**: Type and press Enter to add a new tag for this service. Tags are useful for searching and organizing your own services. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20251020-104153.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321672/image-20251020-104153.png?api=v2)![image-20251020-104335.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321672/image-20251020-104335.png?api=v2)![image-20251020-104404.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321672/image-20251020-104404.png?api=v2)

**Step 2**:Fill in a config of service on Service Parameters box. The platform already provided the initial template for the user likes tableNames and realmId.

List of Tables

```
{
  "tableNames": [
    "table_test_1",
    "table_test_2"
  ],
  "realmId": 1
}
```

Table

```
{
  "tableName": "table_test_1",
  "realmId": 1
}
```

Tables Pattern

```
{
  "tableRegexPattern": ".*",
  "isFilterStream": true,
  "excludeList": [],
  "realmId": 1
}
```

- **tableNames**: Fill in the table name in the format `"table_name"` or `"schema_name.table_name"`.
- **tableRegexPattern**: Define the name pattern to automatically match multiple tables.

  Example:

  ```
  "tableRegexPattern": "^sales_.*",
  ```

This pattern will match all tables starting with `sales_`, such as `sales_2023`, `sales_q1`, `sales_daily`, and `sales_summary`.  
All of these tables will be refreshed automatically according to the service conditions.

- **isFilterStream**: Run only for stream tables (`true`), or for all tables (`false`).

  - `"isFilterStream": true` → the service will refresh **only stream tables**.
  - `"isFilterStream": false` → the service will refresh **all tables**, not limited to stream types.
- **excludeList**: Specify table names to exclude from this job service.

**For example**

![image-20251021-090600.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321672/image-20251021-090600.png?api=v2)

These Service Parameters will refresh all table names that start with "**sales\_**", except for "sales\_2022" and "sales\_2023".

---

## Compact Data (Hot-Warm) Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321694/Compact+Data+Hot-Warm+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:55:38.891Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# **How to create Compact Data (Hot-Warm) Job**

**Step 1**: Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, and put the service tag. Then, click “**Create**” to create this service.

- **File Type Service Name \***: Fill in the name for this service.
- **Job Triggering \***: Define the schedule to automatically run this service.

  - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
  - **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
  - **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.
- **Tags**: Type and press Enter to add a new tag for this service. Tags are useful for searching and organizing your own services. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20251021-091437.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321694/image-20251021-091437.png?api=v2)

**Step 2:** Fill in a config of service on Service Parameters box, the platform also provides UI for users when importing or creating a new table with the State Table Source type, so there is no need to configure it again in the Parameters box. You can see more details about the state tables, go to [State Tables](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322033/State+Table) page.

```
{
  "fromState": "hot",
  "toState": "warm",
  "value": "2",
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "options": {
    "compression": "snappy"
  },
  "realmId": 1
}
```

```
{
  "fromState": "warm",
  "toState": "cold",
  "value": "10",
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "options": {
    "compression": "gzip"
  },
  "realmId": 1
}
```

- **fromState**: Fill in the starting state (e.g., hot or warm).
- **toState**: Fill in the next state you want to switch to (e.g., warm or cold).
- **value**: The number of days that will keep the data in the first state and will move the data to the second state. (The value unit is number of days.)
- **tableName**: Fill in the table name in the format `"table_name"` or `"schema_name.table_name"`.
- **options**: Fill in the compression method including “**gzip**” or “**snappy**”.

The “**Cold**” state requires using the [Table Retention Job](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321725/Table+Retention+Job) with “**State Table Retention**” to remove data from this state.

---

## Table Retention Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321725/Table+Retention+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:55:53.498Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# **How to create Table Retention Job**

**Step 1**: Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, and put the service tag.

- **File Type Service Name \***: Fill in the name for this service.
- **ExistingTemplate List**: Select the template type for this service.

  - **Table Retention**: Fill in internal and delta tables source type is configured with time series.
  - **State Table Retention**: Fill in state tables source type, this service is related to table state management — please review all state service details before using this service type with the [Compact Data (Hot–Warm) Job](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/3oA1gw).
- **Job Triggering \***: Define the schedule to automatically run this service.

  - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
  - **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
  - **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.
- **Tags**: Type and press Enter to add a new tag for this service. Tags are useful for searching and organizing your own services. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20251022-053621.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321725/image-20251022-053621.png?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321725/retention_t.PNG?api=v2)

**Step 2**:Fill in a config of service on Service Parameters box. The platform already provided the initial template for the user.

Table Retention

```
{
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "olderThan": 0,
  "realmId": 1
}
```

State Table Retention

```
{
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "state": "cold",
  "olderThan": 0,
  "realmId": 1
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the table name in the format `"table_name"` or `"schema_name.table_name"`.
- **olderThan**: Set a number of days to remove the table.
- **state**: The table in this state will be removed based on the condition.

**Note**: Minimize the retention period to 90 days. This ensures that old records in the data asset are retained for 90 days or longer before the system automatically removes them.

Table Retention Job now also supports Delta Tables with a time-series, but user need to config from Service Management menu.

---

## Merge Data Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321752/Merge+Data+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-04-22T04:27:39.299Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# **How to create Merge Data Job**

Before starting, please note that this service does not support Delta tables.

**Step 1**:Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, and put the service tag.

- **File Type Service Name \***: Fill in the name for this service.
- **Job Triggering \***: Define the schedule to automatically run this service.

  - **Cron Expression**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
  - **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
  - **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.
- **Tags**: Type and press Enter to add a new tag for this service. Tags are useful for searching and organizing your own services. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20250605-091054.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321752/image-20250605-091054.png?api=v2)

**Step 2**: Fill in a config of service on Service Parameters box. The platform already provided the initial template for the user likes tableName and realmId.

Examples of Configuration.

1. Default configuration without partitions.

```
{
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "options": {},
  "realmId": 1
}
```

2. Set number of output files.

```
{
  "tableName": "table_test",
  "options": {},
  "realmId": 1,
  "numPartitions": 2
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the table name in the format `"table_name"` or `"schema_name.table_name"`.
- **realmId**: Fill in the environment ID where the job will run, for example: `1` for `MASTER_REALM`, `2` for `STAGING_REALM`.
- **numPartitions**: Fill in how many output files the job should generate.

If **numPartitions** is unspecified, the system will automatically determine the optimal number of output files.

Users cannot use “**value**” and “**partitionConditions**” parameter at the same time.

- **partitionConditions:** Setup the condition for a partition.

  - **name**: Fill in the partition field name.
  - **value**: Fill in the partition date name such as 20230116.

![image-20250605-095410.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321752/image-20250605-095410.png?api=v2)

In v4.6.12, general users can’t access or retrieve datasets from the “**default\_schema.**”  
Only the “**Super\_Admin**” can view and operate within it.

---

## Table Update Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321774/Table+Update+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:56:31.895Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# How to create Job Table Update Job

**Step 1**: Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, and put the service tag.

- **File Type Service Name \***: Fill in the name for this service.
- **Job Triggering \***: Define the schedule to automatically run this service.

  - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
  - **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
  - **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.
- **Tags**: Type and press Enter to add a new tag for this service. Tags are useful for searching and organizing your own services. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20251026-154813.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321774/image-20251026-154813.png?api=v2)

**Step 2**:Fill in a config of service on Service Parameters box. The platform already provided the initial template for the user likes tableName and realmId.

```
{
  "tableNames": [
    "table_test_1",
    "table_test_2"
  ],
  "realmId": 1
}
```

- **tableNames**: Fill in the table name in the format `"table_name"` or `"schema_name.table_name"`.  
  This service also supports multiple tables in a single execution.

![image-20251026-155136.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321774/image-20251026-155136.png?api=v2)

![(info)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/information.png) **Tip:** To edit the source option for data updating, the user can add more configuration as per below.

1. Add a configuration named `updateOptions` and set its value to 'true' to enable `options` configuration.
2. Add a configuration named `options` and set the source option value as per needed.

**Optional**: Moreover, the user can change a configuration value of `sinkMode` by setting the value as `append`/`overwrite`.

**Example**

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321774/image-20230309-031137.png?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321774/image-20230309-035005.png?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321774/image-20230309-035031.png?api=v2)

```
{
  "zeusId": 3,
  "realmId": 1,
  "tableName": "table_name",
  "updateOptions": true,
  "sinkMode": "Overwrite",
  "options": {
      "url": "jdbc:sqlserver://hostname:port;databaseName=tablename",
      "user":"username",
      "password" : "password",
      "query": "select * from table"
      "path" : "xxxx"
      "charset" : "UTF-8",
      "fetchsize": 100000 
   }
 }
```

- **option**: To setup a new the source option and overwrite it with the new configuration, which is depend on import type.

File Upload/File path

- **path**: File location path.
- **charset**: Fill in your Encoding format
- **delimiter**: Identify symbol of the delimiter of your source file
- **escape**: To indicate the symbol of value field ending location.
- **header**: (true/false) Enable header or not.
- **inferSchema**: (true/false)
- **multiLine**: (true/false) Indicate to separate the values in the same line and create the new line or not.
- **quote**: To indicate the symbol, which is the starting of the value.

SQL Server

- **url**: Source location.
- **user**: Fill in username.
- **password**: Fill in a password.
- **query**: The SQL statement.
- **path**: Source path.
- **charset**: Fill in your Encoding format.
- **fetchsize**: Limit the number of records extracted from the database per batch.

MySQL

- **url**: Source location like jdbc:mysql://hostname:port/databasename.
- **user**: Fill in username.
- **password**: Fill in a password.
- **dbtable**: Database name.

Stream

- **path**: File location path.
- charset: Fill in your Encoding format.
- **delimiter**: Identify symbol of the delimiter of your source file
- **escape**: To indicate the symbol of value field ending location.
- **failOnDataLoss**: (true/false) To indicate to stop when the data loss or not.
- **header**: (true/false) Enable header or not.
- **inferSchema**: (true/false)
- **multiLine**: (true/false) Indicate to separate the values in the same line and create the new line or not.
- **quote**: To indicate the symbol, which is the starting of the value.
- **startingOffsets**: Select option to start offsets including latest, earliest, and custom.

---

## Export Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321811/Export+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:56:44.903Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# **How to create Export Job**

**Step 1**:Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, the service tag.

- **File Type Service Name \***: Fill in the name for this service.
- **ExistingTemplate List**: Choose a template from the list to automatically fill in the initial service parameters.
- **Job Triggering \***: Define the schedule to automatically run this service.

  - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
  - **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
  - **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.
- **Tags**: Type and press Enter to add a new tag for this service. Tags are useful for searching and organizing your own services. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20251026-160052.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321811/image-20251026-160052.png?api=v2)

**Step 2:** Fill in the service configuration in the Service Parameters box, the platform also provides UI for users when using “[Export File](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2120385706/Export+File#Service-Setting)” after retrieving a query result from the “**Simple Query**”, “**Data Exploration**”, or “**SQL Editor**” menu. so there is no need to configure it again in the Parameters box.

```
{
  "exportType": "filepath",
  "action": "EXPORT",
  "filename": "table_test",
  "sql": "select * from table_test",
  "isConvertSchema": false,
  "sinkMode": "append",
  "sinkFormat": "csv",
  "isInternal": true,
  "sinkOptions": {
    "header": true,
    "delimiter": ",",
    "quote": "\"",
    "escape": "\"",
    "zipFile": true
  },
  "partitions": [],
  "numPartitions": 1,
  "start": "01/01/2020 00:00:00",
  "end": "01/02/2020 23:59:59",
  "realmId": 1
}
```

- **exportType**: This is an export type.
- **action**: This is a job action type.
- **filename**: Fill in the file name.
- **sql**: The SQL command for this table export.
- **isConvertSchema**: Set whether to convert the table schema between source and target. `false` means the schema remains the same.
- **sinkMode**: This is fixed values of export mode, which is append.
- **sinkFormat**: Fill in your export file such as CSV, Excel, JSON, Parquet, and text file.
- **isInternal**: Using Spark to export the output file. (The default is true.)
- **sinkOptions**:

  - CSV format: Fill in the header, delimiter, quote, escape, and zipFile.
  - JSON format: Fill in encoding type and zipFile.
  - Excel format: Fill in the header condition(true or false) and zipFile.
- **partitions**: This part is for the partition setting, users can set the number of file, start date and end date from time range.

---

## Delta Compaction Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321833/Delta+Compaction+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:54:42.356Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

As the Delta table enables data processing with CRUD commands, users can create, read, update, and delete records through the Blendata platform. With each operation, the platform stores a version of the Parquet file in our storage. However, this can impact data processing, potentially leading to slower performance or longer execution times. We have created the Delta Compaction job to decrease the data file size and free the storage space.

![image-20240412-110637.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321833/image-20240412-110637.png?api=v2)

1. Select the Delta table.
2. Click action button:3dots:and select “**Delta Compaction Table**“ option.

![image-20240412-110720.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321833/image-20240412-110720.png?api=v2)

3. The platform will run the Compact Table job as background. If your Delta table set the partition, there is an partition popup showed to select for Delta Compaction Table.

![image-20240412-110923.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321833/image-20240412-110923.png?api=v2)

4. Users can check the job status in the System job(when user enables this option since import the data) or the Delta Compaction Table Job under Service Management menu.

![delta compact in service.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321833/delta%20compact%20in%20service.png?api=v2)

To do the Delta Compaction Job, users need to have the management permission level.

---

## Create New Service in Service Management

User can setup as per below:

```
{
  "deltaCompacts": [
    {
      "tableName": "fruit_test",
      "partitionConditionList": [
        {
          "name": "partitionFieldName",
          "value": "value",
          "comparator": "="
        }
      ],
      "zOrderColumns": [],
      "compressionFileNum": null
    }
  ],
  "poolName": null,
  "realmId": 1
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the table name in the format `"table_name"` or `"schema_name.table_name"`.
- **partitionConditionList**: Setup the condition for a partition.

  - **name**: Fill in the partition field name.
  - **value**: Fill in the partition date name such as 20251104
  - **comparator**: Fill in the relational operators such as equal, grater than, or less than.

If the comparator are the grater than, or less than, the selected field has to be the number type such as integer, floating, double, and etc.

**Comparator Supported options include:**

- `lt` = (`<`) less than
- `gt` = (`>`) greater than
- `lte` = (`<=`) less than or equal
- `gte` = (`>=`) greater than or equal
- `eq` = (`=`) equal

Currently, by default, the file will be compacted on the partition date of the previous day(current date -1). Thus, no need to fill in value in **partitionConditionList** parameter.

- **zOrderColumns**: Fill in the column names (multiple columns supported) to optimize query performance.
- **compressionFileNum**: Fill in the number of compaction files. If you use 'null', it means one file.

---

For example, using “**zOrderColumns**”

![image-20250828-071937.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321833/image-20250828-071937.png?api=v2)

You can verify whether the table has been optimized by running the following command:

```
DESCRIBE HISTORY n_test_delta_patition;
```

![image-20250828-072323.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321833/image-20250828-072323.png?api=v2)

---

## Vacuum Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321875/Vacuum+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:54:56.810Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

As the Delta table enables data processing with CRUD commands, users can create, read, update, and delete records through the Blendata platform. With each operation, the platform stores a version of the Parquet file in our storage. However, this can impact data processing, potentially leading to slower performance or longer execution times. We have created the Vacuum Table job to reduce the data sizing by removing the old data files as per period setting time.

![image-20240412-115056.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321875/image-20240412-115056.png?api=v2)

1. Select your Delta table.
2. Click action button:3dots:and select “**Vacuum Table**“ option.

![image-20240412-113753.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321875/image-20240412-113753.png?api=v2)

3. Select the period to do the vacuum job with Hours, Day, and Week and click “**Vacuum**“ button. (The default period is 7 days.)

![image-20240412-113959.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321875/image-20240412-113959.png?api=v2)

4. Confirm to proceed the data Vacuum by click “**Proceed**“.

![image-20240412-114434.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321875/image-20240412-114434.png?api=v2)

5. Users can check the job status in the System job(when user enables this option since import the data) or the Vacuum Table Job under Service Management menu.

![vacuum job.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321875/vacuum%20job.png?api=v2)

---

## Create New Service in Service Management

Users also use the Data Vacuum in the Service Management to do data vacuum for one table or multi-table at a time.

```
{
  "vacuumTables": [
    {
      "tableName": "fruit_test",
      "retainNum": null,
      "retainUnit": null
    }
  ],
  "vacuumTable": {
    "tableName": "fruit_test",
    "retainNum": null,
    "retainUnit": null
  },
  "poolName": null,
  "realmId": 1
}
```

- **tableName**: Fill in the table name in the format `"table_name"` or `"schema_name.table_name"`.
- **retainNum**: Fill in the number related to retainUnit parameter.
- **retainUnit**: Fill in your unit such as month, day, hour, minute, or second.

In case, you need to do Data Vacuum only one table. You can use below template.

```
{
  "vacuumTable": {
    "tableName": "name",
    "retainNum": null,
    "retainUnit": null
  },
  "poolName": null,
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

---

## Aggregate Table Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321922/Aggregate+Table+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:55:11.023Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

**Step 1**: Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, the service tag, and advance settings.

**Advanced Options**

- **Select the zeus to run:** To specify a zeus for this service.
- **Spark Configuration:** To specify a parameter of Spark during do write parquet file process such as Parquet Index creation setting to improve the performance of internal table reading.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321922/2e45ce00-98ac-4455-9571-c0f3fd5a2271%23media-blob-url=true&id=9bc14e0f-47e9-4d47-acc3-2f12d52b1278&collection=&contextId=15661&mimeType=image%2Fpng&name=image-20220606-073505.png&size=270490&height=845&width=1440&alt=?api=v2)

**Step 2**: Fill in a config of service on Service Parameters box. The platform already provided the initial template for the user.

```
{
  "aggregationConfig": {
    "dataType": "non-time series",
    "sinkOption": {
      "mode": "Append",
      "partitions": [],
      "format": "parquet",
      "interval": 0,
      "numPartitions": 1,
      "options": {
        "isEncryptData": "false"
      }
    },
    "tableName": "table_test",
    "sql": "SELECT * FROM table_test where product = 'Product1'",
    "start": "-7d",
    "isRoundTimeStart": false,
    "end": "now",
    "isRoundTimeEnd": false
  },
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **dataType**: Select between non-time series and time series options.
- **sinkOption**: to set about the partition

  - **mode**: There are two mode including overwrite and append options.
  - **format**: Select the format based on the destination data asset, which are parquet or delta.
  - **numPartitions**: Fill in the number of partition file.
  - **isEncryptData**: Set this data to apply the encryption or not.
- **tableName**: Fill in your data tables name.
- **sql**: Fill in your SQL command.
- **start**: Fill in the number of days from the time series that users need to do the extract. (based on the source data asset)
- **isRoundTimeStart**: This option is use to round up the start time to be midnight, if the user choose true value. Otherwise, it will use the actual time.(There are true or false.)
- **end**: Fill in the end period of this data. (based on the source data asset)
- **isRoundTimeEnd**: This option is use to round up the end time to be midnight, if the user choose true value. Otherwise, it will use the actual time.(There are true or false.)

**Append mode** needs to use the same partition with the source data. User can fill in the non-time series partition values with the set values such as ["column\_partition","column\_partition\_2"].

**Overwrite mode**, user can setup the partition as per destination data asset.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321922/agg-servic.PNG?api=v2)

---

## Alert Scheduler

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321949/Alert+Scheduler |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T04:55:31.180Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# How to set the Alert Scheduler

**Step 1:** Fill in the basic information including Alert Name, Alert Description, and put the service tag.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321949/info.PNG?api=v2)

**Step 2**:Fill in the condition of Alert including SQL query, limit, and the number of events.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321949/condtion.PNG?api=v2)

**Step 3**: Select the frequency of Alert Service such as by minutes, hourly, daily, or using cron expression.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321949/sechduler.PNG?api=v2)

**Step 4**:Select the duration of this alert setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321949/datetimerange.PNG?api=v2)

**Step 5**:Select the Alert actions as per below details.

- **Notification:** Show the alert notification on the platform and able to select the severtiy level.

  ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321949/noti.PNG?api=v2)
  - **Email:** Send the alert notification to email address as per setting and also attach the table result including CSV file or email message.

    ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321949/email.PNG?api=v2)
  - **Run Script**: Create a script on a server. If an activity/event matches with the script on the server, the script will run automatically.

    ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321949/runscript.PNG?api=v2)
  - **Run Service:** Create your own service. If an activity/event matches with the service, the service will run automatically. For more details and examples, see [Run Service Actions Details](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/BICNog)[.](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2201321949/Alert+Scheduler#Run-Service-Actions-Details)

    ![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201321949/runservice.PNG?api=v2)

**Example**: Non-SSL Connection with Kafka service type

```
{
  "target": {
    "receiveTopics": "notification-topic",
    "receiveBrokers": "localhost:9092"
  }
}
```

**Example**: SSH Run Script Connection

```
{
  "target": {
    "userSSH": "admin",
    "ipHost": "192.168.1.100",
    "port": "22",
    "pathFileName": "/home/admin/scripts/backup.sh"
  }
}
```

- Ensure that the private key (`~/.ssh/id_rsa`) has permission to access the remote server.
- The script file on the remote server must have execute permission.

## State Table

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322033/State+Table |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-30T09:48:31.933Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management |

# Data Compaction

By default, our platform sets up the period for each state, but users have the flexibility to customize the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322033/s2.png?api=v2)

- **Hot State**: In this state, data is uncompressed, resulting in faster response times for data extraction.
- **Warm State**: The data is compressed using the Snappy Compressor when transitioning from the Hot State to reduce the data size.
- **Cold State**: The data is compressed using the Gzip Compressor when transitioning from the Warm State to maintain a smaller data size.

## How to setup the customize state job

1. Import the data and select the table source type as “**Internal State Table**”.
2. On setting output options, users can setup the state table on **Services** part.
3. Click “**Change Customization**” and select “**Change to State**”.
4. Choose “**Customize Change to State Jobs**” to customize the states and specify the days for each states.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322033/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

---

# Data Retention

Our platform establishes a default data retention setting to clear unused data, specifically for data with time series characteristics. For non-time series data, users need to create a new table retention service on the Service Management page. See more details, go to [Table Retention Job](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201321725/Table+Retention+Job#How-to-create-Table-Retention-Job) page.

---

## Schedule Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322074/Schedule+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-26T16:47:02.769Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management |

When creating a new service, a popup will appear to configure the service, including Job Triggering. There are three schedule types as follows:

![image-20251026-164011.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322074/image-20251026-164011.png?api=v2)

- **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
- **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
- **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.

## Chain Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322096/Chain+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-26T17:01:26.099Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management |

# How to create Chain Job

1. Go to Service Management menu :service: and click Service Chains tab.
2. Click“**Add Chain**” and then “+ **Create Chains**” :create\_chain:. Then, the platform will show a popup to fill in the service chain name and description.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322096/s11.png?api=v2)

3. A diagram panel will show on the page. Click a action :3dots: on a service box and select “**Assign Service**“ to put the service information.

![image-20251026-165804.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322096/image-20251026-165804.png?api=v2)

- **Diagram**

  - **Service Type**: Select a service type.
  - **Service Name**: Set a service name for Assign Service.
  - **Existing Service Template**: Select the existing services template from the same service type.
  - **File Type Template**: Select the template type for this service.
  - **Service Triggering**: Set a frequency of this Service Chain such as cron expression and specific time.
  - **Service Parameters**: A service code panel. The user able to revise the code on this panel likes editing the table name using SQL query.

4. Click “**Create**” :create: and create a next service by clicking “**Action**”:3dots:and select “**Add Next Service**“.

- **Action**

  - **Add Next Service**: To create a next service.
  - **Edit Service**: To Edit the existing service template and service triggering of the selected service.
  - **Delete Service**: To delete the selected service.

 5. Once the Service Chains diagram is done, click “**Start Service Chain**” to start this service chain.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322096/chain-serivce-start.jpg?api=v2)

6. The platform will show the notification “**Start (Service Chain name) Success**”. The user able to stop the job chain by click “Stop Job Chain“

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322096/chain-serivce-start-already.PNG?api=v2)

# Service Chain Management

You can manage the service chain with action button likes below screenshot.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322096/b2.PNG?api=v2)

- **Start Chain**: To start the selected service chain.
- **Stop Chain**: To stop the selected service chain.
- **Edit Chain**: To edit the selected service chain.
- **Delete** **Chain**: To delete the selected service chain.

---

# Chain Logs

A chain logs displays all service chain history including the service job name, start time, end time, name, result, and details.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322096/b1.PNG?api=v2)

## Chain Logs components

- **ID**: Service ID number.
- **Job Profile Name**: Service program name.
- **Start Time**: The start time of Service Chain.
- **End Time**: The end time of Service Chain.
- **Name**: The owner name of Service Chain.
- **Result**: The status of Service Program.
- **Details**: The status details of Service Program.

## System Jobs

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201322153/System+Jobs |
| **Last modified** | 2026-03-06T08:51:54.793Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management |

# **System Job Functions**

There are some main functions that our platform has already set up as defaults in case users prefer not to customize their own services. Below are the provided functional services:

- Refresh Table Job
- Recovery Job
- Compact (Hot-Warm) Job
- Table Retention Job
- File Retention Job

# **System Job Components**

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322153/s14.png?api=v2)

- **Status Summary**: Summarize all job status.
- **Actions (when select a job on the service panel)**

  - **Start Schedule Job**: To start a selected job.
  - **Stop Schedule Job**: To stop a selected job.
  - **Manage Zeus on Selected**: To select a Zeus for a selected service.
- **Service Panel**: All system jobs information.

  - **Service Name**: The name of Service.
  - **Schedule**: The schedule setting of jobs.
  - **Status**: The Service status.
  - **Latest Result**: The latest status of the service.
  - **Next Fire Time**: The schedule time for the next processing.
  - **Zeus Name**: The name of Zeus that is used for this service.
  - **Last Modified**: The last modified date and time, and username, who edit the service.
  - **Action**

    - **Stop Schedule Job**: To stop the schedule job for a selected service.
    - **Execute Immediately**: To immediately execute a selected service.
    - **Force Stop Process:** To force stop the process in case this service takes too long to execute.
    - **Service History**: To see the service history details.
    - **Service Actions**

      - **View Service**: To see the Service information.
      - **Edit Service**: To edit the Service name and details.
      - **Manage Zeus**: To manage a Zeus for this service.

---

## System Table Retention Job

We have enhanced the system table retention feature to minimize the retention period to **90 days**. This ensures that old records in the data asset are retained for 90 days or longer before the system automatically removes them.

![retention days.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201322153/retention%20days.png?api=v2)

---

## System File Path

In version 4.6.13, the Blendata platform has separated the upload and internal file paths. This helps users manage services more easily and improves the process for each data ingestion type.

- **Trash path**: The storage location for deleted data files. (Default: `/data/zeus/trash`)
- **Temporary path**: The storage location for temporary data files created during processes such as stream importing or file merging. (Default: `/data/zeus/tmp`)
- **Backup path**: The storage location for backup files created when a user deletes a data asset and selects the '**Backup raw data**' option. (Default: `/data/zeus/store_backup`)
- **Upload path**: The storage location for data profiles imported via the '**Upload File**' method. (Default: `/data/zeus/upload`)
- **Internal File path**: The storage location for data profiles imported via the '**Internal File Path**' method. (Default: `/data/zeus/internal_file`)
- **Export path**: The storage location for exported data files. (Default: `/data/zeus/export`)

## Realm Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201419808/Realm+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2026-04-08T14:48:07.603Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Admin Setting Menus |

From [Architecture](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/2027520001/Architecture+Component+overview) overview, Blendata comprises various modules, each serving different processing purposes. In this section, we will explain deeper into the Zeus module and how a Zeus group, also known as a '**Realm**,' operates.

Zeus serves as the core module of Blendata, facilitating data processing tasks such as data querying and data streaming. It is built upon the Spark framework. The Spark table encompasses a datastore, a folder of data storage housing data from Spark tables, and a metastore storing metadata information of Spark tables in a MySQL database.

A Realm denotes a segregated location for data assets, signifying a collaborative working environment for Zeus groups. However, each Realm operates independently, with its own Zeus instance, metastore, and datastore. Consequently, data is not shared between Realms.

This features is for users who has a permission to access the realm management e.g. admin, super\_admin users.

---

# Realm Management Page

A Realm represents an environment that contains compute resources, metadata, and data storage.

![image-20260408-131550.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201419808/image-20260408-131550.png?api=v2)

## Realm Tab

This tab provides an overview of all existing realms in the environment. Users can create new realms or perform actions on existing ones.

- **Realm Name**: The name of the realm.
- **Realm ID**: A unique identifier assigned to each realm for system-level reference and management.
- **Compute**: The compute resources assigned to the realm, used to execute queries, jobs, and workloads.
- **Metastore**: The metadata repository that stores information about database schemas, tables, and structures used within the realm.
- **Datastore**: The physical storage location where actual data is stored, such as file systems.
- **Create By**: The user who created the realm.
- **Action**: Available operations that can be performed on the realm, such as edit, delete, or manage configuration.

  - **Manage Realm**: Used to enable or disable compute resources.
  - **View Realm Details**: View detailed information about the selected realm.
  - **Edit Realm**: Modify the realm configuration.
  - **Delete Realm**: Permanently remove the selected realm from the system.

### How To Create a new Realm

1. Click ![image-20260408-134730.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201419808/image-20260408-134730.png?api=v2) in this page.
2. Enter Realm information.

   ![image-20260408-134810.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201419808/image-20260408-134810.png?api=v2)
   1. **Metastore Configuration**

      1. **Realm Name\***: Enter the Realm name.
      2. **Realm Description**: A brief description of the realm, used to describe its purpose or usage.
      3. **Database Name\***: Enter the Database name.
      4. **Database Type\***: Enter the database type, e.g., `jdbc:mysql` or `jdbc:postgresql`.
      5. **Host\***: Enter the hostname or IP address of the database server.
      6. **Port\***: Enter the port number used to connect to the database (e.g., `3306` for MySQL, `5432` for PostgreSQL).
   2. **Datastore Configuration**

      1. **Type**: Specify the datastore type used for data storage (e.g., `file`, `viewfs`).

         - `file`: Local file system storage
         - `viewfs`: Virtual file system.
      2. **Host**: Enter the hostname or server where the datastore is located.
      3. **Port**: Enter the port used to access the datastore, if applicable.
      4. **Path\***: Specify the directory path where data will be stored (e.g., `/data/zeus/store`).
3. Select a compute resource from the available list.

   1. Click the toggle to enable all compute resources.
   2. Check the box to assign the resource to the realm.

      ![image-20260408-140556.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201419808/image-20260408-140556.png?api=v2)
4. Setting Zeus Default in this Realm.

   1. **Default Zeus \***: Select the default Zeus to be used across the platform.
   2. **Query**: (Optional) Select the Zeus instance to be used for query execution.
   3. **Operation**: (Optional) Select the Zeus instance to be used for running services or other operations.

      ![image-20260408-140943.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201419808/image-20260408-140943.png?api=v2)
5. Review your settings before creating the realm.

---

## Compute Group Tab

This tab displays Zeus Groups available for use in the environment, which are used for load balancing and organizing compute resources.

![image-20260408-142955.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201419808/image-20260408-142955.png?api=v2)

- **Zeus Group Name**: The name of the Zeus Group.
- **Zeus Group ID**: A unique identifier assigned to each Zeus Group for system reference and management.
- **Realm Name**: The name of the realm to which the Zeus Group belongs.
- **Zeus Name**: The name of the Zeus instance included in the group. A group may contain one or more Zeus instances.
- **Metastore**: The metadata configuration associated with the Zeus instance, including database name, type, host, and port.
- **Datastore**: The storage configuration used by the Zeus instance, including storage type, host, and data path.
- **Action**: Available actions that can be performed on the Zeus Group, such as edit and delete.

  - **Edit Zeus Group**: Modify the Zeus Group configuration.
  - **Delete Zeus Group**: Remove the Zeus Group from the system.

### How To Create Zeus Group

1. Click ![image-20260408-143951.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201419808/image-20260408-143951.png?api=v2)
2. Create a target group of Zeus for the load balancer.

   1. **Zeus Group Name\***: Enter a name for the Zeus Group.
   2. **Select Realm\***: Select the realm in which the Zeus Group will be created.
   3. **Select Zeus**: Select one or more Zeus instances to include in the group.

![image-20260408-144359.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201419808/image-20260408-144359.png?api=v2)

---

This menu used for all module managements, where the user ia able to monitor the module processing, module status, and can download Logs of ETL. Moreover, the user can start, stop, and restart the module via this menu.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/1816726299/module.PNG?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/1816726299/module-etl.PNG?api=v2)

## User Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201420007/User+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-26T11:11:22.628Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Admin Setting Menus |

The user management menu is used to manage users, data asset access, and user roles. This page displays user status, latest login date, and user roles. The Admin role can create new users, assign roles, and manage operational permissions for each [user](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2266431496/User) and [role](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2266398727/Role) via this menu.

In our platform, we have an initial setting for users and roles, which the platform generate these roles setting as following:

1. system:system <user:role> can see all menus, able to start/stop the service, and also create additional Tenant.
2. super\_admin:super\_admin is similar to role system but cannot create an additional Tenant.
3. administrator:administrator can manage all users and roles, but cannot access to all menus in the platform.

***Remark**: The platform allow the user to create the user and add into the roles later.*

If you want to assign the super\_admin role to a user, the permission can only be granted by an existing super\_admin.   
No other roles have the authority to assign or modify super\_admin privileges.

## LDAP Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201420244/LDAP+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2025-01-16T05:43:18.787Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Admin Setting Menus |

Used to manage the LDAP configuration for LDAP connection, which helps to import the username and password information into the platform without doing configuration again.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/1816726527/ldap1.PNG?api=v2)

## Create LDAP Configuration

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/1816726527/ldap2.PNG?api=v2)

1. Click “**Create LDAP Configuration**“ and fill in the details below.

- **LDAP Configuration Name**: The name of LDAP Configuration.
- **LDAP Server URL**: The URL to connect with LDAP server.
- **LDAP Search Base**: The source of all LDAP operations related with search base.
- **Administrator DN**: The administrator’s name of LDAP server.
- **Administrator Password**: The administrator’s password of LDAP server.
- **Username Mapping Attribute**: The username that use to map with login username.
- **User Search Filter**: Search filter to select entries to synchronize as users.
- **User DN Patterns**: The distinguished name pattern to map for login user.
- **Use LDAP Role Mapping**: Connect the role between LDAP and Blendata platform.
- **Connection Timeout**: The connection timeout when opening a communication in millisecond.
- **Referral**: To set the referral strategy of LDAP with ignore, follow, throw options.
- **Page Size**: The number of rows to query per time until all result are found.

2. Click “**Create**“.

## LDAP Management

- **Edit**: Edit the LDAP setting.
- **Disable**: Stop the selected LDAP management.

## License Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201420298/License+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2025-08-26T11:27:29.869Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Admin Setting Menus |

License Management is the License management function, where the user activates your license, monitor, and manage your Blendata license. There are three part as following.

![license mng.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201420298/license%20mng.png?api=v2)

# **License**

The current license information that users have subscribed our Blendata platform. There are importance information likes product version and valid date.

![license.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201420298/license.png?api=v2)

---

# **vCPU**

The Gauge visulization presents the current CPU usage. For example, the user have 24 CPU and now the user has used upto 8 CPU for Module (Zeus) processing. The module details is on the Module License part. See more detail about Zeus, Go to [Realm Management](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/pages/createpage.action?spaceKey=BE1&title=Realm%20Management) page.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/1816726573/lisence_2.PNG?api=v2)

---

# **Module Licenses**

This part presents each Module(Zeus) CPU consuming and Module status. Furthermore, users are able to activate, deactivate, or delete the modules.

![module license.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201420298/module%20license.png?api=v2)

## Activate/Deactivate The Module

1. Go to Blendata Enterprise website.
2. Go to “**Setting**” and click “**License Management**“menu :lms:.
3. Click switch button from Activate to Deactivate, or click action option and select “**Activate**” or “**Deactivate**“ option.

![deactivate the license.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201420298/deactivate%20the%20license.png?api=v2)

## Auto Start/Stop The Module

1. Click action button and select “**Auto Start/Stop**” option.
2. Tick the setting option that you want.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2201420298/auto%20start:stop%20module.png?api=v2)

- **Auto Start**: To setup an auto start when triggered by job request.
- **Auto Stop**: Users need to enter the number of inactive minutes for auto-stop.

3. Click “**Submit**” to setup this setting.

## Create Table

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468362/Create+Table |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T05:20:33.859Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Catalog |

# Create Table

To create a new asset schema into our platform.

![create table schema.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2202468362/create%20table%20schema.png?api=v2)

1. Click “**Create table”**, add the field name, and select your field type.

   - **Add new column**: This option will add a new column in this table schema.
   - **Generate Fields from Pattern**: This option require using the platform’s pattern to generate schema.
2. Setup a table details.

![table setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2202468362/table%20setting.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

Default TBLPROPERTIES (`delta.minReaderVersion = '3'`, `delta.minWriterVersion = '7'`) are automatically applied when importing or creating a Delta table.

- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).
- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Advanced Option**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type.

Internal table

![services tab.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2202468362/services%20tab.png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![internal state service (create).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2202468362/internal%20state%20service%20(create).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta service (create).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2202468362/delta%20service%20(create).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.
- **Service Enable**: The related service jobs that come with the source type and Time Domain settings. Users can change the job service settings to either use the system default or customize them via the Change Customization option. See more details about services, go to [Services](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869447391/Services) page.

![services.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2202468362/services.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Create**“ :create: to create a schema of a asset without a data.

## Schema Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T01:57:23.666Z |

Users with the Super Administrator role can manage schemas in the platform. They can create new schemas, specify the storage to use, and manage permissions for each schema.

![image-20250828-041359.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2202468402/image-20250828-041359.png?api=v2)

- **Schema Name**: The name of the schema.
- **Storage Name & Path**: The name of the storage and file path.
- **Description**: Additional information or notes about the schema.
- **Access Permission**: The access rights granted to users or roles.
- **Owner**: The user who created or owns the schema.
- **Latest Modify**: The date and time when the schema was last updated.
- **Actions**: A menu to perform further operations, [see more details](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management#Schema-Management-Action).

---

# Creating Schema

1. Click “**Create New**“.
2. Fill in the schema name, description.
3. Select “**Storage Name**” for this schema, and click “**Save**“.

When you select a schema for importing a data asset, it will automatically be saved to the storage configured for that schema. However, users can still manually change the storage location during the import process.

![image-20250828-041453.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2202468402/image-20250828-041453.png?api=v2)

This specific storage name function is supported in Blendata v4.6.10, v4.6.11, and v4.6.13 and later (v4.6.12 is not supported).
