## REST API

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2229207115/REST+API |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:04:11.095Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset |

**REST API**, which stands for Representational State Transfer Application Programming Interface, comprises a set of rules and conventions for constructing and engaging with web services. The platform enables users to connect to data through a REST API, and the steps to do so are below.

## REST API Setting

1. Fill in the REST API details.

![REST API.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/REST%20API.png?api=v2)

- **HTTP Methods**

  - **GET**: Used to retrieve information from the server using a specified URI.
  - **POST**: Used to send data to the server.
  - **PUT**: Replaces all current representations of the target resource with the uploaded content.
- **Service URL**: the data server URL.

  - Enable putting dynamic parameters in the URL, in case it requires a Job Schedule to run this option on a schedule. For instruction, click “**Dynamic Expression Instruction**”.
- **Authentication**: Enable and select the authentication type.

  - **Basic Authentication**: If the data requires the password, the user can enable this option to fill in the username and password.

    - **Username**: authorized username of this ElasticSearch server.
    - **Password**: password of this server.
  - **Custom Header**: Need to specific the field value. In case users need to do token - authentication, they can use this option and fill in the token information.
  - **Authentication API**: Fill in the HTTP method, Authentication API and API details in code box.

2. Click “**Test Connection**“ button and click “**Next**“ button :next:.
3. Fill in the JSON path.

The Blendata Enterprise supports nested json as object type but cannot use an array index in the JSON path as of now.

4. Click “**Auto Discovery**“ button :auto\_bt: to see the data schema.

![Screenshot 2025-06-13 124230-20250613-054821.jpg](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/Screenshot%202025-06-13%20124230-20250613-054821.jpg?api=v2)

5. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.

For date type, users need to specify the source data's date format for conversion to a Spark date. If users want both date and time values, they should select the timestamp type.

6. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
7. Preview your data table.
8. Click “**Next**“ to set up an output.
9. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [import to existing table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

![RESTAPI output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/RESTAPI%20output%20setting.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.
- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![RESTAPI (upload method).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/RESTAPI%20(upload%20method).png?api=v2)

- **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
- **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

  - **Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type and Upload method.

Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type and upload method.

![custom state setting2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207115/custom%20state%20setting2.png?api=v2)

10. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

---

## RDBMS

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2229207125/RDBMS |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:04:45.039Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset |

## (tick) Supported RDBMS (Structured Database)

- [Oracle](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106136/Oracle) (Version 11g)
- [MS SQL](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106079/SQL+Server) (Version 2012, 2014,2016, and 2017)
- [MySQL](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106032/MySQL) (Versions 5.5, 5.6, and 5.7)
- [PostgreSQL](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106168/PostgreSQL) (Versions 8.2 and newer)

- Cloudera Impala (Versions 2.0 and later)
- HIVE (Version 2.3.9)
- [Other: JDBC](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106200/Other+RDBMS)

## (info) How to import the RDBMS database

1. Database Setting

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/ms4.png?api=v2)

- **URL:** the database server URL with each RDBMS patterns. (You can see the pattern of each database by click each RDBMS database types)
- **Username**: authorized username of this database server.
- **Password**: password of this database server.

2. Click “**Connect Database**“ button to connect with the database server.
3. Select a table Method and click “**Next**“.

   - **Select Database Table**: Select your data tables. The user able to select more than one data tables with Multi-select option. This method cannot import the data into the existing table. If users need to import the data into existing table, please select “**Input Query Command (SQL)**” method go to **Select Database Table** topic.
   - **Input Query Command (SQL)**: Search a data table by using the SQL commands for a single data asset. Go to **Input Query Command (SQL)** topic.

## **Select Database Table Method**

1. Select the data table and click setting icon :setting: to setup your table information.

You can select multiple tables for the data importing.

![sqlserver.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/sqlserver.png?api=v2)

2. Select an upload method, Zeus path(Storage Name), and a data schema.

a. Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

**Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Time setting**: Can select by minutes, hourly, Daily or using cron expression.

**Execute immediately**: Can select to execute this time setting immediately or as per setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/rdbms%20-%20batch.png?api=v2)

b. Select the **Zeus path**(Storage Name), which is a storage path. (The platform set default Zeus as an initial setup)

c. Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

When you select a Schema for importing a data asset, it will automatically be saved to the storage configured for that schema. However, users can still manually change the storage location during the import process.   
[See more details on how to configure storage associated with a schema.](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management)

3. Fill in the database information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), and [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning).

- **Import Data default information field**: To add import data default field (gtp\_timestamp) into the data asset.
- **Time Domain** : Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

  - **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
  - **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

- **Partitions**:With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

  - **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
  - **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.
- **Delta table** : To create the Delta table asset.

  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
  - **Service Enabled**: To enable Delta services including Delta Compaction Job and Vacuum Job.
- **Encrypt Data** : To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
- **Other configurations**

  - Spark Source Configuration
  - Advanced Sink Configuration

4. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.

![image-20251009-084735.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/image-20251009-084735.png?api=v2)

5. Click “**Next**“ button :next: and go back to the Data Catalog page :catalog:.

---

## **Input Query Command (SQL) Method**

1. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

![MySQL (SQL command).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/MySQL%20(SQL%20command).png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.
- **Schema**: To select a schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![RDBMD upload method.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/RDBMD%20upload%20method.png?api=v2)

- **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
- **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

  - **Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type and Upload Method.

Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type.

![upload file custom service setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207125/upload%20file%20custom%20service%20setting.png?api=v2)

2. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

## Databricks

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2229207143/Databricks |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T06:03:53.354Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset |

As Databricks is well-known in the big data industry and widely used worldwide, we have implemented a feature to support data from Databricks, allowing users to ingest data into our platform for processing and analytics.

# Import Databricks via Select Database Tables method

1. Fill in Databricks url with JDBC connection.
2. Enter your access token and click “**Connect Database**“.
3. Select a table Method and click “**Next**“.

   - **Select Database Table**: Select your data tables. The user able to select more than one data tables with Multi-select option. This method cannot import the data into the existing table. If users need to import the data into existing table, please select “**Input Query Command (SQL)**” method go to **Select Database Table** topic.
   - **Input Query Command (SQL)**: Search a data table by using the SQL commands for a single data asset. Go to **Input Query Command (SQL)** topic.

## **Select Database Table Method**

1. Select the data table and click setting icon :setting: to setup your table information.

You can select multiple tables for the data importing.

2. Select an upload method, Zeus path(Storage Name), and a data schema.

![databricks.jpg](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/databricks.jpg?api=v2)

a. Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

**Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Time setting**: Can select by minutes, hourly, Daily or using cron expression.

**Execute immediately**: Can select to execute this time setting immediately or as per setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/rdbms%20-%20batch.png?api=v2)

b. Select the **Zeus path**(Storage Name), which is a storage path. (The platform set default Zeus as an initial setup)

c. Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

When you select a Schema for importing a data asset, it will automatically be saved to the storage configured for that schema. However, users can still manually change the storage location during the import process.   
[See more details on how to configure storage associated with a schema.](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2202468402/Schema+Management)

3. Fill in the database information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), and [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning).

- **Import Data default information field**: To add import data default field (gtp\_timestamp) into the data asset.
- **Time Domain** : Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

  - **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
  - **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

- **Partitions**:With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

  - **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
  - **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.
- **Delta table** : To create the Delta table asset.

  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
  - **Service Enabled**: To enable Delta services including Delta Compaction Job and Vacuum Job.
- **Encrypt Data** : To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
- **Other configurations**

  - Spark Source Configuration
  - Advanced Sink Configuration

4. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.

![image-20251009-084735.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/image-20251009-084735.png?api=v2)

5. Click “**Next**“ button :next: and go back to the Data Catalog page :catalog:.

---

## **Input Query Command (SQL) Method**

1. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

![databricks (sql commands).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/databricks%20(sql%20commands).png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).
- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![RDBMD upload method.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/RDBMD%20upload%20method.png?api=v2)

- **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
- **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

  - **Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type and Upload Method.

Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type.

![upload file custom service setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207143/upload%20file%20custom%20service%20setting.png?api=v2)

2. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

---

## Import Asset (JSON file and Internal Spark)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2229207161/Import+Asset+JSON+file+and+Internal+Spark |
| **Last modified** | 2025-02-20T07:55:34.878Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data |

This option is used when there is migration updated or need to integrate the data from your collaboration’s device to your device, which support the JSON file that export from the data catalog via Blendata Enterprise. The JSON file contains of data structure/schema only. Furthermore, for advanced analytics where data is stored in the Spark Meta-store, our platform is able to import those Spark Meta-store files into the Data Catalog.

# How to import the JSON file

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Assets (JSON file and Internal Spark)**“ menu and select “**Upload JSON File**“ to import the JSON file into the platform.
3. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next:.
4. Preview the data asset that the user crated.
5. Go to “**Data Catalog”** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

---

# How to import the Internal Spark

1. Click “**Import Assets (JSON file and Internal Spark)**“ menu.
2. Select “**Import table from Spark Internal Meta-store**“ option to import the internal Spark into the platform.
3. Click “**Next**” :next:.
4. Review the Imported tables summary
5. Go to “**Data Catalog”** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset from Internal Spark.

![image-20240220-035114.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2229207161/image-20240220-035114.png?api=v2)

Users cannot select some Spark Meta-store files. The platform will import all Spark Meta-store files into Data Catalog page.

---

## ORC

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2230353991/ORC |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:01:32.046Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > Upload File |

**Optimized Row Columnar (ORC)** is another columnar storage format, which used for storing and processing large volumes of data, particularly in the context of big data frameworks.

Here is the sample data:

```
ID:      [1, 2, 3]
Name:    ["John Doe", "Jane Smith", "Bob Johnson"]
Age:     [30, 28, 35]
Salary:  [60000, 55000, 70000]
```

## How to import the ORC file

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Dataset Internal Table**“ menu to import the data into the platform.
3. Select “**Upload File**“ option.
4. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next: .
5. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230353991/ORC.png?api=v2)

- **Add import default information field**: Automated add the timestamp field into the data asset.

6. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
7. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
8. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230353991/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230353991/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
9. Preview your data table.
10. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

    - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), and Service.

![upload file output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230353991/upload%20file%20output%20setting.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type.

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230353991/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230353991/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230353991/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type.

![upload file custom service setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230353991/upload%20file%20custom%20service%20setting.png?api=v2)

## JSON

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2230386948/JSON |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:00:09.346Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > Upload File |

**JavaScript Object Notation(JSON)** is a text format, which typically contains data organized in key-value pairs and arrays, where keys are strings and values can be strings, numbers, booleans, null, objects, or other nested JSON structures. Here's a simple example:

```
{
  "name": "Smith Lee",
  "age": 30,
  "occupation": "Teacher",
  "city": "Bangkok",
}
```

- `"name"`, `"age"`, `"occupation"`, and `"city"` are **Key**.
- `"Smith Lee"`, `30`, `"Teacher"`, `"Bangkok"` are **Value**.

## How to import the JSON file

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Dataset Internal Table**“ menu to import the data into the platform.
3. Select “**Upload File**“ option.
4. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next: .
5. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386948/JSON.png?api=v2)

- **Multiline options**: To separate the values in the same line and create the new line.
- **Add import default information field**: Automated add the timestamp field into the data asset.

6. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
7. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
8. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386948/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386948/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
9. Preview your data table.
10. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

    - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), and Service.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.

  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

## Text

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2230386968/Text |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:00:21.875Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > Upload File |

**Text file** is a plain text file, unstructured data file that requires data transformation before utilization in data processing. A common instance of this file type is a log file, where identifying data patterns is crucial for importing it into a platform. Users can use SQL queries with regular expressions (regex) during the import step or utilize SQL queries within the Data Exploration feature to extract and categorize data into fields.

## How to import the CSV file

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Dataset Internal Table**“ menu to import the data into the platform.
3. Select “**Upload File**“ option.
4. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next: .
5. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386968/text.png?api=v2)

- **Add import default information field**: Automated add the timestamp field into the data asset.

6. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
7. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
8. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386968/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386968/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type.

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386968/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386968/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386968/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type.

![upload file custom service setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230386968/upload%20file%20custom%20service%20setting.png?api=v2)

11. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

## XML

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2230517803/XML |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:00:55.243Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > Upload File |

**Extensible Markup Language (XML)** is text-based document. It uses tags to define elements, their hierarchical relationships, and also represent the complex data structures.

Here is the sample data:

```
<breakfast_menu>
	<food>
		<name>Belgian Waffles</name>
		<price>$5.95</price>
		<description>Two of our famous Belgian Waffles with plenty of real maple syrup</description>
		<calories>650</calories>
	</food>
	<food>
		<name>Strawberry Belgian Waffles</name>
		<price>$7.95</price>
		<description>Light Belgian waffles covered with strawberries and whipped cream</description>
		<calories>900</calories>
	</food>
	<food>
		<name>Berry-Berry Belgian Waffles</name>
		<price>$8.95</price>
		<description>Light Belgian waffles covered with an assortment of fresh berries and whipped cream</description>
		<calories>900</calories>
	</food>
	<food>
		<name>French Toast</name>
		<price>$4.50</price>
		<description>Thick slices made from our homemade sourdough bread</description>
		<calories>600</calories>
	</food>
	<food>
		<name>Homestyle Breakfast</name>
		<price>$6.95</price>
		<description>Two eggs, bacon or sausage, toast, and our ever-popular hash browns</description>
		<calories>950</calories>
	</food>
</breakfast_menu>
```

### XML Components:

- `<breakfast_menu>` is the **root element**; *food, name, price, description, and calories* are other element names.
- `<name>`, `<price>`, `<description>`, and `<calories>` are nested elements.
- `Belgian Waffles` or `$5.95` are the content.

## How to import the XML file

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Dataset Internal Table**“ menu to import the data into the platform.
3. Select “**Upload File**“ option.
4. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next: .
5. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230517803/XML.png?api=v2)

- **Row Tag**: Indicate the element of your data, which are defined using tags enclosed in angle brackets < >
- **Add import default information field**: Automated add the timestamp field into the data asset.

6. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
7. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
8. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230517803/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230517803/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
9. Preview your data table.
10. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

    - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), and Service.

![upload file output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230517803/upload%20file%20output%20setting.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type.

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230517803/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230517803/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230517803/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type.

![upload file custom service setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230517803/upload%20file%20custom%20service%20setting.png?api=v2)

11. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

## Shape file

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2230517876/Shape+file |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:01:47.397Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > Upload File |

**Shape file** is a widely used geospatial vector data format in Geographic Information System (GIS) software, developed by Esri. It stores both geometric data (points, lines, or polygons) and attribute data (information about geographic features) in a set of related files. Shapefiles could represent more complex geometries (lines, polygons) and contain a larger set of attributes for each geographic feature. These files are widely used for spatial analysis, mapping, and visualization in GIS applications.

A typical set of files associated with a Shapefile includes:

1. **.shp:** The main file that contains the geometric data.
2. **.shx:** An index file that enables faster access to the geometric data.
3. **.dbf:** A dBASE table file that stores attribute data associated with the geographic features.
4. **.prj:** A file that contains information about the coordinate system and projection used.

Sample data might look like this:

- **.shp (Geometric Data):**

  - Point 1: Latitude 40.7128, Longitude -74.0060
  - Point 2: Latitude 34.0522, Longitude -118.2437
  - Point 3: Latitude 41.8781, Longitude -87.6298
- **dbf (Attribute Data):**

```
+----+------------+------------+
| ID | Name       | Type       |
+----+------------+------------+
| 1  | Central Park| Park       |
| 2  | Hollywood   | Entertainment|
| 3  | Millennium Park | Park    |
+----+------------+------------+
```

With Blendata Enterprise version 4.6.0, users no longer need to zip the folder, and the names of the zip file and shape files can be different.

## How to import the shape file

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Dataset Internal Table**“ menu to import the data into the platform.
3. Select “**Upload File**“ option.
4. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next: .
5. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230517876/shape.png?api=v2)

- **Add import default information field**: Automated add the timestamp field into the data asset.

6. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
7. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
8. 9. Preview your data table.
10. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

## CSV

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2230648866/CSV |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T01:59:57.802Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > Upload File |

**Comma-separated values (CSV)** is a file format for storing tabular data with values separated by commas by default. Each line in a CSV file typically represents a single data record, and records have the same number of fields. Commas separate these fields, and if a field contains a comma, it is enclosed in quotation marks to maintain data integrity.

## How to import the CSV file

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Dataset Internal Table**“ menu to import the data into the platform.
3. Select “**Upload File**“ option.
4. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next: .
5. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648866/I-CSV.png?api=v2)

- **Header options**: If your file consists of the header row, please activate this options.
- **Detect type options**: Automatically detect the data type option.
- **Delimiter**: Identify symbol of the delimiter of your source file. To rearrange your data into the column style. (The default of Delimiter is comma.)
- **Quote**: To indicate the symbol, which is the starting of the value. (The default symbol of CVS format is double quote.)
- **Escape**: To indicate the symbol of value field ending location. (The default symbol of CVS format is double quote.)
- **Multiline options**: To separate the values in the same line and create the new line.
- **Add import default information field**: Automated add the timestamp field into the data asset.

6. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
7. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
8. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648866/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648866/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
9. Preview your data table.
10. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

    - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), and Service.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

## Excel

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2230648939/Excel |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:01:10.656Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > Upload File |

**Excel file** is a spreadsheet file created and managed using Microsoft Excel. The files can include multiple sheets, each with rows and columns of data, formulas, charts, and other elements.

Excel file limitation:

- Only one sheet can be imported at a time by specifying the sheet name along with the exact row and column coordinates in the '**Data Address**'.
- Do not use formulas in any cells.
- Do not merge any cells.
- Comments and notes will not be displayed on the platform.
- Hyperlinks will be imported as string type.
- A cell containing only a space is not considered null.

**Recommend cleaning your data before importing.**

## How to import the excel file

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Dataset Internal Table**“ menu to import the data into the platform.
3. Select “**Upload File**“ option.
4. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next: .
5. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648939/excel.png?api=v2)

- **Header options**: If your file consists of the header row, please activate this options.
- **Data Address(Optional)**: Recommend to indicate the data address from the excel file.
- **Add import default information field**: Automated add the timestamp field into the data asset.

6. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
7. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
8. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648939/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648939/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
9. Preview your data table.
10. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

    - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), and Service.

![upload file output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648939/upload%20file%20output%20setting.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type.

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648939/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648939/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648939/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type.

![upload file custom service setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230648939/upload%20file%20custom%20service%20setting.png?api=v2)

11. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

---

## Parquet

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2230911056/Parquet |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:00:37.213Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > Upload File |

**Parquet file** is an open source and columnar storage file format optimized for use with big data processing frameworks. It is designed to be highly efficient for both storage and processing. Parquet files are particularly well-suited for use with data processing engines like Apache Spark, Apache Hive, Apache Impala, and others.column style.

The data would be stored in a columnar format as per below example:

```
Name: [Smith, Bob, Janny]
Age:  [25, 30, 22]
City: [BKK, CNX, BKK]
```

## How to import the Parquet file

1. Click **Import Data** menu :import\_ic:
2. Click “**Import Dataset Internal Table**“ menu to import the data into the platform.
3. Select “**Upload File**“ option.
4. Click “**Open File Browser**“ button :select\_file: to choose your data file (upload one file at a time) and click “**Next**” :next: .
5. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230911056/parquet.png?api=v2)

- **Add import default information field**: Automated add the timestamp field into the data asset.

6. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
7. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
8. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230911056/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230911056/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
9. Preview your data table.
10. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

    - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), and Service.

![upload file output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2230911056/upload%20file%20output%20setting.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type.
