## Amazon S3

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2232811567/Amazon+S3 |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T06:02:21.967Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > File Path |

1. Fill in the internal file path and click “**Next**“.

![amazonS3.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/amazonS3.png?api=v2)

Users can use the wildcard character (\*) for all file types except Excel files, which require the full filename to be specified.

2. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

For date type, users need to specify the source data's date format for conversion to a Spark date. If users want both date and time values, they should select the timestamp type.

3. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
4. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
5. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
6. Preview your data table.
7. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

![s3 file path output options.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/s3%20file%20path%20output%20options.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![s3 upload method.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/s3%20upload%20method.png?api=v2)

- **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
- **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

  - **Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.
- **Stream Upload**: Able to upload the data with Stream service.

**Excessive data storage warning!**

Table source type 'Internal table' allows Stream upload, but it may lead to  
excessive data storage. We recommend using Time Series and **switching to 'Internal State table'**

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type and Upload method.

Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)![internal (stream).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/internal%20(stream).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)![state (stream).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/state%20(stream).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **Stream Service**: Once you select upload method as Stream, this stream service will enable automatically. Users can setup the stream service setting via **Change Customization**.

  - **Interval**: Fill in the stream interval. (Second unit)
  - **Number of File Output**: Fill in your number of file.

![stream (internal table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/stream%20(internal%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream (state table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/stream%20(state%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/stream%20setting.png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type and upload method.

![custom state setting2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232811567/custom%20state%20setting2.png?api=v2)

8. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)![state (stream).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/state%20(stream).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **Stream Service**: Once you select upload method as Stream, this stream service will enable automatically. Users can setup the stream service setting via **Change Customization**.

  - **Interval**: Fill in the stream interval. (Second unit)
  - **Number of File Output**: Fill in your number of file.

![stream (internal table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/stream%20(internal%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream (state table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/stream%20(state%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/stream%20setting.png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type and upload method.

![custom state setting2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232844409/custom%20state%20setting2.png?api=v2)

8. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

## Google Cloud Storage

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2232975418/Google+Cloud+Storage |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:02:56.306Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > File Path |

1. Fill in the internal file path and click “**Next**“.

![GG cloud storage.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/GG%20cloud%20storage.png?api=v2)

Users can use the wildcard character (\*) for all file types except Excel files, which require the full filename to be specified.

2. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

For date type, users need to specify the source data's date format for conversion to a Spark date. If users want both date and time values, they should select the timestamp type.

3. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
4. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
5. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
6. Preview your data table.
7. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

![file path output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/file%20path%20output%20setting.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![file path output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/file%20path%20output%20setting.png?api=v2)

- **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
- **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

  - **Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.
- **Stream Upload**: Able to upload the data with Stream service.

**Excessive data storage warning!**

Table source type 'Internal table' allows Stream upload, but it may lead to  
excessive data storage. We recommend using Time Series and **switching to 'Internal State table'**

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type and Upload method.

Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)![internal (stream).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/internal%20(stream).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)![state (stream).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/state%20(stream).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **Stream Service**: Once you select upload method as Stream, this stream service will enable automatically. Users can setup the stream service setting via **Change Customization**.

  - **Interval**: Fill in the stream interval. (Second unit)
  - **Number of File Output**: Fill in your number of file.

![stream (internal table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/stream%20(internal%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream (state table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/stream%20(state%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/stream%20setting.png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type and upload method.

![custom state setting2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2232975418/custom%20state%20setting2.png?api=v2)

8. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

## Connect to Blendata with JDBC/ODBC

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2233303063/Connect+to+Blendata+with+JDBC+ODBC |
| **Last modified** | 2025-08-13T03:19:48.206Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Integration |

# Distributed SQL Engine

Spark SQL can also act as a distributed query engine using its JDBC/ODBC or command-line interface. In this mode, end-users or applications can interact with Spark SQL directly to run SQL queries, without the need to write any code.

## Running the Thrift JDBC/ODBC server

The thrift JDBC/ODBC server implemented here corresponds to the `HiveServer2` in built-in Hive. You can test the JDBC server with the beeline script that comes with either Spark or compatible Hive.

## Ex. Connect to the JDBC/ODBC server in beeline with:

```
beeline> !connect jdbc:hive2://localhost:9999
```

Beeline will ask you for a username and password. In non-secure mode, simply enter the username on your machine and a blank password. For secure mode, please follow the instructions given in the [beeline documentation](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/Hive/HiveServer2+Clients).

## Ex. Connect to the Power BI

Power BI is command-line interface to connect with our data asset from the Blendata Enterprise and convert our data asset to visualization dashboard.

## Ex. Connect to the DBeaver with HIVE JDBC

DBeaver is open source, which support various of database types including Blendata Enterprise platform. The DBeaver requires Hive JDBC connection to connect with our Blendata asset. The user need to install the HIVE JDBC Library and put the username and password.

Credit By: <https://spark.apache.org/docs/3.5.3/>

---

## Tableau Desktop Connecting to Blendata Enterprise

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2233303082/Tableau+Desktop+Connecting+to+Blendata+Enterprise |
| **Last modified** | 2025-02-24T07:49:49.548Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Integration > Connect to Blendata with JDBC/ODBC |

# **Install driver : Spark SQL driver**

1. Install spark driver connector on the system that tableau is running.   
   Recommend Version: SimbaSpark-64-bit  
   Link to Download: <https://shorturl.at/prHP4>
2. In case installation is successful, spark driver connector will be displayed in ODBC Administrator (64-bit) window

   - Type “**ODBC**”  in Start Menu
   - Go to “**System DSN**”, Simba Spark ODBC Driver will be shown

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303082/24bGR7EioOHTRjGOSuWNJDFxNQcTejMLB-LE9VeiwxlnVxrJgWSrqz-4edg_sNwTs86-ZGRJYRUZP-PX8o5-vW-GXjiE7eyZJbYAny3V-8MZBX8fxVKfNCkGTMCJyeaN4TfTHTA1NvfnDjsSMf059iA?api=v2)

3. Click “**Add**“ to setup the spark driver as per below details.

![odbc.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303082/odbc.png?api=v2)

- **Host(s)**: Server name or IP that Blendata spark core engine is running.
- **Port**: Blendata spark core engine port (default 9999)
- **Username**: The username for authentication.
- **Password**: The password.

4. Click “**Test**“ to test connection between Tableau desktop and Blendata spark thrift server and click “**OK**“.

# **Tableau Desktop add a new datasource**

### Step by Step

1. Select “**Add Connections (เชื่อมต่อกับข้อมูล)**”

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303082/dbqztTW3P0lJ-rXCXoAfZ3S21KORBMb84fMxjI0Fpf5PWl_Sla9qaA1nijsH48WNpZW3qm_E9M1HhhRZJ8meIiiktLEYWrup5F4sC7ElOZpRSFydeUbZIQdHVkLsh5JOrWQ8Z3Mq2yVJ00nhiuOO4c4?api=v2)

2. Type and Select “**Spark SQL**”

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303082/JwxQdtFY3Mj_tqWZSYIdHLLuGON4A6dHqD7NgjoHLG03Km7EzQvB1AT1DhyPPZZ2BpmM9Wkfzxz1EzU-gejtUHu691Mp6zrCHEhs48tujpTpKU8uQX30Q8FUIxWw8lNAuRczhbUvhwDx616AFDNeYso?api=v2)

3. Fill in required fields in Spark SQL connector input and select “**เข้าสู่ระบบ (Login)**”

   - **Connector (การเชื่อมต่อ)**: SparkThriftServer (Spark version 1.1 up)
   - **Serverเซิฟเวอร์ (เซิฟเวอร์)**: Host IP Address (Example 1.2.3.4)
   - **Port (พอร์ต)**: Host Port Number (Example 4321)
   - **Authenticationการ(การตรวจสอบสิทธิ์)**: The username and password.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303082/qfM6MxJY1TxFGGfuJ6HiRJwygYHFec4GxdaFVSxCsaZ6Vw9hInOZrJhMEHPRUYIcxF5CuGBdocdBm7C2rex0Tj-_nbfjn_VlspjrubCW-3xkioNH9Savw_aqjZBM17uD9m_KgvN5v7A0cuQG_1L37kQ?api=v2)

4. After complete fill in required fields, Tableau will added data source (in form of table) to the system which user can type in to find table.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303082/loJ7pBIQmW-Y6q88PQcU6FT-Bf1N2eXXzzVOfYyQnF5PIx3Tbed6roQXY2C_eR2RiA0IS-blBfcVyNVXAhDi0R4dh9ns5IXbrUBlnuWAyDkqSAmkJibyI59qCmtEsNzYFpHfWO-WEH_pH56FmBxlSSM?api=v2)

5. User is able to use “**New Custom SQL (SQL แบบปรับแต่งใหม่)**” function to filter data.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303082/u-ufoHtDlQ2TpflT5kbif1kmUb95ZLSOASht98PP00UteMazhcMLbY--shO4ulUEwSns-jVar5irt4WcelvKYj8OV5owxhWWSLk5Kqlzigp2KADQ_YCL_QqPyUxA-39WleYEdcnbJDZtOVHVel3-iSw?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303082/YmzD23Gk4zz_wS03DKat-7DlwYKbSwr_0yHQyNN4E8D84OYeWamL5ZVObaaqZJlQTnpwpyyVqTn_m32pM3SAjcy4JfYIDF4wW34YNU52dlk7YwO91nWqll9jeJ_6HDaawW6veyX0DzeNNUqwKqos7p4?api=v2)

6. System will then display data based on filtering condition.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303082/bTj4ktZlnQUtOxSjGzlnT8PlvvVb9VDnKkAWr0_6cFR8BtfnUZT5acywQdT0JtL7CU_3zaVi4qJ4kG-63f5HHDhmv5LNwjx2HRAZ6rp_BceP0FPOkfda24JIaDweHilBbmRkBaAfPc1dQNYOrjMEOxU?api=v2)

## Power BI Connecting to Blendata Enterprise

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2233303146/Power+BI+Connecting+to+Blendata+Enterprise |
| **Last modified** | 2025-02-24T07:49:51.459Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Integration > Connect to Blendata with JDBC/ODBC |

## (blue star) Pre-requirement

Install the Power BI program from [Microsoft Download Centre](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=58494).

The Power BI program is not compatible with the Blendata Enterprise platform, if the user installs the Microsoft PowerBI from Microsoft Store or compact files with the Microsoft Office set. The user need to install the Microsoft Power BI from Microsoft Download Centre(above link) only.

## (blue star) How to connect with the Power BI

1. Open the Power BI program.
2. To create the connection, Click “**Get Data**“ menu > ”**More…**”
3. Once the pop-up menu show, select the driver by clicking at “**Other**“ > click “**Spark**” and click “**Connect**” button.
4. Fill in the Spark connection.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303146/spark.png?api=v2)

**a. Server Details**:

**- Zeus ip address**: depend on configuration

- **Zeus jdbc/odbc port**: default 9999

**b. Protocol**: select standard option.

**c. Batch (optional)**: recommend to specific number of each batch.

5. Select the data asset that you need to create the connection and click “**Load**“ button :load: .

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303146/powerBI1.png?api=v2)

Recommend to analyze data on Blendata Enterprise first and load the analyzed data asset into the PowerBI.

6. Click :connect: icon on the left menu to check the complete data asset.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303146/powerBI3.png?api=v2)

## Power BI Connecting to Blendata Enterprise via Simba Spark ODBC

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2233303187/Power+BI+Connecting+to+Blendata+Enterprise+via+Simba+Spark+ODBC |
| **Last modified** | 2026-03-11T04:41:15.215Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Integration > Connect to Blendata with JDBC/ODBC |

## (blue star)**Pre-requirement**

**Install the Power BI Program**

1. Install Power BI program from [Microsoft Download Centre](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=58494).

2. Select “**PBIDesktopSetup\_x64.exe**” from the list and click “**Download**” button.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303187/AD_4nXfBVK3MOXS1mD4girQ6inhi7q8bjTQ3e7FMjX5QbGqJURYRFNGrZIAAB9bvgxvjqjSZ_Fx-T6pfS7ahSy0BFEAqjyWGJxM3FeWQ_erFTe8vRONnpirOJ5Cqlm5XwSZt92CMD7ZTVxmBpYGsVlYEVFNYbgiQ%3Fkey=FgOljc2ufdCeACoSJu7dmw?api=v2)

**Install Simba Spark ODBC Driver**

1. Download and install Simba Spark ODBC Driver [SimbaSparkODBC-2.10.0.1002-Windows-64bit.zip](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QZuChMJ9CwfMV0J7hw9tgO8XRLV6kwin/view?usp=sharing)

2. Configure Simba Spark ODBC Driver by selecting ODBC Data Sources (64-bits) then select “**System DSN**” and select “**Configure**”.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303187/AD_4nXeXamMkr0nmYYCV_yk0-j1hqqqkx8MuRVEWONApvma2Ow6bvtL6CLSSWDLAdX_EQaKTeG7U1zkCkH4usdXzZYnQ_VOiQfdtsRaxS8Mgsmx4q5AkHcijjKyFWtLqznva9a9TVdCZUjQhCQcnu6v8DBdR21NT%3Fkey=FgOljc2ufdCeACoSJu7dmw?api=v2)

3. Fill in information in the configuration window.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303187/AD_4nXcwfaCxaVCzISoqK1ItG7w-6ebKRkJ7fFEnoNwLVDs_KLRM_FLsIDK53FatUg7Zt5UXFQ8mh4dUf7xx5TnPD8CosfDxhS0niKqeqosV6HDheCpH-cLkwPPZXix1imNkU5TSDydRvYQYHi0DOEZhCIHqgGY%3Fkey=t5oPtADI32a9F_qsSyfX-Q?api=v2)

4. Test Connection and select “**OK**”. (The driver version depends on your installed package.)

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303187/AD_4nXd90VTrL4lJxgllgqv_HI3kSCIGz69qrkDCi9WeYK2HPAHJ8Xm_8etVQ8RwGykqz_eNHQ9c_oggjjevqWcwcIpEJTUjNmb5Rdm8NBeuckNbZrWg6jt_VSzmmsOa3ZlqSGQ2LtrC18xwaYEAZuz4SnToW1r2%3Fkey=t5oPtADI32a9F_qsSyfX-Q?api=v2)

## (blue star) How to connect with the Power BI

1. Open the Power BI Program.

2. To create the connection, Click the “**Get Data**“ menu and select ”**More…**” option.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303187/AD_4nXfL8zUxTsDzExVWBjPDFcmUp6Z_3PiZL08AcVsKYz-0VS8Q3qsJB4mfIAzcFkLDsQ6RRevihBkRDmYGUIXYGl9bKkmx2s9ngTBbtgyXhIptkM8nQqTAq_H59mOyr_qQOor6lO-yenbb_677UMMbKwbAaS4%3Fkey=FgOljc2ufdCeACoSJu7dmw?api=v2)

3. Once the pop-up menu is displayed, select the driver by clicking at “**Other**“ then click “**ODBC**” and click the “**Connect**” button.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303187/AD_4nXehqe0hE0ZEw0PQKvgTwDXQtMeEIlJWRQCc8qrijvIvwKhLdd1iYbPrqvY0dg-uUFoyLj1wmrFfRwbhauaqPEM8wfiQuEOlxPR4nqd0X05jhfkYHpFFSR5stRcXK4lRTvbaNCFyperNVb53grMD8vFV6C73%3Fkey=FgOljc2ufdCeACoSJu7dmw?api=v2)

4. Select “**Simba Spark**” from list of Data source name (DSN).

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303187/AD_4nXdgMAbItl7Z2FuuLgZOzYm_uua4tmw-qBY6y34xN89I6GOgXZt3OivPA_ZAkDqrKFzECJbK_igyRmU2YCwfZIdujO0KDThNralg3wc8CpXJtL0M6HjX5hyCiJDCTckeM4CKjVXNu72GVvagm2q_bc-TLBnC%3Fkey=FgOljc2ufdCeACoSJu7dmw?api=v2)

5. Fill in the SQL Statement and select “**OK**”.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303187/AD_4nXdrU7OOf-V-5X2jGWE7pJxubPO4gqIn-Kj_nvnLONsyZzBRo-kIONKC2-qEiJpNVEmVPTOIuq59m94-GeEPwtNqzcI8xneyf0YANP4EecJLWreU62sSOAUUN8RB_EtVnfQhlrJq2J9wvQIPxjU-sMVGJ6iB%3Fkey=FgOljc2ufdCeACoSJu7dmw?api=v2)

6. Preview table is displayed, select “**Load**” to import data to Power BI.

## DBeaver Connecting to Blendata Enterprise with Hive JDBC

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2233303263/DBeaver+Connecting+to+Blendata+Enterprise+with+Hive+JDBC |
| **Last modified** | 2025-02-24T07:49:54.637Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Integration > Connect to Blendata with JDBC/ODBC |

## (blue star) Pre-requirement

1. Install the DBeaver program from <https://dbeaver.io/download/>.
2. HIVE JDBC Library (version 2.3.9)

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303263/hive.png?api=v2)

## (blue star) How to connect with the DBeaver

1. Open the DBeaver program.
2. Add the HIVE JDBC Library.

   Edit Driver Setting > Add Folder > Select Library Folder > Find Driver Class

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303263/DBeaver.png?api=v2)

3. Configure the Connection and Test the Connection.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233303263/DBeaver2.png?api=v2)

4. Create the script with the SQL quires by click “**SQL**“ button :SQL: and click “**Run**” to preview the data.

---

## Hadoop HDFS

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2233532417/Hadoop+HDFS |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:03:13.451Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > File Path |

1. Fill in the internal file path and click “**Next**“.

![hadoop.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/hadoop.png?api=v2)

Users can use the wildcard character (\*) for all file types except Excel files, which require the full filename to be specified.

2. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

For date type, users need to specify the source data's date format for conversion to a Spark date. If users want both date and time values, they should select the timestamp type.

3. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
4. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
5. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
6. Preview your data table.
7. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

![file path output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/file%20path%20output%20setting.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![file path output setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/file%20path%20output%20setting.png?api=v2)

- **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
- **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

  - **Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.
- **Stream Upload**: Able to upload the data with Stream service.

**Excessive data storage warning!**

Table source type 'Internal table' allows Stream upload, but it may lead to  
excessive data storage. We recommend using Time Series and **switching to 'Internal State table'**

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type and Upload method.

Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)![internal (stream).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/internal%20(stream).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)![state (stream).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/state%20(stream).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **Stream Service**: Once you select upload method as Stream, this stream service will enable automatically. Users can setup the stream service setting via **Change Customization**.

  - **Interval**: Fill in the stream interval. (Second unit)
  - **Number of File Output**: Fill in your number of file.

![stream (internal table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/stream%20(internal%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream (state table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/stream%20(state%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/stream%20setting.png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type and upload method.

![custom state setting2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233532417/custom%20state%20setting2.png?api=v2)

8. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

---

## MongoDB

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2233598026/MongoDB |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:03:42.873Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > No SQL |

**MongoDB** is document-oriented database, storing data in flexible, JSON-like BSON (Binary JSON) documents. (Supported version 3.6, 4.0, 4.2, 4.4, and 5.0) You can follow the below instruction to connect with your MongoDB.

### **Database Setting:**

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233598026/nosql-mongo2.png?api=v2)

1. Fill in the database connection details as per below:

- **Host:** the database host number.
- **Port:** the port number for database connection. (Default is 27071.)
- **Database:** the database table name.
- **Authentication:** if the database requires the password, the user can enable this option to fill in the username and password.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Username**: authorized username of this database server.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Password**: password of this database server.

2. Click “**Connect Database**“ button to connect with the database server.
3. Select the data table and click setting icon :setting: to setup your table information.

You can select multiple tables for the data importing.

![NoSQL.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233598026/NoSQL.png?api=v2)

4. Select an upload method, Zeus path, and a data schema.

   a. Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

   ![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.

   ![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

**Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Time setting**: Can select by minutes, hourly, Daily or using cron expression.

**Execute immediately**: Can select to execute this time setting immediately or as per setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2233598026/rdbms%20-%20batch.png?api=v2)

b. Select the **Zeus path**, which is a storage path. (The platform set default Zeus as an initial setup)

c. Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

5. Fill in the database information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), and [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning). Then click “**Next**“ button :next:.

**Time Domain** : Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Delta table** : To create the Delta table asset.

- **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Service Enabled**: To enable Delta services including Delta Compaction Job and Vacuum Job.

**Encrypt Data** : To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)

6. Go back to the Data Catalog page :catalog: .

---

## Cassandra

| Field | Value |
|---|---|

**Apache Cassandra** is an open-source NoSQL database system and a wide-column store database, organizing data into tables with rows identified by a unique key and columns that can vary from row to row. (Supported version 2.1 and 2.2) You can connect with the Cassandra database by following the instruction below.

### **Database Setting:**

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2234023970/nosql-cassandra.png?api=v2)

1. Fill in the database connection details as per below:

- **Host**: the database host number.
- **Port**: the port number for database connection.
- **Keyspace**: the database table name.
- **Authentication**: if the database requires the password, the user can enable this option to fill in the username and password.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Username**: authorized username of this database server.

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Password**: password of this database server.

2. Click “**Connect Database**“ button to connect with the database server.
3. Select the data table and click setting icon :setting: to setup your table information.

You can select multiple tables for the data importing.

4. Select an upload method, Zeus path, and a data schema.

   a. Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

   ![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.

   ![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

**Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.

**Time setting**: Can select by minutes, hourly, Daily or using cron expression.

**Execute immediately**: Can select to execute this time setting immediately or as per setting.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2234023970/rdbms%20-%20batch.png?api=v2)

b. Select the **Zeus path**, which is a storage path. (The platform set default Zeus as an initial setup)

c. Select an accessible schema for your data asset.

5. Fill in the database information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), and [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning). Then click “**Next**“ button :next:.

**Time Domain** : Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Delta table** : To create the Delta table asset.

- **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Service Enabled**: To enable Delta services including Delta Compaction Job and Vacuum Job.

**Encrypt Data** : To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)

6. Go back to the Data Catalog page :catalog: .

## Architecture Component overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323172/Architecture+Component+overview |
| **Last modified** | 2025-06-20T02:20:33.057Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > General References |

![BDE Architecture.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255323172/BDE%20Architecture.png?api=v2)

**Blendata** is an End-to-End big data analytics platform. The platform composes of three main components; Hera module, Cerberus module, and Zeus module. From the client, Hera provides both REST API service and a user interface (web-based) to access Blendata service. While processing user verification, Hera contacts Cerberus module for authentication and authorization. Also, Hera acts as an API gateway to call Zeus service for data processing. The backend of Zeus module utilizes Spark Engine to manage big data and is compatible with myriad types of data storage including File system, HDFS, Object storage. Moreover, the clients can access Spark table data via JDBC/ODBC driver. The additional modules is Notification Server, which provides a way to receive messages and sends messages to multiple outbound eg. email, WebSocket(EROS), shell script, web service, etc. for notification.

## Modules Description

- **Cerberus:** Cerberus is a module for security management including authentication, authorization, and integration with third-party authentication (LDAP).
- **Hera:** Web API Service and License Control. Provides two ways REST API and Client Web HTML(EROS), use for data integration, data management, data processing, and data utilization. Including data protection (assets) and be able to share and export.
- **Eros (On Hera Server):** User Interface Web Application Client. One web and no coding to empower users to use data integration, data management, data processing, and data utilization. Including protecting assets and being able to share and export.
- **Zeus (Spark Application[Driver]):** Web API to Manage and Execute Spark Jobs, which is a module designed for processing data and computing services e.g. querying, streaming, etc. As it is a Spark application, the module can use the performance of the Spark Optimization engine to process big data.
- **Notebook (Zeppelin):** REST APIs for interaction and remote activation of zeppelin functionality.
- **Workflow (Airflow):** REST APIs for manage the state, scheduling, and monitoring batch-oriented workflows.
- **Spark Standalone Cluster:** Spark Application and Resource Management.
- **Notification Server:** Web API Server Manage inbound and outbound.
- **Brown Cow (Agent):** Agent Monitor Server and Control Modules.
- **Metastore Database:** stores metadata about your data files, such as their location, format, and column names and , etc.
- **Redis:** Sync Hera cache data for HA.
- **Zookeeper:** Sync state spark master for HA.

---

## Reference Architecture

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323244/Reference+Architecture |
| **Last modified** | 2025-03-26T09:08:27.390Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > General References |

This reference architecture defines a platform framework, Source, Ingest, Process, Manage, and Utilization.

![Reference Achitecture.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255323244/Reference%20Achitecture.png?api=v2)

- **Source**:

Connect and collect data from all sources including flat files (CSV, JSON, Plain text, Parquet, etc.), structure and unstructured databases (Oracle, MS SQL, MySQL, etc.), Cloud and Stream through built-in connectors without coding.

- **Ingest:**

  - **Dynamic data ingestion** - Automated ingest data from flat files, log, databases, cloud storage via a bunch of built in connectors with the following methods

    - Scheduled replication (Batch, Stream)
    - Data virtualization
    - Changed data capture (CDC)
  - **Transform data while doing ingestion (ETL)** - Data transformations on-the-fly with Spark SQL, providing a rich set of transformational functions with a big volume of data.
  - **Automated schema discovery** - whether it’s file, database, or streaming through kafka. Our engine will try to discover its characteristics and auto-create the schema for every ingests table, users can also modify it via GUI. also modify it via GUI.
- **Process**:

  - **SQL Analytics** - Prepare, transform, correlate, aggregate, and analyze data. Data exploration UI allows data analysts or data engineers to write a query in familiar syntax, ANSI SQL + Spark SQL functions and let them explore, manipulate, or schedule the job on their data.
  - **Data prep workbench** - With the same engine and capabilities as SQL analytics (Data exploration UI), but provide easier ways for non-technical users to low/no-code prepare their data sets in minutes.
  - **Notebook interface** - First class Data engineers and ML developers experience with notebook interface. Support all famous developing languages including Python, R, Scala, and SQL. (for version 4.1 and later)
  - **Scheduler + Chain job** - Scheduling all defined jobs with a built-in job scheduler or chaining the job together to streamline data from end to end.
- **Manage**:

  - **User & role-based access control** - Control all access rights by tailoring roles or users for every asset such as data, visualization, dashboard, and Blendata - enterprise functions.
  - **Data encryption with KMS integration support\*** - Encrypt your data at rest on table, or even column by your own created key or integrating with an external key management system (KMS) to synchronize the encryption ( customization required).
  - **Data lifecycle policy** - Manage data retention per table, apply stage of data by hot/warm/cold to define compression and storage type of data.
  - **Multi storage** - Mount big data storage to multiple target, enable data tiering to multiple storage devices or specify storage device per table.
  - **Advanced data management** - A bunch of advanced data management techniques.
- **Utilization**:

  - **Visualization & Dashboard** - Turn query results into meaningful visualizations by drag and drop interface with a wide choice of charts and quickly grouping them together into a dashboard. Filter and drill-down features create a big data application that can be shared with your colleagues.
  - **Notification** - SQL-based condition system that can push alert and data via web-console, e-mail, script, or calling API (customization required)
  - **API** - Provide REST API that is secured with OAuth2 to call any data with standard HTTP request. Or connect to our Spark ODBC/Hive2server JDBC to low-level integrate to your data *(\*Apply Blendata Security in ODBC/JDBC connection  such as Authentication, Authorization, and Row level security in v.4.2 or lastest)*
  - **Export**- BDE support exporting the processed data into various formats and a variety type of downstream whether it’s file, database, or cloud storage.

---

## Product Version Definition

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323266/Product+Version+Definition |
| **Last modified** | 2025-03-26T09:08:27.748Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > General References |

# **1.0.0**

Blendata product version consists of three digits representing the major, minor, and patch levels for each version, as detailed below:

- **Core or Major version**: The first digit represents a significant change that impacts the working process, such as changes to the technology engine version, core libraries, log storage, or the addition of new features or functions.
- **Minor version**: The second digit indicates improvements to features or functions. This includes adding minor features to the platform that do not affect the core process, such as additional data source imports, enhancements to the SQL editor, or improvements to log storage.
- **Patch version**: The last digit reflects bug fixes, issue resolutions, and minor improvements.

Blendata Enterprise, our platform utilizes a website user interface as the front-end and is compatible with the following browsers:

- Firefox version 120 or newer
- Chrome version 120 or newer

## Internal File Export: Download Exported File

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323315/Internal+File+Export+Download+Exported+File |
| **Last modified** | 2025-03-26T09:08:28.144Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Blendata Specification |

**Limitation**: File size <= 5GB  
**Recommendation**: For the big file size, should FTP to download file instead

| **Metrics** | **Calculation** |
| --- | --- |
| Estimate Time To Download (s) | (File size(bytes)\*8) / (Network speed \* 10^6) |
| Overhead Time (%) | ((Actual Time - Expected Time) \*100) / Expected Time |

Maximum download connection timeout is limited by the configuration in  
- Hera 'application.properties'

```
...
spring.mvc.async.request-timeout = 3600000
spring.mvc.async.timeout = 3600000
...
```

- Zeus 'application.properties'

```
...
spring.mvc.async.request-timeout = 3600000
spring.mvc.async.timeout = 3600000
...
```

If Time to Download (File Size/Network Speed) is longer than timeout configuration, the system will show error 'Chrome net::ERR\_INCOMPLETE\_CHUNKED\_ENCODING error'

## How to implement Blendata UDF

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323332/How+to+implement+Blendata+UDF |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-12T08:06:57.444Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Related Documents |

## Instructions

1. Import Zeus libs and extends class `DefaultUDF`
2. Build jar file and deploy at path [Zeus home path]/extra-jars
3. Stop/Start Zeus or call web services {{zeus\_url}}:{{zeus\_port}}/zeus/v1/private/extraJars/reloadAll
4. After Zeus load UDF, jars file will be moved to [Zeus home path]/jars and rename automatically (add suffix) if duplicated.

## Use Third-Party Library

1. The Custom UDF can import a dependency library and pack as an assembly jar to load the dependency class into the jar file. Then, deploy the package jar to Zeus for loading and calling this as per an example below.

   Note: If a dependency library is used for deployment but don’t need to load the dependency class, need to put this “**<scope>provided</scope>**” at dependency part on pom.xml to prevent the library crash.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255323332/d86bbaac-84a6-4c38-b7e9-92bd45a5b3ec%23media-blob-url=true&id=939dfde9-ebcf-4c99-a9e0-de2e280e02b7&contextId=11848&collection=?api=v2)

Plugin maven pack assembly

```
<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-assembly-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>3.0.0</version>
    <configuration>
        <finalName>${project.groupId}-${project.artifactId}-${project.version}</finalName>
        <descriptorRefs>
            <descriptorRef>jar-with-dependencies</descriptorRef>
        </descriptorRefs>
    </configuration>
    <executions>
        <execution>
            <id>generate-release-plugin</id>
            <phase>package</phase>
            <goals>
                <goal>single</goal>
            </goals>
        </execution>
    </executions>
</plugin>
```

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255323332/c16d29c6-0d51-4b79-b8f2-043b78d2089f%23media-blob-url=true&id=27fa281d-cad5-4ba7-ab85-6447dd492330&contextId=11848&collection=?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255323332/1feaf929-af44-4ad0-91bf-10d9ef3d5620%23media-blob-url=true&id=fd62a5b4-01da-4bad-810a-29e85153d926&contextId=11848&collection=?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255323332/7aff84b1-e778-4685-82e2-ca43096f2c95%23media-blob-url=true&id=56374bd8-7e0e-4803-9a7c-84939078475f&contextId=11848&collection=?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255323332/ac613da4-db7e-45ad-b34b-35467a35b265%23media-blob-url=true&id=5573296e-6084-4845-9366-d81e9a82e0c7&contextId=11848&collection=?api=v2)![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255323332/d7d51096-6092-4601-801c-f2c4df6a8d1c%23media-blob-url=true&id=22535816-0d60-4f1c-bb0a-ebeb41433c04&contextId=11848&collection=?api=v2)

## Hera Authentication

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323429/Hera+Authentication |
| **Last modified** | 2025-03-26T09:08:29.987Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > General References > Blendata API Document > Hera API (Data Management Services) |

First of all, when you need to call some service. You must authenticate for getting a token then add the `Authorization` to the HTTP request header with it.

## Instructions

Blendata step-by-step Authentication

1. Call HTTP Authentication with URL: https://{{hera\_url}}:{{hera\_port}}/auth/login
2. Copy access\_token from the response
3. Add `Authorization` to the HTTP request with the access\_token

Token has been generated by JWT. You can extract the authorizations from the token.

## API Document

**Authentication API**

HTTP Method : POST

{{hera\_url}}:{{hera\_port}}/auth/login

An endpoint to authenticate and get token for registered user.

## Request Params

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| username | login username |
| password | login password |
| tenantId | login tenant id |

**HEADERS**

```
Content-Type : application/json
```

**BODY**

```
{
    "user": {
        "username": "super_admin",
        "password": "password"
    },
    "tenantId": 1
}
```

**EXAMPLE RESPONSE**

```
{
  "oauth": {
    "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ0ZW5hbnRfaWQiOiIxIiwidXNlcl9uYW1lIjoidGhhbmlkYS50MSIsInNjb3BlIjpbInJlYWQiLCJ3cml0ZSJdLCJleHAiOjE1NjczMTA2OTksImF1dGhvcml0aWVzIjpbIkRldmVsb3BlciIsIkFkbWluaXN0cmF0b3IiXSwianRpIjoiYzBhMGIwYWMtYTQyNi00OGMzLTliMTgtZjAxYjRlZTdmYjM1IiwiY2xpZW50X2lkIjoiZ3RlbXBsYXItY2xpZW50In0.BcJZdHoRSdTnlaXNl0LWzqEQtFvCysgJNMyQrqJl-B8",
    "token_type": "bearer",
    "refresh_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..dY1dPD3UMjiUyZU32SvCZN7LSzlvishRMfPbPf9jdDY",
    "expires_in": "1799",
    "scope": "read write",
    "jti": "c0a0b0ac-a426-48c3-9b18-f01b4ee7fb35"
  },
  "oauthUser": {
    "id": 3,
    "username": "super_admin",
    "firstName": "super_admin",
    "lastName": "admin",
    "email": "super_admin@blendata.co",
    "phone": "12345678",
    "status": "ENABLED",
    "roles": [
      {
        "id": 2,
        "roleName": "super_admin",
        "version": 0
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "roleName": "Developer",
        "version": 0
      }
    ],
    "tenantId": 1,
    "version": 0,
    "permissions": [
      "menu:afdc7061a-8344-4671-ac84-64b8671fed12:edit",
      "menu:367d53d8-2853-4f92-a76d-5b1be33995e7:view",
      "menu:8bbcb2f6-94e2-4e47-8b46-1b43d7901ffe:view",
      "application:e7d458bb-ceb9-4a4b-bf41-3b84b2594043:edit",
      "application:e7d458bb-ceb9-4a4b-bf41-3b84b2594043:delete"
    ],
    "isActive": true
  }
}
```

## Query

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323445/Query |
| **Last modified** | 2025-03-26T09:08:30.207Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > General References > Blendata API Document > Hera API (Data Management Services) |

You can explore your data with an ANSI SQL statement.

## Instructions

Query step-by-step guide:

1. Call `REGISTER Service` for getting the job group id.
2. Call `QUERY Service` for getting your data.

You can check query status or kill query process with job group id.

## **API Document**

## Register JOB Group Id

HTTP Method : POST

{{hera\_url}}:{{hera\_port}}/private/sparkjobmanage/job/register

## Request Params

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| realmId | Realm ID. You must define realm to register |
| realmName | Realm Name. If you set realmId this param is not active. |
| zeusId | Zeus ID. You can define compute engine for query with Zeus ID. |
| zeusName | Zeus Name. If you set zeusId this param is not active. |

## **Headers**

```
Authorization : {{token}}
Content-Type : application/json
```

## **Body**

```
{
	"realmId": {{realmId}}
}
```

## **Example Response**

```
{
  "jobGroupId": "dd8e3c6b-1ac9-4aae-8999-758b9d4e981d",
  "applicationId": "local-1585882196847",
  "action": "",
  "status": "REGISTER",
  "description": null,
  "remark": null,
  "createdDate": 1585883694981,
  "createdBy": "administrator",
  "modifiedDate": 1585883694981,
  "modifiedBy": null,
  "optLock": 0,
  "zeusId": 5
}
```

---

## Query Service

HTTP Method : POST  
{{hera\_url}}:{{hera\_port}}/private/explore/query

An endpoint to query data.

## Request Params

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| jobGroupId | job group id for query process |
| sql | query sql |
| limit | query limit |
| realmId | selected realm id |
| zeusId | (optional) selected Zeus id |

## HEADERS

```
Authorization : {{token}}
Content-Type : application/json
```

## BODY raw

```
{
	"jobGroupId":"{{jobGroupId}}",
	"sql":"select * from sale",
	"limit":100,
	"realmId": {{realmId}},
	"zeusId": {{zeusId}}
}
```

## Example Response

```
{
  "fields": [
    {
      "comment": null,
      "dataType": "timestamp",

## Describe Table

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323461/Describe+Table |
| **Last modified** | 2025-03-26T09:08:30.346Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > General References > Blendata API Document > Hera API (Data Management Services) |

This API provides a detailed description of the table such as column name, column type, and comments.

## **API Document**

## Describe Table Service

HTTP Method : POST  
{{hera\_url}}:{{hera\_port}}/private/explore/describe/table

An endpoint to get a description of table.

## Request Params

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| tableName | The name of table that you need to get a description. |
| realmId | selected realm id |

## HEADERS

```
Authorization : {{token}}
Content-Type : application/json
```

## BODY raw

```
{
	"tableName": "{{table_name}}",
	"realmId": 1
}
```

## Example Response

```
{
  "fields": [
    {
      "name": "col_name",
      "comment": "name of the column",
      "metaData": "{\"comment\":\"name of the column\"}",
      "nullable": false,
      "dataType": "string"
    },
    {
      "name": "data_type",
      "comment": "data type of the column",
      "metaData": "{\"comment\":\"data type of the column\"}",
      "nullable": false,
      "dataType": "string"
    },
    {
      "name": "comment",
      "comment": "comment of the column",
      "metaData": "{\"comment\":\"comment of the column\"}",
      "nullable": true,
      "dataType": "string"
    }
  ],
  "rows": [
    {
      "col_name": "date",
      "data_type": "string",
      "comment": null
    },
    {
      "col_name": "time",
      "data_type": "string",
      "comment": null
    },
    {
      "col_name": "transaction",
      "data_type": "string",
      "comment": null
    },
    {
      "col_name": "item",
      "data_type": "string",
      "comment": null
    },
    {
      "col_name": "gtp_timestamp",
      "data_type": "string",
      "comment": null
    },
    {
      "col_name": "gtp_timestamp1",
      "data_type": "timestamp",
      "comment": null
    }
  ]
}
```

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2255323476/Get+Datasource+Profiles |

| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > General References > Blendata API Document > Hera API (Data Management Services) |

## **API Document**

## Describe Table Service

HTTP Method : POST  
{{hera\_url}}:{{hera\_port}}/private/datasourceProfiles

An endpoint to get all datasource profile in realm.

## Request Params

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| realmId | selected realm id |

## HEADERS

```
Authorization : {{token}}
Content-Type : application/json
```

## BODY raw

```
{
	"realmId": 1
}
```

## Example Response
