# **Blendata enterprise platform upgrade**

1. **Collect all information of Blendata enterprise platform modules.**

   1. What and Where all information that is required before upgrade the product version.

      1. Version of each module before upgrade
      2. Advance configuration
      3. Special libraries deployment
      4. Custom service deployment

2. **Stop all data services and BDE modules.**

**a. All data service (online data service configuration from web browser)**

i. Stop all stream services in the Service Management menu.

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXeJJimKaO1qUo4615hdFf2r_wBrmw4IcdAihdBrAcuc6nBbB--9S3Ce6K-DnPk0YGTEfRTTykA75m4BfPdhuYhi5Yp50muAHlfELkx-slaePvWytg0c6mkgtA_qI6nY7VL6WgQjAcb8ZSKgZ3Alv4UOvxU?key=LT90p70YKm2QF1UwI4QkaEzG)

ii. Stop all table update job services in the Service Management menu.

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXd7GiWw1rEop7sv8HhK-G0N1L74t3EdAv0OBAeT2ArljJfMYNLCOZeEXW8fOiVei3EVj_3vgXEDV_OIjSznjmewAD5y5D3_tlRPWSnK7PLiMz17hLSDPwNShtrvJ4VFMfBZVrKapC-fLjhTG5vomGCGINM?key=LT90p70YKm2QF1UwI4QkaEzG)

iii. Verify other services in the Service Management menu that it is not started at that time.

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcLbfHBfAt_aBmHB0mp7qX1SBhMwwst5UgjnlZz0peBZNjLHihUHZYIbbXdmYhABQVs69q0bp0Q4zBdpWVdnixpplNWLl3MAV5Dhs68mfIg9mOUQvJjUf1ZHCIkcdNGZ2lgPdzZeh8msJu0YF-F6CCoKLA?key=LT90p70YKm2QF1UwI4QkaEzG)

iv. Stop all aggregate table job services in the data processing section.

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXfojDiYDLGjkiZb1PQEYgT3e_JLbZZ749-eUN1yKhaEaFa9EEfScq9K4Wb4W2cSLuWQK7sO-lkjbSjCFAGlMlvNoSMwCWv-xwOjtGUzB2gnAbrsvgc3picyCBfBWP8vnDLZmo8XcM9iq0yky-wHAZYAwMrL?key=LT90p70YKm2QF1UwI4QkaEzG)

v. Verify other services in the data processing section that it is not started at that time.

vi. (Optional) Verify custom services (if use) that have not started at that time.

vii. (Optional) Stop and verify chain service (if use) services that have not started at that time.

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdeuWfr-0ipeEoipYsfRdFPvCqF1rNGx4BYbAzt44o0guEAVvNOJTf71-nKWGWiUmOW3FzhaQcgynQUzyeW54PPiRFyZrtmxjvDy7-kiBdW0TsM56qME7CjkkYeErxjDVqWZ_4gc2R4RT5Bag-6-LbOgSnK?key=LT90p70YKm2QF1UwI4QkaEzG)

viii. Stop and verify all workflows in the Workflow Management menu (if use) that has not started at that time.

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXd0TUrIqOj3EtSqOVvThHRCgR5Cv0GrEU3T0ZEaZXk7HQAZuCmrXuWYurr3W96XY3uuRuDf3pfmaAkxbE_1DEk4tQmcBdk2uDhtbJNvwY2YwFETYbN5gycPdaKZAJJ8ty1E_8rptC0IT2kap12s_kJADPzQ?key=LT90p70YKm2QF1UwI4QkaEzG)

ix. Stop and verify all notebooks in the Notebook menu (if use) that has not started at that time (schedule) including stream service or others.

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXf6BqiOQEYlCKWmBlAzCI1tz8Cbdsizi_HOC6n4IHJk-VS_AEXRcjYKKaH2B66jpO5Yx3Ft3ruCLl7Ym77tYrXUb5-32CUKL1LpLyDUW4sG8JGMQFG3byspVFffn3p8HGJ8c_Ok-vN8hoL7S6JkLq-eM-HS?key=LT90p70YKm2QF1UwI4QkaEzG)

**b. Zeus core application module**

i. Use command SSH to connect with zeus server(s).

ii. Go to zeus home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /path/to/zeus/directory
```

iii. Stop zeus module (Use the command below).

```
bin/stop-zeus.sh
```

iv. Verify that zeus service has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

**c. Spark module**

i. Use command SSH to connect spark master server(s)

ii. Go to spark home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /path/to/spark/directory
```

iii. Stop all spark workers via spark master (Use the command below).

```
./sbin/stop-slaves.sh
```

iv. Verify that spark master ui that all spark workers have dead.

v. Stop spark master (Use the command below).

```
./sbin/stop-master.sh
```

vi. Verify that spark master ui can not access.

**d. Hera core web API module**

i. Use command SSH to connect hera server(s)

ii. Go to Hera home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /path/to/hera/directory
```

iii. Stop zeus module (Use the command below).

```
bin/stop-hera.sh
```

iv. Verify that Hera service has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

**e. Cerberus authentication module:**

i. Use command SSH to connect cerberus server(s)

ii. Go to Cerberus home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /path/to/cerberus/directory
```

iii. Stop cerberus module (Use the command below).

```
bin/stop-cerberus.sh
```

iv. Verify that the cerberus module has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

**f. Zeppelin authentication module:**

i. Use command SSH to connect zeppelin server(s)

ii. Go to Zeppelin home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /path/to/zeppelin/directory
```

iii. Stop zeppelin module (Use the command below).

```
bin/stop-zeppelin.sh
```

iv. Verify that the zeppelin module has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

**g. Airflow and Workflow Agent module:**

i. Use command SSH to connect airflow server(s)

ii. Go to the workflow home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /path/to/workflow-agent/directory
```

iii. Stop workflow-agent module (Use the command below).

```
bin/stop-workflow-agent.sh
```

iv. Verify that the workflow-agent module has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

v. Go to the airflow home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /path/to/airflow/directory
```

vi. Stop airflow-webserver and airflow-scheduler module (Use the command below).

```
bin/stop-airflow-webserver.sh
bin/stop-airflow-scheduler.sh
```

vii. Verify that the airflow module has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

3. **Backing up**

**a. Backup BDE database**

There are many databases in our Blendata Enterprise. See more details about each database as per below:

- **Hera**: Store all assets in Blendata including data assets, visualizations, and dashboards. Additionally, this database keeps records of historical processes such as service history, stream history, and query history, along with module information for module management and access.
- **Cerberus**: Used to store user, role, and permission data, including users within each tenant and data elements such as platform assets and user access rights for each element.
- **Spark Metadata**: A central database that stores all table schemas, partitions, column metadata, and information on serializers and deserializers used for reading and writing data.
- **Noti-Server**: Stores platform notification history data, including both inbound and outbound information.
- **Audit DB**: Used to store an audit log.

**\*\*Important\*\*** For step database backup if the platform has many tenants and realms in your cluster. You must backup all databases that relate with all configuration tenants and realm management.

You must additionally backup the database when you have more than one compute cluster or tenant, for example, additionally backup the cerberus\_tenant02 database when you create the tenant02 tenant, or additionally backup the metastore02 database when you have a new compute cluster that has that metastore02 configuration database in hive-site.xml.

i. Dump all services steps as per below:

1.) Dump all services’ database individually by running the following commands:   
MySQL:

```
export BDE_BACKUP_DATE="`date+%F`"

export BDE_BACKUP_PATH="<destination path>/bde-backup-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}"

mkdir -p ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}

mysqldump -u <username> -p cerberus > ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/cerberus-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysqldump -u <username> -p hera > ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/hera-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysqldump -u <username> -p metastore > ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/metastore-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysqldump -u <username> -p zeus > ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/zeus-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysqldump -u <username> -p metastore_zeppelin > ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/metastore_zeppelin-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysqldump -u <username> -p noti > ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/noti-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
```

PostgreSQL:

```
DB_USER="<username>"
DB_PASS="<database password>"
DB_HOST="<database host>"
DB_PORT="<database port>"
OUTPUT_PATH="<destination path>/$(date +%Y%m%d)"

mkdir -p $OUTPUT_PATH
export PGPASSWORD=$DB_PASS

pg_dump -U $DB_USER -h $DB_HOST -p $DB_PORT -d cerberus -f $OUTPUT_PATH/cerberus_backup.sql
pg_dump -U $DB_USER -h $DB_HOST -p $DB_PORT -d hera -f $OUTPUT_PATH/hera_backup.sql
pg_dump -U $DB_USER -h $DB_HOST -p $DB_PORT -d zeus -f $OUTPUT_PATH/zeus_backup.sql
pg_dump -U $DB_USER -h $DB_HOST -p $DB_PORT -d metastore -f $OUTPUT_PATH/metastore_backup.sql
pg_dump -U $DB_USER -h $DB_HOST -p $DB_PORT -d metastore_zeppelin -f $OUTPUT_PATH/metastore_zeppelin_backup.sql
pg_dump -U $DB_USER -h $DB_HOST -p $DB_PORT -d noti -f $OUTPUT_PATH/noti_backup.sql

pg_dump -U $DB_USER -h $DB_HOST -p $DB_PORT -d airflow_db -f $OUTPUT_PATH/airflow_db_backup.sql
```

2.) (Optional) Dump PostgreSQL database for Workflow feature. If you enable the Workflow feature, this step is required.

```
export BDE_BACKUP_DATE="$(date +%F)"
export BDE_BACKUP_PATH="/destination/path/bde-backup-postgresql-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}"

mkdir -p ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}

pg_dump -h your_host -U your_username -d airflow_db -F c -f ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/airflow-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
```

3.) (Optional) Dump PostgreSQL Timescale database for BDE Access Log feature. If your cluster enables a time scale database, this step is required.

```
export BDE_BACKUP_DATE="$(date +%F)"
export BDE_BACKUP_PATH="/destination/path/bde-backup-postgresql-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}"

mkdir -p ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}

pg_dump -h your_host -U your_username -d timescale_db -F c -f ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/timescale-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
```

**b. BDE Hera module and configuration**

i. Login to BDE Hera module.

ii. Create backup directory.

```
export BDE_HERA_BACKUP_DIR="`date +%F`-BDE-HERA"
echo $BDE_HERA_BACKUP_DIR
mkdir -p $BDE_HERA_BACKUP_DIR
```

iii. Backup the BDE Hera web API module:

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_HERA_BACKUP_DIR/hera.tar.gz $BDE_HERA_HOME
```

**c. BDE Cerberus module and configuration**

i. Create backup directory.

```
export BDE_CERBERUS_BACKUP_DIR="`date +%F`-BDE-CERBERUS"
mkdir -p ${BDE_CERBERUS_BACKUP_DIR}
```

ii. Backup the BDE Cerberus web API module:

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_CERBERUS_BACKUP_DIR/cerberus.tar.gz $BDE_CERBERUS_HOME
```

**d. BDE Spark module and configuration**

i. Create backup directory.

```
mkdir -p $BDE_SPARK_BACKUP_DIR
```

ii. Backup the BDE Spark module:

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_SPARK_BACKUP_DIR/spark.tar.gz $BDE_SPARK_HOME
```

**e. BDE Zeus module and configuration**

i. Create backup directory.

```
mkdir -p ${BDE_ZEUS_BACKUP_DIR}
```

ii. Backup the BDE Zeus module:

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_ZEUS_BACKUP_DIR/<ZEUS_NAME>.tar.gz $BDE_<ZEUS_NAME>_HOME
```

**f. BDE Zeppelin module and configuration**

i. Create backup directory.

```
mkdir -p ${BDE_ZEPPELIN_BACKUP_DIR}
```

ii. Backup the BDE Zeppelin module:

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_ZEPPELIN_BACKUP_DIR/zeppelin.tar.gz $BDE_ZEPPELIN_HOME
```

iii. (Optional) If Zeppelin notebook path is configured to be in a different directory other than $**BDE\_ZEPPELIN\_HOME**/notebook. Run the following command to back up the notebook path.

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_ZEPPELIN_BACKUP_DIR/zeppelin-notebook.tar.gz $BDE_ZEPPELIN_NOTEBOOK_PATH
```

**g. BDE Notification module and configuration**

i. Create backup directory.

```
export BDE_NOTI_BACKUP_DIR="`date +%F`-BDE-NOTI"
mkdir -p ${BDE_NOTI_BACKUP_DIR}
```

ii. Backup the BDE Notification module:

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_NOTI_BACKUP_DIR/noti.tar.gz $BDE_NOTI_HOME
```

**h. BDE Brown Cow module and configuration**

i. Create backup directory.

```
export BDE_BROWN_COW_BACKUP_DIR="`date +%F`-BDE-BROWN-COW"
mkdir -p ${BDE_BROWN_COW_BACKUP_DIR}
```

ii. Backup the BDE brown cow module:

```
sudo -E tar -cf $BDE_BROWN_COW_BACKUP_DIR/brown_cow.tar.gz $BDE_BROWN_COW_HOME
```

iii. (Optional) If the module H2 database is configured to be in the different path other than **$BDE\_BROWN\_COW\_HOME**, run the following command to back up brown cow H2 database.

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_BROWN_COW_BACKUP_DIR/brown_cow_h2_database.tar.gz $BDE_BROWN_COW_H2_DATABASE_HOME
```

**i. (Optional) if the BDE version being migrated is from 4.4.0 or higher, the instruction steps show how to backup BDE workflow module and configuration**

i. Create backup directory for airflow package.

```
export BDE_AIRFLOW_BACKUP_DIR="`date +%F`-BDE-AIRFLOW"
mkdir -p ${BDE_AIRFLOW_BACKUP_DIR}
```

ii. Backup the BDE airflow module:

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_AIRFLOW_BACKUP_DIR/airflow.tar.gz $BDE_AIRFLOW_HOME
```

iii. (Optional) If the airflow DAG path is configured to be in the different path other than $BDE\_AIRFLOW\_HOME, run the following command to back up airflow DAG files.

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_AIRFLOW_BACKUP_DIR/airflow_dag.tar.gz $BDE_AIRFLOW_DAG_HOME
```

iv. Create backup directory for workflow agent package.

```
export BDE_WORKFLOW_AGENT_BACKUP_DIR="`date +%F`-BDE-AIRFLOW"
mkdir -p ${BDE_WORKFLOW_AGENT_BACKUP_DIR}
```

v. Backup the BDE workflow agent API module:

```
sudo -E tar -zcf $BDE_WORKFLOW_AGENT_BACKUP_DIR/workflow_agent.tar.gz $BDE_WORKFLOW_AGENT_HOME
```

4. **BDE upgrade**

**\*\*Important**\*\***:** Before BDE upgrade, you should recheck the [versions supported upgrade](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/edit-v2/2255324167#Supported-Upgrade) table and already proceed the previous steps including collect all information of Blendata enterprise modules, and follow the steps in BDE product migrate guide [See more](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/BIDhfw).

**BDE package download**: To download BDE software need to contact our support team

Email: [support@blendata.co](mailto:support@blendata.co) (The Blendata Enterprise download link is valid for one week.)

**a. Upgrade Cerberus**

i. Upload package to BDE Cerberus module server.

ii. Extract BDE Cerberus release package.

```
tar -xzvf cerberus-x.x.x-final.tgz
```

iii. Verify the ‘config’ directory compare backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_CERBERUS_HOME}/config/application.properties
```

iv. Follow configuration migration step from [Version Migration](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2145484804/Blendata+Enterprise+Version+Migration) page to apply new configuration added to new version BDE of Cerberus.

**b. Upgrade Hera**

i. Upload package to BDE Hera module server.

ii. Extract BDE Hera release package.

```
tar -xzvf hera-x.x.x-final.tgz
```

iii. Verify the ‘config’ directory compare backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_CERBERUS_HOME}/config/application.properties
vi ${BDE_CERBERUS_HOME}/config/service.properties
vi ${BDE_CERBERUS_HOME}/config/web.conf
```

iv. Follow configuration migration step from [Version Migration](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2145484804/Blendata+Enterprise+Version+Migration) page to apply new configuration added to new version BDE of Hera.

**Note:** if Hera being migrated contains custom services, run the following commands to transfer custom services to new Hera package

**c. Upgrade Spark**

i. Upload package to BDE zeus module server.

ii. Extract BDE zeus release package.

```
tar -xzvf zeus-x.x.x-final.tgz
```

iii. Verify the ‘conf’ directory compare backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_SPARK_HOME}/conf/spark-env.sh
vi ${BDE_SPARK_HOME}/spark/conf/spark-defaults.conf  (Optional)
```

iv. Follow configuration migration step from [Version Migration](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2145484804/Blendata+Enterprise+Version+Migration) page to apply new configuration added to new version BDE of Spark.

**d. Upgrade Zeus**

i. Upload package to BDE Zeus module server.

ii. Extract BDE zeus release package.

```
tar -xzvf zeus-x.x.x-final.tgz
mv zeus <zeus_name>
```

iii. Verify the ‘**bin | config**’ directory compare backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/bin/zeus-env.sh
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/bin/start-zeus.sh
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/bin/stop-zeus.sh
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/bin/checkPIDStatus.sh

vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/config/application.properties
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/config/service.properties
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/config/spark.conf
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/config/hive-site.xml
```

iv. Verify the ‘**spark/conf**’ directory compare backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/spark/conf/spark-env.sh
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/spark/conf/fairscheduler.xml  (Optional)
vi ${BDE_ZEUS_HOME}/spark/conf/spark-defaults.conf  (Optional)
```

v. Follow configuration migration step from [Version Migration](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2145484804/Blendata+Enterprise+Version+Migration) page to apply new configuration added to new version BDE of Zeus.

vi. (Optional) if Zeus being migrated contains custom services, run the following commands to transfer custom services to new Zeus package

**e. Upgrade Zeppelin**

i. Upload package to BDE Zeppelin module server.

ii. Extract BDE Zeppelin release package.

```
tar -xzvf zeppelin-x.x.x-final.tgz
```

iii. Verify the ‘**config**’ directory compare backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_ZEPPELIN_HOME}/config/interpreter.json
vi ${BDE_ZEPPELIN_HOME}/config/zeppelin-env.sh
vi ${BDE_ZEPPELIN_HOME}/config/zeppelin-site.xml
```

iv. Verify ‘**spark/conf**’ directory from backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_ZEPPELIN_HOME}/spark/conf/spark-env.sh
```

v. Verify ‘**notebook**’ directory from backup directory in step 3.

```
cp -rp ${BDE_ZEPPELIN_OLD}/notebook/* ${BDE_ZEPPELIN_HOME}/notebook
```

vi. Follow configuration migration step from [Version Migration](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2145484804/Blendata+Enterprise+Version+Migration) page to apply new configuration added to new version BDE of Zeppelin.

**f. Upgrade Airflow \*\*\***

i. Upload package to Airflow & Workflow module server.

ii. Extract Airflow & Workflow release package.

```
tar -xzvf airflow-x.x.x-final.tgz
tar -xzvf workflow-agent-x.x.x-final.tgz
```

iii. Verify file ‘**airflow/airflow.cfg**’ from backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_AIRFLOW_HOME}/airflow.cfg
```

iv. Verify ‘**workflow-agent/config**’ directory from backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_WORKFLOW_HOME}/config/application.properties
```

v. Follow configuration migration step from [Version Migration](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2145484804/Blendata+Enterprise+Version+Migration) page to apply new configuration added to new version BDE of Airflow & Workflow.

**g. Upgrade Brown-Cow \*\*\***

i. Upload package to BDE Brown-Cow module server.

ii. Extract BDE Brown-Cow release package.

```
tar -xzvf brown-cow-x.x.x-final.tgz
```

iii. Verify ‘**config**’ directory from backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_BROWNCOW_HOME}/config/application.properties
vi ${BDE_BROWNCOW_HOME}/config/service.properties
vi ${BDE_BROWNCOW_HOME}/config/install.json
```

iv. Follow configuration migration step from [Version Migration](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2145484804/Blendata+Enterprise+Version+Migration) page to apply new configuration added to new version BDE of Brown-Cow.

**h. Upgrade Notification \*\*\***

i. Upload package to BDE Noti-Server module server.

ii. Extract BDE Noti-Server release package.

```
tar -xzvf noti-server-x.x.x-final.tgz
```

iii. Verify ‘**conf**’ directory from backup directory in step 3.

```
vi ${BDE_NOTI_HOME}/conf/notiserver.conf
```

iv. Follow configuration migration step from [Version Migration](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2145484804/Blendata+Enterprise+Version+Migration) page to apply new configuration added to new version BDE of Noti-Server.

5. Verify all services. User can check the version of each module as per below.

   1. **Using the SSH PS command** to check the process of below modules.

      1. Cerberus
      2. Hera
      3. Noti-Server
      4. Brown-Cow
      5. Workflow-agent

         1. Use a below command to check jar file path of each module.

```
ps -ef | grep hera
ps -ef | grep cerberus
ps -ef | grep noti-server
ps -ef | grep brown-cow
ps -ef | grep workflow-agent
```

2. Check the Blendata version number from jar path.

![cerberus jar path.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255324167/cerberus%20jar%20path.png?api=v2)

**b. Airflow**

i. Go to your device that installed the Airflow.

ii. Find the Airflow environment path using below command.

```
ps -ef | grep airflow
```

iii. Use **cd** command to go to airflow environment path.

iv. Use a below command to check the Airflow version.

```
$ bin/activate
$ source bin/activate
$ airflow version
```

**c. Zeus**

i. Use the command below to check the Zeus version.

```
ps -ef | grep zeus | grep "-server"
```

**Note**: For Multiple Zeus, you have to recheck versions for all Zeus.

**d. Zeppelin**

i. Use the command below to check the zeppelin location.

```
ps -ef | grep zeppelin | grep ZeppelinServer
```

ii. Check version number from jar path.

**e. Spark**

i. Check the Spark version from master website (link: <http://43.225.143.41:8080>)

6. **Rollback restore**

To revert to the backup version before upgrade. The restore step covers component modules in your create from step backup only.

**a. Ensure all BDE modules are stopped**

i. BDE spark master and spark workers process.

1.) Login to BDE Spark master server.

2.) Run the following command to stop Spark master and all Spark workers.

```
$BDE_ZEUS_HOME/spark/sbin/stop-workers.sh

$BDE_ZEUS_HOME/spark/sbin/stop-master.sh
```

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) Check all Spark processes are stopped.

ii. BDE Zeus application process.

1.) Login to all nodes that run BDE Zeus application servers.

2.) Run the following command to stop BDE Zeus application.

```
 $BDE_ZEUS_HOME/bin/stop-zeus.sh
```

3.) Check BDE Zeus application is stopped.

```
$BDE_ZEUS_HOME/bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) The BDE Zeus application process is “**stopped**”.

iii. BDE Hera core web API process.

1.) Login to BDE Hera core web API server.

2.) Run the following command to stop BDE Hera core web API.

```
$BDE_HERA_HOME/bin/stop-hera.sh
```

3.) Check BDE Hera core web API is stopped.

```
$BDE_HERA_HOME/bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) The BDE Hera core web API process is “**stopped**”.

iv. BDE Cerberus security management process

1.) Login to BDE Cerberus security management server.

2.) Run the following command to stopBDE Cerberus security management.

```
$BDE_CERBERUS_HOME/bin/stop-cerberus.sh
```

3.) Check BDE Cerberus security management is stopped.

```
 $BDE_CERBERUS_HOME/bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) The BDE Cerberus security management process is “**stopped**”.

v. BDE Zeppelin process

1.) Login to Zeppelin server.

2.) Run the following command to stop the Zeppelin.

```
$BDE_ZEPPELIN_HOME/bin/stop-zeppelin.sh
```

3.) Check  the Zeppelin application is stopped.

```
$BDE_ZEPPELIN_HOME/bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/25aa.png) The Zeppelin process is “**stopped**”.

vi. BDE Airflow + Workflow agent

1.) Login to BDE Workflow agent server.

2.) Run the following command to stop the BDE Workflow agent and Airflow.

```
$BDE_WORKFLOW_HOME/bin/stop-workflow.sh
$BDE_AIRFLOW_HOME/bin/stop-airflow-scheduler.sh
$BDE_AIRFLOW_HOME/bin/stop-airflow-webserver.sh
```

3.) Check  the BDE Workflow agent and airflow is stopped.

```
$BDE_WORKFLOW_HOME/bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

4.) The BDE Workflow agent process is “**stopped**”.

```
$BDE_AIRFLOW_HOME/bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

5.) The BDE Airflow scheduler and web server process is “**stopped**”.

**b. Restore all BDE platform modules.**

i. BDE Spark master and Spark workers

1.) Run the following command to extract the backups.

```
cd $BDE_SPARK_BACKUP_DIR
tar -xf spark.tar
```

2.) Restore BDE Spark modules directory from backup taken during the upgrade process.

```
cp -rp  $BDE_SPARK_BACKUP_DIR/* $BDE_SPARK_HOME/
```

ii. BDE Zeus application

1.) Run the following command to extract the backups.

```
cd $BDE_ZEUS_BACKUP_DIR
tar -xf zeus.tar.gz
```

2.) Restore BDE Zeus application modules directory from backup taken during the upgrade process.

```
cp -rp  $BDE_ZEUS_BACKUP_DIR/* $BDE_ZEUS_HOME/
```

iii. BDE Hera core web API

1.) Run the following command to extract the backups.

```
cd $BDE_HERA_BACKUP_DIR
tar -xf hera.tar.gz
```

2.) Restore BDE Hera core web API  module directory from backup taken during the upgrade process.

```
cp -rp  $BDE_HERA_BACKUP_DIR/* $BDE_HERA_HOME/
```

iv. BDE Cerberus security management

1.) Run the following command to extract the backups.

```
cd $BDE_CERBERUS_BACKUP_DIR
tar -xf cerberus.tar.gz
```

2.) Restore BDE Cerberus security management module directory from backup taken during the upgrade process.

```
cp -rp  $BDE_CERBERUS_BACKUP_DIR/* $BDE_CERBERUA_HOME/
```

v. BDE Zeppelin application

1.) Run the following command to extract the backups

```
cd $BDE_ZEPPELIN_BACKUP_DIR
tar -xf zeppelin.tar.gz
```

2.) Restore BDE Zeppelin application modules directory from backup taken during the upgrade process.

```
cp -rp  $BDE_ZEPPELIN_BACKUP_DIR/* $BDE_ZEPPELIN_HOME/
```

vi. BDE Airflow

1.) Run the following command to extract the backups.

```
cd $BDE_AIRFLOW_BACKUP_DIR
tar -xf airflow.tar.gz
cd $BDE_WORKFLOW_BACKUP_DIR
tar -xf workflow.tar.gz
```

2.) Restore airflow & workflow directory from backup taken during the upgrade process.

```
cp -rp  $BDE_AIRFLOW_BACKUP_DIR/* $BDE_AIRFLOW_HOME/
cp -rp  $BDE_WORKFLOW_BACKUP_DIR/* $BDE_WORKFLOW_HOME/
```

vii. Rollback all databases (metastore, airflow): users need to restore the database as per below steps.

1.) Run the following command to restore databases.

MySQL:

```
mysql --user=cerberus -p cerberus <  ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/cerberus-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysql --user=hera -p hera < ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/hera-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysql --user=zeus -p zeus < ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/zeus-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysql --user=zeus -p metastore < ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/metastore-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysql --user=zeppelin -p metastore_zeppelin < ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/metastore_zeppelin-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
mysql --user=noti -p noti < ${BDE_BACKUP_PATH}/noti-${BDE_BACKUP_DATE}.sql
```

PostgreSQL:

2.) Run the following command to verify all databases after restore.

MySQL:

```
mysql -u cerberus -p
use cerberus;
show tables;

mysql -u hera -p
use hera;
show tables;

mysql -u zeus -p
use zeus;
show tables;

mysql -u zeus -p
use metastore;
show tables;

mysql -u zeppelin -p
use metastore_zeppelin;
show tables;

mysql -u noti -p
use noti;
show tables;
```

PostgreSQL:

**Note**: Restore all databases.

7. **Verify all services.** User can check the version of each module as per below.

   1. **Using the SSH PS command** to check the process of below modules.

      1. Cerberus
      2. Hera
      3. Notification
      4. Brown-Cow
      5. Workflow-agent

         1. Use a below command to check jar file path of each module.

   ```
   ps -ef | grep hera
   ps -ef | grep cerberus
   ps -ef | grep noti-server
   ps -ef | grep brown-cow
   ps -ef | grep workflow-agent
   ```

2. Check the Blendata version number from jar path.

![cerberus jar path.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2255324167/cerberus%20jar%20path.png?api=v2)

**b. Airflow**

i. Go to your device that installed the Airflow.

ii. Find the Airflow environment path using below command.

```
ps -ef | grep airflow
```

iii. Use **cd** command to go to airflow environment path.

iv. Use a below command to check the Airflow version.

```
$ bin/activate
$ source bin/activate
$ airflow version
```

**c. Zeus**

i. Use the command below to check the Zeus version.

```
ps -ef | grep zeus | grep "-server"
```

**Note**: For Multiple Zeus, you have to recheck versions for all Zeus.

**d. Zeppelin**

i. Use the command below to check the zeppelin location.

```
ps -ef | grep zeppelin | grep ZeppelinServer
```

ii. Check version number from jar path.

**e. Spark**

i. Check the Spark version from master website (link: <http://43.225.143.41:8080>)

---

## Role

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2266398727/Role |
| **Last modified** | 2026-04-03T07:51:36.099Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Admin Setting Menus > User Management |

The Blendata platform will initial generate the three roles.

- **Super\_Admin** can access to all function menus of Blendata platform.
- **Administrator** can access only User Management and LDAP Management.
- **System** can access to all function menus of Blendata platform and able to create the Tenant via the backend.

# **Create Role**

Administrator role can create a new role or import the role from LDAP server into the Blendata.

1. Click “**Create Role**”.
2. Fill in the Role information.
3. There are also additional permissions that can be assigned via toggles (Optional)

   - **Metadata SQL Command**: Toggle to grant permission for using Metadata SQL commands.
   - **Default Workspace Management**: Toggle to grant permission for using “**Set as Default**” action in Notebook Workspace.
4. Select the components for this role.

![image-20250926-144426.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266398727/image-20250926-144426.png?api=v2)

5. (Optional) Configure the LDAP mapping.

   - Users can import the user account from LDAP server into the Blendata via the **LDAP Mapping** tab.
   - Click “**Add mapping configuration**” and fill in the LDAP Server details including LDAP Server and Search Base.
6. Setup the Realm and Compute by setting a default compute and selecting other computes.

![image-20250402-092409.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266398727/image-20250402-092409.png?api=v2)

Administrator role is able to setup one default compute and multiple computes for '**Another Compute By Role**,'.

7. Select the Role and click “**Create**”.

---

# Roles Management

If the user need to edit or add more member of each role, the user can click action:3dots:button.

![role management.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266398727/role%20management.png?api=v2)

- **View Role Details**: To see the selected role details, access level, and the connected roles from LDAP.
- **Edit Role**: Edit all functions permission in the Blendata or link the role from the LDAP server with role in the Blendata.

  - **Components**: To enable platform components(menus) and set the operation permission for each components.
  - **LDAP Mapping**: The administrator can setup a configuration for LDAP Mapping.
  - **Realm & Compute**: The administrator can manage the Realm and Zeus compute for each role by setting one default compute and multiple computes for '**Another Compute By Role**,' which will serve as replacements when the default compute is down.

![image-20250128-104445.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266398727/image-20250128-104445.png?api=v2)

![image-20250402-092409.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266398727/image-20250402-092409.png?api=v2)

- **Update Members**: To add the member or remove the member of the role.
- **Delete Role**: To delete the selected role.

---

## User

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2266431496/User |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T08:56:25.913Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Admin Setting Menus > User Management |

# Create a New User

The administrator is able to create a new user with 3 options clicking “**Create User**“ button.

![create user ops.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/create%20user%20ops.png?api=v2)

## 1. Create New User

To create a new user by the Administrator user and Administrator user needs to fill in the user information.

1. Click “**Create User**“ and select the “**Create New User**“ option.
2. Fill in the user information.

![create user - BDE source.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/create%20user%20-%20BDE%20source.png?api=v2)

![create user - SSO.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/create%20user%20-%20SSO.png?api=v2)

- **Username**: The username.
- **E-mail**: The e-mail of the user. (used for password reset for the first time)
- **First Name**:The first name of the user.
- **Last Name**: The last name of the user.
- **Telephone Number**: The telephone number of the user.
- **Expired Date**: To specific the expired date for user. The default expired is within 60 days since the user created.
- **Primary Role**: A primary role is the main function or responsibility assigned to an individual within an organization or system, defining their access level and tasks that can be performed. (The primary role is relates to Zeus compute setting)
- **Secondary Role**: A secondary role serves as a supplementary function or responsibility, granting additional permissions or tasks beyond an individual's primary role. Another compute cannot be used for secondary role.
- **Authentication Source**:

  - **Blendata Enterprise**: There are two password generate method as following:

    - **Send new password to user E-mail**: The platform will send the first password via email. Then, The user can login into the platform with this password and can reset the password after the first login.
    - **Generate offline password**: The platform won’t send password to user via email but the platform will display the username and password. The administrator need to share this username and password to user directly.
  - **SSO**

    - **User Source**: An existing SSO configuration in Blendata Enterprise platform.
- **Password Generate Method**:

  - **Send password to user E-mail**: The platform will send the email with the new password to the user.
  - **Generate offline password**: The platform will show the popup with username and password to user for login.
- **PCI rules**: Blendata Enterprise implements PCI DSS Policy in User Management as below list:

![PCI roles.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/PCI%20roles.png?api=v2)

Users able to disable this PCI policies, when creating a new user.

**Note:** The field with \* symbol is a required field. These fields need to be filled in before proceed the next steps.

3. Click “**Create**“ to create this new user.

## 2. Sync from LDAP

This method connects to the LDAP server and imports the Username and Password into the Blendata platform. It synchronizes the roles from LDAP with those in the system and syncs LDAP users with users in the system.

1. Click “**Create User**“ and select the “**Sync from LDAP**“ option.
2. Fill in the LDAP server information such as LDAP Server, Search Base, and Search Filter.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/u4.PNG?api=v2)

3. Click “**Search Users**”, and the platform will display the username list from the LDAP server. Then, the user needs to select a single user or multiple users by ticking the boxes.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/u5.PNG?api=v2)

4. When the LDAP already synced, the LDAP Synced Result will display the synced status, username, and the synced details.

There are three user importing status as below.

- For the existing user, the status will show **Success** and the Synced Details column displays an updated detail of that user.
- For the new user, the status will show **Success** and no details on Synced Details column.
- For the duplicated user, the status will show **Existed** and the Synced Details column displays “**Username is already exists**“.

## **3. Import Users**

To import the user information into the platform via xls or xlsx files.

![import user popup.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/import%20user%20popup.png?api=v2)

1. Click “**Create User**“ and select the “**Import Users**“ option.
2. Click “**Download User Import Template File**” to download the template and fill in the user information such as username, email, first name, last name, and role.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/1aaf6993-2a00-4fc7-aae7-d835a6e45337?api=v2)![import user template.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/import%20user%20template.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Open file browser**” and “**Upload**“. Then, the platform will show the Sync Result popup, which show the example of the imported users.
4. Select “**User Source**”, which is existing SSO configuration or default.
5. Click “**OK**”.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/image-20210428-125228.png?api=v2)

---

# **Review User Status**

To review the user status including Enable, Disable, Expired, and Locked.

![user mng.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/user%20mng.png?api=v2)

- **Enabled**: The user is active.
- **Disable**: The user is disable or the user has not logged in more than 90 days since the user account created.
- **Expired**: The user account is expired.
- **Lock**: The user account is locked when the user enters an incorrect password more than 6 times, preventing them from logging into the platform. In this case, the user needs to wait for the Administrator to unlock the account.
- **Request**: The SSO user profile creation request will appear once a user has submitted it. The account remains in a pending state awaiting administrator approval.

---

# User Management

## **Search**

The user or administrator can search all users via search bar with username, email, role name, and date and time of the latest active date.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/user-manage-search.PNG?api=v2)

## User Action Management

There are various actions that users can use to manage the user account.

![user management.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/user%20management.png?api=v2)

- **View User Details**: The user details will display on the popup such as username, full name, telephone, expired date, and assigned role.

![user detail.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/user%20detail.png?api=v2)

- **Edit User Information**: The platform will show the popup to administrator to edit the user information. Administrator can edit all details excepts username.

![image-20250926-105519.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/image-20250926-105519.png?api=v2)

- **Edit User Access Rights**: The popup displays the operation permission of each component in our Blendata that administrator need to switch each component from 'Disable' to 'Enable' to allow users to see the components in our Blendata platform.  
   There are also additional permissions that can be assigned via toggles, such as Metadata SQL Command and Default Workspace Management.

  - **Metadata SQL Command**: Toggle to grant permission for using Metadata SQL commands.
  - **Default Workspace Management**: Toggle to grant permission for using “**Set as Default**” action in Notebook Workspace.

![image-20250926-104605.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/image-20250926-104605.png?api=v2)

- **Transfer Asset**: Transfers of all assets, including data assets, services, service chains, and workflow assets, or some assets to another person via the User Management menu. See more details, go to [Transfer Asset](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201255951/Transfer+Asset) page.

  - **Transfer all assets**: Transfer all asset to a specific person.
  - **Transfer some assets**: Select some assets and transfer to another.

![image-20250116-075649.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/image-20250116-075649.png?api=v2)

Administrator role can transfer the asset owner only.

- **Reset password**: Administrator can set a new password to the selected user by clicking the Reset Password menu with two methods as below.

  - **Send password to user E-mail**: The platform will send the email with the new password to the user.
  - **Generate offline password**: The platform will show the popup with username and password to user for login.

![reset password.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/reset%20password.png?api=v2)

- **Disable / Enable User**: The administrator can choose to disable or enable the user.
- **Delete User**: This is for administrator or super-admin, who can remove the user.

**Note**:

- If the administrator or super-admin deletes the user, there is a confirmation popup shows to reconfirm again. Then, the deleted user won’t access to Blendata platform anymore.
- If users have assets in the platform, all assets have to be transferred to other users before deleting the users.

- **View User Request**: Admins must review the request, update any necessary account details, and accept it.

![image-20260223-085014.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2266431496/image-20260223-085014.png?api=v2)

---

# User And Password Policy

| **Policy** | **Criteria** |
| --- | --- |
| Password Length | Between 8 to 16 characters |
| Password Pattern | - At least one uppercase letter (A–Z) - At least one lowercase letter (a–z) - At least one number (0–9) - At least one special character (!@#$%^&\*(),.?"[]/<=>-+) |
| Cannot Use Previous Password | Yes (latest four used passwords) |
| Password Expiration / Validity | Account valid for 60 days;  users must renew or reset after expiration |
| Login Attempt Limit | Max 6 incorrect password attempts allowed |
| Inactivity Account Lock | Account becomes inactive after 90 days of no login |
| Password Change Frequency | Password must be changed every 3 months (after login with old password, the UI will force users to change their password). |
| Session Timeout | Auto logout after 15 minutes of inactivity |

Account expiration does not trigger a forced password reset, except when the password expiration date complies with the defined policy. In such cases, users must reset their password after renewing the account.

---

## Audit Log Setup and Timescale (Online Installation)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2276065281/Audit+Log+Setup+and+Timescale+Online+Installation |
| **Last modified** | 2026-03-25T03:45:53.445Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 |

To install the Audit Log service on a server with internet access

Supported TimescaleDB Versions.

- **PostgreSQL 16**: TimescaleDB 2.13–2.25
- **PostgreSQL 17**: TimescaleDB 2.17–2.25
- **PostgreSQL 18**: TimescaleDB 2.23–2.25

## Installation Steps

1. Installation the **PostgreSQL** package (postgresql-16) as per below steps:

   1. Update and list PostgreSQL package.

```
sudo su
apt update
apt list postgresql*
apt install postgresql-16*
```

b. Go to **pg\_hba.conf** file.

```
# vi /etc/postgresql/16/main/pg_hba.conf
```

c. Edit the configuration as per below:

```
local   all             postgres                                peer
# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# "local" is for Unix domain socket connections only
local   all             all                                     peer
# IPv4 local connections:
host    all             all             0.0.0.0/32              md5
# IPv6 local connections:
host    all             all             ::1/128                 scram-sha-256
# Allow replication connections from localhost, by a user with the
# replication privilege.
local   replication     all                                     peer
host    replication     all             0.0.0.0/32              md5
host    replication     all             ::1/128                 scram-sha-256

host timescale_db timescale_user <internal_hera_ip>/32 md5
host timescale_db timescale_user <internal_zeus_ip>/32 md5
host timescale_db timescale_user <internal_spark_worker_ip>/32 md5
```

d. Go to **postgresql.conf** file

```
vi /etc/postgresql/16/main/postgresql.conf
```

e. Edit the configuration as per below:

```
data_directory = '/data/postgresql/16/main'             
hba_file = '/data/postgresql/16/main/pg_hba.conf'      
ident_file = '/data/postgresql/16/main/pg_ident.conf'

listen_addresses = '*'
log_directory = '/data/logs/postgresql'
log_filename = 'postgresql-%Y-%m-%d.log'
```

f. Create directory for PostgreSQL data.

```
mkdir -p /data/postgresql/16/main
chown -R postgres:postgres /data/postgresql/
```

g. Create directory for PostgreSQL data.

```
mkdir -p /data/postgresql/16/main
chown -R postgres:postgres /data/postgresql/
```

h. Copy config to data directory.

```
cp -avr /var/lib/postgresql/16 /data/postgresql/
cp -avr /etc/postgresql/16/main/postgresql.conf /data/postgresql/16/main
cp -avr /etc/postgresql/16/main/pg_hba.conf /data/postgresql/16/main
cp -avr /etc/postgresql/16/main/pg_ident.conf /data/postgresql/16/main
```

i. Create PostgreSQL service.

```
vi /usr/lib/systemd/system/postgresql.service
```

```
[Unit]
Description=PostgreSQL RDBMS

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/true
ExecReload=/bin/true
Environment=PGDATA=/data/postgresql/16/main
RemainAfterExit=on

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

j. Start PostgreSQL service.

```
systemctl start postgresql
tail -1000f /var/log/postgresql/postgresql-16-main.log
```

2. Installation the **TimescaleDB**(timescaledb-16) as per below steps:

a. Add the TimescaleDB package.

```
echo "deb https://packagecloud.io/timescale/timescaledb/ubuntu/ $(lsb_release -c -s) main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/timescaledb.list
```

b. Install the TimescaleDB GPG key.

```
# wget --quiet -O - https://packagecloud.io/timescale/timescaledb/gpgkey | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/timescaledb.gpg
```

c. Update local repository list.

```
sudo apt update
```

d. Install TimescaleDB using a below command. (Recommend TimescaleDB version 2.13-2.25)

For other PostgreSQL versions such as version 15 or 18, requires to change the parameter like `timescaledb-2-postgresql-15 postgresql-client-15` or  `timescaledb-2-postgresql-18 postgresql-client-18`.

```
sudo apt install timescaledb-2-postgresql-16 postgresql-client-16
```

3. Run command below.

```
timescaledb-tune
```

4. Restart the PostgresSQL.
5. Login PostgresSQL as Postgres user.
6. Create an user to use for writing log.

```
create user timescale_user with password 'password';
```

7. Create a database for operation.

```
create database timescale_db;
```

8. Grant all permissions to this user.

```
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE timescale_db TO timescale_user;
```

9. Then connect to newly created database and grant a schema public to this user.

```
\c timescale_db
GRANT ALL ON SCHEMA public TO timescale_user;
```

10. Then login with newly created user.

```
Psql -h localhost -U timescale_user -d timescale_db
```

11. Enable a timescale extension.

```
CREATE EXTENSION timescaledb;
```

12. Edit the **postgres.conf** listeners to `listen_addresses = '*'` to allow a remote connection.
13. Edit the **pg\_hba.conf** to allow an access of this user and this database from remote access.

```
host timescale_db timescale_user {privateHeraIp}/32 md5 
host timescale_db timescale_user {privateZeusIp}/32 md5
host timescale_db timescale_user {privateSparkWorkerIp}/32 md5
```

- Replace your private Hera IP with privateHeraIp.
- Replace your private Zeus IP with privateZeusIp.
- Replace your Spark worker IP with privateSparkWorkerIp

14. Setup on the Hera with this config below:

![image-20241030-045127.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2276065281/image-20241030-045127.png?api=v2)

- **create.access.log**: Set as true.
- **default.access.log.retention.time**: The default retention time applies to logs saved in the database. If a log exceeds the set retention time, it will be deleted.
- **postgres.host** : The host of the PostgresSQL.
- **postgres.port** : The port of the PostgresSQL that is installed from above steps.
- **postgres.username**: The username of user that is created from above steps.
- **postgres.password**: The password of user that is created from above steps.
- **postgres.databas**e: The database name that is created from above steps.
- **default.hypertable.chunk.interval**: The properties that define the audit log table's partitioning into chunks. If set to 1 day, then the partition for the audit log is set to every day. If set to 2 days, then the audit log is set to partition every 2 days, so the chunk should not be separated too much, and the data inside the chunk should also not be too much. (The default is 1 day)

15. **Enable the audit log** on Blendata Enterprise by following the steps.
16. Open the Blendata Enterprise.
17. Import the PostgresSQL database into the Blendata Enterprise using the information from Audit Log setting steps.
18. Select “**Select Database Tables**“ option and click “**Next**“.

![postSQL.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2276065281/postSQL.png?api=v2)

4. Select the **public\_access\_log** table and set table name as “**access\_log**“.
5. Click “**Next**“ to complete the PostgreSQL table import.

![postSQL seelct table.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2276065281/postSQL%20seelct%20table.png?api=v2)

6. You can see the audit log from data preview option, Data Exploration menu, or create an table visualization to filter the fields.

![viz log.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2276065281/viz%20log.png?api=v2)

**public\_access\_log** is a database that stores all events or activities occurring in Blendata Enterprise, which consists of the following fields:

- **id**: Log id value.
- **created\_date**: The date when the action was performed.
- **username**: The username who performed an event or activity.
- **method**: The API method types including POST, GET, PUT, and DELETE.
- **end\_path**: The API urls.
- **response\_code**: The response code value.
- **request\_body**: The request body includes the parameter of the API. (Sensitive data, such as password value or API key in the request\_body, will be masked.)
- **error\_message**: The error message that display after running the API.

This audit log also has a housekeeping process configured in the settings. By default, logs are stored in the database for 7 days. Once a log exceeds the retention period, it is automatically deleted.

---

## SDK Drop Table

| Field | Value |
|---|---|

This function is used to drop a table from Blendata Data Catalog and Notebook.

1. Import **blendata\_util**.
2. Fill all **Parameters**.
3. Call function **blendata\_util.hera\_api\_dropTable(…)**.

**For example**: Pyspark

```
%pyspark
import blendata_util

tableName = "table_a"
blendata_util.hera_api_dropTable(sc, tableName)
```

- **sc:** Spark context.
- **tableName:** The table name.

This paragraph can drop one table at a time.

[blendata\_util\_pyspark\_ex…](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2305425427/SDK+Drop+Table?preview=%2F2305425427%2F2305425489%2Fblendata_util_pyspark_example.zpln)

## SDK Get Module Notebook Name

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2305720350/SDK+Get+Module+Notebook+Name |
| **Last modified** | 2025-05-27T03:57:33.694Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data > Notebook |

This function is used to get the module notebook name or the environment name of the notebook.

1. Import **blendata\_util**.
2. Call function **blendata\_util.getModuleNotebookName(…)**.

**For example**: Pyspark

```
%pyspark
import blendata_util

blendata_util.getModuleNotebookName()
```

**You can find the SDK template below**:

[blendata\_util\_pyspark\_ex…](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2305720350/SDK+Get+Module+Notebook+Name?preview=%2F2305720350%2F2305622088%2Fblendata_util_pyspark_example.zpln)

---

## Sync Notebook Profile between Zeppelin and Git

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2308833295/Sync+Notebook+Profile+between+Zeppelin+and+Git |
| **Last modified** | 2025-09-01T14:40:07.752Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data > Notebook |

# How to push the Notebook to Git Server

## Commit the version via Notebook

1. Open your Notebook.
2. Click “**Version Control**” option and fill in a revision name.

![commit.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/commit.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Commit**” button.
4. Confirm to commit the Notebook in current repository.

The Notebook is now automatically pushed to the Git branch. Users can view previous commits and restore them for use in the current version.

## Push to Git Action on UI

1. Select a single Notebook or multiple Notebooks.
2. Click action button:3dots:and select **Push to Git** option.
3. Confirm to commit message in current repository and click “**Confirm Push**”.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/image-20250530-065851.png?api=v2)

This action is applicable for **Edit** and **Manage** permission levels.

Once users setup the Github configuration and push the Notebook to Git server, there are two status tag displayed on Notebook as below:

- **Unstaged**: This note has been modified in your local notebook environment, but hasen’t been pushed to the Git server yet.
- **Untracked**: This note was created locally and doesn’t exist on the Git server yet.

![image-20250828-051847.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/image-20250828-051847.png?api=v2)

---

# Sync Notebook profile Action

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/git%20sync%20+%20action.png?api=v2)

## Sync

Sync option is used to update the Notebook profile between the local notebook environment and Git server. In case of a conflict where both the Git location and Zeppelin are updated with the same Notebook profile simultaneously, an error may occur, '**Hard Pull**' feature, which forces synchronization by overwriting Zeppelin with the latest data from Git.

![image-20250828-052219.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/image-20250828-052219.png?api=v2)

1. Click “**Sync**” option.
2. Fill in your current Git branch name.
3. (Optional) Enable “**Hard Pull Sync from Git**”.

   - If users select this option, a backup option will be display, which all Notebooks in your local environment will be backup on backend.

![image-20250828-052255.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/image-20250828-052255.png?api=v2)

If you need to restore a backup file, please contact the super administrator to restore the backup Notebook in your environment.

4. Click “**Sync**”button to update the Notebook profile.

## Branch

This is the branch location your current Git server.

![image-20250829-044147.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/image-20250829-044147.png?api=v2)

### Checkout

This option is used to change the notebook environment by switching the Git branch. In case, users need to overwrite all Notebooks in the current local environment with selected branch, you can enable “**Hard Checkout**“ option.

![checkout.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/checkout.png?api=v2)

1. Select a branch.
2. (Optional) Enable the “**Hard Checkout**“ option.

   - If you select this option, you need to fill in the current local branch name.

![checkout with hard option.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/checkout%20with%20hard%20option.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Checkout**“ button.

### Create New Branch

To create a new branch in your environment, but the new branch will duplicated from current branch.

![create new branch.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/create%20new%20branch.png?api=v2)

1. Fill in a new branch name.
2. Click “**Create New Branch**“.

### Manage Git Branch

Users are able to manage the Git branch and local branch by deleting the branch in this option.

![manage git branch.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/manage%20git%20branch.png?api=v2)

1. Click ![(error)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/error.png) icon to delete the branch.
2. Enter your branch name to confirm.

![image-20250829-052107.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2308833295/image-20250829-052107.png?api=v2)

3. Click “**Delete**“ button.

---

## Enable Github Storage Plugin in Notebook

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2309783569/Enable+Github+Storage+Plugin+in+Notebook |
| **Last modified** | 2025-09-12T09:08:25.953Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Post-Installation > Configuration > Notebook (Zeppelin) Configuration |

# Zeppelin Configuration

1. Edit **conf/zeppelin-site.xml** in Zeppelin.

   - **zeppelin.notebook.storage:** Fill in “org.apache.zeppelin.notebook.repo.GitHubNotebookRepo”
   - **zeppelin.notebook.git.remote.url:** Fill in URL like *https://<USERNAME>:<TOKEN>@github.com/<USERNAME>/<REPOSITORY>.git*
   - **zeppelin.notebook.git.remote.username:** Fill in username of Git.
   - **zeppelin.notebook.git.remote.access-token:** Fill in Git token from topic 2.
   - **zeppelin.notebook.git.remote.origin:** Fill in an origin (origin or origin of used git repo). For example: https://auttakrit:REDACTED_TOKEN@bitbucket.org/blendata/note235.git

```
<property>
 <name>zeppelin.notebook.git.remote.url</name>
 <value>https://username:token@github.com/username/repository.git</value>
 <description>remote Git repository URL</description>
</property>
```

# GitHub Remote Access Token

1. Click “[token](https://github.com/settings/tokens)” option on Github [develop settings](https://github.com/settings/apps) page.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2309783569/AD_4nXfHejzTlQsL1oG-HMwLVb2Zse5T7rftzztOb5pOSYU-Hm45ngFU5nBHsBFtvZmx_9GlJfNQLUgcP5pwFICRIYMKQV2G60gDWFH12wv3ig7dvisfa-gnZJkRD5qoP-Oz_nzywzsCWA%3Fkey=9KHFq8loMvw16u3DIxC45tkz?api=v2)

2. Click “**Generate new token**” button and select the method including classis or specific scope.
3. Fill in the generated token from step2 in **zeppelin.notebook.git.remote.url** and **zeppelin.notebook.git.remote.username** parameter.

# Setup Git in Local Storage Path

To indicate the primary branch for Git and Notebook connections, as illustrated in the example below, the 'develop' branch is used as the main branch.

1. Go to **path /notebook** on Zeppelin.
2. Put **git checkout -b zeppelin\_2**
3. Put **git push origin zeppelin\_2**
4. Put **git checkout -b develop origin/zeppelin\_2**
5. Put **git branch --set-upstream-to=origin/zeppelin\_2 zeppelin\_2**

After users config these steps, you need to restart the Zeppelin. Then you can use **Sync Notebook Profile between Zeppelin and Git** in the Notebook.

In case, users encounter a problem that **cannot fetch Git origin**, users can follow below steps:

```
git checkout -b main
git push origin main
```

Then, select the main branch and it will work as normal.

---

## Notebook Permission

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2360377350/Notebook+Permission |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-26T10:50:19.003Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data > Notebook |

In the Notebook menu, there are two layers of permission management: the Notebook environment and individual Notebooks. The permissions for each action are provided as shown below.

## Notebook Environment

In this layer, users with the following roles are allowed to set up the Notebook environment via the Blendata Enterprise UI under the Realm Management and User Management menus, as shown below.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **System** | **Super Administrator** | **Administrator** |
| Edit Environment | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Permission Management | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Delete Environment | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Sync and Git Management | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

## Notebook

Each individual Notebook has its permissions configured by the Administrator role. There are three permission levels: View, Edit, and Manage. Each permission level allows different actions, as shown in the table below.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **View** | **Edit** | **Manage** |
| Open Notebook | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Edit Notebook | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Permission Management | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Push to Git/ Commit | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Run Paragraph/ Run Notebook | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Export Notebook | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Export All Notebooks | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Delete Notebook | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |

---

## Schema Permission

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2372829194/Schema+Permission |
| **Last modified** | 2025-08-01T17:35:51.522Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Admin Setting Menus > Schema Management |

## Schema Management Menu

If you have access to this menu, it means you have the permission to:

1. Create new schemas
2. View existing schemas (You must have view permission or higher on that schema.)
3. Edit existing schemas (You must have edit or manage permission on that schema.)
4. Delete existing schemas (You must have delete or manage permission on that schema.)
5. [**Manage Permissions**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2372829194/Schema+Permission#Manage-Permission-Details) on schemas (You must have manage permission on that schema.)
6. Add permission to access multiple schemas (You must have manage permission on that schema.)

Access to this menu can be granted by **System**, **Super Administrator**, and **Administrator** roles via the “**User Management**” menu.

Except for the System and Super Administrator roles, which have access to this menu  
by default.

---

## **Manage Permission Details**

For more details about Manage Permission, clicking the action will navigate you to the section below.

- **Schema Permission:** grants access to manage schema-level operations such as viewing, editing, or deleting schemas.
- **All asset Privileges:** grants access to manage all data assets (e.g., tables) within the selected schema.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **Schema Permission** | | | | **All asset Privileges** | | | |
| **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** | **Create** | **View All** | **Edit All** | **Delete All** |
| View selected schema only *(No access to tables)* | (blue star) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Edit selected schema info and all tables *(Auto-grants "View Schema" "View All" and "Edit All")* | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: |  |
| Delete selected schema and all tables *(Auto-grants "View Schema" "View All" and "Delete All")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  | (blue star) |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: |  | :checkbox\_gray: |
| Full access to permitted schema and all tables, including permission management. | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: |
| Enables creating new tables within the schema. *(Auto-grants "View Schema")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  | (blue star) |  |  |  |
| Enables viewing all tables within the schema. *(Auto-grants "View Schema")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  |  | (blue star) |  |  |
| Enables modifying all tables and their data. *(Auto-grants "View Schema" and "View All")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |  |
| Enables deleting all tables and their data. *(Auto-grants "View Schema" and "View All")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: |  | (blue star) |

Even when access is granted automatically, users can still manually **assign additional** permissions.

---

By default, our platform creates an initial schema named “default”, and every user is granted permissions as described below.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Schema named** | **Schema Permission** | | | | **All asset Privileges** | | | |
| **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** | **Create** | **View All** | **Edit All** | **Delete All** |
| default *\*\*This is the default schema in our platform\*\** | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  |

Another case is when you are the owner of a schema *(creating a schema requires access to the* [*Schema Management menu*](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2372829194/Schema+Permission#Schema-Management-Menu)*)*; as the owner, you will be granted the permissions described below.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **Schema Permission** | | | | **All asset Privileges** | | | |
| **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** | **Create** | **View All** | **Edit All** | **Delete All** |
| Full Access to Selected Schema and all assets *\*\*If you are the owner of the schema\*\** | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: |

---

## Activate The New License file

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2381185026/Activate+The+New+License+file |
| **Last modified** | 2026-03-09T07:29:58.693Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Post-Installation > Related Document |

# Activate The New License file

1. Download the license file from the Blendata License Management website or contact the Blendata Support team.
2. Go to Blendata Enterprise website.
3. Go to “**Setting**” and click “**License Management**“menu :lms:.
4. Click three dots button and select “**Active New License**“.

![lm12.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2381185026/lm12.png?api=v2)

5. Click “**Open File Browser**” to Upload the License file.

![lm13.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2381185026/lm13.png?api=v2)

6. Click “**Activate**” button :lm2: to activate the license.

---

## Workflow Permission

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2392293385/Workflow+Permission |
| **Last modified** | 2025-07-23T11:44:35.323Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Workflow Management |

The Workflow permission has its own permission levels, which are used to manage workflows, as shown in the table below. In addition, data asset permissions and Notebook permissions are also required when running services or task jobs in the workflow.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** |
| Edit a Workflow Configuration | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Deploy a Workflow | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Activate/Deactivate a Workflow | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Execute Immediately | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Execute with Configuration | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Manage Permission | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Duplicate a Workflow | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Delete a Workflow | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Export a Workflow | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Set Notification | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |

If a user is not the owner and executes the workflow, either immediately or with configuration, that user will use the execute permission rights of the owner.

---

## Services Permission

| Field | Value |
|---|---|

Service Management has its own permission levels, which are used to manage service job in the Blendata Enterprise, as shown in the table below. These below permission is not applicable for system service job.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** |
| Start/Stop a Service | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| View a Service | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Execute a Service\* | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Rerun a Service\* | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Edit a Service and Manage Zeus | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Delete a Service | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Manage Permission | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |

\***Execute** and **rerun a service** need the data asset permission at least View level.

| Refresh Table | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Recovery Table | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
