| Update Table | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Refresh Table | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Recovery Table | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Upload New Data (upload file) | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Truncate Table | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Publish Asset | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Change Google Drive Profile | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Import Data to Existing Table | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |

---

## Notebook Permission (V 4.6.9)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2411397176/Notebook+Permission+V+4.6.9 |
| **Last modified** | 2025-08-21T05:53:34.427Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data > Notebook |

In the Notebook menu, there are three layers of permission management: the Notebook environment, Workspace and Notebooks. The permissions for each action are provided as shown below.

## Notebook Environment

In this layer, users with the following roles are allowed to set up the Notebook environment via the Blendata Enterprise UI under the Realm Management and User Management menus, as shown below.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **System** | **Super Administrator** | **Administrator** |
| Edit Environment | (blue star) | (blue star) | ❌ |
| Permission Management | (blue star) | (blue star) | (blue star) |
| Delete Environment | (blue star) | (blue star) | ❌ |
| Sync and Git Management | (blue star) | (blue star) | (blue star) |

## Workspace

Our platform provides **two types of permission management**, depending on the workspace type:

1. **Git Workspace**: Permissions are managed only at the workspace level, Users cannot assign permissions for individual notebooks.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** |
| Allows users to view workspace, create notes and view all notes | (blue star) |  |  |  |
| Allows users to Edit, Export, and Push all notes to Git, and Edit Workspace info. *(Auto-grants: view workspace, create notes and view all notes)* | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |  |  |
| Allows users to delete the workspace and all notebooks within it. *(Auto-grants: View & Edit workspace information, create notes, View, Edit, export, and push all notes to Git)* | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |  |
| Full access to permitted workspace and all notes in permitted workspace, perform Git sync, and including permission management. | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |

2. **Local Workspace**: Permissions can be managed at both the workspace level and all notebooks (all asset in workspace) level.

   - **Workspace**: Manage permissions at the workspace level to control access to all notebooks within the workspace.
   - **All Asset Privileges**: Grants broad access to all notebooks inside the workspace

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **Local Workspace** | | | | **All Asset Privileges** | | |
| **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** | **View All** | **Edit All** | **Delete All** |
| Allows users to access workspace and create Notes. | (blue star) |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Allows users to edit workspace info, export and push all notes to Git. *(Auto-grants: access workspace, create notes, view & edit all notes in permitted workspace)* | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: |  |
| Allows users to delete workspace . (Auto-grants: access & edit workspace info, create notes, view, edit delete, export and push all notes to git in permitted workspace) | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |  | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: |
| Full access to permitted workspace and all notes in permitted workspace, including permission management. | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: |
| Allows users to view all notes in permitted workspace. *(Auto-grants: access workspace, create notes)* | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  | (blue star) |  |  |
| Allows users to edit, export, and push all notes to Git. (Auto-grants: access workspace, create notes, and view all notes in permitted workspace.) | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |  |
| Allows users to delete all notes in permitted workspace. (Auto-grants: access workspace, create notes, and view, edit, export, and push all notes to Git in permitted workspace.) | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |

**Run Paragraph / Run Notebook :** Available to users with view or higher permission on the notebook.

---

## Workspace Management

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2471329794/Workspace+Management |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:52:30.202Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data > Notebook |

Before version 4.6.9, the Workspace feature was not available in the Notebook, and users could only view individual notes directly. After upgrading to version 4.6.9, all existing notes can be migrated to the “**Shared Workspace**” by [initializing it via the HERA API](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BRN/pages/2486501381/Product+Migration+Guide+For+Notebook+workspace+From+Version+4.6.8+to+4.6.9#Step-5%3A-Shared-Workspace-Creation). (This is required not only for upgrades but also for new installations of version 4.6.9.)

# **Workspace Creation**

**1. Git Workspace**: is connected to a Git repository and provides an area for storing notebooks under a Zeppelin environment. Our platform offers a user-friendly interface to support the connection and synchronization process. See more details in [Sync Notebook Profile between Zeppelin and Git](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/DwCeiQ).

![image-20250829-032515.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250829-032515.png?api=v2)

- **Workspace Name**: Fill in your workspace name.
- **Description**: Fill in your workspace description.
- **Tags** **(Optional)**: This option is useful for organizing Workspaces for different purposes. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![tag notebook workspace (v4.6.13).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/tag%20notebook%20workspace%20(v4.6.13).png?api=v2)

- **Type**: Select the Git option.
- **Git URL**: Enter the Git repository URL and our platform supports three Git providers:

  - GitHub
  - GitLab
  - Bitbucket
- **Git Username**: Fill in your Git Username.
- **Token**: Fill in the token generated from Git using a Personal Access Token (PAT).
- **Test Connection**: Click to verify the connection to your Git repository.

**Git workspace**: Permissions are managed at the workspace level only. Individual notebook permissions cannot be set.

**2. Local Workspace**: is not connected to Git and provides an area for storing notebooks under a Zeppelin environment.

![image-20250801-155314.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250801-155314.png?api=v2)

- **Workspace Name**: Fill in your workspace name.
- **Description**: Fill in your workspace description.
- **Tags** **(Optional)**: This option is useful for organizing Workspaces for different purposes.
- **Type**: Select the Local option.

---

# Upload Note

User can upload note via Workspace by fill in details as below

![image-20250829-031209.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250829-031209.png?api=v2)

- **Upload File**: Upload your Notebook file (currently supported formats are .zpln, .ipynb, and zip file for multiple Notebooks)
- **Workspaces**: Select your workspaces for uploading notes. (Supports multi-select workspaces)
- **Tags** **(Optional)**: This option is useful for organizing Workspaces for different purposes.
- **Mark environments**: Enabled an option to prevent upload if the Note ID already exists in the current environment.

---

# Workspace Actions

Platform provide actions to support both Git Workspace and Local Workspace, as shown below.

![image-20250926-102950.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250926-102950.png?api=v2)

![image-20250926-102902.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250926-102902.png?api=v2)

This action is now available to users who have the “**View**” permission and those who have enabled the “**Toggle”** Default Workspace Management permission under the “**User Access Rights**” menu.

## Edit Workspace Details

Users are able to edit the workspace description and tags details only.

![image-20250828-060036.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250828-060036.png?api=v2)

1. Edit the description and tag details
2. Click “**Save**” to update the new information.

## Set as Default

To set the selected workspace as your default so that when using services related to Notebook, you can leave the workspace parameter as `NULL` and the system will automatically apply your default workspace.

![image-20250926-150312.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250926-150312.png?api=v2)

## Manage Permission

Our platform provides two types of permission management, depending on the workspace type.

To configure permissions:

1. Select your Workspace.
2. Click “**Action**” button and select “**Manage Permission**” option.
3. Setup the permission by role or user and specific the permission level.

![image-20250829-033945.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250829-033945.png?api=v2)

![image-20250829-034015.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250829-034015.png?api=v2)

Only manage permission level can manage the Notebook permission. See more details about permission action, go to [Notebook Permission](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/OAC7jw) page.

## Delete Workspace

Our platform provides two types of Delete actions, as shown below.

![image-20250829-034223.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250829-034223.png?api=v2)

1. Select your workspace. (Multiple select)
2. Click action button and select **Delete** option.
3. Popup will display and users need to click “**Delete**” button.
4. (Optional) Check box to delete all note from Git repository.

![image-20250829-034242.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250829-034242.png?api=v2)

1. Select your workspace. (Multiple select)
2. Click action button and select **Delete** option.
3. Popup will display and users need to click “**Delete**” button.

## Refresh Git Connection

Use this action to refresh the connection status with the Git repository, If you experience connection issues, try refreshing to re-establish the link.

## Git Settings

Use this action to update Git credentials (username and token) when expired or changed.

![image-20250829-100529.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2471329794/image-20250829-100529.png?api=v2)

To setup this:

1. Fill in your Git Username.
2. Fill in the token generated from Git using a Personal Access Token (PAT).
3. Click Test Connection to verify the connection to your Git repository.

---

## SDK importNoteName

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2477686796/SDK+importNoteName |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-04T05:33:36.510Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Explore and Process Data > Notebook |

This function used to run and import function from another notebook

1. Import blendata\_util
2. Call function **blendata\_util.importNoteName(…)**.

- **notebookName:** The Notebook name.
- **workspaceName:** The workspace where the target notebook resides.

  - If you **provide a value**, the function will look for the notebook in the specified workspace.
  - If you do **not provide a value**, the function defaults to using the workspace where the current notebook is located.

**For example**: Pyspark

```
%pyspark
#Example2 importNoteName with specific workspace
import blendata_util

workspaceName = "workspaceA"  #Note: The variable workspaceName should be set to match the actual name created by the user
notespace_with_name = blendata_util.importNoteName("note_import", workspaceName)
globals ().update(notespace_with_name)

#call functions from note_import
print(sum(20,30))

print(hello("user"))
```

You can find the SDK template in below:

[blendata\_util\_pyspark\_ex…](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2477686796/SDK+importNoteName?preview=%2F2477686796%2F2476507150%2Fblendata_util_pyspark_example.zpln)

blendata\_utill without workspaceName and Conf parameters.

[blendata\_util\_pyspark\_ex…](/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2477686796/SDK+importNoteName?preview=%2F2477686796%2F2476507157%2Fblendata_util_pyspark_example_4_6_10.zpln)

blendata\_utill with workspaceName and Conf parameters.

Because some parameters use workspaceName and config Conf, this function is available for Blendata version 4.6.9 and above.

---

## Notebook Environment

Our platform provides **two types of permission management**, depending on the workspace type:

1. **Git Workspace**: Permissions are managed only at the workspace level, Users cannot assign permissions for individual notebooks.

### **Access Selection Types**

![(blue star)](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/s/-2069522349/6452/eaae94c6a2affaf35e508f35b397601c3535ccda/_/images/icons/emoticons/72/2705.png) : Selected by user

## Administrator User Manual

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2516221954/Administrator+User+Manual |
| **Last modified** | 2025-09-15T13:57:13.511Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 |

# Administrator

Administrator consists of two role level including user administrator and system administrator as below explanation:

**User administrator**: can manage all users and roles on the platform. Thus, this role can access to [User Management](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201420007/User+Management) and [LDAP Management](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2201420244/LDAP+Management) menu only.

![image-20250912-074851.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2516221954/image-20250912-074851.png?api=v2)

**System administrator** is a team, whois able to install the Blendata Enterprise platform, setup the system configuration, start/stop the service, and manage the platform.

## Schema Management (for end user)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2523955201/Schema+Management+for+end+user |
| **Last modified** | 2026-01-13T05:05:18.384Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Setting |

Users with permission to manage schemas in the platform. They can create new schemas, specify the storage to use, and manage permissions for each schema.

![image-20250828-041359.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2523955201/image-20250828-041359.png?api=v2)

- **Schema Name**: The name of the schema.
- **Storage Name & Path**: The name of the storage and file path.
- **Description**: Additional information or notes about the schema.
- **Access Permission**: The access rights granted to users or roles.
- **Owner**: The user who created or owns the schema.
- **Latest Modify**: The date and time when the schema was last updated.
- **Actions**: A menu to perform further operations, [see more details](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2523955201/Schema+Management+for+end+user#Schema-Management-Action).

---

# Creating Schema

1. Click “**Create New**“.
2. Fill in the schema name, description, and click “**Save**“.

![image-20260113-050420.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2523955201/image-20260113-050420.png?api=v2)

---

# Schema Management Action

![schema action.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2523955201/schema%20action.png?api=v2)

- **Edit Schema**: Edit schema description and storage.
- **Manage Permission**: Edit a schema permission.
- **Delete Schema**: Delete your selected schema.

---

# Manage Permission

1. Select a role or user to your schema and manage a permission of your selected role and user.

![image-20250715-120924.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2523955201/image-20250715-120924.png?api=v2)

Schema permission detail : <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/CoBujQ>

2. Save your schema permission setting.

---

## Schema Permission for User

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2523955290/Schema+Permission+for+User |
| **Last modified** | 2025-09-15T03:41:46.534Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Setting > Schema Management (for end user) |

## Schema Management Menu

If you have access to this menu, it means you have the permission to:

1. Create new schemas
2. View existing schemas (You must have view permission or higher on that schema.)
3. Edit existing schemas (You must have edit or manage permission on that schema.)
4. Delete existing schemas (You must have delete or manage permission on that schema.)
5. [**Manage Permissions**](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2523955290/Schema+Permission+for+User#Manage-Permission-Details) on schemas (You must have manage permission on that schema.)
6. Add permission to access multiple schemas (You must have manage permission on that schema.)

Access to this menu can be granted by **System**, **Super Administrator**, and **Administrator** roles via the “**User Management**” menu.

Except for the System and Super Administrator roles, which have access to this menu  
by default.

---

## **Manage Permission Details**

For more details about Manage Permission, clicking the action will navigate you to the section below.

- **Schema Permission:** grants access to manage schema-level operations such as viewing, editing, or deleting schemas.
- **All asset Privileges:** grants access to manage all data assets (e.g., tables) within the selected schema.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **Schema Permission** | | | | **All asset Privileges** | | | |
| **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** | **Create** | **View All** | **Edit All** | **Delete All** |
| View selected schema only *(No access to tables)* | (blue star) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Edit selected schema info and all tables *(Auto-grants "View Schema" "View All" and "Edit All")* | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: |  |
| Delete selected schema and all tables *(Auto-grants "View Schema" "View All" and "Delete All")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  | (blue star) |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: |  | :checkbox\_gray: |
| Full access to permitted schema and all tables, including permission management. | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: |
| Enables creating new tables within the schema. *(Auto-grants "View Schema")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  | (blue star) |  |  |  |
| Enables viewing all tables within the schema. *(Auto-grants "View Schema")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  |  | (blue star) |  |  |
| Enables modifying all tables and their data. *(Auto-grants "View Schema" and "View All")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: | (blue star) |  |
| Enables deleting all tables and their data. *(Auto-grants "View Schema" and "View All")* | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: |  | (blue star) |

Even when access is granted automatically, users can still manually **assign additional** permissions.

---

By default, our platform creates an initial schema named “default”, and every user is granted permissions as described below.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Schema named** | **Schema Permission** | | | | **All asset Privileges** | | | |
| **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** | **Create** | **View All** | **Edit All** | **Delete All** |
| default *\*\*This is the default schema in our platform\*\** | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  | :checkbox\_gray: |  |  |  |

Another case is when you are the owner of a schema *(creating a schema requires access to the* [*Schema Management menu*](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BEV/pages/2523955290/Schema+Permission+for+User#Schema-Management-Menu)*)*; as the owner, you will be granted the permissions described below.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Action** | **Schema Permission** | | | | **All asset Privileges** | | | |
| **View** | **Edit** | **Delete** | **Manage** | **Create** | **View All** | **Edit All** | **Delete All** |
| Full Access to Selected Schema and all assets *\*\*If you are the owner of the schema\*\** | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: | :checkbox\_gray: |

---

## NFS-Server

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2533752833/NFS-Server |
| **Last modified** | 2025-09-19T05:00:15.070Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Post-Installation > Related Document |

Zeppelin requires to access data path that Zeus is stored to process data. In some cases, Zeus and Zeppelin are not on the same machine which can cause some features or interpreters to be improperly functional. Here are two parts when setting up NFS, the server and the client. The server, in this case, is where the Zeus machine is and the client will be the Zeppelin machine. We’ll be sharing the data folder at `/data/zeus/store`.

# Prerequisites

Installing nfs-utils

```
yum install nfs-utils
```

# Setting up NFS-server

1. Choose a directory to share, if not present create one. Example: mkdir -p /data/zeus/store

```
mkdir -p /data/zeus/store
```

2. Add correct permissions and ownership to the shared directory

```
chmod -R 755 /data/zeus/store
chown <user>:<group> /data/zeus/store
```

3. Start the Nfs services

```
systemctl enable rpcbind
systemctl enable nfs-server
systemctl enable nfs-lock
systemctl enable nfs-idmap

systemctl start rpcbind
systemctl start nfs-server
systemctl start nfs-lock
systemctl start nfs-idmap
```

4. Configuring the export files for sharing

```
#Open the exports file and add these lines.
vi /etc/exports

#Fill in the the file-shared path and clients details in /etc/exports.
#eg. 192.168.48.101 - Client's IP
/data/zeus/store    <Zeppelin IP>(rw,sync,no_root_squash)
```

5. Restart the service

```
systemctl restart nfs-server
```

6. [CentOS 7 only] Override the firewall for NFS

```
firewall-cmd --permanent --zone=public --add-service=nfs
firewall-cmd --permanent --zone=public --add-service=mountd
firewall-cmd --permanent --zone=public --add-service=rpc-bind
firewall-cmd --reload
```

## Setting up NFS-Client

1. Create the mounting point, this our case will be `/data/zeus/store`

```
mkdir -p /data/zeus/store
```

2. Mounting the filesystem, eg. 10.0.0.1 server's IP

```
### mount -t nfs -o nfsvers=3 <NFS Server's IP>:<Server's shared dir> <Dest dir to mount>
mount -t nfs -o nfsvers=3 <Zeus IP>:/data/zeus/store /data/zeus/store
```

3. Verify if the folder has been mounted correctly using “df -kh”

```
[blendata@zeppelin ~]$ df -kh
Filesystem                    Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
...
192.168.48.100:/data/zeus/store   48G   13G   36G  27% /data/zeus/store
...
```

4. Mounting the folder permanently ( To prevent unmounting on reboot )

```
### <NFS Server's IP>:<Server's shared dir> <Dest dir to mount> nfs defaults 0 0
echo "192.168.48.100:/data/zeus/store /data/zeus/store nfs defaults 0 0" >> /etc/fstab
```

---

## Internal File Path

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2585100653/Internal+File+Path |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-09T02:02:04.831Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Import Data > Import Dataset > File Path |

1. Fill in the internal file path and click “**Next**“.

![image-20251008-135656.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/image-20251008-135656.png?api=v2)

Users can use the wildcard character (\*) for all file types except Excel files, which require the full filename to be specified.

2. Select the File type and identify the pattern of data.

For date type, users need to specify the source data's date format for conversion to a Spark date. If users want both date and time values, they should select the timestamp type.

3. Click “**Auto Discovery**”button :auto\_bt: to preview your data.
4. **Revise the field name** or **change the field value type** by clicking the type of field name to change the data type including String, Integer, Timestamp, Date, and etc.
5. (Option) ![image-20250613-034640.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/3b624f52-87e2-4911-97d7-0f116df5e2d9?api=v2) Enable this mode to access the interface for entering and executing SQL commands for direct data processing, featuring ![image-20250613-034513.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/68753df1-d834-4195-a831-0e26c9fd8ed8?api=v2) button that assists in writing commands with multiple field names.
6. Preview your data table.
7. Select your import option, which you need to create a new table or [Upload to Existing Table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121106502/Import+the+Data+into+Existing+table).

   - **Create New Table**: Fill in the table information such as Table name, Schema, [Time Domain](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Table-Data-Type), [Partition](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2121105672/Import+Data#Partitioning), Upload Method, and Service.

![setting ouput - internal file path.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/setting%20ouput%20-%20internal%20file%20path.png?api=v2)

**Basic Information**

- **Table Source Type**: Select the source type of table.

  - **Internal Table**: To create normal internal table.
  - **Delta table**: To create the Delta table asset.
  - **Internal State Table**: To create the data tiering from state table. Your table needs to have time field first.

- **Internal Table:** replicate the data from the various sources into our Blendata storage, which automates the ETL process for data processing and analysis.
- **Delta Table**: is Data Lake, which has operational options for data processing. This method allow the user to use CRUD commands (create, read, update, and delete the record) to extract the data. Additionally, this type of data asset can perform service vacuuming and service delta compaction, processes designed to remove old Parquet files that are no longer referenced by the current data, and to compress data files in order to reduce their size.
- **Internal State Table**: allows users to separate data storage into individual state tables to enhance data query performance and improve storage management. By customizing the duration of each state during data import with the Stream upload option, users can control how data is compressed and stored in different states such as Hot, Warm, and Cold.

- **Schema**: Select an accessible schema for your data asset.
- **Table Name:** Fill in a table name.
- **Storage Name**: The location of data storage.
- **Storage \***: The location of data storage. (Show when shown when Internal stage Table is selected).

You can set the initial storage for a schema by following this: <https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/x/MgBHgw>   
Only users with the required permissions can configure this setting.

- Other options depend on table source type selection.

  - **Encrypt Data**: To encrypt your data for the data security. (for internal table and internal state table types)
  - **Select field to Primary Key** (Optional): For Delta asset, user need to specific a field for primary key.
- **Ingest Advanced options**: Select a Zeus and pool for this table creation task.

**Time Domain**

Setup the data types, which are Non-time series and Time-series.

- **Non-time series**: The data does not have date or time field in the table for example, product data, which has product name and details or employee information.
- **Time-series**: A table may include a date or time value, such as order data with date and time or member data with a Log-in time field. Users can choose a field to indicate time, restricted to the timestamp type.

Time series option will be disabled, if there is no time field to select. In case you want to create this data as time series, please add time field from the previous step.

**Partitions**

With time series data or unique value, the platform provides an options to separate and store the data into different tables for each timing series or unique data value.

- **No partition**: For non-time series data, a partition will not be created. On the other hand, the platform will automatically generate a partition table for a time series data table.
- **Create Partitions**: Create a partition based on user settings to enhance the efficiency of data searching, processing, and reduce data extraction time.

**Upload Method**:

Select **Upload Method**: There are many options for upload the data into our platform.

![upload method.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/upload%20method.png?api=v2)

- **One-Time Upload**: a one time import the data into the platform.
- **Schedule** **Upload**: Able to schedule the time to import data into internal table as a batch.

  - **Save mode**: Select Append or Overwrite.
  - **Move to Trash after finishing import data**: This option will move the source files to trash after finishing import data to avoid duplicated records.
- **Stream Upload**: Able to upload the data with Stream service.

**Excessive data storage warning!**

Table source type 'Internal table' allows Stream upload, but it may lead to  
excessive data storage. We recommend using Time Series and **switching to 'Internal State table'**

**Services**

Show all your related source jobs information based on Table Source Type and Upload method.

Internal table

![internal (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/internal%20(schedule).png?api=v2)![internal (stream).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/internal%20(stream).png?api=v2)

Internal state table

![state (one time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/state%20(one%20time).png?api=v2)![state (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/state%20(schedule).png?api=v2)![state (stream).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/state%20(stream).png?api=v2)

Delta table

![delta (one-time).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/delta%20(one-time).png?api=v2)![Delta (schedule).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/Delta%20(schedule).png?api=v2)

- **Stream Service**: Once you select upload method as Stream, this stream service will enable automatically. Users can setup the stream service setting via **Change Customization**.

  - **Interval**: Fill in the stream interval. (Second unit)
  - **Number of File Output**: Fill in your number of file.

![stream (internal table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/stream%20(internal%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream (state table).png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/stream%20(state%20table).png?api=v2)

![stream setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/stream%20setting.png?api=v2)

- **State Tables:** To setup the data storage is separated into individual state tables via **Change Customization**. (available for Internal State Table source type only) See more [State table](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BE1/pages/1869709782/State+Table) page.

![custom state setting.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/custom%20state%20setting.png?api=v2)

For the state table service setting, the Cold state must set a minimum storage time of 90 days for data in the platform following the standard log management policy.

- **Change Customization**: This option allows users to set the service related your import data type and upload method.

![custom state setting2.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2585100653/custom%20state%20setting2.png?api=v2)

8. Click “**Next**” and go to **Data Catalog** menu :catalog: to see your new data asset.

---

## Single Value

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2618982441/Single+Value |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-17T10:12:45.006Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

[data-colorid=fv8tbspbzw]{color:#ff5630} html[data-color-mode=dark] [data-colorid=fv8tbspbzw]{color:#cf2600}To present the data as a single value on the visualization such as total sold, or overview numbers.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2618982441/single%20value.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Value(numeric)\***: Select the numeric field of dataset and represent in a Gauge chart.
- **Sort**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Single Value Option**

  - **Background Color**: Setup the background color of your data.
  - **Value Color**: Setup the text value color.
  - **Value Size**: Setup the text size such as 10px or 20 px.
- **Sub Value Option**

  - **Show Sub Value**: Enable or disable to display the sub-value, which is the comparison with the main number.
  - **Compare With**: Select the comparison options such as last value, average value, or first value.
  - **Sub Value Diff**: Enable to display the difference between latest value and second-to-last value.
  - **Sub Value Color**: Setup the sub-value text color.
  - **Sub Value Size**: Setup the sub-value size.
  - **Arrow Size**: Setup the arrow size.
- **Text Option**

  - **Prefix Text:** Fill in the prefix of your text, which is located above your value.
  - **Prefix Text Size**: Setup the prefix text size.
  - **Prefix Text Color**: Setup the prefix text color.
  - **Suffix Text**: Fill in the suffix of your text, which is located below your value.
  - **Suffix Text Size**: Setup the suffix text size.
  - **Suffix Text Color**: Setup the suffix text color.
- **Spark Line Option**

  - **Show Sparkline**: Enable the line charts below the output value.
  - **Connect Null Data**: To connect the line between null values and values greater than 1.
  - **Line Tension**: Setup the tension line.
  - **Line Color**: Setup the line color.
  - **Line Width**: Setup the width of line.
  - **Line Height**: Setup the height of line.
- **Icon Option**

  - **Icon**: Fill in the icon name, which you can use from this [website](https://fontawesome.com/v4.7.0/icons/). (Using the name like “fa fa-male”)
  - **Icon Color**: Setup the icon color.
  - **Icon Size**: Setup the icon size.

---

## Wordcloud

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2624421890/Wordcloud |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-20T09:46:19.506Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

This wordcloud chart is used to display the frequency or importance of words in a text dataset, with the font size indicating their weight. The words that appear more frequently or have higher importance are shown in larger text than others.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2624421890/wordcloud.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Value(numeric)\***: Select the numeric field of dataset and represent in a chart.
- **Word**: Select the string field of dataset and represent as a word in a chart.
- **Sorting**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Wordcloud Option**

  - **Shape**: Select the chart shapes such as circle, diamond, square, triangle, cardioid, or star.
  - **Font Color**: Setup the font color.
  - **Background Color**: Setup the background color.
  - **Word Spacing**: Setup the space between words.
  - **Word Scale**: Setup the word scale in the chart.
- **Title**

  - **Title Text**: Fill in the title text of the chart.
  - **Title Text Color**: Setup the title text color.
  - **Title Text Size**: Setup the title text size.

---

## Topology

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2625339416/Topology |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-21T13:47:40.630Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

A Topology Chart is used to visually represent the relationships and connections between components in a system, such as servers, databases, networks, or applications. It helps users understand how different elements interact, identify dependencies, and detect potential points of failure.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2625339416/topology.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Node Value**: Select a field from dataset to be a node.
- **Status**: Select a field from dataset to be a node status.
- **Total**: Select a field from dataset to be a number of node.
- **Node Name**: Specify the value for node display.
- **Link**: Indicate the value, which used to create the connection line between nodes.
- **Sorting**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Topology Option**

  - **Margin Top**: Specifies the top margin distance of the chart.
  - **Margin Left**: Specifies the left margin distance of the chart.
  - **Margin Bottom**: Specifies the bottom margin distance of the chart.
  - **Margin Right**: Specifies the right margin distance of the chart.
  - **Distance X**:  Setup the length of x-axis line.
  - **Distance Y**: Setup the length of y-axis line.
  - **Icon Width**: Setup the icon width.
  - **Icon Height**: Setup the icon height.
  - **Font Color**: Setup the font color.
  - **Font Size**: Setup the front size.
  - **Font Label Size**: Setup the label size.
  - **Image Icon**: Setup the image icon using the icon file name such as “**server.png**”.

---

## Leaflet Map

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2627436557/Leaflet+Map |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-21T14:59:01.957Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

Leaflet Map is an open-source JavaScript library used to create interactive and responsive web maps. It allows users to visualize geographic data with features like markers, layers, and popups. Commonly used in dashboards or data analytics platforms, Leaflet Map helps display location-based insights, such as customer distribution or routes, in a clear and interactive way.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2627436557/map.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Latitude**: Select a latitude field from the dataset.
- **Longitude**:  Select a longitude field from the dataset.
- **Measure**: Select a measure field from the dataset that will be represent in the chart.
- **Sorting**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Map option**

  - **Tilelayer Server**: Fill in the URL link to create a map in the chart.
  - **Default Location**: Specify the latitude and longitude to use when previewing the chart.
  - **Zoom Level**: Fill in the zoom level between 0-18.
  - **Max Zoom**: Fill in the maximum zoom between 0-18.
  - **Popup Template**: Fill in the information value as “**{value}**” format.

---

## Heatmap

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2628288522/Heatmap |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-22T05:50:35.134Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

A heatmap is a data visualization chart that represents the magnitude or intensity of values using color gradients. It helps users quickly identify patterns, trends, and outliers in large datasets by visually mapping higher or lower values to different colors. Heatmaps are commonly used to display correlations, activity levels, or frequency distributions. For example, showing website click activity, sales performance by region, or temperature variations over time.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2628288522/heatmap.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Y Label**:  Select a field from the dataset to represent the values on the Y-axis.
- **X Label**:  Select a field from the dataset to represent the values on the X-axis.
- **Value**:  Select a field from the dataset to represent the value as quantity number.
- **Sorting**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **HeatMap Option**

  - **Range**: Specific the value range for this chart.
  - **Label Set**: Set the label for each range.
  - **Fill Color Set**: Fill in the set of color as color code format such as “**#fd995d, #e78049**”.
  - **Font Size**: Setup the font size. (Default size is 15 px)
  - **Item Size**: Setup the item size. (Default size is 40)
  - **X Axis Position**: Select the X-axis position such as top and bottom.
  - **X Axis Rotation**: Setup the rotation for X-axis.
  - **Y Axis Position**: Select the Y-axis position such as left and right.
  - **Y Axis Rotation**: Setup the rotation for Y-axis.
  - **Margin Top**: Specifies the top margin distance of the chart.
  - **Margin Left**: Specifies the left margin distance of the chart.
  - **Margin Bottom**: Specifies the bottom margin distance of the chart.
  - **Margin Right**: Specifies the right margin distance of the chart.
- **Label Config**

  - **Label Value Type**: Show the label on a graph by setting type as per below.

    - **Value**: Show the real value on a graph.
  - **Precision**: To show the precision for percentage value. (Put a value from 0-5)
  - **Font Size**: Setup the font size.
  - **Font Color**: Setup the font color.

---

## Sankey

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2628878342/Sankey |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-21T14:29:06.544Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Visualization & Dashboard > Visualization > Visualization Creation |

A **Sankey** chart is a type of flow diagram that visualizes the movement or flow of data, resources, or energy between different stages or categories. The width of the arrows or paths represents the quantity or magnitude of the flow. It is commonly used to illustrate processes like energy transfers, cost breakdowns, or user journey paths.

![](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2628878342/sankey.PNG?api=v2)

## Data

- **Source**: Select a field from the dataset to represent the source value on the left side of the chart.
- **Target**: Select a field from the dataset to represent the target value on the right side of the chart.
- **Value**: Select a field from the dataset that represents the relationship value between the source and target. The width of the arrows is determined by this value.
- **Sorting**: Sort the data by Ascending(ASC) or Descending(DESC) order and also use the Custom Sort to sort the data by specific the field name and apply ASC or DESC order.
- **Filters**: Filter some data to present in the chart.
- **Limit**: To limit the number of data records in the chart.

## Options

- **Sankey Option**

  - **Margin Top**: Specifies the top margin distance of the chart.
  - **Margin Left**: Specifies the left margin distance of the chart.
  - **Margin Bottom**: Specifies the bottom margin distance of the chart.
  - **Margin Right**: Specifies the right margin distance of the chart.
  - **Height**: Setup the chart height.
  - **Width**: Setup the chart width.

## File Retention Job

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2631467022/File+Retention+Job |
| **Last modified** | 2026-02-23T05:56:04.399Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Data Policy & Service Management > Services |

# **How to create File Retention Job**

**Step 1**: Fill in the service information including File Type Service Name, set a notification via Job Triggering, and put the service tag.

- **File Type Service Name \***: Fill in the name for this service.
- **Job Triggering \***: Define the schedule to automatically run this service.

  - **Cron Schedule**: To set a schedule for the service using cron expression (Runs repeatedly).
  - **Specific Time**: To specify the date and time to run this service (One-time execution).
  - **Immediately**: To run this service immediately, after this service job is created.
- **Tags**: Type and press Enter to add a new tag for this service. Tags are useful for searching and organizing your own services. (For version 4.6.13 or newer, super administrators are able to remove tags from the tag list in the platform UI.)

![image-20251022-104358.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2631467022/image-20251022-104358.png?api=v2)

**Step 2**: Fill in a config of service on Service Parameters box. The platform already provided the initial template for the user.

```
{
  "path": "/data/zeus/store/table_test/host=*/dt=*",
  "olderThan": 30,
  "isTrash": true,
  "excludePaths": [
    "/data/zeus/up*/export*"
  ],
  "zeusId": 1,
  "zeusGroupId": null
}
```

- **path**: The file location. Wildcard characters (\*) are supported for matching multiple folders or files.
- **olderThan**: Fill in the number of days condition to check the old file for the retention service.
- **isTrash**:

  - **isTrash**: **true**, the files are moved to the trash for temporary retention and can be restored later.
  - **isTrash**: **false**, the files are permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.

**Note**: Minimize the retention period to 90 days. This ensures that old records in the data asset are retained for 90 days or longer before the system automatically removes them.

- **excludePaths**: Fill in the excluded path. The files under this location won’t be removed.

**For example**: Using Wildcard characters (\*).

```
{
  "path": "/data/temp_test/*/*/*.har",
  "olderThan": 90,
  "isTrash": true,
  "excludePaths": [
    "/data/temp_test/*/*2"
  ],
  "realmId": 1
}
```

This configuration instructs the system to remove all`.har`files located under the path `/data/temp_test/*/*/` that are older than 90 days.

**For example**, these two files will be deleted if they are older than 90 days.

```
/data/temp_test/2025/01/file.har
/data/temp_test/user01/tmp/data.har
```

---

## Zeppelin Metastore Configuration (Shared Metastore with Zeus)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2655748097/Zeppelin+Metastore+Configuration+Shared+Metastore+with+Zeus |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-29T09:42:18.691Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Post-Installation > Configuration > Notebook (Zeppelin) Configuration |

Previously, Zeppelin and Zeus used their own Metastore databases. Therefore, when users needed to access a data asset from the Data Catalog, they had to add the data asset to the Notebook menu. Now, users can configure the system to use the same Metastore as Zeus by following the settings below.

# Enable Shared Metastore with Zeus

1. Open **conf/Interpreter.json** file from Zeppelin directory.
2. Find the Spark interpreter section. It will look like below example:

```
"spark": {
  "id": "spark",
  "name": "spark",
    .
    .
  "properties": {
    .
    .
    .
    },
  }
}
```

3. Update properties value of `spark.sql.extensions` to be "**io.delta.sql.DeltaSparkSessionExtension,co.blendata.spark.SparkExtension**"

```
"spark.sql.extensions": {
  "name": "spark.sql.extensions",
  "value": "io.delta.sql.DeltaSparkSessionExtension,co.blendata.spark.SparkExtension",
  "type": "textarea"
},
```

4. Open the **conf/zeppelin-site.xml** file from the Zeppelin directory.
5. Update properties value of `zeppelin.enable.same.metastore` to be **true.**

```
<property>
    <name>zeppelin.enable.same.metastore</name>
    <value>true</value>
    <description>Zeppelin Enable Spark same metastore</description>
</property>
```

6.Restart Zeppelin.

---

# Disable Shared Metastore with Zeus

1. Open **conf/Interpreter.json** file from Zeppelin directory.
2. Find the Spark interpreter section. It will look like below example:

```
"spark": {
  "id": "spark",
  "name": "spark",
    .
    .
  "properties": {
    .
    .
    .
    },
  }
}
```

3. Update properties value of `spark.sql.extensions` to be "**io.delta.sql.DeltaSparkSessionExtension**".

```
"spark.sql.extensions": {
  "name": "spark.sql.extensions",
  "value": "io.delta.sql.DeltaSparkSessionExtension",
  "type": "textarea"
},
```

4. Open the **conf/zeppelin-site.xml** file from the Zeppelin directory.
5. Update properties value of `zeppelin.enable.same.metastore` to be **false.**

```
<property>
    <name>zeppelin.enable.same.metastore</name>
    <value>false</value>
    <description>Zeppelin Enable Spark same metastore</description>
</property>
```

6.Restart Zeppelin.

---

## Zeus Renaming

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2664628229/Zeus+Renaming |
| **Last modified** | 2025-10-31T10:14:25.986Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual > Post-Installation > Configuration > Zeus Configuration |

If users need to change the default Zeus name, follow the instructions below to modify the Zeus name.

1. Access Hera database.

```
psql -U hera -h <ip_database> -p 5432 -d hera
```

2. List the information from the **module\_zeus** table to prepare for updating the **module\_zeus** table.

```
select id,name,ref_key from module_zeus;
```

3. List the information from the **module\_license** table to prepare for updating the **module\_license** table.

```
select id,name,module_id,module_reference_key from module_license;
```

4. Update these tables with the new Zeus name using a command below.

```
update module_zeus set name = '<new_zeus_name>' where id = <id_from_step2>;
update module_zeus set ref_key = '<new_zeus_name>-11000' where id = <id_from_step2>;
update module_license set module_reference_key = '<new_zeus_name>-11000' where id = <id_from_step3>;
update module_license set name = '<new_zeus_name>' where id = <id_from_step3>;
```

5. Edit the configuration in **application.properties** to fill in your new Zeus name.

```
…
gtemplar.application.name = <new_zeus_name>
…
```

6. Restart Zeus.
7. Call API to clear the cache in Hera.

```
Method: POST   
URL: https://<hera_ip>:11010/auth/login
Header: Content-Type : application/json
Body:
    {
        "user": {
          "username": "super_admin",
          "password": "<password>"
        },
        "tenantId": 1
    }
==============================================
Method: GET   
URL: https://<hera_ip>:11010/private/module_zeus/cache/invalidate
Header: 
    Content-Type : application/json
    Authorization : {{token}}
    Body:
        {
            "realmId" : 1
        }
==============================================
```

---

## How to Change Hera Port

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2687303682/How+to+Change+Hera+Port |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-11T03:49:28.924Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual |

In case users need to use a different Hera port that is not the standard port from the Blendata Enterprise package, please follow the steps below.

# Step 1: Request License File

1. Go to Blendata License website.
2. Create a new license file and setup the custom feature as per below.

![image-20251106-040151.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2687303682/image-20251106-040151.png?api=v2)

**Example**: Your project is using Hera port 11011, which is not the standard Hera port (11010). Therefore, you must set the parameter in the Custom Features section as follows:

- **Name**: Fill in this parameter “**app\_port**”.
- **Value**: Fill in the port value as “**11011**”.

3. Submit the license request and download the new license file.

# Step 2: Revise Hera Port Setting

1. Go to `hera/config/application.properties` and update the new port number.

   - `server.port`: Fill in a new Hera port number.
   - `gtemplar.application.url`: Fill in Hera service url and port.
   - `airflow.hera.url`: Internal Hera service url.
   - `airflow.hera.public.url`: Public Hera service url.

```
server.port: 11011 
gtemplar.application.url = https://hera_hostname:11011
.
.
.
airflow.hera.url = https://hera_hostname:11011
airflow.hera.public.url=https://hera_hostname:11011
```

2. Go to `hera/config/web.conf` and update the new port number.

   - `apiServer`: Hera hostname or server IP, and port which is reachable from the client machine.

```
"apiServer": {
      "hera": "https://hera_hostname:11011"
},
```

3. Go to `zeus/conf/service.properties`

   - `service.hera.url`: Hera module service url which Zeus connects to.

```
service.hera.url = https://hera_hostname:11011
```

4. Go to `$ZEPPELIN_HOME_PATH/conf/zeppelin-site.xml`

   - `hera.server.port`: Fill in a new Hera port number.
   - `hera.server.public.port`: Fill in a new Hera port number.

```
<property>
    <name>hera.server.port</name>
    <value>11011</value>
    <description>Hera Server Port</description>    
</property>
...
<property>
    <name>hera.server.public.port</name>
    <value>11011</value>
    <description>Hera Server Public Port</description>    
</property>
```

5. Go to `browncow/config/service.properties`

   - `service.hera.url`: Hera module service url which Brown cow connects to.

```
service.hera.url = https://hera_hostname:11011
```

# Step 3: Activate The New License file

1. Go to Blendata Enterprise website.
2. Go to “**Setting**” and click “**License Management**“menu :lms:.
3. Click three dots button and select “**Active New License**“.

![lm12.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2687303682/lm12.png?api=v2)

5. Click “**Open File Browser**” to Upload the License file.

![lm13.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2687303682/lm13.png?api=v2)

6. Click “**Activate**” button :lm2: to activate the license.

---

## Restart All Modules Guideline

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **URL** | https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/bde460/pages/2706800642/Restart+All+Modules+Guideline |
| **Last modified** | 2025-11-17T11:36:16.119Z |
| **Breadcrumb** | Blendata Enterprise V.4.6.0 > Administrator User Manual |

# Step 1: Stop all data services and Modules

## Stop all data services

All data services. (online data service configuration from web browser)

1. Stop all stream services in the Service Management menu.

![image-20251113-083527.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2706800642/image-20251113-083527.png?api=v2)

2. Stop all table update job services in the Service Management menu.

![image-20251113-085811.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2706800642/image-20251113-085811.png?api=v2)

3. Verify other services in the Service Management menu that it is not started at that time.

![image-20251113-085848.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2706800642/image-20251113-085848.png?api=v2)

4. Stop all aggregate table job services in the data processing section.

![image-20251113-085939.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2706800642/image-20251113-085939.png?api=v2)

5. Verify other services in the data processing section that it is not started at that time.
6. (Optional) Verify custom services (if use) that have not started at that time.
7. (Optional) Stop and verify chain service (if use) services that have not started at that time.

![image-20251113-101222.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2706800642/image-20251113-101222.png?api=v2)

8. Stop and verify all workflows in the Workflow Management menu (if use) that has not started at that time.

![image-20251113-101252.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2706800642/image-20251113-101252.png?api=v2)

9. Stop and verify all notebooks in the Notebook menu (if use) that has not started at that time (schedule) including stream service or others.

![image-20251113-101335.png](https://blendata.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/2706800642/image-20251113-101335.png?api=v2)

## Notification

1. Go to Notification directory.

```
cd /opt/blendata/noti
```

2. Run a command `bin/stop-noti.sh`.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 noti-server]# bin/stop-noti.sh
stopping process id [19015]
wait for 5s before recheck if process is killed successfully...
```

3. Check Process Status.

   - Go to Notification directory and run command `bin/checkPIDStatus.sh`.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 noti-server]# bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
===== Templar Noti Server Check Process Status =====
    noti-server [ 19015 ] is  stopped
==========================================
```

## Workflow and Airflow

1. Use command SSH to connect airflow server(s).
2. Go to the workflow home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /opt/blendata/workflow-agent
```

3. Stop workflow-agent module (Use the command below).

```
bin/stop-workflow-agent.sh
```

4. Verify that the workflow-agent module has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

5. Go to the airflow home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /opt/blendata/airflow
```

6. Stop airflow-webserver and airflow-scheduler module (Use the command below).

```
airflow/bin/stop-airflow-webserver.sh
airflow/bin/stop-airflow-scheduler.sh
```

7. Verify that the airflow module has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

## Zeppelin

1. Use command SSH to connect zeppelin server(s).
2. Go to Zeppelin directory.

```
cd /opt/blendata/zeppelin
```

3. Run a command below to stop Zeppelin.

```
bin/stop-zeppelin.sh
```

4. Verify that the zeppelin module has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

## Zeus

1. Use command SSH to connect with zeus server(s).
2. Go to Zeus directory.

```
cd /opt/blendata/zeus
```

3. Run a command `bin/stop-zeus.sh` to stop Zeus.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 zeus]# bin/stop-zeus.sh
stopping process id [19762]
wait for 5s before recheck if process is killed successfully...
zeus is stopped successfully...
zeus server is stopped...
```

4. Verify that zeus service has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

## Spark Worker and Spark Master

1. Use command SSH to connect spark master server(s)
2. Go to spark home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /opt/blendata/spark
```

3. Stop all Spark workers via Spark master.

```
./sbin/stop-slaves.sh
```

4. Verify on Spark master UI that all spark workers have dead.
5. Stop a Spark master.

```
./sbin/stop-master.sh
```

6. Verify that users cannot access to the Spark master UI.

## Hera

1. Use command SSH to connect Hera server(s).
2. Go to Hera directory using command below.

```
cd /opt/blendata/hera
```

3. Run a command `bin/stop-hera.sh` to stop Hera.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 hera]# bin/stop-hera.sh
stopping process id [11476]
wait for 5s before recheck if process is killed successfully...
hera is stopped successfully...
hera server is stopped...
```

4. Verify that Hera service has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

## Cerberus

1. Use command SSH to connect Cerberus server(s).
2. Go to Cerberus directory.

```
cd /opt/blendata/cerberus
```

3. Run a command `bin/stop-cerberus.sh` to stop Cerberus.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 ~]# cd /opt/blendata/cerberus
[root@blendata-dev-01 cerberus]# bin/stop-cerberus.sh
stopping process id [27206]
wait for 5s before recheck if process is killed successfully...
cerberus is stopped successfully...
cerberus server is stopped...
```

4. Verify that the Cerberus module has stopped (Use the command below).

```
bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
```

## Database

1. Check the database service status using command below.

- PostgreSQL database:

```
systemctl status postgresql
```

- MySQL database:

```
systemctl status mysql
```

2. Stop the database service using command below.

- PostgreSQL database:

```
systemctl stop postgresql
```

- MySQL database:

```
systemctl stop mysql
```

---

# Step 2: Start all Modules

## Database

1. Start the database using command below.

- PostgreSQL database:

```
systemctl start postgresql
```

- MySQL database:

```
systemctl start mysql
```

2. Check the database status.

- PostgreSQL database:

```
systemctl status postgresql
```

- MySQL database:

```
systemctl status mysql
```

## Cerberus

1. Go to Cerberus directory and run command `bin/start-cerberus.sh`.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 ~]# cd /opt/blendata/cerberus
[root@blendata-dev-01 cerberus]# bin/start-cerberus.sh
Starting CERBERUS server...
```

2. Check Process Status

   - Go to Cerberus directory and run command `bin/checkPIDStatus.sh`.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 ~]# cd /opt/blendata/cerberus
[root@blendata-dev-01 cerberus]# bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
===== G-Templar Check Process Status =====
    cerberus [ 28137 ] is  running
```

## Hera

1. Go to Hera directory and run command `bin/start-hera.sh`.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 hera]# cd /opt/blendata/hera
[root@blendata-dev-01 hera]# bin/start-hera.sh
Starting HERA server...
```

2. Check Process Status.

   1. Go to Hera directory and run command `bin/checkPIDStatus.sh`.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 hera]# bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
===== G-Templar Check Process Status =====
    hera [ 11476 ] is  running
==========================================
```

## Spark Master and Spark Worker

1. Run command `bin/start-spark.sh`.
2. Use command SSH to connect spark master server(s)
3. Go to spark home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /opt/blendata/spark
```

4. Start all Spark master.

```
./sbin/start-master.sh
```

5. Verify that users can access to the Spark master UI.
6. Start a Spark workers via Spark master.

```
./sbin/start-slaves.sh spark://<ip_master>:7077
```

7. Verify on Spark master UI that all spark workers appear in the Spark master UI.

## Zeus

1. Go to Zeus directory and run a command `bin/start-zeus.sh`.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 zeus]# bin/start-zeus.sh
Start engine..
Spark submit /opt/blendata/zeus/lib/zeus-4.6.0.jar 2g
...
```

2. Check Process Status.

   - Go to Zeus directory and run command `bin/checkPIDStatus.sh`.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 zeus]# bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
===== G-Templar Check Process Status =====
    zeus [ 19762 ] is  running
    spark.deploy.master.Master [ 32687 ] is  running
    spark.deploy.worker.Worker [ 312 ] is  running
==========================================
```

## Zeppelin

1. Go to Zeus directory and run a command `bin/start-zeppelin.sh`.

```
cd /opt/blendata/zeppelin
bin/start-zeppelin.sh
```

This command is used when your Zeppelin uses the same Spark application as Zeus.

2. Check Process Status.

   - Go to Zeppelin directory and run command `bin/checkPIDStatus.sh`.

If your project uses a Spark cluster, start Spark with the command below instead.

```
cd /opt/blendata/zeppelin/spark
sbin/start-all.sh
```

## Airflow/Workflow

1. Go to the airflow home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /opt/blendata/airflow
```

2. Run commands below to start airflow-webserver and airflow-scheduler.

```
bin/start-airflow-scheduler.sh
bin/start-airflow-webserver.sh
```

3. Check Process Status.

   - Go to Airflow directory and run command `bin/checkPIDStatus.sh`.

```
$ bin/checkPIDStatus.sh
===== Blendata Check Process Status =====
Webserver
    airflow-webserver [ 38511 ] is  running
    airflow-webserver-monitor [ 38508 ] is  running
    airflow-webserver-workers [ 553653 620626 687798 754981 822151 889533 ] is  running
Scheduler
    airflow-scheduler [ 38009 ] is  running
    daq-processor-manager [ 38195 ] is  running
==========================================
```

4. Go to the workflow home path (Use the command below).

```
cd /opt/blendata/workflow-agent
```

5. Run a command below to start the Workflow agent.

```
bin/start-workflow-agent.sh
```

6. Check Process Status.

   - Go to Workflow directory and run command `bin/checkPIDStatus.sh`.

## Notification

1. Go to Notification directory and run command `bin/start-noti.sh`.

```
[root@blendata-dev-01 ~]# cd /opt/blendata/noti
[root@blendata-dev-01 noti-server]# bin/start-noti.sh
```

2. Check Process Status.

   - Go to Notification directory and run command `bin/checkPIDStatus.sh`.
